When Christ arises from the dead and appears in a bodily form (Luke 24:37), He answers: ["A spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me have." ]{.class_4258}A bodily apparition, therefore, is perfectly understood by the disciples as being a [spirit ]{.class_4257}and not a ghost. The insertion of "ghost" in Matthew 14 is the inserting of a modern word to no intent. ["A spirit"]{.class_4258} is an apparition or phantasis (Gk.) in either Testament (cf. Job 4:15--16). Properly speaking, a "ghost" is the appearance of someone deceased who has returned from the dead. No disciple has this in mind in Matthew 14, for Christ has not yet died. When He [does ]{.class_4257}die and rise, they suppose Him to be a ["spirit"]{.class_4258}[ ]{.class_4257}(not a ghost)!

There is no explanation for Peter's curious request to "come to Him on the water" unless he just didn't believe that it was Jesus and wanted to find out. At any rate, he gets his request. The ["if it be thou"]{.class_4258} is strangely reminiscent of the infidelity displayed by the dying thieves in Luke 23 and by Satan himself in Luke 4:1--8.

14:30 But when he saw the wind boisterous, he was afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried, saying, Lord, save me.

31 And immediately Jesus stretched forth his hand, and caught him, and said unto him, O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?

32 And when they were come into the ship, the wind []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1439.xhtml_page_250}ceased.

33 Then they that were in the ship came and worshipped him, saying, Of a truth thou art the Son of God.

Speaking doctrinally, the entire passage drives home again and again the idea that, here, a [genuine miracle is ]{.class_4257}taking place. There is no hop‑scotch on ice pans going on here. The wind is blowing. Peter is sinking in water, not slipping on ice, and his deliverance is hailed by those in the boat as a miracle deservative of [worshipping Christ. ]{.class_4257}Verse 33 is one of the most difficult verses in the Bible for Westcott and Hort to erase from the text, and this will await the work of future "translations." In the meantime, the Liberal is content with saying that these poor "superstitious fishermen" just didn't understand "natural phenomenon" (which in itself is the grossest conceit and most blind reasoning---who would understand the natural phenomenon of Galilean waters better than [commercial fishermen? ]{.class_4257}Certainly not preachers in New York and Chicago!). This is like a bachelor who had never been married (the pope) making a dogmatic decree that he is against "birth control"! Some authority!

Peter's short prayer is instructive. It has three words, and it accomplishes the same thing as a petition repeated a dozen times containing two dozen words. The "vain repetitions" of the heathen (see Matt. 6:7) cannot accomplish anything, let alone a hair‑raising rescue from drowning. Peter is condemned by most expositors for his lack of faith in the attempted seawalk, but rarely does one find a commentator astute enough to observe that Peter is the [only one ]{.class_4257}in the boat that has enough faith [to try it.]{.class_4257}

["And immediately" ]{.class_4258}is the response to the cry of any man asking for salvation (see Rom. 10:13). There may be delays on healing, bills, notes, jobs, family troubles, etc., but the prayer for salvation, in the New Testament, is answered immediately (Acts 2:21). Peter does not have to agonize for fifteen minutes at an altar, and he certainly doesn't waste time thumbing through the index of a prayer book or missal to find out "what kind of prayer an apostle should pray while drowning." Access to Christ is immediate for the Christian in this age (Heb. 4:15--16); and while the Catholic priests make a living acting as bell‑boys, secretaries, car hops, vice-[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1439.xhtml_page_251}presidents, armed guards, liason men, assistant office boys, and go‑betweens, the real Christian simply ignores them, knocks on the door of the High Priest, goes in, and sits down in the living room as long as he likes (1 Tim. 2:5). [Why waste time with the janitor when you can see the president?]{.class_4257}

[​]{.class_4259}["And when they were come into the ship\...." ]{.class_4258}The boat stops rocking when Jesus gets on board (and the typology is on the peace of mind and heart which comes from Eph. 2:13--14). Christ can be in a rocking boat, and it will still rock until He commands it to be still (8:26). This is the typology of the saved man who has "the Lord in the vessel" (see 2 Cor. 4:7).

["Of a truth thou art the Son of God." ]{.class_4258}Again, He receives worship without comment. Verses like this one, and John 20:28, disqualify Jesus, forever, from being any kind of a "Teacher" or "Example," if He were not "God manifest in the flesh" (1 Tim. 3:16). No amount of pagan interpretation (like that of Fosdick, Peale, Blake, Kagawa, Gandhi, Gibran, et al.) placed over the passages can nullify the glaring fact that Jesus treats Himself like He was the final answer to everyone's problems. Notice John 14:6---no other way to God the Father; John 14:19---"you can't live unless I live"; John 15:5---without Him you can't do anything; John 16:9---"everybody in the world is a sinner because he will not believe on ME"; John 10:8, 11--12---"everybody but Me is a thief."

One of the great ecumenical minds of our day has remarked that Jesus was given to "overstatement" concerning Himself, and another has said that His "egotism" was almost "neurotic." Both of these statements are certainly true---if He was not God [manifest in the flesh. ]{.class_4257}The robed Girl Scouts of the NCCC, who cannot face the results of teaching that Christ was an example (exactly as they stick their head in the sand when viewing the results of teaching evolution in the schools), gloss over such passages as Matthew 11:28 by interpreting the words to mean that Jesus had enough "spiritual insight" to grasp [their own ]{.class_4257}interpretation of the passage. That is: "What Jesus is referring to here, is not His own Deity, but rather He is trying to thrust a deep spiritual truth, or generalization, through the carnal minds of His novice followers, by using common forms of speech which they could understand. In actuality, He is pointing out []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1439.xhtml_page_252}that the true spiritual rest is to be obtained by following the principles of\...." (But you can only drive a car so far on four flats and a dead generator.) ["We]{.class_4258} [cannot tell what he saith"]{.class_4258} (John 16:18) would be the proper comment, and it should be engraved over the door lintel of every liberal church in the world.

A man who deceives people into thinking that "ocean calms" (14:32) and miraculous deliverances (14:30) are due to the fact that He is enough God to be worshipped is nothing but a hoax and a charlatan, and hanging is too good for him (see Rev 13:14--15). If Jesus Christ were NOT God manifest in the flesh, He was one of the biggest religious fakirs that ever lived, second only to a "pope."

14:34 And when they were gone over, they came into the land of Gennesaret.

35 And when the men of that place had knowledge of him, they sent out into all that country round about, and brought unto him all that were diseased;

36 And besought him that they might only touch the hem of his garment: and as many as touched were made perfectly whole.

A little hard for an "Example" or "Teacher" to do, if He were not God incarnate! (You don't find it every day!) The passage needs little comment (see 8:28 with comments and 9:35, 11:5). ["The hem of his garment"]{.class_4258} (see comments on 9:21).

[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1527.xhtml}

# Chapter 15 {.heading_84-0}

15:1 Then came to Jesus scribes and Pharisees, which were of Jerusalem, saying,

2 Why do thy disciples transgress the tradition of the elders? for they wash not their hands when they eat bread.

Notice again that opposition follows the work of the Holy Spirit (see 9:3, 34, 12:2, 14, 24). The NCCC of [that day ]{.class_4257}never rejoiced at a miracle of healing or a miracle of feeding. Everytime, they questioned Jesus' doctrine and authority. (See Commentary on Genesis, Gen. 3:1.)

["Why do thy disciples transgress the tradition of the elders?" ]{.class_4258}At least the question is an honest one, which is more than you can say for the ones in Matthew 22. ["The tradition of the elders"]{.class_4258} (Mark 7:1--13) embraces a vast body of trivia, which would be too laborsome to list. In addition to the Talmudic traditions referred to here, there are "the traditions of men" (Col. 2:8), which include the teachings of Pliny, Plato, Hobbes, Josephus, Origen, Eusebius, Einstein, Jerome, Papias, Hume, Clement, Irenaeus, Westcott and Hort, Griesbach, Lachmann, Tischendorf, et al., and the "vain traditions" (1 Pet. 1:18), which include all "plans of redemption" conditioned on works. Included in the overall picture, one will find Easter bunnies, goose for Christmas, haloes on the heads of demons (excuse me, "saints!"), mistletoe, ashes in the palm and on the forehead, phylacteries the size of biscuits, beads and candles, robes and tonsures, bells and holy water, prayer wheels and Greek grammars, statues and the Didache, transubstantiation and baptismal regeneration, missals and handkerchiefs on the heads, Christmas and Mardi Gras, Nestle's Critical Apparatus and the [ASV]{.class_4257}; and if there be any other thing contrary to sound doctrine, it is briefly comprehended under the saying: [rubbish]{.class_4257}.

Washing before eating every Whataburger or Ham-and-Cheese on Rye is not in ["the commandment of God."]{.class_4258} It might be found in a hygiene book on home medicine, and it might keep you from getting infested with worms in an Oriental country, but it has nothing to do with the Mosaic Law. "Washings" under the Law were for specific things at specific times, not before every snack []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1527.xhtml_page_254}[or even before every meal]{.class_4257} (note Luke 11:38).

15:3 But he answered and said unto them, Why do ye also transgress the commandment of God by your tradition?

4 For God commanded, saying, Honour thy father and mother: and, He that curseth father or mother, let him die the death.

5 But ye say, Whosoever shall say to his father or his mother, It is a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me;

6 And honour not his father or his mother, he shall be free. Thus have ye made the commandment of God of none effect by your tradition.

In His usual rude way, the Lord ignores the question and does not even bother to answer it (see Matt. 21:23).

The passage is important to the Bible believer, for it shows that the greatest damage that can be done to the word is not that which is done by papists burning Bibles or Communists confiscating Bibles. It is done by Christian scholars who insert tradition over the word in such a way as to make the Bible ineffective. (Note Mark 7:13---["word of God,"]{.class_4258} not "commandments" only.) The passage used by priests to prove that "tradition" is of equal authority to the Scripture is 2 Thessalonians 2:15. While aborting this passage from the context (2 Thess. 3:6, 14---the Pauline instructions to New Testament churches) and applying it to the writings of Cyprian, Ignatius, and Papias, the Roman communion goes a step beyond and accepts in practice traditions [contrary to the word ]{.class_4257}(see Matt. 1:25 and comments on Matt. 23:9 and 16:16--18); and traditions contrary to the word take precedence over the word itself. This is the method used by Westcott and Hort in the construction of the Greek text of the [RSV ]{.class_4257}and [ASV]{.class_4257} (1901). Where a Greek reading contradicts the remaining verses in the New Testament, it is often accepted as being superior to the reading which agrees with the remaining verses (note Luke 2:33).

Commentators delight in limiting their comments (and the words of Scripture) to the local situation of the historical past in which the statement was made. This in itself is []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1527.xhtml_page_255}["making the word of God of none effect."]{.class_4258} The sin of the scribes and Pharisees in Mark chapter 7 [is to be applied to the scribes and Pharisees of today.]{.class_4257}

["For God commanded\...."]{.class_4258} Jesus is quoting Exodus 20:12 and 21:17.

["But ye say\...."]{.class_4258} Exactly! That is the whole point. The Holy Spirit said that Jesus was ["God...manifest in the flesh"]{.class_4258} ["but ye say"]{.class_4258} ([RSV, ASV, RV]{.class_4257}, etc.) something else. God said that ["his own blood"]{.class_4258} purchased the church (Acts 20:28), ["but ye say"]{.class_4258} ([ASV]{.class_4257}) something else. God said to follow Him (Eph. 5:1), ["but ye say"]{.class_4258} ([RV, ASV, RSV]{.class_4257}) something else. God said that Mary had other children besides Christ (Psa. 69:8), ["but ye say"]{.class_4258} (priests, popes, and cardinals) something else.

["For God commanded\...but ye say"]{.class_4258} is not an isolated text which ran out of gas in A.D. 32. It comes right on down the freeway, at 120 m.p.h., and sails slap through every convoy of traditions, on either side of the road, for 1,960 miles. In view of what God ["commanded"]{.class_4258} does what ["ye say"]{.class_4258} really amount to anything at all (Rom. 3:3--4)?

(But let us return to the text, lest we be accused of "poor scholarship"! This is an expression reserved for anyone who makes an [application of text ]{.class_4257}to the scholars themselves!)

["Whosoever shall say to his father or his mother, It is a gift\..."]{.class_4258} (cf. Mark 7:11). The best comment on the passage is Proverbs 28:24. (See also Deut. 14:22--26.) Proverbs 28:27 is an additional comment. The situation is that a young man wishes to salvage some of his earnings for a rainy day, and he fears that his aged parents will "eat it up" with sickness and debility. Instead of caring for his parents (after their retirement) with this "hard earned money," the young man goes to his nearest "priest" and says, "Holy Father, what I have deposited in the First National Bank of Bethsaida, I now declare 'Corban' for the glory of Holy Mother Church," etc. "Bless you, my son," purrs the priest, as he makes the sign of Tammuz (revised by Constantine) in the air, and goes back to the Council to declare some more untaxable income. The young man, happy to have God's blessing (!) on his Social Security plan, returns home with a clear conscience. He can now put Mommy and Daddy in a county []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1527.xhtml_page_256}rest home and not spend a dime on them, because money declared ["Corban"]{.class_4258} is dedicated to the temple, and thus, it has been put to a more holy use than "supporting aged parents." Remarkable contrivance (see Matt. 27:6)! After Momma and Daddy have passed away, the young man gives the priest 50 percent of the money, and he keeps 50 percent, and everyone is happy. ["God commanded\...but ye say."]{.class_4258}

["Thus have ye made the commandment of God of none effect by your tradition."]{.class_4258} (For further details, read [American Freedom and Catholic Power,]{.class_4257} by Blanshard.)

15:7 Ye hypocrites, well did Esaias prophesy of you, saying,

8 This people draweth nigh unto me with their mouth, and honoureth me with their lips; but their heart is far from me.

9 But in vain they do worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.

["Ye hypocrites"]{.class_4258} (a favorite designation for religious leaders---see 23:13--14, 6:2, 5, 16; Luke 6:42, 11:44; Mark 7:6; Matt 7:5, 15:7, 16:3, 22:18). The "followers of Jesus," in the NCCC, seem unable to apply the title to anyone but Bible-believing Independents! [The "hypocrites" of Christ's day were the ruling religious class which was in good standing with the government.]{.class_4257} Someone has made a misapplication of text!

Jesus pats Isaiah on the back with one hand and then puts His other hand on the trouble---heart trouble as usual (Prov. 23:7, 4:23; Rom. 10:9--10). ["Their heart is far from me."]{.class_4258} That is, their mouth is reciting "Hail Mary," "Our Father," the Doxology, the Postlude, Prelude, Benediction, Offertory, and Gloria Patri; but their heart is meditating on car payments, new lawn furniture, grass, golf scores, pro football games, hunting and fishing seasons, banking accounts, sales deals, opportunities for fornication and adultery ("fun" in the language of new morality), etc.

Nor can scholarship avoid the implications of the text. There is no scholar (Hebrew or Greek) who does not understand the passage. Limiting it to the contemporary rabbis of that day, and then going []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1527.xhtml_page_257}into a detailed account of Talmudic ritual, is only an attempt to make the ["word of God of none effect."]{.class_4258}

["Commandments of men"]{.class_4258} taught as ["the doctrines of God"]{.class_4257} is not a reference to some dimly remote practice which died out before French\
rationalism. If one made a list of the commandments of men which are taught today as the commandments (or doctrines) of God, he could fill a telephone book with them.

["In vain they do worship me."]{.class_4258} God seeks for worshippers who worship in spirit and truth (John 4), and the ritualistic, sacramental hodge-podge which goes on today is little more than a magpie's nest of traditions. The Babylonian Talmud has nothing on it. The passage in Matthew 15:6--9 is as much a rebuke of modern Christianity as ancient Rabbinical Judaism. (You can drag a piece of string all over town, but you can't push it to save your life. You will not push the word anywhere, even if you drag it to death.)

15: 10 And he called the multitude, and said unto them, Hear, and understand:

11 Not that which goeth into the mouth defileth a man; but that which cometh out of the mouth, this defileth a man.

12 Then came his disciples, and said unto him, Knowest thou that the Pharisees were offended, after they heard this saying?

13 But he answered and said, Every plant, which my heavenly Father hath not planted, shall be rooted up.

14 Let them alone: they be blind leaders of the blind. And if the blind lead the blind, both shall fall into the ditch.

["Hear, and understand!"]{.class_4258} The Master Teacher is about to teach; what He teaches will be the ["Commandments of God,"]{.class_4257} and not ["the doctrines of men."]{.class_4257}

["That which cometh out of the mouth"]{.class_4258} (see vss. 18--19). Mortal things enter the mouth but immortal things come out of it. The context here is the [good things ]{.class_4257}which come out of the mouth while [bad things ]{.class_4257}are in the heart---see 12:33--35. It is the singing of "I love to tell the story," when you haven't told it for fourteen years. It []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1527.xhtml_page_258}is the standing of the congregation to sing: "Throw out the life-line," when they don't have enough spiritual strength to hang up a clothesline. Although [bad things ]{.class_4257}come out later (vss. 19--20), it is plain that the thing which has defiled the religious leaders is a lot of pious clap-trap and mouthy religion that had no connection with their true state of mind.

(In my private collection of pictures I have two excellent paintings by German Protestants--- which will never make the Louvre! One is a monk praying over a meal, and the other is a first century Pharisee crossing the street and about to run head-on into a building. Close examination reveals that the Pharisee is looking at a well-dressed woman down the block, and the monk is squeezing a lemon into a punchbowl!)

"This defileth a man."

["Knowest thou that the Pharisees were offended, after they heard this saying?"]{.class_4258} (I reckon they were!) I rather imagine David got upset at Nathan's saying (2 Sam. 12), and I don't suppose that Ahab exactly had a "shoutin' spell" over Elijah's message (1 Kings 21:20--24). Felix and Agrippa were not exactly "delighted" with Paul's sermon, and the testimony of the centuries is that where sin is [cherished]{.class_4257}, truth is not [relished]{.class_4257}. The Pharisees recognized the shoe-size as being their own, and they got a shoe-horn and stepped in; the shoes fit good (Matt. 21:45). Any Bible preacher is familiar with this truth. He is not responsible for "strays" on the firing range after the warnings have been posted; he simply fires at the target, and anything that gets in the way is bound to get hit.

["Every plant, which my heavenly Father hath not planted\...both shall fall into the ditch."]{.class_4258} Matthew 23:13, 15 is the same song in another key. The plant, here (as that of Matt. 3:10), is not so much a hyper-Calvinist designation of reprobated individuals, as it is a statement on the Pharisees' system: doctrines (vs. 9), sayings (vs. 12), traditions (vs. 3), and all.

The "planting," here, is the equivalent of Jeremiah 32:41; 2 Samuel 7:10; Isaiah 17:10; and Exodus 15:17. The "rooting up" is plainly the process described in Deuteronomy 29:28 and Proverbs 2:22. The ["blind leaders of the blind"]{.class_4258} (see John 9:41) are blinder than blind Bartimaeus, and represent a blind nation (Rom. 11:7--9) []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1527.xhtml_page_259}typified by "Bar Jesus" in Acts 13:6--10.

["Blind leaders of the blind"]{.class_4258} simply means that the religious leaders of Christ's day were unconverted (John 12:40), hell-bound (Matt. 23:15), white-washed (Matt. 23:27), self-righteous (Luke 18:11), unclean (Matt. 23:26) serpents (Matt 23:33). Their converts were "like unto them," and not a one of them, from Caiaphas to Judas, had any assurance of eternal salvation. \[See comments on "Fruit-Stand Religion" (Gen. 4:3--6)\]. Today, those men are easy to spot (see comments on Matt. 23 and 16:6, 11).

["Both shall fall into the ditch."]{.class_4258} Not a very nice thing to say about priests and Rabbis! [The ditch, in Proverbs 23:27, is the Ecumenical Lady of Revelation chapter 17.]{.class_4257} She, also, is a type of Hell (Prov. 23:27, 22:14).

15:15 Then answered Peter and said unto him, Declare unto us this parable.

16 And Jesus said, Are ye also yet without understanding?

17 Do not ye yet understand, that whatsoever entereth in at the mouth goeth into the belly, and is cast out into the draught?

18 But those things which proceed out of the mouth come forth from the heart; and they defile the man.

19 For out of the heart proceed evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false witness, blasphemies:

20 These are the things which defile a man: but to eat with unwashen hands defileth not a man.

The discourse is loaded. Somehow the commentators (all inclusive) have failed to notice that verse 19 is a prophecy on the sins about to be committed by the Pharisees who ["were offended"]{.class_4258} at the saying. The passage is not only a tremendous discourse on psychology---from the point of view of absolute truth, not "behaviorism," "gestalt," etc.---it is God telling the Pharisee what is going on in his heart, which he will eventually [perform]{.class_4257}. Notice carefully: ["evil thoughts"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 12:25, 24:48, 22:15), ["murders"]{.class_4258} (John 12:10---They were going to murder Lazarus!), ["adulteries"]{.class_4258} (John 8:1--8---Where was the man involved in the adultery? How did they catch the [woman ]{.class_4257}without the [man]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1527.xhtml_page_260}? \[Lev. 20:10\]), ["fornications"]{.class_4258} (the only one in the list not mentioned later), ["thefts"]{.class_4258} (see 21:38), ["false witness"]{.class_4258} (26:60), and ["blasphemies"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 27:41). That is, the religious leaders are guilty on six out of seven counts in the indictment. (In the account in Mark 7:17--23, they are guilty on eleven out of thirteen.) While avoiding entering into Gentile buildings,[ "lest they should be defiled" ]{.class_4258}(John 18:28), the long-robed "fathers" stain their hands with the blood of a sinless Man and debauch their souls with "handwashing traditions" (15:2). \[The attitude has few precedents on earth, unless one remembers that there are twentieth-century Americans who consider it a sin to sing hymns in a Bible-believing church, while at the same time they can prayerfully dump cigarette ashes on their bingo cards!\]

["Declare unto us this parable."]{.class_4258} There is no parable to it; it is a reality. Peter and the other disciples often read it [spiritual ]{.class_4257}when it is [literal]{.class_4257}, and vice versa (see Matt. 16:7, 18).

["Do not ye yet understand?"]{.class_4258} He has just cried, ["Hear, and understand"]{.class_4258} (vs. 10) and, therefore, had a right to be indignant about Peter's request.

["Whatsoever entereth in at the mouth goeth into the belly\...."]{.class_4258} The popes would take exception to this Divine Decree of Absolute Truth. They would insist that when "some things" enter the mouth and go ["into the belly,"]{.class_4258} they are NOT ["cast out into the draught."]{.class_4258} The fact that Jesus says this is so never bothered a "papa." To the popes, the fermented liquor and bread ("host") taken in the Eucharist receives some magical properties, in the hands of the long-robed father, which enable them to pass through the bones and stomach and intestines (osmosis perhaps?) and become part of the soul (or spirit) of the body which does the eating. This is explained at great length in the writings of the Neo-Aristotelian and Neo-Platonic schoolmen, and the sophistries involved are sufficient to make a full curriculum of studies at Loyola University, New Orleans.

It is pathetic that our Lord Jesus Christ did not have the benefit of this higher type of scholasticism, for in His own crude way He said:

1\. ["The flesh profiteth nothing"]{.class_4258}[ ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1527.xhtml_page_261}(John 6:63---the context is His "flesh and blood!").

2\. [Whatever ]{.class_4257}goes into the mouth goes into the belly and eventually down the sewer.

This crude and primitive theology "offended" Jerome, Augustine, and Thomas Aquinas nearly as badly as it did the Pharisees (see John 6:61; ["offend"]{.class_4258}). So they helped our Lord Jesus Christ out by dressing up his theology in something decent---Plato and Aristotle---and now it is fit for respectable society!

Notwithstanding, the absolute truth on the matter---by the author of all truth---is ["Whatsoever entereth in at the mouth goeth into the belly, and is cast out into the draught."]{.class_4258}

And lest anyone should fail to grasp the meaning: ["are ye ALSO yet without understanding?"]{.class_4258} (It is evident that a good many of them are, and they didn't die out with Herod and Pilate.)

["The draught"]{.class_4258} (2 Kings 10:27) is plainly a disposal for human manure. Darius words it quite strongly in Ezra 6:11, and there are several Army expressions for it which I will not repeat. The facts are plain.

1\. What you eat goes into your mouth (or your blood stream via medicine).

2\. It goes into your ["belly"]{.class_4258} (or your intestines) as cast off waste.

3\. It leaves your body and goes to a place of disposal.

4\. It is carried off in sewage, or by water, to other places in the ground.

[This plain statement of clear fact is the one that is rejected in the Roman Mass.]{.class_4257} Cannibalism plus Black Magic does not spell "C-H-R-I-S-T-I-A-N-I-T-Y," not even where the "Church Fathers" make the mistake of calling the Lord's Supper a "sacrifice."

["But to eat with unwashen hands defileth not a man."]{.class_4258} First Timothy 4:1--5 takes care of clean and unclean meats, and clean and unclean hands. Where the heart is right and the truth has been accepted (and the meal has been blessed with prayer and the word), [steak ]{.class_4257}on Friday or [pork ]{.class_4257}on Saturday will be more beneficial to your health and well being than following the diet recommended by the ["blind leaders of the blind."]{.class_4258}

15:21 Then Jesus went thence, and departed into the coasts of Tyre and Sidon.

22 And, behold, a woman of Canaan came out of the same coasts, and cried unto him, saying, Have mercy on me, O Lord, thou son of David; my daughter is grievously vexed with a devil.

23 But he answered her not a word. And his disciples came and besought him, saying, Send her away; for she crieth after us.

24 But he answered and said, I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

The ["coasts"]{.class_4258} would indicate the [borders ]{.class_4257}of Phoenicia which touch the Galilean border for fifty to fifty-five miles. The word ["coasts"]{.class_4258} is defined in an [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 as meaning "border" (see Exod. 10:19; Deut. 2:4; Judg. 11:20). The old English often has two meanings: for example, ["let"]{.class_4258} meaning to oppose or [hinder]{.class_4257}, but also to [allow]{.class_4257} or [yield to]{.class_4257}; again, ["prevent"]{.class_4258} meaning to [stop something]{.class_4257} or to ["go ahead of a thing."]{.class_4257} Notice how the [AV ]{.class_4257}uses "SEA coasts" when indicating the ocean (Luke 6:17).

["A woman of Canaan."]{.class_4258} (She is a Greek, according to Mark 7:26---["A Syrophenician by nation."]{.class_4258})

The woman is a ["Greek"]{.class_4258} only in that she comes from a Greek province (Syria), which is at this time a part of the Roman Empire. Her race (see Commentary on Genesis, Gen. 10) is Syria-Hamitic. (The "Hittites" were of this extraction, as were the Phoenicians.) With this background, the following conversation becomes intelligible. The woman is[ "a servant of servants,"]{.class_4258} in addition to being a Gentile "sinner" (Gal. 2:15). ["Out of the same coasts"]{.class_4258} implies she is "out of bounds" when she approaches Jesus. The careful student will observe, however, that [He is out of bounds also ]{.class_4257}when she reaches Him (see Matt. 10:5--6)! (Contrary to the theories of Bullinger and Calvin, it would appear that if you can catch God in an undispensational situation, you can get a prayer answered even if you weren't one of the "elect"!)

["Have mercy on me, O Lord, thou son of David."]{.class_4258} The woman is not as dumb as she looks. (I don't know how she looked, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1527.xhtml_page_263}but that is the expression!) She comes with the sinner's plea, assuming the proper place and asking for the right thing, in the right way. She addresses her benefactor as ["Lord"]{.class_4258} (which He is, Phil. 2:8--11) and outdoes the Pharisees in her knowledge of Scripture, for she gives Jesus His correct Messianic title! ["O\...thou son of David"]{.class_4258} (see 21:9).

["But he answered her not a word."]{.class_4258} The daughter, ["grievously vexed with a devil,"]{.class_4258} is going to stay that way from all appearances. The Canaanite draws a blank. The heavens are "brass." The woman evidently follows the disciples for several miles, whimpering and crying as only a woman can, and the disciples get fed up with it and ask Jesus to send her away. Their request, however, stems from the same source as the unjust judge (Luke 11:8). They are not saying: "Tell her to get out of here." They are saying: "Lord, for goodness sake give the woman her petition and send her packing! She's a pain in the neck." (That this is the drift of the request is apparent by His answer.)

["I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel."]{.class_4258} Then they did not ask Him merely to dismiss the Canaanite, but to dismiss her by giving her her request (see notes on 9:27, 10:6).

15: 25 Then came she and worshipped him, saying, Lord, help me.

26 But he answered and said, It is not meet to take the chi1dren's bread, and cast it to dogs.

27 And she said, Truth, Lord: yet the dogs eat of the crumbs which fall from their masters' table.

28 Then Jesus answered and said unto her, O woman, great is thy faith: be it unto thee even as thou wilt. And her daughter was made whole from that very hour.

Here is a beautiful study in prayer.

1\. We are turned down to see if we are in earnest.

2\. We are turned down to show us how helpless we are in the face of emergencies.

3\. We are turned down until we get the selfish element out of our prayers.

4\. A refusal is not always a permanent thing.

5\. Continuing in prayer strengthens our faith.

The ["servant of servants"]{.class_4258} accomplished, in the above verses, what the best religious brains of the day could not do in three and one-half years (see 22:15)---[she entangled Him in His talk.]{.class_4257}

["Then came she and worshipped him."]{.class_4258} You would have thought this would have done it, but He still turns her down one more time. Note: ["Lord, help me!"]{.class_4258} Three words just like Simon Peter (see 14:30), but this time, instead of "immediate aid," Jesus does the most humiliating thing He could do. He calls her [racial characteristics ]{.class_4257}to her attention, and without using the word "nigger," He answers just about as rudely and uncouthly as a man could. As to whether or not the term "nigger" is worse than "bitch" is hard to say, but in plain English (never mind Greek scholarship, they don't know what He said!), that is what He called her!

Since none of the new translations or translators or commentators (or scientists or educators) dare speak plainly about matters of race, we shall (see comments on Gen. 9:25, 10:20).

In the passage the term is "DOG." A female dog in every country in the world has a name that appears very seldom in any literature other than that written by men like William Faulkner, James Baldwin, James Joyce, Ernest Hemingway, Stein­beck, and Norman Mailer. [This is the gist of verse 26, and any woman in the world would understand it even if the Greek word for ]{.class_4257}["dog"]{.class_4258}[ had a masculine ending]{.class_4257}. The Syro-Phoenecian understands it perfectly. But instead of answering, "What right do you have to talk to me like that? What makes you Jews think you're better than us Afro-Palestinians? How dare you. I'm going to turn you in to the 'Anti-Defamation League.' You know what that District Judge said about discrimination! I want my rights\...." This woman, with the wisdom of Solomon, says: "That's the truth, Lord. The Bible is right. If we got a curse on us, it'll have to stay, and we'll just have to serve; but Lord, since you did classify me with dogs, is it not also the truth that ["a righteous man regardeth the life of his beast']{.class_4258}?" (Prov. 12:10).

"Ye-ee-s."

"Well, then, Your children didn't finish their spinach, and they left meat on the drumsticks, and it's going to [the garbage and get wasted]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1527.xhtml_page_265}, isn't it?"

"Well, huh, yes."

"All right, in that case, how about putting some of it right down here where us dogs can get it!!"

And she has Him.

She has Him dead to rights, and He couldn't back out without denying the Old Testament (and He came to "fulfill the law"). Besides---"the Scriptures cannot be broken." That old Hamite just backed Jesus up in a corner and got her petition. She stooped to conquer and was not ashamed to stoop clean under the table and wait till the family got through with the meal! That kind of faith will get results every time, while the NAACP is still trying to jack-up the welfare checks!

["Then Jesus answered and said unto her, O woman, great is thy faith\...."]{.class_4258} And the daughter was healed in ["that very hour."]{.class_4258}

15:29 And Jesus departed from thence, and came nigh unto the sea of Galilee; and went up into a mountain, and sat down there.

30 And great multitudes came unto him, having with them those that were lame, blind, dumb, maimed, and many others, and cast them down at Jesus' feet; and he healed them:

31 Insomuch that the multitude wondered, when they saw the dumb to speak, the maimed to be whole, the lame to walk, and the blind to see: and they glorified the God of Israel.

The events have been commented on before (8:16, 11:5).

The Sinaitic Manuscript (350---Aleph), found by Tischendorf in St. Catherine's Monastery, has omitted ["the maimed to be whole."]{.class_4258} \[This is only one in a series of over one hundred omissions which mark the Sinaiticus manuscript as one of the corrupt extractions of Origen's Hexapla (230).\] Other noteworthy omissions, in line with the still more corrupt [RSV ]{.class_4257}(1952), are Romans 8:1; Acts 8:37; Luke 23:42; Mark 16:16; etc. (A full list will be found in the work by J. J. Ray, [God Wrote Only One Bible,]{.class_4257} Junction City, Oregon.)

The writer of Sinaiticus (probably Eusebius at Caesarea) thought that the ["God of Israel"]{.class_4258} was getting a little [too much glory ]{.class_4257}out of restoring ["maimed people"]{.class_4257} (i.e., people minus a finger, ear, hand, or foot), so in the best interests of critical scholarship and "scientific analysis," he deleted ["the maimed to be whole."]{.class_4258} Unfortunately, the repeat performance in Luke 22:51 was a little too obvious to revise, so it was left in its crude, primitive, and offensive form: i.e., [its original form.]{.class_4257}

15:32 Then Jesus called his disciples unto him, and said, I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days, and have nothing to eat: and I will not send them away fasting, lest they faint in the way.

33 And his disciples say unto him, Whence should we have so much bread in the wilderness, as to fill so great a multitude?

34 And Jesus saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven, and a few little fishes.

35 And he commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground.

36 And he took the seven loaves and the fishes, and gave thanks, and brake them, and gave to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.

37 And they did all eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets full.

38 And they that did eat were four thousand men, beside women and children.

39 And he sent away the multitude, and took ship, and came into the coasts of Magdala.

There has been considerable comment on this re-occurring scene, under the feeding of the 5,000 (Matt. 14:21). ["I have compassion on the multitude\...."]{.class_4258} This is the common remark in connection with the seething mass of humanity who followed Him; it is never said of the scribes, Pharisees, Herodians, Sadducees, Essenes, or anyone like them. (See Mark 1:41, 5:19; Luke 7:13; Isa. 49:15; Micah 7:19.)

["They continue with me now three days."]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1527.xhtml_page_267} In modern America, the terms can hardly be understood, for everyone "roaming around" over the country is carrying picnic lunches, Coleman insulated coolers, stainless steel Thermos jugs, and Hershey bars. If we go barehanded, we can stop at any gas station (the national average is three per mile in the city limits and one every fifteen miles outside the city limits) and pick up Cokes, peanut bars, "wrapped sandwiches," Eskimo pies, and Milky Ways. The first-century, Jewish population is traveling without money or food, and when the loaf of bread they left home with is eaten, that is all there is. They are in danger of "fainting by the way."

["Seven, and a few little fishes."]{.class_4258} Notice that they do not ask again: ["What are they among so many?"]{.class_4258} (John 6:9). They have learned one lesson---at least temporarily (see Matt. 16:9--10)!

["And they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets full."]{.class_4258} This is not as remarkable as the last time, but still to get seven baskets full of bread out of seven loaves is an operation that any baker in the world would be willing to pay \$4,000.00 to learn---and this was the [residue]{.class_4257} after feeding over 6,000 people. (The men alone totaled 4,000.) The "basket," here, is ["spuridas"]{.class_4257} (Gk.) which is about the size of a good wicker laundry basket. One basket could easily hold thirty loaves of bread!

["Magdala."]{.class_4258} The village is located on the west shore of Galilee, ten miles south of Capernaum and five miles north of Tiberias. It was a part of Zebulun's original inheritance.

[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml}

# Chapter 16 {.heading_84-0}

16:1 The Pharisees also with the Sadducees came, and tempting desired him that he would shew them a sign from heaven.

2 He answered and said unto them, When it is evening, ye say, It will be fair weather: for the sky is red.

3 And in the morning, It will be foul weather to day: for the sky is red and lowring. O ye hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky; but can ye not discern the signs of the times?

Here the Catholics (Pharisees---see notes on 16:6) and the Liberals (see comments on 16:6) "gang up," so to speak, on the Lord Jesus and ask him to show them ["a sign"]{.class_4258} for which Israel is told to look. (See Exod. 4:8, 30 and 1 Cor. 1:22.) ["A sign from heaven"]{.class_4258} indicates a supernatural wonder connected with the upper atmosphere. This is proper for an Israelite to expect, in view of the plagues which Moses brought on Egypt (Exod. 4--12) and the "fire from Heaven" which Elijah produced (1 Kings 18:38), not to mention the initial kindling for the altar of burnt offerings (Lev. 9:24). Unfortunately, for any "sign seeker," both signs and miracles can be counterfeited (Rev. 13:13), and the way in which the Man of Sin will convince Science and Religion that he is the "Messiah" will be by using the [electrical forces ]{.class_4257}(Dan. 11:38, not "fortresses" as the obscure [RSV ]{.class_4257}and [ASV ]{.class_4257}read) of the universe to bring down "fire from heaven" (Rev. 13:12--13).

["He answered and said unto them\...."]{.class_4258} What follows is a beautiful rebuke to the amillennial "scholar" of today who cannot see beyond the nose on his face because of his method of placing all the Bible into the past tense. The reasoning is, "You sit up waiting for the 10 and 11 o'clock weather report; you are all interested in prophecy for you all prophesy about the weather, and everybody talks about the weather. How is it that none of you talk about the Second Coming?" If a man can hazard a guess on tomorrow's weather by [present appearances]{.class_4257}, he can certainly hazard a guess on world events tomorrow on the basis of present appearances. The [appearance ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_269}of Christ's time is the preview for 1900--2000 (see notes on Matt. 3:1--2 and 2:1).

An uneducated man says: "There's a ring around the moon tonight; gonna' rain tomorrow." An aerologist at the Naval Air Station says: "The barometer is falling; there's a storm on the way." ["But can ye not discern the signs of the times?"]{.class_4258} The Christian knows them [perfectly ]{.class_4257}according to 1 Thessalonians 5:1--6, and yet there are dispensationalists who insist that since "Israel is the subject of prophecy," that the church cannot tell where it is on God's timetable. The same Christian will try his hand at weather forecasting a hundred days in the year! If the Lord's coming is "from heaven" and the Heavenly cavalry of Revelation chapter 19 comes down out of the sky (see Hab. 3; Joel 2), then how is it that a man who can predict weather cannot predict the coming of Christ? The coming of Christ is characterized by ["the sign of the Son of man in heaven"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 24:30). There is no doubt about Jesus' drift of thought, for they are asking for ["a sign from heaven."]{.class_4258}

The Christian position is clear. Although the signs may be given to [Israel]{.class_4257}, anyone who can read ([who wants to read!]{.class_4257}) can read them. If I am going from Pensacola to Memphis, I can ignore the signs which say "twenty miles to Meridian," "forty miles to Tupelo," "eight miles to Citronelle." I may say to myself: "Memphis is the subject of revelation, but Tupelo, Meridian, and Citronelle are the subjects of prophecy." [But I am a blank fool if I do not know that the nearer I get to any of them going from Pensacola, the nearer I am to Memphis.]{.class_4257} The Christians who take this position (which is acceptable to any amillennial reformer) are, in the large, that great new breed of "new-translation-day-school-Christian-culture-child-evangelism-campus-crusade" type of Christians. That is, they are Christians who use the New Testament as a tool for building a "testimony." [They are not Bible believers who believe in "meditating in the word day and night."]{.class_4257}

["Red and lowring."]{.class_4258} The Greek here, ["stugnadzo,"]{.class_4257} is applied to a man's countenance falling (Mark 10:22), and the expression matches Cain's glowering face when he beheld the sign from heaven consume Abel's sacrifice (see commentary on Genesis, Gen. 4:3--5).

Rabbinical schools in Christ's day made a point of teaching []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_270}weather-lore, and even foretold the time of the rains for the coming year. Such wisdom and insight could hardly miss "the signs of the times" given by Isaiah in Isaiah chapter 40 and Malachi in Malachi chapters 3--4!

["O ye hypocrites."]{.class_4258} That is, those who put on a show or pretence. They were pretending in the first place (vs. 1), which is perfectly obvious by the fact that they had refused to accept the first 5,000 signs that had already showed up (cf. 12:22, 14:14, 35--36, 15:30--31, 37)! Why give a man a searchlight when he won't try a flashlight first?

Their motive in asking, in the first place, was to tempt Him, exactly like the temptation offered to Jesus by Satan in Matthew 4:5--6. ["Tempting him"]{.class_4258} (vs. 1) is the thought behind the inquiry, as it is also in 22:15. There is a difference between a ruined sinner coming to the word for help and crying,[ "God, what is the answer?"]{.class_4257} and a self-righteous, deluded skeptic going to a Greek lexicon and saying: ["All right, God, let's see you show me something!"]{.class_4257} (The Lord is more interested in what astronaut Armstrong was thinking when he saw his picture in [Time ]{.class_4257}magazine than any "scientific data" he may have gathered on the back side of the moon!)

16:4 A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas. And he left them, and departed.

5 And when his disciples were come to the other side, they had forgotten to take bread.

["A wicked and adulterous generation"]{.class_4258} seems to be a favorite expression of Jesus. Exactly why those who profess to "follow Him as their example" (Peale, Kagawa, Croin, Douglas, Blake, Rap Brown, et al.) never use the expression is a mystery. It occurs in Matthew 12:39 and Mark 8:38 as well as here. In two of the three contexts in which it appears, the words are aimed at the "Ecumenical Council" of Jewish synagogues.

["No sign\...but the sign of the prophet Jonas."]{.class_4258} The statement has been given before with additional information in Matthew 12:39--40; here, it is directed at the Pharisees and scribes (12:39).

The problem, here, is to understand how a Messiah with the "signs" of Moses, sent to a people who were looking for a sign, could flatly turn them down; but then, we must ["search the scriptures."]{.class_4258} The [incomplete statement ]{.class_4257}in Mark 8:11--12 is to be interpreted in the light of the [complete statements ]{.class_4257}in Matthew, exactly as the passage on divorce is to be interpreted (Mark 10:11 with Matt. 19:9).

1\. The Jews "seek for a sign," and they got it (Acts 10:38).

2\. But ["signs and wonders"]{.class_4258} don't solve the problem of sin (John 4:48).

3\. ["Signs and wonders"]{.class_4258} of the most spectacular sort cannot even convince a religious leader of the truth (John 12:37--40).

4\. The "sign of Jonas" would point out the greatest miracle that ever took place on this earth: the bodily resurrection of a sinless Man who could never die again (Matt. 12:40).

5\. This sign pointed to the solution for sin (see Rom. 6:3--12).

6\. Therefore, it will be a sign that is spoken against (Luke 2:34). \[For further details, see comments on Matt. 12:40.\]

Since no one in the group will accept a genuine sign, Jesus says, ["no sign,"]{.class_4258} but since He must give a sign to Israel---a nation born with the signs of Moses and Aaron---He adds, ["the sign of the prophet Jonas."]{.class_4258} Thus, Jesus answers the question without satisfying the inquirers, fulfills the Scripture without violating the prophecies, points to the New Testament plan of salvation without "giving it away," and confounds every commentary printed since A.D. 33---for not even the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4257}attempted to expound the passage!

"And he left them, and departed."

Verse 5 is self-explanatory and needs little comment. The conversation which follows takes place on the boat while they are crossing Galilee (Mark 8:13--14).

16:6 Then Jesus said unto them, Take heed and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.

The verse is a crucial verse in the history of the church and the end-time, as it is related to the Body of Christ. The leaven has been defined (see comments on Matt. 13:33), and without repeating a great deal, let it be noted again that the Bible definition (we are not interested in what some church [thinks the definitions are]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_272}) of the Pharisee and Sadducee is as follows:

1\. The Pharisee is an almsgiving, tithing, righteous, fasting, praying, orthodox, long-robed "father" who believes in spirits and angels and a resurrection (Matt. 23; Luke 18; Acts 23). He has a "universal church" which covers land and sea to get converts and his converts are twice as damned as he is (Matt. 23).

2\. A Sadducee is an almsgiving, tithing, righteous, fasting, praying, liberal, long-robed [skeptic ]{.class_4257}who denies the existence of any spiritual beings or a literal resurrection (Matt. 23; Acts 23; Matt. 22). He will join any ecumenical movement as long as it is against the word of God (Matt. 16:1).

If the reader cannot locate those gentlemen in contemporary society (twentieth century), he is to be more pitied than censured. The Bible admonishes us to ["comfort the feebleminded"]{.class_4258} (1 Thess. 5:14)!

16:7 And they reasoned among themselves, saying, It is because we have taken no bread.

8 Which when Jesus perceived, he said unto them, O ye of little faith, why reason ye among yourselves, because ye have brought no bread?

9 Do ye not yet understand, neither remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?

10 Neither the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?

The disciples seem to have the problem which Catholics have in regard to "the Lord's Supper"; [they insist on being literalists where it concerns something to put in their mouths.]{.class_4257} (See Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, Gen. 3:3--6.) The priest throws the belief in literal interpretation right back in the Christian's face when he gets to Matthew 26:26 (4 x 13!) by saying: "We Catholics accept it [literally ]{.class_4257}not [symbolically.]{.class_4257}" (Which of course is nonsense. No priest accepts Matthew 26:27--28 [literally]{.class_4257}; otherwise he would be grinding his molars out on the metal cup instead of drinking the "shinny" that is in it!) But literal interpretation where it is NOT literal is just as big []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_273}an error as vice versa.

Note: in Matthew 16:7, they take him [literally]{.class_4257}, when they should have [spiritually]{.class_4257}. In Matthew 15:15--16, they are taking Him [spiritually ]{.class_4257}when He should have been taken [literally]{.class_4257}. And in John 6:52, they (as all good Catholics) are taking Him [literally ]{.class_4257}when verse 63 states that it is to be taken [spiritually]{.class_4257}. We see how the Bible is written. It is a two-edged sword that is designed to deceive as well as edify, and nothing could be more destructive (nor deceptive) than a Bible wielded in the hand of a black-robed, Latin "Father" who is counting on such superstitious black magic as a "sacrament" to save him.

Jesus rebukes the disciples and points out to them that He could not have been speaking literally, because if bread were the problem, they could always get bread out of thin air! Hadn't they seen it done [twice ]{.class_4257}(Matt. 14:21, 15:37)?

16:11 How is it that ye do not understand that I spake it not to you concerning bread, that ye should beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees?

12 Then understood they how that he bade them not beware of the leaven of bread, but of the doctrine of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.

Jesus considers false doctrine a greater danger than running out of bread (Matt. 4:4). And if this is true, the Bible believer should put a great deal of weight on 2 Timothy 2:15; 1 Timothy 4:15--16; Ephesians 4:14; and Romans 16:17--18.

Since the main things that will characterize the last days of the church on this earth will be false teaching and rejection of the word (1 Tim. 4:1--5; Rev. 3:16--21; Matt. 24:11, 24), the Bible believer should form the habit of rejecting from his spiritual system the writing, preaching, teaching, or work of any Christian leader who [adds a word to a verse, subtracts a word from a verse, or takes a verse out of the context to prove any point. ]{.class_4257}\[This does not mean that a verse should be so chained to the context that it loses all future, prophetic application or spiritual application to the individual (see 2 Tim. 3:16). However, it means that where [doctrinal issues ]{.class_4257}are at stake in determining the [exact truth ]{.class_4257}of a passage, it cannot be []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_274}wrested from its context and used to prove what it does not match. \]

16:13 When Jesus came into the coasts of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, Whom do men say that I the Son of man am?

14 And they said, Some say that thou art John the Baptist: some, Elias; and others, Jeremias, or one of the prophets.

15 He saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am?

["Caesarea Philippi"]{.class_4258} is one of the most northerly cities in Palestine. It went by two names, "Banias" or "Paneas," and may have been identical with the former city of "Dan" in the Old Testament.

["Thou art John the Baptist."]{.class_4258} This was the opinion of Herod (14:1--2), and consequently, it was probably picked up by his courtiers and servants. John the Baptist was mistakenly confused with Jesus Christ by the Pharisees in John 1:19--20, 25.

["Elias."]{.class_4258} The Jews had every right to expect Elijah to show up before the Messiah, and so they looked upon John the Baptist (John 1:21, 25) as the old scion of Ahab's ivory palaces. They are still expecting the second coming of Elijah (Matt. 27:47) [after ]{.class_4257}the death of John, and have a right to do so (Mal. 4:5), and will not be disappointed forever (see Rev. 11:3--8 and comments on Matt. 17:10--11)!

["And others, Jeremias."]{.class_4258} This bizarre guess doesn't seem to match very much information found within the Scriptures. Nowhere are we told (nor is it intimated) that Jeremiah will arise from the dead and return. Still Jeremiah 1:5 seems like an unfulfilled commission; it is a commission to the Gentiles, exactly like that found in Jeremiah 25:15. Jeremiah is a unique prophet in many ways. He is the only man in the Bible commanded not to marry or have children. He is the author of "Lamentations." He is the last prophet of the last kingdom (Judah) to go into captivity. And he is the only prophet to be carried bodily (as a prisoner) out of the land of Israel by his [own people]{.class_4257}. British Israelism has erected a tremendous superstructure of nonsense on this fact, and it is peddled to gullible shoppers in the twentieth century by Garner Ted, the magazine salesmen, and Herbert W.---better known as "Mr. []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_275}Confusion."

The Catholic choice of the salient passage for the [foundation ]{.class_4257}of their religion turns out to be:

["Thou art the Christ\...."]{.class_4258} This statement is what the apostate NCCC (1970) finally settled on for a "creed," or "confessional" of orthodoxy, trying to get just as close to Rome as possible, without dropping over into verse 18! "Blessed art thou, Simon Barjona: for flesh and blood []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_277}hath not revealed it unto thee."

The new birth of the Christian in the Scriptures is equated with [the revelations given to Peter and Paul! ]{.class_4257}Now, that is something to consider, for it means:

The required "new birth" is NOT ["flesh and blood"]{.class_4258} because ["that which is born of the flesh ]{.class_4258}[is flesh]{.class_4268}[,"]{.class_4258} that which is born of water [is water]{.class_4257} (or fishes! see Gen. 1!), and ["that which is born of the Spirit ]{.class_4258}[is spirit."]{.class_4268}

One mistake in fourth-grade reading, at this point, will put a man [in the Lake of Fire]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_278}, for the new birth, as Paul's and Peter's revelation, is not a flesh-and-blood matter.

["Thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church."]{.class_4258} Here is the blood-stained text that was used to cause the death of Waldenses, Albigenses, Huguenots, Jews, Protestants, Anabaptists, Reformers, Paulicians, Hussites, Lollards, and "Arians" down through the centuries (and presently has cost the lives of nearly 400,000 men, women, and children in Vietnam). Aside from the fact that all four proofs are about as authentic as []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_279}Darwin's imaginary zoo, the following plain and conclusive facts must be faced by an [honest Catholic]{.class_4257}.

1\. Origen's Commentary (A.D. 184--254) reads: "If you should think that the whole church was built by God only on that one, Peter, what will you say of John\...or each of the apostles?" "Prince" fits Satan in John 14:30 as neatly as Machiavelli's "Prince" matches the Prince of Ephesians 2:2. 2\. Augustine (A.D. 378) (Retractationes, Vol. I, p. 33), "I have []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_280}somewhere said, concerning the apostle Peter, that the Church was founded on him, as a "petra" or rock; but I know that [I have since]{.class_4257} [very often explained what our Lord said to signify Him whom Peter confessed\...."]{.class_4257}

3\. Chrysostom (A.D. 345) states, "Upon this rock, that is, on the faith of his confession."

The "church fathers" (so beloved and so preferred by the popes to the Pauline epistles) are a little divided in their testimony on Matthew 16:18, for Jerome, Gregory of Nyssa, Cyril of Jerusalem, Hilary, and Ambrose all [reject ]{.class_4257}the Roman Catholic identification of these two words---Peter and rock--- which are spelled differently in three different languages.

4\. In the "Collect from the Gregorian Sacramentary" and in the Roman Missal on "The Vigil of St. Peter and St. Paul" are found the words, "Grant that thou wouldest not suffer us, [whom thou hast established on the rock of the apostolic confession ]{.class_4257}to be shaken by any commotions."

But the Church Fathers are a little dull on Bible study and evidently do not know enough Scripture to qualify for much more than church babies. Whereas Jerome, Augustine, Origen, and Chrysostom have to mess with the "tweeters and woofers" to tune up the set, the old, solid-state [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 finds the wave length immediately and brings immediate results.

1\. Simon Peter [himself ]{.class_4257}confessed the Rock was Christ, not himself (1 Pet. 2:8, 4).

2\. Paul himself confessed that the Rock was [Christ ]{.class_4257}(1 Cor. 10:1--4).

3\. Jesus Himself said that [He Himself was the Rock]{.class_4257} (Matt. 21:42, 44, Dan. 2!).

6\. And the way you can tell this is to ask the "enemy" (Deut. 32:31), or simply notice the difference in the Lord's Suppers between the taste of the grape juice (new wine) going down the throat of the Bible believer and the dragon's old wine (from the vine of Sodom) []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_281}trickling down the throat of the Italian "Prince" (Deut. 32:32--33).

Since the Scriptures themselves have located our problem, defined the terms, established the definitions, analyzed the possibilities, tested the application, and resolved the problem, further discussion is unnecessary. Trusting Peter as a rock foundation would be like trusting light bulbs to hold up the Empire State building.

Peter was married (Matt. 8); the popes are not. \[See publication [Rome---The Great Private Interpreter,]{.class_4257} 1969.\] He had children (1 Pet. 5:13) the popes [do not]{.class_4257}. \[That is, some of them don't---Pope John XXIII (yes, you read it right) (1410--1415) violated virgins, nuns, and married women, and lived in adultery with his brother's wife, was guilty of sodomy and nameless vices. Peter was wrong in his doctrines (Gal. 2:11; 2:14), and the popes are supposed to believe true doctrines.

Peter said the supreme political power on earth was [the king]{.class_4257}, not the pope (1 Pet. 2:13).

Peter said Christians were priests who were called to offer [spiritual sacrifices ]{.class_4257}(1 Pet. 2:5), not Nicolaitanes repeating literal sacrifices (Heb. 10:11).

Peter would not allow a man to bow down in front of him (Acts 10:26); he baptized only [adults ]{.class_4257}(Acts 2), and baptized Gentiles [after ]{.class_4257}they received the Holy Spirit, [not at birth]{.class_4257} (Acts 10:44--47); and Peter wore no long robes, had no crown, sported no ring on the finger and, in his latter years, preached the free salvation of God as a gift of grace, [apart from sacraments of any kind ]{.class_4257}(Acts 15:11).

As a Catholic, Peter would have made a good Mohammedan, and as a pope, he would have made a good bartender. "Apostolic succession" is a farce, and a man that believes it is a self-deluded dunce. (I say that with charity, of course!) Whatever the "rock" is, it is certainly not Simon Peter. Simon Peter is no more kin to Giuseppi Rancalli and Giovanni Montini than General William Booth was to Machine Gun Kelly.

"Petros" (Peter) is a rock the size of a baseball or basket ball (or at the most a boulder as big as an automobile); "petra" ("the rock-ledge") is a slab of rock big enough to land a DC-5 on. The only way you can make the two equal is to deny your God-given senses of reasoning. (This is called "faith" in the Roman communion. It is called "puffed-up flesh" in Col. 2:18.)

Jesus, undoubtedly, is pointing to Himself when He says, ["upon this rock,"]{.class_4258} and if it really just is the confession of faith by Simon Peter, it is a strange Rock indeed [for demons and devils do not hesitate to make the same confession under pressure!]{.class_4257}

Goodspeed, Williams, Weymouth, and the New English Bible accept the curtsey and follow suit.

This is the root word for ["gate"]{.class_4258} in Luke 16:20; Revelation 21:13; Acts 10:17, 12:13; Matthew 7:13; Luke 7:12; Acts 3:10, 9:24, etc. How has a plain word which anyone can understand---a one syllable word at that!---suddenly becomes robed in mysterious splendor and evaporated into an abstract "picture" of something no one can locate? Surely with the word "power" occurring more than 120 times in the New Testament, the Holy Spirit didn't have a lapse of memory when He got to a Roman Catholic prooftext! (Did He forget the word []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_283}"cousin" when He needed it? See the comments on Matthew 1:25.)

[There is a Negro in the lumber yard ]{.class_4257}(twentieth-century translation) [somewhere]{.class_4257}.

The twentieth century has augmented this unbelief in the supernatural until Christians are at the place where they can grasp by faith only the simplest New Testament doctrines. 1\. Hell is a [place]{.class_4257}, not a condition of mind or "evil power" (Deut. 32:22; Job 11:8; Amos 9:2; Matt. 11:23; Acts 2:27; Mark 9:43). A discussion of "Hades" and "Sheol" is not in order yet, at this point.

(In comes "modern scholarship"---with all of its members in good standing with the Yea Hath God Said Society---and intone, in cadence: "It is figurative! It is figurative! It is figurative! Rah! Rah! Rah!" [Don't be too sure about that]{.class_4257}.)

The ["key of David"]{.class_4258} may be figurative, and the ["keys"]{.class_4258} of Matthew 16:19 may be figurative, but don't be too sure about getting out of Hell without a key when you get in there! And don't be too sure about any necessity of making this key figurative, for the ["church,"]{.class_4258} referred to in the text, plainly cannot be the "Body of Christ" of which Paul speaks. The ["church,"]{.class_4258} of which Matthew speaks (Matt. 16:18), [gets out of Hell through the gates after it gets in it! ]{.class_4257}(see Rev. 6:9; Matt. 27:50--54; Eph. 4:6--12; Isa. 45:2).

As usual, the correct placement of the Jew---this time the Jewish remnant in the tribulation---preserves the sense and meaning of the words without alteration or manipulation. All deviations from the text are, as mentioned earlier, the efforts of cheap, religious opportunists taking advantage of the world's ignorance and indifference to the revelation of God.

1\. The church referred to is trying to get [out ]{.class_4257}of Hell, not [in ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_284}it.

3\. The ["gates of hell"]{.class_4258} are not the [powers ]{.class_4257}of Hell, but the ["gates."]{.class_4258}

4\. If the church referred to was the [Body of Christ]{.class_4257}, it could not possibly be the Catholic Church, for all Catholics (including popes, priests, bishops, archbishops, monks, nuns, and cardinals) believe that a Christian [can lose salvation ]{.class_4257}and go to Hell, with the Christian "falling out of the Body of Christ" (thus mutilating Christ's body again!). In Luke 16:23, it is revealed that not all the "fire" is in "hell fire" ([Geenan]{.class_4257}, Greek), for the rich man in Hell [(]{.class_4263}ᾅδη[ς]{.class_4263}) is tormented in flame. Since many of the inhabitants of Sheol (Jacob, Job, Hezekiah) in the Old Testament were not [tormented in flame]{.class_4257}, the Holy Spirit reveals to us that Sheol had two compartments (note Luke 16:19--26) in the Old Testament. Where Hell (Greek, ["hades"]{.class_4257}) is given as the equivalent of Sheol, [it would include both compartments.]{.class_4257} Where it refers to the lost (Matt. 11:23; Luke 10:15; Rev. 20:14), it refers to [one compartment]{.class_4257}. \[Note that this is true also of the Old Testament, where "Sheol" sometimes refers to the lost only---Ezek. 31:15; Isa. 14:11---and sometimes includes lost and saved, as above (Gen. 44:31; Job 17:13; Isa. 38:10).\]

The [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 has done a beautiful job here in preventing a Greek or Hebrew scholar from finding the truth, even after locating the meanings of "Hades" and "Sheol." (Notice how the Jehovah's Witnesses mangle the whole Bible after stumbling at the translation of ["grave."]{.class_4258}) We cannot but admire the amazing undesigned coincidences in the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611, which were inserted unintentionally on []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_285}the part of the human instruments, but intentionally on the part of the Author. The English is clear to an honest man (Prov. 8:9), and in it he can solve the problems of textual dilemmas (Prov. 22:18--21), which "the clearer revisions" only hopelessly confuse. After changing ["hell"]{.class_4258} to "Hades" in the new Bibles, the bewildered revisers change ["gates"]{.class_4258} to "powers" ([not found in any Greek text!]{.class_4257}) and still miss the point!

As the text stands, without alteration or emendation by religious leaders, Greek professors, Catholic tradition, or critical commentators, it says:

Christ has a "Church" (Gk. [ekklesia]{.class_4257} "called out assembly") which will be formed at a future date. This church, as the Old Testament church (Acts 7:38), will not have a completed salvation, nor will its members be baptized into Christ's body by the Spirit (1 Cor. 12:13). They were prisoners who went out through [gates ]{.class_4257}at the resurrection of Jesus Christ! Since the Old Testament is a parable (in the prophetic sense as well as the spiritual sense, Psa. 78:1--4), one would not be surprised to find a body of saints back in Sheol, in the "saved" compartment of Hades, awaiting a resurrection at the end of the Tribulation (Rev. 6:9--11; 7:13--15). They have to go out through the gates of "hell" after being taken captive again, for their standing in the Tribulation is the faith and works standing of an Old Testament saint, under the law (Rev. 12:17, 14:12)!

16:19 And I will give unto thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.

20 Then charged he his disciples that they should tell []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_286}no man that he was Jesus the Christ.

The Catholic reading (privately interpreted without any regard for the verses which interpret the text) would come out: "Thou art the Prince of the Apostles, and I will give unto thee the keys of Janus so that no one can get through the gate of Heaven unless old Saint Peter lets them in. If you make a statement about politics or literature down here, I'll just have to abide by your decision up there, Pete, because you will be infallible."

Nor is this "overstating" the case. The popes of Italy are so far "gone" in private interpretation that they presume that:

In answer to that last "crock," "Phillip the Fair," of France (1301), wrote laconically to the Holy Father: "Superlative fatuity, small greetings or none\...We are subject to no one in [temporal ]{.class_4257}matters....those who think otherwise, we account [fools and madmen]{.class_4257}." Christian or Atheist, Phillip had more sense than the CIA, and the Vietnam "advisors" who followed the leadership of J. F. Kennedy.

Pope Alexander VI (the father of the bastard children mentioned in comments under 16:18) declared that he had power to give to the King of Catholic Spain all the land west of Cape Verde, [from pole to pole]{.class_4257}. This document, which says that the United States, Canada, Mexico, and South America are Spain's by [Divine decree]{.class_4257}, is the Papal Bull made to Castille, given at Rome, 1493 ([Hakluytus Posthumus,]{.class_4257} Stansby and Fetherstone, London, 1625.).

["The keys of the kingdom of heaven."]{.class_4258} To correct the maniacal ravings of the Roman pontiffs, it should be observed that ["keys"]{.class_4258} are for [gates ]{.class_4257}and [doors ]{.class_4257}(1 Cor. 16:9). The plain English of the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 leaves no doubt about interpretations either---a ["door" ]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_287}is the opportunity to publish the glad tidings (2 Cor. 2:12; Rev. 3:7--8). If it be objected that we have "spiritualized" the passage, let it be noted that the ["keys"]{.class_4258} are not for "getting in or out of Heaven" (like the sulphurous keys of vs. 18 were connected with Hell); they are for ["the kingdom of heaven."]{.class_4258} This lands us back at Matthew 3:2 and 4:17, which we have thoroughly covered to the satisfaction of any honest researcher.

["The kingdom of heaven"]{.class_4258} is not "Heaven," never will be Heaven, and could not be Heaven if it tried. ["The kingdom of heaven,"]{.class_4258} wherever it occurs, under any conditions of time or fortune, is the literal, visible, physical dominion on earth of [a man or nation]{.class_4257} (Adam, Noah, Abraham, et al.).

Peter is given a power which is exclusively his, only where it designates an opportunity to open the door of faith to Jew and Gentile; note ["keys,"]{.class_4258} the plural. He exercises this authority in Acts 2 and 10, and further use of the keys is superfluous. Peter throws his keys in the Jordan after Acts 15 (or the Euphrates!---1 Pet. 5:13), with Jeremiah's good for nothing underwear (Jer. 13:1--7.) The crossed keys of the Vatican have a patrimony far more ancient than Peter's ["keys,"]{.class_4258} for the College of "Augurs" (Vatican: "the place of prophecy") adopted the Keys of Janus, the two-headed god, for their symbols of authority, and they are no more the keys of Matthew 16:19 than the car keys you carry on your keychain.

["And whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth...."]{.class_4258} Right away, we learn that this power is given to all twelve apostles, including Judas (!) in Matthew 18:18. The deluded communicants of the Roman church have been taught just enough fundamentals of the faith (Nicene Creed) to think that further study in the word is unnecessary. The "Catholic," therefore, looks at a "non-Catholic" as some weird species of Bible-rejecting infidel. He confounds the "fundamentals of the faith" with the superstitions and traditions of a Bible-rejecting political organization, since the organization [uses the fundamentals to build its following]{.class_4257}. Hence, the average Catholic has never seen Matthew 18:18, would be afraid to read it, and would be scared to death that [if he believed what it teaches]{.class_4257}, that this would lead to further rejection of [Catholic teaching]{.class_4257}, and consequently, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_288}rejection of the fundamentals of the faith, which are true in the Nicene Creed! That is, the Catholic is held in slavery by being convinced that if he loses his faith in the [infallibility of his church]{.class_4257}, he has lost his trust [in the word of God and Jesus Christ.]{.class_4257}

The two are not connected.

Matthew 18:18 shows that all the apostles had power to "bind and loose," and Peter was no more a Prince at this business than Simon the Canaanite (Matt. 10:2­--4).

More than twelve disciples receive the power "to remit and retain sins" (John 20:23; Luke 24:33), and the power of Matthew 16:19 is shared by fifteen to thirty common Christians of Christ's day and time. The Roman Catholic could not believe this even if it were true, for it implies that the political hierarchy of his church is not only powerless in spiritual matters, but also is deceptive and dishonest in handling the word of Truth (2 Cor. 4:2). Afraid to break from such an outfit (if he knew the truth), the Catholic closes his eyes and continues on in darkness and conceit, convinced that he has the truth because of the few fundamental truths his church taught him which any real Bible believer believes.

Catholics ignore Matthew 18:18 for the same reason they ignore Matthew 23:9 and 1 Timothy 2:5. The results of believing the truth are too serious to act on it. Relying on a church and a priest to take the place of individual investigation of the Truth, the Catholic rests securely in darkness, trusting implicitly that his leaders are leading him right. [All of Hitler's followers did the same thing. ]{.class_4257}And Adolph was a BAVARIAN CATHOLIC!

["And whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth\...."]{.class_4258} Out come the lexicons and "newer translations" to help the poor old [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 out of the maze. But it is unnecessary. "Shall have been bound and shall have been loosed" (perfect tense), "Shall have already been loosed," "Had already been loosed," etc., doesn't do anything but smog the passage up. \[Hyper-Calvinism doesn't solve any problems in the passage. The "heavens" pretty well control everything down here anyway (Dan. 10:20--21, 10:13--14, 4:26), and there is no point to announce this truism in the context of a "non-flesh-and-blood" revelation from the Father.\] Nor is there any reason for anyone to think that "binding and loosing" can be lifted from the context of []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_289}gospel preaching and placed down on inquisitions, anathemas, absolutions, excommunications, interdicts, Galileo trials, Vietnam advisors, DeGaulle elections, or Hitler-Mussolini concordats.

Examples of "loosing and binding," in the sense of Matthew 16:18, occur in Galatians 2:1--8 and Acts 1:15--26 and Acts 15. Acts of "loosing and binding," in the sense of Matthew 18:18, occur in 2 Corinthians 2:10 and Ephesians 4:30--32. The "remitting and retaining of sins" is a power given to all priests. Since [all Christians are "priests" ]{.class_4257}(1 Pet. 2), the humblest believer has been given the authority to tell a man his sins are forgiven on the basis of Acts 13:38--39, or tell him that they are unforgiven on the basis of John 8:24. The Christian does not "judge" the sinner when he exercises this power, for the unsaved man is [already judged ]{.class_4257}and condemned (John 3:36, 3:18), and the saved man has already been tried and acquitted (John 5:24).

The Roman Catholic Church, as a religious-political body, has never had power to do [anything ]{.class_4257}on the face of this earth, except the things that [any political body ]{.class_4257}has power to do, by the permissive will of God,under Satan: power to coerce, intimidate, swear out warrants and summons, arrest street preachers, manipulate rulers and police authorities, politic, spread propaganda, raise children to believe lies, throw eggs at Ian Paisley, obtain tax-exempt status, put pressure on congressmen with lobbies, burn John Huss, confiscate government and private property on religious grounds, and deceive sinners into thinking that she is the Way of Life (see book by that name, Knights of Columbus pub.). [Not one power listed above is a Christian virtue or anything connected with the fruit of the Holy Spirit. ]{.class_4257}(See comments on Matt. 28:18.) What Rome mistakes for the "power of the Holy Ghost" or the power of Christ [is the temporal power which Satan has always granted to anyone who will bow down and worship him ]{.class_4257}(Luke 4; Matt. 4). \[See [The Sure Word of Prophecy.]{.class_4257}\]

["Then charged he his disciples\...."]{.class_4258} If the news got out from his twelve disciples, it would be considered an authoritative first-hand source of information, unlike the rumours of Mark 1:45. Such an admission would classify Jesus with the two "revolutionaries against the establishment"---Judas and Theudas (Acts 5:36--37). \[In []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_290}John 6:15, Jesus perceives that this type of thing is developing, and He abandons it and those connected with it. Trotsky and S. Charmichael would have stepped in as "King." [King ]{.class_4257}Cassius, [King ]{.class_4257}Cole, M. L. [King]{.class_4257}, [King ]{.class_4257}Kong, et al.\]

16:21 From that time forth began Jesus to shew unto his disciples, how that he must go unto Jerusalem, and suffer many things of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and be raised again the third day.

22 Then Peter took him, and began to rebuke him, saying, Be it far from thee, Lord: this shall not be unto thee.
