23 But he turned, and said unto Peter, Get thee behind me, Satan: thou art an offence unto me: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.

We are to assume that Jesus is addressing Satan through Peter. This seems to be an easy assumption for most of the commentators, but when they hit the "Anointed Cherub" (Ezek. 28), addressed in the person of the Prince of Tyrus, somehow they forget their laws of interpreting (see Moffatt's translation of Ezek. 28).

["Jesus]{.class_4258} \[began\][ to shew unto his disciples."]{.class_4258} What He shows them is remarkable if He were not God manifest in the flesh, for He gives them [ten details of His decease, ahead of time]{.class_4257}. If Jesus Christ were only a great teacher or "example," how in the world did He know ten details of His death ahead of time, more than a year before He died? The wisest men who ever lived could not name the place of their death or the time of their death or the manner of their death within a year of it, and where they attempted to do it, Providence made a liar out of them. Jesus says He will be [rejected; betrayed]{.class_4257}; delivered to [Gentiles ]{.class_4257}for [mocking, scourging]{.class_4257}, and [suffering]{.class_4257}; the elders, scribes and priests will be in on it; it will be unto [death]{.class_4257}; it will be death by [crucifixion]{.class_4257} at [Jerusalem; ]{.class_4257}and He will rise [three days later ]{.class_4257}(see Mark 8:31, Luke 9:22). See if you can find a parallel to that in 6,000 years of written history!

["Be it far from thee, Lord"]{.class_4258} shows Peter's interest and concern over the well-being of his Saviour. The remark is well-intended, but it is ill-\
executed since it overlooks the central truth of premillennialism. [No crown before the cross]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_291}. No reigning without suffering (2 Tim. 2:12; Rom. 8:17). It overlooks (as Presbyterians and Catholics overlook today) the correct [placing ]{.class_4257}of the Advents. He comes for the crown and kingdoms [later ]{.class_4257}(Rev. 11:15; 19:12), not now. \[See how Jesus drives this into Pilate's thick skull with the word ["now"]{.class_4258} in John 18:36. The [Amplified Version ]{.class_4257}carefully slanders the premillennial position by omitting the word. The [Amplified ]{.class_4257}is the standard catechism put out by the amillennial, infant-sprinkling, covenant-theology, Reformed "Christian" Church.\]

["This shall not be unto thee."]{.class_4258} Peter is prompted by love, yet at the same time does not realize that he is being used as an instrument in the hands of Satan---and of course thousands of "God-is-love People" today find themselves in the same snare. Peter does not want Jesus to be caught and imprisoned and to die; he is sincere in his belief, as he well proves himself in the garden (Matt. 26:51). But as all Old Testament Jews and all post- and amillennial Gentiles, [he cannot divide ]{.class_4257}["the sufferings"]{.class_4258}[ from the ]{.class_4257}["glory that should follow"]{.class_4258}[ ]{.class_4257}(1 Pet. 1:10--12). The passages on the suffering and the glory occur so close together (as in Isa. 61, where only a comma separates them), that any man who cannot "rightly divide the word of truth" has no hope of getting them straight. And strangely enough, God has only given this ability to those [who believe the revelation of Jesus Christ ]{.class_4257}(Rev. 1:1). It is not given to educated men, to Greek linguists, to theologians, or church scholars (Luke 10:21).

["Get thee behind me, Satan"]{.class_4258} is addressed to the devil IN Peter. A similar case is found in Acts 5:3 and 1 Samuel 18:10. Exactly how Rome failed to read the verse, after all the hellish uproar they raised on verses 16--19, is a mystery. If Peter is head of their church, then most certainly their church is Satan's church, and he is the head of it, for the visible head of it ([by their own catechism of faith]{.class_4257}) is the vice-regent of Christ, the pope, [ascended from Peter]{.class_4257}, who is called ["Satan."]{.class_4258} (Let us not join any church headed up by Simon Peter!)

["The things that be of God."]{.class_4258} The statement shows that Satan's true interest is with men (Gen. 6), and as a "god" (2 Cor. 4:4), he desires their worship [ahead of God ]{.class_4257}(Luke 4:7). This clarifies a number of things:

1\. Satan's sphere of activity is primarily [religious]{.class_4257}. He therefore will seek the place of worship in high [religious spheres]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_292}, to unite the world into one church, worshipping HIM.

2\. The "things of God" include suffering and a crucifixion of the flesh (Gal. 2:20, 6:14), and men do not "savour" (like) these things (see Luke 16:15).

3\. Peter has not yet seen the personal suffering HE must go through before "the kingdom comes" (John 21:18--19), and Jesus is rebuking him for it.

The [RSV ]{.class_4257}(always good for a laugh) comes through here with a translation that is so corrupt that it is beyond recovery. It is such a spurious gloss that one can hardly hope to recover the mutilated fragment from which it may have been borrowed. The reading given is, ["You are not on the side of God,"]{.class_4257} which of course does not occur in any Greek manuscript, uncial or miniscule, in any type of text: Egyptian (Hesychian), Byzantine (Syrian), Western or Jerusalem. It is typically representative of that botched and garbled type of work for which the translating committee is famous. ("Ta" (Gk.) is the neuter accusative plural. Neuter [is not somebody's "side,"]{.class_4257} and ["on ]{.class_4257}the side" would have to be in another case. Tough apples.)

16:24 Then said Jesus unto his disciples, If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me,

25 For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it.

26 For what is a man profited, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul? or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?

The passage (as that preceding it) leaps, in a practical sense, across the death, burial, and resurrection, into the Church Age. Several thoughts will enforce this reading, if given careful attention by the searcher of Scripture.

1\. ["If any man will come after me"]{.class_4258} is clearly an indication of [after his death]{.class_4257}, for the ["cross"]{.class_4258} is found in the verse. The verse, it is true, is addressed (doctrinally) to His immediate Jewish disciples, for the word ["life"]{.class_4257} is used interchangeably with ["soul"]{.class_4257} (for which, see the involved discussion in [Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis]{.class_4257}, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_293}under Gen. 1:27 and 2:7). \[For those not having this volume, the references in the Bible teach plainly that in the Old Testament the soul is bodily-shaped and stuck to the body, and, therefore, is used as synonymous with physical life---Lev. 22:6, 11, 23:30; Psa. 59:2--3; Gen. 17:14, 19:20, etc. In the New Testament, this soul is spiritually [circumcised ]{.class_4257}and cut loose from the flesh, and, hence, is "saved" (Col. 2:11--13; Rom. 8:8--10).\]

2\. However, verse 26 can be laid right down on the unbeliever after Pentecost, and you can point him to the cross with it. The Bible is careful to distinguish the losing of a [soul ]{.class_4257}by [damnation ]{.class_4257}(Matt. 16:26) and the losing of a [testimony ]{.class_4257}by being [cast away ]{.class_4257}(Luke 9:25; 1 Cor. 9:27). Again, the unsearch­able riches of the [AV ]{.class_4257}reveal a truth hidden to the "findings" of Wuest, Alford, Trench, and Westcott and Hort.

["His own soul"]{.class_4258} emphasizes the truth, again, that the soul is the [ego ]{.class_4257}part of man (or as Madam Blavatsky would say: "His astral body"). It is man's peculiar individualistic property that distinguishes him from [other ]{.class_4257}souls, and it is not kin to his "SPIRIT," which is said to be common property of any man (1 Cor. 2:11; Eccl. 3:21; Prov. 20:27).

Verses 24 and 25 are self-explanatory and teach that great contradictory principle of truth reiterated in 10:38--39. You get it by losing it, you lose it by getting it, you go up by getting down, and you go down by getting up (Luke 18:14). If a man comes to Jesus (and there is no other way but to number yourself among the transgressors, my friend!), he will wind up at a bloody cross. There he can accept Christ and die with Him (Rom. 6:2--11), or he can about-face and go back to Vanity Fair in the valley of destruction, to wine-and-dine until the unsheeted wrath of God cuts him down like Belshazzar. Do you want to [live]{.class_4257}? Come to Christ (John 10:10) and [die]{.class_4257}. Do you want to [die]{.class_4257}? Leave Christ out of your life and go on ["living it up."]{.class_4257}

Look at it this way, in verse 26. Suppose you [could ]{.class_4257}get the whole county you live in? No, that's too small; suppose you could get the whole state---no, the world! (You can't, but suppose you [could]{.class_4257}!) Well, you could take two such worlds up to Jupiter and sell them there, and you wouldn't get enough money to pay your hotel bill in the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_294}morning. Adolf Hitler, Alexander, Kaiser Bill, Caesar, and Genghis Khan [nearly got the world]{.class_4257}. Would you want to trade places with them now? (Think carefully and read a little history before you decide, for not a man in this group enjoyed life when he had it!)

Look at it this way. [Can you insure a house with an open fire burning in the cellar? ]{.class_4257}Not 1,000 miles below your feet it is so hot you'd die of heat prostration with a portable air-conditioner. The center of this ruined planet is a lake of nickel fire (James 3:6) . What do you want to gain it for? To burn up with it? If you got it, could you keep it? What would you do with it when you got it? No profit. \[It's like the colosseum manager said to Nero: "We've got to get rid of those lions; they're eatin' up all the [prophets]{.class_4257}!"\] No profit without Jesus. No profit, if you lose your soul.

16:27 For the Son of man shall come in the glory of his Father with his angels; and then he shall reward every man according to his works.

28 Verily I say unto you, There be some standing here, which shall not taste of death, till they see the Son of man coming in his kingdom.

The "glorious coming" (by all cross references) is the Advent after the Tribulation (see Matt. 24:30; Luke 21:27; Rom. 8:18). ["With his angels"]{.class_4258} would be the coming[ "with all his saints"]{.class_4258} mentioned by Paul in 1 Thessalonians 3:13, who are the end product of the First Resurrection (see Matt. 22:30 and note the lengthy comment on the passage) .

["He shall reward every man according to his works."]{.class_4258} The passage is [spiritually true ]{.class_4257}of the Christian (1 Cor. 3:5--10) but is even more [doctrinally true ]{.class_4257}of Matthew 25:30--41. The promised reward in Revelation 22:12 is in the same context.

["There be some standing here, which shall not taste of death\...."]{.class_4258} What follows (17:1--13) clearly sets the pattern for our thinking. There may be a thousand ways to take the passage, but in view of the scores of times we have seen the difference between a spiritual kingdom beginning at Pentecost, [which will die out]{.class_4257}, and a literal, visible Messianic kingdom which will come in cataclysmically [and stay]{.class_4257}, the ["some standing here"]{.class_4258} can only refer to what follows []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1646.xhtml_page_295}with Peter, James, and John.

The [Standard Commentary ]{.class_4257}(ignoring the clear statements of Matt. 2:2, 6, 3:2, 4:17, 5:35, 10:7, 23) accepts the Postmillennial theory of Augustine or the Amillennial theory of Calvin and assumes that the disciples who are [then present ]{.class_4257}live to see the spiritual "reign of Christ" come in Acts chapter 2, and therefore, this is ["the Son of man coming in his kingdom."]{.class_4258} \[We have commented at length on this doggerel and disgraceful method of private interpretation.\]

["The Son of man coming in his kingdom"]{.class_4258} is the event most dear to God's heart and the event referred to more times in the Bible than any other two subjects combined. The conceit of the modern Christian (described so well in Rom. 11) is that God will keep his hands off politics, money, church buildings, and governments, and limit Himself to "spiritual realities," while ["we men handle the ]{.class_4257}\
[really important matters."]{.class_4257} That is, the modern Christian, as Peter, savours the things of MEN, not God (16:23). [The postmillennial and amillennial systems were erected by this kind of Christian]{.class_4257}. The trick is to "run things" until the end. What is going to happen is after man has "run things" [into the ground ]{.class_4257}and the devil has come to help him out, God is going to intervene with "power and great glory" (Matt. 26:64). ["The Son of man coming in his kingdom"]{.class_4258} is a literal, visible, earthly kingdom, which will replace the bastard, Roman-socialistic-Gentile-integrated "Christianity."

The men referred to by "There be some standing here" are Peter, James, and John, who were standing there, who did NOT die till they saw the Son of Man coming, but what they saw was not a spiritual kingdom at Pentecost, but a glorified Christ attended by Moses and Elijah (see Mal. 4; Matt. 17:1--5; 2 Pet. 1:16--19). The actuality of the fulfillment of this vision is as sure as sunrise and sunset.

[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml}

# Chapter 17 {.heading_84-0}

17:1 And after six days Jesus taketh Peter, James, and John his brother, and bringeth them up into an high mountain apart,

2 And was transfigured before them: and his face did shine as the sun, and his raiment was white as the light.

3 And, behold, there appeared unto them Moses and Elias talking with him.

["After six days"]{.class_4258} is a goldmine of information, which, of course, is "a closed shaft" to an amillennialist. Going back to our Bible definition for "a day" (2 Pet. 3:8), we find that the Second Coming of Christ, to set up His kingdom, is dated in the Bible to the year; contingent, of course, on two things: first of all, that our calendar is correct, and secondly, that the date is clearly understood to be the date of the Advent---not the secret rapture of the Body of Christ (1 Thess. 4; 1 Cor. 15), which is undated.

Matthew uses ["after six days"]{.class_4258} to give a picture of the second return of Christ in glory, but Luke uses ["about an eight days"]{.class_4258} (Luke 9:28). It does not take a Th.D. to discern that ["after six"]{.class_4258} and ["about an eight"]{.class_4258} is SEVEN (7), nor should this come as a surprise, for God does everything by sevens, including swearing by Himself \[for the Hebrew word for swear (Heb. shaba') is the word for seven (Heb. sheba')\]. Consequently, God finishes a dispensation with SEVEN and renumbers with EIGHT. \[There is not a single truth in Scripture more forcibly presented than this one, but of course, it is a hidden enigma to the postmillennial and amillennial systems of misinterpretation.\]

In Leviticus chapters 23, 25, and Exodus chapter 22, you will find the systems laid out. Work six days, rest seven. Run six weeks; Pentecost after the seventh week. Work seven months; Feast of Tabernacles in seventh month. Work six years; rest the seventh. Work forty-nine years (7x7); blow the Jubilee at the fiftieth. The earth is destined to be here 6,000 years, and the last period (by every law of exegesis self-contained in the Bible) HAS TO be a Millennial sabbath of rest (Rev. 20:1--6), and thus it is denominated []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_297}in Isaiah 11:1--11 and 14:7-- 14. The amillennial and postmillennial addicts, who ignore this great and central truth running clear through the Bible, are guilty of despising one of the greatest revelations the Holy Spirit ever gave. Dividing time off into two grand periods, we see that Adam to Christ is 4,000 (exactly as the old archbishop figured), and Christ from "suffering to glory" is an even 2,000. The Millennium follows. There is no Jehovah's Witness-type figuring here, and there is no Seventh-day Adventist fumbling around in the "days of Daniel 12" to get a French Revolution. This is the mathematically correct system set up by the Bible itself for its own interpretation. It will certainly not err as much as a chemist will in making a bottle of hydrogen peroxide.

By this system, the seven periods of 1,000 years are divided at four and three. 4000 B.C. to Christ (four days)---Christ (4 B.C.) to the Kingdom A.D. 2000. (two days). The Kingdom, therefore, is set up at the beginning of the seventh day, A.D. 2000 to A.D. 3000. If this is taken by Fundamentalist brethren as being "speculation" or "here- sy" or "surmising," let it be stated emphatically that John 1:29, 35, 43, 51(!), 2:1, 4:40; Luke 13:6--9; Exodus 19:16; and Hosea 6:1--3 are written with this exact doctrine in mind, and it fits EVERY area of truth that deals with the end-time, directly or indirectly. Even the seventh day of Genesis 2:1 has no "evening and morning" as the other days, for it is an endless day (Rev. 20:1--6) that terminates at the gates of eternity, to usher in an eternal morning (Rev. 20:7--12, 21:1--6). That last glorious book of all books in the Bible loudly cries: seven trumpets, seven vials, seven seals, seven churches, seven candlesticks, seven spirits, and seven stars! Finish!

["After six days,"]{.class_4258} then, dates the Advent. What follows is a detailed description (in type) of the Second Coming of Jesus Christ to Israel at the end of the Tribulation.

["Into an high mountain apart."]{.class_4258} The exact prototype is Exodus 19:16, where it is Sinai (the location of the Second Advent appearance, previous to the landing of Zechariah 14:1--4 on the Mount of Olives). This is a fact carefully concealed from Scofield, Larkin, DeHaan, and other premillennialists who rejected the [AV ]{.class_4257}for the "Greek." The Second Coming route is the route of Israel in the Old Testament: Sinai, Mt. Seir, Mt. Paran, Edom, Selah, Sihon, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_298}Heshbon, Rabbath, Gilgal, Jericho, Jerusalem (Judg. 5; Num. 21; Deut. 32, 33; Hab. 3; and many others).

It is interesting to note that wherever the [Authorized Version ]{.class_4257}is rejected and the Roman tradition of Greek criticism (from Alexandria in Africa) is ACCEPTED, God immediately stops future revelation. There is no way to account for this but to believe that the Holy Spirit bears witness to the teaching and preaching of an [AV]{.class_4257} in the way He will to no other version. This is considered "ignorance" or "prejudice," or both, by Fundamentalists who wish people to think that they are masters in interpretation, because of proficiency in Greek. However, at this point, the Holy Spirit plays a wonderful prank. He refuses to let them find anything "new'' in the Scriptures; they hash and rehash what they've been taught until they "dead-end."

Hence, the Reformers came no further than Calvin. The Lutherans stopped with Martin, and the Fundamentalists stopped with Bullinger and Scofield. All have one thing in common: they make corrections in the [AV]{.class_4257}. When a "nugget" is found, it is nothing but a re-emphasis of something Stam and Larkin already found. When something really "new" comes up (like Rice's Gethsemane "cup" or Wuest's---2 Thess. 2---"apostasy"), it is nothing but a denial of truth found in other verses. Our commentators and expositors have lost the key by rejecting the authority. They are like Picasso and Matisse experimenting with "NEW" forms and creating nothing but disassembled monstrosities and inferior duplications of primitive art. Or musically, they are as Stravinsky and Copeland, pressing forward in the name of EVOLUTION (pretending they are misunderstood geniuses), while all the time, anyone understands them: they are businessmen trying to make a name by experimentation. Revelation ceases where faith in the absolute authority of God's word ceases. Thus with the [RV ]{.class_4257}of 1885 and the [ASV ]{.class_4257}of 1901 and the [RSV ]{.class_4257}of 1952---art, music, literature, poetry, and Bible revelation cease. Communications and transportation take over.

["Into an high mountain apart"]{.class_4258} is a scene to be repeated sometime after January 2000 at Mt. Sinai, NOT the Mount of Olives.

["And was transfigured before them."]{.class_4258} Hence, the "Mount of []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_299}Transfiguration" as in Revelation 1:14--15; 2 Peter 1:16--18; and Exodus chapter 19. The figure is "transformed"; hence, "transfiguration." John bears witness to it (1 John 1:1--2). Notice that the ["light"]{.class_4258} is considerably brighter than the SUN, and thus ["light"]{.class_4258} (Gen. 1:2--5) is defined as something other than sunlight (see Psa. 74:16). This error, in reading, has caused the [Scofield Bible ]{.class_4257}to talk about sunlight being present before the fourth day of creation, but kind of "poking through" the atmosphere on the fourth day! Again, we should be careful to notice how the [AV ]{.class_4257}uses words. "LIGHT" is not always "sunlight" (1 John 1:5).

"Face did shine as THE SUN, and his raiment was white as THE LIGHT."

The reference to the sun restates the truth, stated so many times before, that the Second Coming is likened to the Sun (Christ) coming up in the Fourth or Morning Watch, to bring in "the millennial day" (see, for example, Matt. 13:43 and especially the comments on Matt. 16:2--3). This establishes the chronology of Matthew 17:1--3 as being an exact picture of the Advent in glory, not the Rapture in secret.

["Moses and Elias."]{.class_4258} The two names occur here with Jesus on a ["high mountain apart,"]{.class_4258} so we should not be surprised to find the name of Jesus, the name of Moses, the name of Elijah, and the name of the mountain found in the last four verses in the Old Testament, before God closes the revelation for 400 years.

The salient names, then (in relation to the Second Coming), are not "the last judgment" or "the golden age" or "world unity" or "bringing in the kingdom." The names are ["Horeb," "the Lord," "Moses" ]{.class_4258}and ["Elijah."]{.class_4258} The words spell doom for the Gentile-Catholic juggernaut which professes "Christianity," for all the names are Jewish (see Jer. 46:28 and Rom. 11:20--33).

The ["two witnesses,"]{.class_4258} then, of Revelation chapter 11 are identified in no uncertain terms to be Moses and Elijah. (See comments on Matt. 3:4 and 16:13­­--16.)

The false teaching of Fundamentalists that the two witnesses are ELIJAH and ENOCH is, of course, some of their perennial inheritance from Rome, who left nothing behind to anyone but an omnibus of perverted interpretations. \[See [Rome the Great Private Interpreter,]{.class_4257} 1969.\]

1\. Elijah, Moses, and Jesus are all on a mountain (Exod. 24:18; 1 Kings 19:8; Matt. 4:8).

2\. Elijah, Moses, and Jesus are all fasting 40 days and nights (Exod. 24:18; Luke 4:2; 1 Kings 19:8).

3\. Elijah, Moses, and Jesus all stand in the "presence of the LORD" (Exod. 33:21; 1 Kings 17:1; Acts 2:25, 33--35; Heb. 8:1, 12:1--3).

4\. Elijah, Moses, and Jesus were all sent to Israel (Exod. 2­--4; 1 Kings 17--18; Luke 4; Matt. 10:1--8).

5\. Elijah, Moses, and Jesus all crossed water miraculously (Exod. 14--15; Matt. 14; 2 Kings 2).

6\. Elijah and Moses ended their lives where Jesus was baptized (Deut. 34; 2 Kings 2; John 1:28), east of Jordan, at the crossing.

7\. Elijah was caught up living, but will die again (2 Kings 2; Rev. 11).

8\. Moses was caught up after death, but will die again (Deut. 34; Rev. 11).

9\. Moses, Elijah, and Jesus all personally selected a minister to take their place (Deut. 3:24--28; 2 Kings 2; Rom. 15:8--9; Acts 9:15--16).

10\. All three destroy enemies by fire, which comes at their spoken command (Num. 16:31--35; 2 Kings 1; Mal. 4; Rev. 11, 19).

11\. All three have power over the natural elements (Exod. 5--10; 1 Kings 17:1; James 5:17; Rev. 11:3--6; Matt. 14:31--33).

It is therefore stubborn, willful ignorance to insert "ENOCH" simply because you believe that all men "have to die once" (and Enoch never did), on the basis of the comparative, not doctrinal, statement of Hebrews 9:27. The statement is not doctrinally true, for Jonah dies twice, the widow of Nain's son dies twice, Lazarus dies twice, the son of the Shunamite dies twice, the little daughter of Jairus dies twice! So Moses died in Deuteronomy chapter 34 and was buried (Jude 9) and was resurrected and will come to earth again (Rev. 11) and die again (Rev. 11:9--12).

If Enoch were the other witness of Revelation chapter 11, then there would be no man in the Bible who would be a type of the Christian who is living in this age when Christ comes, for a living Christian is caught up without dying, never to die in the future (John []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_301}11:26)! Enoch, then (not Elijah or Moses), is a picture of the rapture, and Elijah and Moses DO NOT represent living and dead Church-Age saints at the rapture, but represent living and dead Jewish saints in the Tribulation at the Advent (see Rev. 6:9--11, 7:1--9).

The confusion in the mind of the rejector of the [AV ]{.class_4257}is from the same source as all confusion: trying to change (see notes on Matt. 4:5--6) the [Authorized Version ]{.class_4257}to make it fit the false system that you have been taught. What you should do, my dear reader, is repent and change your man-made system to fit the verses! Then you would get some light from heaven instead of a flashlight some pope has been carrying around in his cassock.

The pope inserts "PETER" as Christ's chosen minister, whereas the Bible choses PAUL. And the Reformers choose ENOCH for Elijah, as they (with one consent) refuse to believe the revelation of Jesus Christ (Rev. 1:1).

All one can say about this is that the abundance of false doctrine and the abundance of opposition to the [AV ]{.class_4257}shows that in high and low places, throughout the world, CHRISTIANS have long ago lost interest in the Book. Their leaders have rejected the Holy Spirit as the Interpreter and turned back to Rome for help. It is said, "Well, each insists he is right and believes in what he believes as much as you do, etc.", or "Well, I do not believe we can be dogmatic about it, because many good men have believed contrary, etc."

Have they really?

Such lame alibis are the mark of a lazy, stubborn, conceited man, for no man could do research in religious opinions without being struck by the fact that all disagreement, everywhere, lies in one source every time---refusal to accept the [AV ]{.class_4257}as the authority. No one differs on anything who believes every verse in it, as found, where you find it; but who believes that anymore? Rome has succeeded, underground, in accomplishing the destruction of the Protestant's faith in his Bible. He is split over Greek infinitives and torn asunder by adjectival participles in the "original" until he doesn't have anything but a Westcott and Hort text left, and this is "VATICANUS," the private property of the private interpreters!

Mark it and note it! Any man that would teach that Enoch is the witness of Revelation 11, and NOT Elijah, is a man who prefers his learning above the text of the [AV]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_302}. Mark it! \[There are no exceptions in any branch of the Christian church. What appears as a minute point in prophecy turns (with time) into the head-and-tail of the whole behemoth of interpretations. Laziness and rebellion are the causes of false readings and interpretations.\]

17:4 Then answered Peter, and said unto Jesus, Lord, it is good for us to be here: if thou wilt, let us make here three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.

5 While he yet spake, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them: and behold a voice out of the cloud, which said, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased; hear ye him.

["If thou wilt"]{.class_4258} is a dew-drop of humility that is rarely found in the cloudbursts of Peter's speech. In Acts 10, it is: ["not so, Lord"]{.class_4258} (Acts 10:14) and in Matthew 16, it is: ["Be it FAR from thee, Lord."]{.class_4258} You will notice that the old, commercial fisherman is always careful to insert ["Lord"]{.class_4258} at the proper place to give the thing a sort of "spiritual" sound, but this time it really IS spiritual. Instead of bursting in and saying, "Lord, we're going to build three tabernacles here," he says, ["If thou wilt"]{.class_4258} (James 4:15). Notwithstanding, the desire is still wrongly placed, as usual! For ["This is my beloved Son,"]{.class_4258} in verse 5, dispels all doubt as to the proper place for the Law (Moses) and the Prophets (Elijah). Christ is not "A" son of God (like some saintly Moses or Elijah), but ["my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased."]{.class_4258} Moses and Elijah, Buddha and Mohammed, Confucius and Zarathustra, St. Patrick and St. Francis are not to be compared for a moment to Jesus Christ, when it comes to "building three tabernacles." The tabernacle erected for Jesus is a human body (1 Cor. 6:13, 3:16; Acts 7:48--50; John 2:21), thus showing that Jesus is the Creator of that body (Eph. 2:20--22). Since none of the other religious leaders, teachers, philosophers, martyrs, saints, ascetics, "holy men," monks, dervishes, seers, sages, scholars, or crusaders are anything but men, you can build a building for them while you reserve your body for your private pleasure (Rom. 12:1--2). And of course, this is what all unsaved people want---a building []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_303}where they can stick their God (or a direction that they can face), and then do what they want to do with the remainder of the day or year.

As a modern cynic has accurately observed: "The first thing people do when they get confused is say, ['Let us build\...']{.class_4258}" (Gen. 11:1--4). Peter, in the text, is confused. He makes the mistake Fosdick and McCracken make in approving of buildings where statues of Jesus, Buddha, and Confucius stand side-by-side. The surest proof in the world that they have nothing in common is the profession of their followers, for no Buddhist or Confucianist professes knowledge of certain salvation after death, any more than their pagan companions---the Catholics, Protestants, and Jews. A Bible-believing Christian does profess this and can turn to it in black and white, as the Holy Spirit wrote it. Jesus, Moses, and Elijah have similarities in their background, training, calling, and ministry; but Moses and Elijah have human fathers, they have sins and faults, in failure they ask for death, they disobey the Lord on occasions, they die for nobody's sins, they have to ask about Christ's death for information (Luke 9:31), and they have to pray for forgiveness. ["The feast of tabernacles,"]{.class_4258} then, although it pictures the restoration of Israel in the Tribulation (attended by Moses and Elijah), is primarily a ["feast of the ]{.class_4258}[Lord]{.class_4267}[."]{.class_4258} Only one man is worthy of the erected tabernacles. God said it when He says: ["This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased; hear ye him."]{.class_4258}

["Hear ye HIM"]{.class_4258} (see 1 Tim. 2:5), not Moses, Elijah, Pope Johnny, Mohammed, H. G. Wells, Einstein, Spengler, Tolstoy, Faulkner, Goethe, Lawrence, Goldsmith, Cervantes, Darwin, Paine, Jung, Maugham, Hilton, Hugo, Aquinas, Hobbes, Anderson, Cellini, Molliere, Anatole France, Thurber, Thackery, Bronte, Kipling, Joseph, Mary, Fatima, Herodotus, Pogo, Balzac, Boccacio, Amos and Andy, Nietzsche, Spencer, Wylie, Van Loon, Humpty Dumpty, Poe, Machiavelli, Mark Twain, Kennedy, Martin and Lewis, Sammy Davis, Peter Lawford, Lena Horne, and Quasimodo! HEAR YE HIM! Never mind that bunch of depraved, self-righteous, religious humanitarians! HEAR YE HIM! (Isa. 55:3; Rev. 22:17; John 5:24).

Brother, where He speaks and where they speak, don't give a second thought to "them"; ["Hear ye HIM."]{.class_4258} All you can do for []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_304}"THEM" is either pray for them, or if they are dead, try what you can to avoid winding up in the same place with them. \[I believe that the government should institute a national "pray-for-smart-alecks week"; especially for the benefit of the religious intelligentsia in America (and the Communists), who think God is too dumb to put them into the Lake of Fire (Rev. 20).\]

The ["bright cloud"]{.class_4258} of verse 5 and the ["voice out of the cloud"]{.class_4258} again identify the time of the typology; it is, without argument, the Second Advent. You say, "So-and-so says"; yes, but so-and-so went nowhere to Scripture to find the similitude, and if he did, he changed the [AV ]{.class_4257}to prove his point. Disqualified automatically! Scriptures do not yield truth to people who come to them in this manner. (See in Exod. 19:16 and 19:19 and compare them with the Advent passages in Matt. 24:30 and Rev. 14:14.) This CLOUD is not the being ["caught up together with them in the clouds"]{.class_4258} of 1 Thessalonians chapter 4, but the ["great cloud"]{.class_4258} of Ezekiel 1:4, heralding the portentious and climactic coming of Jesus Christ in wrath and power, to reign on earth (Rev. 5:10).

Moses, then, will represent a Tribulation saint who dies and is buried and comes up again at the end of the Tribulation, to go into the Millennium to die again (and would keep on dying if it were not for the Tree of Life given to the saints who "keep His commandments" in that period---Rev. 22:14, 14:12, 12:17).

Elijah represents the living saints who are caught up at the post-Tribulation rapture (Isa. 26; Psa. 50; Job 37; Matt. 24:30--31), who go alive into the Millennium to die there a natural death \[as Elijah goes up living in 2 Kings 2, but dies in the tribulation (Rev. 11:6--14)\].

17:6 And when the disciples heard it, they fell on their face, and were sore afraid.

7 And Jesus came and touched them, and said, Arise, and be not afraid.

Verse 6 indicates the right response of the Jew in the Tribulation, who (as Paul on the road to Damascus) is converted to full faith in Christ by a resurrection appearance. This will take place again and it is described in the Psalms a dozen times in the context of the word []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_305}["shine."]{.class_4258}

["Arise, and be not afraid"]{.class_4258} is vaguely reminiscent of the passages in Luke chapter 24 and John chapter 20, where Christ arises from the dead and personally appears to the apostles. Note the ["arise"]{.class_4258} is used on Paul in Acts 9:6. Thus, the Scriptures, by intrinsic construction, reinforce (for any reader) the thought that Jesus Christ will make a personal appearance to Israel, in the Tribulation, and convert them to Himself (Isa. 66:7--24; 1 Cor. 15:8).

The importance, therefore, of the word "tribulation" is seen by the student of the Scripture. This doctrine (dealt with in more than 500 passages in the Old Testament directly and 1,000 passages indirectly) is the time of "Jacob's Trouble" or "Daniel's Seventieth Week." Since the Reformed and Catholic scholarship of A.D. 500--A.D. 1900 has placed "Daniel in the critic's den" (the "lions" being Darwin's theory applied to Bible prophecy, Rousseau's theory that man is born good but corrupted by his environment, Horace Bushnell's fiasco that God is everybody's "Father," and Rauschenbush's idea that the "Kingdom of God" was evolving), the Christian's historical, Biblical doctrine of the seven-year period at the end-time has been all but obliterated. Even in the mind of Bible-believing Baptists, not one out of ten has any idea what the conversion of Israel involves, when it takes place, where it takes place, how it takes place, and where it is described in the Scripture. Matthew 17:1--7 is the prototype.

17:8 And when they had lifted up their eyes, they saw no man, save Jesus only.

9 And as they came down from the mountain, Jesus charged them, saying, Tell the vision to no man, until the Son of man be risen again from the dead.

10 And his disciples asked him, saying, Why then say the scribes that Elias must first come?

11 And Jesus answered and said unto them, Elias truly shall first come, and restore all things.

["No man, save Jesus only."]{.class_4258} The vision is over, and there remains the earthly Christ in His humility, standing (without the Law or the Prophets), ready to die for the transgressions of law-violators []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_306}and prophet-disregarders.

["Save Jesus only."]{.class_4258} The construction is remarkable in the [AV ]{.class_4257}for the word ["save"]{.class_4258} is curiously linked with ["Jesus"]{.class_4258} ("Jehovah saves") as soon as Moses and Elijah disappear. ["Save Jesus only"]{.class_4258} has been taken out of context to mean that there is no Trinity, and that ["Jesus only"]{.class_4258} is all of God there is. This perversion comes from refusal to grasp the essential characteristics (laid out in child's language in the Bible) of body, soul, and spirit. God's Soul is the Father. God's Son is the Body. God's Spirit is the Holy Spirit (see notes on Matt. 13:43). All three persons of the Godhead are found separately in Matthew 3:16--17. ["Jesus only"]{.class_4258} is correct in that He is the only visible, bodily, human representation of the Father ever seen (1 John 5:20; John 14:9). (Quit skipping over the references and go look up a few of them! You can't run on recaps all your life!)

["Tell the vision to no man"]{.class_4258} is explained under Matthew 16:20.

["Risen again from the dead."]{.class_4258} Not all of the interplay of thought is found in Matthew, and we have to pick up Mark 9:10 where the disciples are puzzled over the statement ["from the dead."]{.class_4258}

"And they kept that saying with themselves, questioning one with another what the RISING FROM THE DEAD SHOULD MEAN."

Their bewilderment did not die out in A.D. 33, for ["the rising from the dead,"]{.class_4258} indicates clearly a resurrection from the dead, preceding the resurrection of the dead!

Here, we come to the heart of the matter and discern the monstrous caricature presented by Rome and adopted by the Reformers (the Lutherans and Episcopalians). This caricature is a "finished drawing" in Peter Paul Rubens and Michelangelo, who help Rome foist on the ignorant public the idea that all the dead come up at one time at a "general judgment."

Rome finds a quorum in Berkhof, Dabney, Kuyper, Hodges, Strong, and every Southern Baptist Theological School in the country, for lo and behold, they all turn out an official theology of amillennialism with one resurrection and one judgment. Thus, they entirely ignore (or deliberately refuse to believe) the Lord Jesus []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_307}Christ.

1\. The resurrection of the just (Luke 14:14) is spoken of as being apart from the resurrection of the ["just AND unjust"]{.class_4258} (Acts 24:15).

2\. The resurrection of the ["quick and the dead"]{.class_4258} are said to be two separate resurrections: one at His ["appearing,"]{.class_4258} and one at His ["kingdom"]{.class_4258} (2 Tim. 4:1--2).

3\. The FIRST resurrection is plainly stated to precede the SECOND by 1,000 years (Rev. 20:1--6).

4\. The First Resurrection itself is said to be in three installments (exactly as any garden is plucked): firstfruits, harvest, gleanings (1 Cor. 15:22--24). The Second Resurrection is always in ONE installment.

Despite the massive weight of evidence for these truths, Rome, the Liberal, and many Fundamentalists (today) teach "one general resurrection" and no resurrection ["FROM the dead."]{.class_4258}

The issue is so decisive that every opposition of unbelieving Judaism from Matthew chapter 23 to Acts chapter 28 is based on the fact that unsaved people refuse to believe that anyone can get enough righteousness from God to merit a ["resurrection from the dead,"]{.class_4258} Christ being the first object of unbelief, and that all (as the self-righteous, works-trusting, religious humbugs) must come up at one time and get to heaven or hell on the basis of their own religious righteousness. This error reveals the fundamental unbelief and hatred in the human heart for the Lord Jesus and for His words. It is a sad commentary on Protestantism that there are thousands of preachers mouthing this Roman blasphemy from their pulpits.

Notice in every case the issue is: Can someone come up and be resurrected before the last judgment? Study carefully, please!

(Matt. 27:64--- "Don't let Him come up!")

(Matt. 28:13--- "Don't let Him come up!")

(Acts 4:2--- "Don't let Him come up!")

(Acts 5:31--33--- "Don't let Him come up!")

(Acts 23:6--- "Don't let Him come up!")

(Acts 17:32--- "Don't let Him come up!!")

The first and main objection to the Gospel in the Bible (1 Cor. 15:1--6) is that if Christ did come up from the dead, then He proved that He was righteous and no one else is. If such is the case, no one can arise ["from"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_308} the dead to meet God's favor as Christ did, unless Jesus Christ Himself is received by that person and comes to indwell that person's body (Rom. 10:1--6).

Thus, the resurrection ["FROM the dead"]{.class_4258} proves the Virgin Birth, the sinless life of Jesus Christ, the surety of the believer to go up before the last judgment, and the absolute certainty of a literal, bodily resurrection when it comes. Martha, being a Catholic-Presbyterian (serving, but never learning!), makes this amillennial mistake in John 11:24, where Jesus is quick to tell her that her theological training and "church raising" is just so much "dust in the eye." Look at what He says to her in verses 25--26, and surely enough, out comes Lazarus ["FROM the dead,"]{.class_4258} to prove that a Jew in the Tribulation (as Moses) has a resurrection in a natural body to go into the Millennium. The Christian in this age, to the contrary, cannot die again (John 11:26), for he obtains the glorified body of Philippians 3:20--21; 1 John 3:1--3; and Luke 24.

["FROM the dead,"]{.class_4258} therefore, automatically condemns, finally, every man, religion, nation, teaching, sect, cult, denomination, and body that teaches that heaven is gained by good works in this age. It is a proof called ["infallible"]{.class_4258} in Acts 1:2--4, and it is such a tremendous demonstration that Christ's "going to the Father" at the resurrection is the doctrinal theological statement in John 16:10, that He is righteous and the entire world is unrighteous (note John 16:8).

To teach, therefore, a general resurrection and "last judgment" of ALL dead in ALL ages (while at the same time professing to believe in Jesus' resurrection ["FROM the dead"]{.class_4258}) is to present an antithetical plank of theology so cross-grained that a rip saw couldn't make a dent in it. If HE came up ahead of time, all those "in Him" will come up ahead of time (1 Cor. 15:20--25). If all stay down until the last judgment, then bless your soul, He is still down waiting for it too!

\[Don't shilly-shally with it and delude yourself into thinking such a statement is "over-simplification." Just admit that you haven't got any sense and prayerfully study a few passages until God gives you the wisdom to know what you believe and why you believe it.\]

His disciples did not know what "that" meant: ["FROM the dead."]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_309} A large number of Christians in America don't know it either!

["Elias must first come\...Elias truly shall first come."]{.class_4258} The passage has already been commented on in great detail, under Matthew 11:14, 17:3, 16:14, and 14:2. To sum it up, the Lord does NOT say that Elijah will NOT come, but that He will come. The additional statement in verse 12 points out John as a "type" of Elijah. It warns the reader of what was stated in Matthew chapter 1--3; i.e., that all conditions at the First Coming of Christ (up until Acts 7) were in line with all the Old Testament prophecies on the Second Coming. Think about this---Elijah, like John, is beheaded, when he does show up (Rev. 11:6--14). You see, it is not a matter of anyone thinking they alone are right; it is a matter of the Scripture always being right on anything, whether or not anyone believes it.

17:12 But I say unto you, That Elias is come already, and they knew him not, but have done unto him whatsoever they listed. Likewise shall also the Son of man suffer of them.

13 Then the disciples understood that he spake unto them of John the Baptist.

See the notes under Matthew 11:13 and notice that the ominous ["He that hath ears to hear, let him hear"]{.class_4258} occurs immediately, warning the theologian that he would do well to approach the passage reverently and studiously, and not go hop, skip, and jump through it, in his denominational robes, until he crashes through the greenhouse roof. To make John be Elijah and knock out the prophecies given under Matthew 16:14 and 17:3 and Malachi 4 is equivalent to tearing up 5 percent of your Bible and throwing it into file thirteen.

["Whatsoever they listed."]{.class_4258} (Old English for "liked" or "wanted to do" or "were inclined to do.") There is no need for changing it as all bream fishermen and mackerel fishermen, sailors, and marines understand what a boat does when it "lists" to one side. Only Bible revisers have trouble with the plain English words; they are a kind of "a hide in the bookshelf" folks, anyway, and cannot be expected to grasp the language of everyday, practical working life.

["Likewise shall also the Son of man suffer..."]{.class_4258} Moses, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_310}Elijah, John, and Jesus all suffer death at the hand of unbelieving Israel, in conjunction with Gentile authority (see Rev. 11:7--13; Matt. 14:1--10, 20:19). ["Son of man"]{.class_4258} identifies, again, His Messianic claims to the throne of David as Israel's Deliverer (Luke 1:76--79, 1:30--33).

17:14 And when they were come to the multitude, there came to him a certain man, kneeling down to him, and saying,

15 Lord, have mercy on my son: for he is lunatick, and sore vexed: for ofttimes he falleth into the fire, and oft into the water.

16 And I brought him to thy disciples, and they could not cure him.

We have finished comment on the important paragraph of Matthew 17:1--13. It contains such material that no one but a premillennialist could ever hope to uncover its riches (and then he would have to be careful that the "tradition of the elders" didn't give him a Roman Geiger counter that only ticked when it detected garbage).

Descending from the glorious mount, we come from the sublime to the ridiculous; for while Peter, James, and John (a type of the converted remnant of Israel) are witnessing the foreshadow of the Second Advent, Israel, after the flesh, is down at the bottom of the mountain in hand-to-hand combat with the demons, and unable to prevail, in spite of their Talmud, synagogues, and "original manuscripts."

["For he is lunatick."]{.class_4258} The word is literally "moonstruck." (As in Von Braun, [LIFE ]{.class_4257}magazine, Russia, the U.S.A., Carpenter, and Glenn.) "Obsessed by the moon"; hence, the frequent references in popular music to invoke the "starry night" and the "moonglow" to help in an act of immoral seduction. It is "Luna" in the Latin and Spanish. The Greek is [seleniazomai ]{.class_4257}(etai) in the text. The [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 is correct, and the student might be interested to know that, here, the [RSV ]{.class_4257}comes to the aid of Dark Age, Roman medicine by associating physical diseases with demon possession. The [RSV ]{.class_4257}translates LUNATIC as "epileptic"! This creates within the mind of the reader []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_311}that anyone who has "EPILEPSY" is demon-possessed, and this was the teaching of Catholicism from A.D. 500 to A.D. 1500. Hence, prisons and chains and dungeon for epileptics. This man, however, in Matthew 17:15 is NOT an epileptic. The [RSV ]{.class_4257}makes him into an epileptic, but then again the [RSV ]{.class_4257}is not to be taken seriously when it comes to the matters of historical accuracy, medical correctness, or even reasonable honesty. \[One of the [RSV ]{.class_4257}translators' "near cousins," at Mercer University (Macon, Ga.), said to his class, "We must not believe that Paul experienced any actual supernatural conversion on the road to Damascus. To the contrary, the evidence would indicate that he experienced an epileptic fit." To which a student, a "Georgia cracker" from the red-clay country, said prayerfully, "Lord God, give us more epileptics!!"\]

["He falleth into the fire, and oft into the water."]{.class_4258} Again we are reminded that the "demons" have an affinity for warm, wet places. (See the numerous explanatory notes under Matt. 8:29--32, comparing them with the detailed Scriptural account in Mark 5:1--15.)

Our Lord strongly rebukes not only his disciples (vs. 16), but evidently the crowd that could not believe (vs. 17), also. Although the disciples take the ["O faithless and perverse generation"]{.class_4258} to heart (vs. 19), still the word ["generation"]{.class_4258} indicates it is not entirely the disciples' fault that the man is uncured.

17:17 Then Jesus answered and said, O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him hither to me.

18 And Jesus rebuked the devil; and he departed out of him: and the child was cured from that very hour.

19 Then came the disciples to Jesus apart, and said, Why could not we cast him out?

20 And Jesus said unto them, Because of your unbelief: for verily I say unto you, If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this mountain, Remove hence to yonder place; and it shall remove; and nothing shall be impossible unto you.

21 Howbeit this kind goeth not out but by prayer and []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_312}fasting.

To paraphrase verse 17, it would be: "Every time I turn around down here I find a bunch of people who will not believe because they are crooked, and sometimes I wonder how much longer I can put up with it! When is my Father going to let me finish this work and get on Home?!"

["How long shall I suffer you?"]{.class_4258} is beautiful; it defines the word ["suffer"]{.class_4258} as being more than just allowing somebody. You hurt when you allow it!

The exorcism practised in the text is like that found in many places (Mark 5:13, 16:9; Acts 19:13--18). The last text shows that the demons are under the authority of Paul and Jesus (see Matt. 28:18; Eph. 2:20--21); whereas, the unregenerate are trying to handle something over which they have no control. Before the crucifixion, this power is only given on occasions; note that the apostles do have this power in Matthew 10:8, but seem to have lost it in Matthew 17:18--19. The only answer is that the special, spiritual disease this boy has is unusual, and it takes more than a "commission" to expel the demon---it takes ["prayer and fasting."]{.class_4258} There is no verse 21 in an [RSV ]{.class_4257}as there is no verse 21 in Nestle's Greek text! It is rather amusing to notice the [RSV ]{.class_4257}running 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 11! Or 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 30! \[This subtracting from the word of God is a constant regular occurrence in an [RSV]{.class_4257}, yet preachers (Premillennialists included) will fight to publicize a "tolerant" attitude toward it!\] Look at the [RSV ]{.class_4257}in Acts 15:34, 24:7; Mark 7:16; Matt. 23:14, 21:44, 12:47. Thus, we see that the [RSV ]{.class_4257}is not really a Bible at all! It is a mutilated fragment of a spurious Vatican "mess. "

Critically speaking, this amputating of the word of God is justified by the scholars on the basis of a fabulous theory concocted by Westcott and Hort. (Actually, they did not concoct it originally, but borrowed it from the infidel C. H. Weisse \[1838\].) This is the theory that Matthew and Luke made common use of two documents ("two document theory"): the gospel of Mark and a mythical "Q" document in Aramaic, which by the way justifies the Catholic interpretation of Peter as the rock (see Matt. 16:16--18)! This would account for Luke and Matthew agreeing with Mark in some places and agreeing with []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_313}each other (though not with Mark), where they resorted to the Santa Claus, "Q" document. Streeter ([The Four Gospels]{.class_4257}) explained this myth, in 1924, and proved again that scholars are not immune from believing ghost stories and fairy tales. The theory, by the way, forbids Matthew and Luke to disagree with the wording in Mark, but the manuscripts used to omit our text (Matt. 17:21), "B" and "Aleph" (Vaticanus and Sinaiticus), do agree many times against Mark!

To defend this tangle of barbed wire reasoning, Streeter finally resorted to adopting any reading (regardless of textual evidence) if it would maintain his theory (Four Gospels, pp. 330--341). This led eventually to Streeter saying that Mark 6:45 to 8:26 was torn out of the copy that was sent to Luke for Luke to "recopy," so Luke doesn't have it!

Westcott and Hort, in 1885, fought their way through the Revision Committee with this kind of nonsense to prove that certain passages in Matthew were spuriously borrowed from Luke and Mark in the Textus Receptus; hence, no Matthew 17:21 in an [RSV]{.class_4257}. Further, the Revision Committees of 1885 ([RV]{.class_4257}) and 1901 ([ASV]{.class_4257}) and 1952 ([RSV]{.class_4257}) incorporate Streeter's hallucinations in their translations as though GOD was responsible for them!

["Ye shall say unto this mountain."]{.class_4258} The mountain is not identified although tradition places it as Mount Hermon, about fifty miles north of Galilee. The saying occurs twice: once here and once in Matthew 21:21, where the reference is to the Mount of Olives. This is odd, for the Mount of Olives does remove at the Second Coming when Christ lands right where He is walking in Matthew 21:21 (see Zech. 14:1--6). No further application can be made, as no one has yet sorted out the references on the double route the Second Coming takes; one northward down the sea coast, the other southward up from Sinai, and this commentary is limited to exposition of the text.

The practical lessons are obvious. Praying and fasting are sometimes necessary to obtain an answer to prayer (see Matt. 9:14--15 and comments). A little faith can accomplish a lot. Unbelief is the main hindrance to prayer. Nothing is impossible (Gen. 18:14) though much is improbable. Mountains have been literally moved by earthquakes, avalanches, dynamite, bombs, and bull-dozers, so who is to say that it was not an answer to someone's prayer?

17:22 And while they abode in Galilee, Jesus said unto them, The Son of man shall be betrayed into the hands of men:

23 And they shall kill him, and the third day he shall be raised again. And they were exceeding sorry.

The passage is identical in teaching to the one in Matthew 16:21. It is repeated in 20:17--18 and shows clearly that Jesus senses the time is drawing nigh and the fourth Passover of His ministry will find Him crucified as the Passover Lamb.

17:24 And when they were come to Capernaum, they that received tribute money came to Peter, and said, Doth not your master pay tribute?

25 He saith, Yes. And when he was come into the house, Jesus prevented him, saying, What thinkest thou, Simon? of whom do the kings of the earth take custom or tribute? of their own children, or of strangers?

26 Peter saith unto him, Of strangers. Jesus saith unto him, Then are the children free.

27 Notwithstanding, lest we should offend them, go thou to the sea, and cast an hook, and take up the fish that first cometh up; and when thou hast opened his mouth, thou shalt find a piece of money: that take, and give unto them for me and thee.

["They that received tribute,"]{.class_4258} of course, is a reference to the despised "publicans."

["Doth not your master pay tribute?"]{.class_4258} The answer is ["Yes"]{.class_4258} (see Rom. 13:1--4). Yet when Israel's religious leaders try to kill Christ, one of the first charges is ["We found this fellow\...forbidding to give tribute to Caesar"]{.class_4258} (Luke 23:1--2)---even after He had talked with them about it and settled the question publicly (Luke 20:21--26). This reveals the tremendous amount of hypocrisy involved in the betrayal of the Lord Jesus.

["Jesus prevented him,"]{.class_4258} as in the sense of "stopping him" verbally; that is, Jesus read his thoughts ahead of time and before he got clear into the room, Jesus interrupts his thoughts with: ["What []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_315}thinkest thou, Simon?"]{.class_4258}

Simon Peter's answer has always been a great personal blessing to the author. ["Of strangers."]{.class_4258} There it is. That is Peter. No messing around. No attempting to synthesize and amalgamate and "integrate" the complex "inter-relatedness" of the philosophical problem.

"Children or strangers, Pete?"

["Of strangers!"]{.class_4258} That's rich. If you don't believe it, look at the religious leaders when they are asked a simple question!

Luke 7:42--43---["which of them will love him most?" "I suppose that\...."]{.class_4258}

Matthew 21:25---["From heaven, or of men?" "If we shall say\...."]{.class_4258}

John 18:34--35---"Did you get it first hand or second hand?" ["Am I a Jew?"]{.class_4258}

My, what a refreshing thing it is to hear Jesus ask a man a loaded question and hear the man (without hesitation or forethought) just lay it right smack on the line. "Strangers or children, Pete?" ["Of strangers."]{.class_4258}

"That's kind of dogmatic, isn't it, Peter?" ["Of strangers."]{.class_4258}

"Doesn't that depend upon the circumstances and situation?" ["Of strangers."]{.class_4258}

"What do you mean by 'children'?" ["Of strangers."]{.class_4258}

"You are kind of prejudiced, aren't you, Peter?" ["Of strangers."]{.class_4258}

"I believe education might broaden your outlook." ["Of strangers."]{.class_4258}

"But what about the heathen who do not know what you know?" ["Of strangers."]{.class_4258}

"Aren't you being rather hasty in that decision?" ["Of strangers."]{.class_4258} \[And I rather imagine that if someone had argued the point with him he would have walked away. If he had stayed he would have answered it the same way seven times, and then pulled out a fish knife and taken some modernist's hide off, hair and all (Matt. 18:21, 26:51).\]

That passage is really a blessing. I cannot leave it alone.

Somehow in my mind's eye, I see an old Pharisee following Peter into the house and just about the time Jesus pops the question, the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_316}old Pharisee whispers hoarsely in Peter's ear, "Hold it! If you say the ['children,' ]{.class_4258}He'll quote some Old Testament to prove you are not God's child! If you say ['strangers,']{.class_4258} He'll quote a verse to prove you've got to pay! Don't answer Him!" And I see Peter looking at the Pharisee the way he would look at a sea-robin who got caught on snapper bait, and without a word, he turns back to Jesus and says, ["Of strangers."]{.class_4258}

Behold, an Israelite in whom there is no guile! Jesus asked him what he thought and he told Him (Heb. 4:12).

I like that. I've always liked that. If such a man will ever submit to the authority of the Scripture, he will pass from death to life in a moment under the regenerating power of the Holy Spirit; no matter what he is---Buddhist, Catholic, Jew, Protestant, Mohammedan, or Taoist.

["Then are the children free."]{.class_4258} The implication is: "I am the King and you disciples are my children" (see Matt. 9:15, 18:3, 5--6). The resolution follows: lawfully and legally, therefore, you do not have to pay tax to the Holy Roman Empire under Caesar! The ["strangers,"]{.class_4258} then, is a definition of the unconverted who do not belong to the King's household (see Matt. 7:23; Eph. 2:12).

["Not withstanding, lest we should offend them"]{.class_4258} points out the great New Testament truth of the Pauline epistles. It is that the Christian has liberty to do anything (1 Cor. 6:12, 10:23) since he is not under the law or its curse (Gal. 3:13; Rom. 6:14--15), but for the sake of the first two commandments (the law of love, Rom. 13:8--10), he purposely refrains from conduct that would offend the unsaved (see 1 Cor. 10:32--33). Jesus Christ's word to Simon Peter is the essence of post-pentecostal Christianity.

["Cast an hook"]{.class_4258} shows the fishermen using all three methods exactly as they do in salt water today. We have the throw net of the mullet fishermen, the seine net of the commercial mackerel fishermen, and the "hook" with which to catch the grouper, red snapper, blue fish, and tuna.

It is amusing and fascinating to notice our Lord's absolute authority over the natural creation, revealed in this passage; for with more than l0,000,000 fish in the Sea of Galilee, how does He know which one has swallowed a piece of money accidentally? The []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1836.xhtml_page_317}odds are too great for the infidel; this man Jesus Christ is what He professes to be: God the Father, in bodily form (John 14:3--8).

["For me and THEE"]{.class_4258} is a tender thought. It illustrates Philippians 4:19 remarkably and also Matthew 6:32. You see, He pays it for you! This is illustrated again vividly in John 17, where practically all that Jesus Christ has "from the Father" is given to the believer to share.

[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1985.xhtml}

# Chapter 18 {.heading_84-0}

18:1 At the same time came the disciples unto Jesus, saying, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?

2 And Jesus called a little child unto him, and set him in the midst of them,

3 And said, Verily I say unto you, Except ye be converted, and become as little children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.

The [RSV ]{.class_4257}(always excellent for comparisons of falsehood) wrongly writes "turn" for "convert." This is a theological perversion arrived at by thinking that [repent ]{.class_4257}("to turn") is identical to [convert ]{.class_4257}("to change"). Of course, neither the English, Spanish, Hebrew, Greek, Latin, French, German, Russian, Japanese, or Filipino have this default in their language. You have to buy an [RSV ]{.class_4257}to find it. A "convertible," for example, can "convert" to a roofless car without [turning anywhere. "To ]{.class_4257}turn‑into something" by "changing" is not the same as to [turn around. ]{.class_4257}Repentance implies "turning," and this can be done [without turning into anything ]{.class_4257}(see Judas in Matt 27:3--4). [No "converting" to it.]{.class_4257}

[​]{.class_4259}["Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?" ]{.class_4258}The Kingdom, again, is the reference to the literal, Jewish, Davidic, Messianic, visible kingdom covenanted to Israel (see remarks on Matt. 10:7, 4:23, 3:2). Only the "myster­ies" of the kingdom of heaven are applicable to the present age, which everywhere is denominated the "day of grace," "the dispensation of grace," or the "church age," but [never ]{.class_4257}["the kingdom of heaven." ]{.class_4258}The only practical application that can be made to THIS age is to teach that [conversion to Christ is necessary ]{.class_4257}(see vs. 3 and 6) and that children are automatically saved without any sacraments, for notice that to receive the ["little child" ]{.class_4258}(vs. 4--5) or ["little ones" ]{.class_4258}(vs. 6) is the equivalent of receiving Christ! (How ridiculous it is to take these verses and handle them like Kuyper, Dabney, and Hodge, throwing them in with Matthew 19:14 and Ezekiel 36:25--28 to prove "covenant theology," or to handle them like some deluded Papist and claim that the verses only refer to those children [sprinkled ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1985.xhtml_page_319}by a Roman!)

Notice that the CONVERSION precedes the "becom­ing humble" (vs. 3). Again, observe that the term ["conversion" ]{.class_4257}in the Bible NEVER refers to conversion to an organization, sacrament, teaching, theology, religion, belief, dogma, tradition, church, sect, cult, or ritual. It [always ]{.class_4257}(without any exception in this age) refers to a personal conversion to the Lord Jesus Christ Himself (John 5:40, 1:12--13). This, of course, brings a howl of objection from amillennialists, who are busy converting the world [to a world‑wide, united-Catholic-religio-politico system, ]{.class_4257}where the "convert" dumps his conscience into a priest's lap and goes on professing "christianity," while thumbing his nose at the Bible. Reformed theology is also in line with this universal, pagan concept, except in the latter case the convert is [sprinkled ]{.class_4257}into an automatic salvation system on the merits of his father's religion (1 Cor. 7:14), and so takes [conversion to Jesus Christ for granted ]{.class_4257}whether it occurs or not.

18:4 Whosoever therefore shall humble himself as this little child, the same is greatest in the kingdom of heaven.

5 And whoso shall receive one such little child in my name receiveth me.

6 But whoso shall offend one of these little ones which believe in me, it were better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and that he were drowned in the depth of the sea.

Verse 4 is an obvious reference to the [attitude ]{.class_4257}and heart condition of the real "convert" in any age. This is stated clearly in the Psalms for an Old Testament saint (Psa. 131), in the Gospels for a Jewish saint in the transition period (Luke 10:21), and in the Epistles for a Christian (Rom 12:16, 1 Pet. 2:1­--3). This explains why the official church theologians and leaders of nearly any period of church history are just blind guides stumbling around in the dark.

Child‑like humility is the first requisite for learning the Bible, not knowledge of the "original languages."

Here the reader can kiss Alford, Robertson, Trench, Wuest, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1985.xhtml_page_320}Griesbach, Scholtz, Lachmann, Tregelles, Tischendorf, and Moffatt [good‑bye. ]{.class_4257}Not a man in the group [ever submitted himself ]{.class_4257}to the authority of the Bible that God used from 1611--1901 to convert over 40,000,000 people. Every man in the group set himself up as a self‑appointed committee of one to help "us poor dumb folks" under­stand our "archaic" Authorized Version.

Verse 5 is as Luke 9:48 and Mark 10:14; the believer cannot fail to see the peculiar appropriateness the passages have to the famous [as-much-as-ye-have-done-it-to-one-of-these ]{.class_4257}passage in Matthew 25:30--46. This passage is a "tribulation" passage (as is the entire Matt. 25), and again, it serves as a bolster to remind us that the[ "kingdom of heaven" ]{.class_4258}is not (doctrinally speaking) the Church Age, but the age of the Second Coming of the King of the Jews, to reign over the house of Jacob (Luke 1:30--33).

Our Lord's contempt for "brainy wisdom" is clearly shown in the passage. As someone has so aptly comment­ed: "At the time He made these remarks, He was talking to a crowd that had enough brains to fill a good‑sized [garbage can." ]{.class_4257}We might also learn not to despise the ["small things"]{.class_4258} from the lesson (Zech. 4:10). It was a child who showed the Prussian General Blucher a short cut to Waterloo with reinforcements, while old Napoleon was mopping up the British Army. It was a child, also, who told Governor Curtin (of Pennsylvania) of Lee's intended maneuver to go north of Hagerstown, Maryland, and approach Gettysburg. The boy overheard two Confederate generals talking nearly a week before the Southern armies marched north, parallel to the Emmittsburg Road. It is a child that secures Naaman's healing (2 Kings 5), and it is a child that eludes the wrath of Herod, stumps the intellectuals of the first century, and sets the dating of all dates in history from His birth (Luke 2:26,27).

["A millstone were hanged about his neck" ]{.class_4258}implies that a man would do better to drown himself than to ["offend"]{.class_4258} a believer in Christ. \[Note that in [this ]{.class_4257}age, believers are all[ "little children"]{.class_4258} until they have matured (1 Pet. 2:1--3; 1 Cor. 3:1--4; Heb. 5:12--14).\] Hence, Paul's warnings not to ["offend"]{.class_4258} the brethren (Rom. 14:21--23; 1 Cor. 8:11--13). Doctrinally, of course, the warning fits the Kingdom of Heaven passages in the Tribulation (Matt. 25) and in the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1985.xhtml_page_321}Millennium (Matt. 5--7).

18:7 Woe unto the world because of offences! for it must needs be that offences come; but woe to that man by whom the offence cometh!

8 Wherefore if thy hand or thy foot offend thee, cut them off, and cast them from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life halt or maimed, rather than having two hands or two feet to be cast into everlasting fire.

9 And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire.

The word ["offences"]{.class_4258} here is the kind of offence that would cause a person to [stumble ]{.class_4257}(Gk. [skandala) ]{.class_4257}and fall down (see 1 Cor. 10:12--13). Verse 7, incidentally, perfectly sums up the divine recognition of man's free will in conjunction with God's Sovereign Will. Both occur together in the verse---perfectly stated. That is, someone has to "mess up" (for it is predestinated), but don't let it be you (that [is not ]{.class_4257}predestinated!). Calvin could duck out of this, but of course, he would have to reject the plain, literal, English sense, as written, to do it.

Verse 7 can be a direct slam at Judas Iscariot, for the same type of thing appears at the Last Supper (see 26:24). The reader will notice the peculiar element of works connected with "not going to hell" in the passage before us. It is almost as though the Lord was saying that if you [did cut off a hand ]{.class_4257}or [foot ]{.class_4257}or pluck [out an eye ]{.class_4257}that it would get you to Heaven instead of Hell. Again, we are struck with the absolute, imperative need for understanding [both ]{.class_4257}advents of the Saviour, for without this understanding there is no way ["the word of truth"]{.class_4258} can be "rightly divided" (see 2 Tim. 2:15). Not only is this knowledge imperative to understanding "apparent contradictions," it becomes obvious (with increased study) that it is impera­tive in understanding even such simple subjects as "which day to observe" and "water baptism." [Every division in the Body of Christ, doctrinally, is caused by refusal to study and rightly divide.]{.class_4257}

The age‑old maxim holds that when ["the word of truth"]{.class_4258} is rightly divided, the Body of Christ is united. When ["the word of truth"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1985.xhtml_page_322} is wrongly divided, or not divided at all (like Rome putting up an Old Testament priesthood, Old Testament intermediate state after death, Old Testament ritual, Old Testament "faith plus works," and Old Testament titles), then the Body of Christ is [divided. ]{.class_4257}Christians will NOT study the Bible. They will read religious literature about it, romantic novels which use it, denominational tracts that exploit it, "study books" that pervert it, church tracts which rearrange it, and text books which make money off it---[but they will not study it]{.class_4257}!

Every heresy abroad today within professing Christendom is an attempt to insert [Jewish passages to Israel ]{.class_4257}into the body of Church Age truth. This insertion is made because the authors and followers of such heresy [do not know of any other age in which to insert the passages. To ]{.class_4257}the last man, they fail to recognize the three great periods of the New Testament: the Day of Grace (Pentecost to the Rapture), the Tribulation (Rapture to Advent), and the Millennium (Advent to Judgment). Matthew 18 is to pre‑crucifixion, Old‑Testament Jews, before Pentecost. And the element of "works" is present in the text (18:7--10).

Origen (the first of the really "critical" Bible exposi­tors) took Matthew 18:7--10 so literally (in relation to getting out of Hell [by works]{.class_4257})[ ]{.class_4257}that he mutilated himself in the member that he figured "offended" him. Without going into the gory details, let it be noted that Origen, as all amillennialists, [never accepted the common versions of the Scripture, abroad in his day among the common people, as the correct Bible. ]{.class_4257}His Hexapla (A.D. 200--230) was invented to put his own version in the church as the true, authoritative book. Constantine got fifty copies of it from Eusebius (A.D. 313--350).

Getting back to the text, the statement is not: "If you cut off this or that, you'll go to heaven." The statement is: ["Whatever is keeping you from being converted to me and believing on me, you'd do better without." ]{.class_4257}If there is any doubt about this, look at the context: verse 3, ["converted"]{.class_4258}; verse 6, ["believe in me"]{.class_4258}; verse 11, "get SAVED." So if the verse is applied to the Church Age in the day of grace, the teaching is typical in regard to salvation, as in Matthew 16:28. However, the student must not be carried away with Gentile []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1985.xhtml_page_323}"spiritualizing" of passages to make them fit twentieth‑century theology. He must never forget that true interpreting can only be found by comparing [Scripture with Scripture, ]{.class_4257}and "Scripture with Scripture" runs Matthew 18:9 right smack into the Sermon on the Mount (Matt. 5:22, 29).

The verses are practically identical, including the much-mocked and despised expression ["hell fire." "Hell fire,"]{.class_4258} of course, is our Lord's expression, used from His own vocabulary, and it would be puerile indeed to take anyone seriously who professes "Christianity" but resents ["hell fire"]{.class_4258} preaching. This would be the equivalent of saying, "I am a follower of Christ, but frankly I do not believe that He knows what He is talking about." This is what many do, however, in keeping with their hypocritical natures. They disguise their disgust and hatred for the Lord by saying, "He was speaking in [parables." ]{.class_4257}See Ezekiel 20:46--49 for God's comment on this time‑worn and out‑moded response to teaching on literal fire in Hell.

[Scripture with Scripture, ]{.class_4257}the passage is addressed to Old Testament Jews waiting for (what we now know to be!) the Second Advent (Matt. 10:23). The passage is to be applied doctrinally during Daniel's Seventieth Week (see com­ments in Matt 24--25). All Scriptures bear this out, and the element of faith‑PLUS‑works is so clear in the Hebrew Christian Epistles, it is amazing how the application could be perverted to fit the Church Age. Notice "HEBREWS" is addressed to "HEBREWS," where they are called "my people." Hebrews 10:26--30 has this combination of "holding out by works" (Heb. 6:1--8, 3:1--6, 10:5--31), and James[ "to the twelve tribes"]{.class_4258} (James 1:1) devotes [an entire chapter to it---]{.class_4257}James 2.

[Thus God used the Catholics to dog Martin Luther's footsteps until he said moodily one day, "Some day I'm going to light my stove with James," never guessing that God was using heretics (as He always has) to drive a true believer to correct Bible interpretation through study. ]{.class_4257}Martin never did get the Second Coming of Christ (premillennial) fixed right, and so he never learned how to handle James 2.

Not knowing how to handle James 2, Hebrews 6, and Hebrews 10 puts a man in rough shape when he comes to the same passages in Revelation 12:17, 14:12, and 22:14; [so every lazy, insincere, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw1985.xhtml_page_324}religious leader in the world applies these verses to the Church Age and loads the poor unregenerate sinner down with a backbreaking burden of good works in order to lessen his damnation in hell. ]{.class_4257}And of course, this is all done under the pretence that he is [earning his salvation in heaven! ]{.class_4257}Thus it is revealed that without a proper understanding of the premillennial coming of Christ (easily accessible to any humble sinner who receives the Lord Jesus), even the basic doctrines of salvation and perseverance are misread, misunderstood, mistaught, and misapplied. Peter officially stated at the council at Jerusalem ([after the resurrection]{.class_4257})[ ]{.class_4257}that any system that included works for salvation in this age [was not from the Holy Spirit ]{.class_4257}(Acts 15:8--11, 19--29. Don't strip your gears; go on and look it up!).

The passage, then, doctrinally applied, fits the future date. Historically, it is in Old Testament times, and spiritually, it teaches that it is better to be born lame, maimed, or blind than to go to Hell. This proves, conclusively, that Hell is most certainly NOT "the grave," for [all ]{.class_4257}go to the "grave" anyway, saved or lost, crippled or whole.

The word[ "offend,"]{.class_4258} in the [RSV]{.class_4257}, is curiously translated "temptations to sin." This proves again (as it has been proved on numerous occasions) that access to the "oldest manuscripts" does not in the least insure a translation's genuineness.
