Persecution for acting like a fool, meddling in political intrigues, standing up for Castro, opposing Castro, fighting for the "church," enforcing "church doctrines," preaching visions and hallucinations, trying to get everyone into one church, trying to get everyone under Diem's government in Viet Nam, or passing on information about government secrets is not included in the passage of Mark 10 or Matthew 19. It is persecution for Christ's sake or ["the gospel's"]{.class_4258} and no amount of adding, "Well, Christ estab­lished His [church, ]{.class_4257}and it represents Him so\..." can change the fact that you are lying and don't know what you are talking about.

It is one thing to be thrown in jail, or be beheaded, for preaching Bible truth (see Acts 16 and Mark 6), and it is quite another thing to be martyred for trying to enforce the superstitions and intolerance of a deluded Italian prince! Don't flatter yourself (Prov. 25:14).

"But many that are first shall be last; and the last shall be first."

The last verse is explained in a parable in Chapter 20, which is called a parable of ["the kingdom of heaven."]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2117.xhtml_page_366} (This is the ninth parable in the series of twelve. The eighth one is in 18:23, and the first seven are in Matt. 13.)

[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml}

# Chapter 20 {.heading_84-0}

20:1 For the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which went out early in the morning to hire labourers into his vineyard.

2 And when he had agreed with the labourers for a penny a day, he sent them into his vineyard.

3 And he went out about the third hour, and saw others standing idle in the marketplace,

4 And said unto them; Go ye also into the vineyard, and whatsoever is right I will give you. And they went their way.

5 Again he went out about the sixth and ninth hour, and did likewise.

6 And about the eleventh hour he went out, and found others standing idle, and saith unto them, Why stand ye here all the day idle?

7 They say unto him, Because no man hath hired us. He saith unto them, Go ye also into the vineyard; and whatsoever is right, that shall ye receive.

The parable (as the one in Chapter 18) can be transferred into the Church Age for spiritual application, remembering of course that in this age, the Kingdom of Heaven is in a mystery form (see Matt. 13). This is purposely hidden from the amillennialist and postmillennialist. God hides it for a very simple reason. The reason is that the two systems, by nature of their own profession, are designed to [deny ]{.class_4257}two central truths revealed in the word of God. The first truth, denied by the amillennialist, is that Jesus Christ will return to this earth to reign, but this is the theme of 1,200 verses in the Old Testament! The second truth (denied by the postmillennialist) is that man, being depraved, [always ]{.class_4257}misuses his opportunities and grows WORSE with the passing of time. Postmillennialism is the "religious Darwin" of Bible-rejecting church members.

Since God knows the "heart" and sees all, He has no trouble at all in fooling these two classes of "Christians" by putting the kingdom mysteries into "parables" (Matt. 13:11--15); once they try to lay hold of the truth in these parables, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_368}[their heart condition betrays them, ]{.class_4257}and their hands become dark-stained and deep-dyed with the two anti-Christian systems of private interpretation: [amillennialism]{.class_4257} and [postmillennialism.]{.class_4257}

Matthew 20:1--16 can apply to the mystery period of the kingdom as it is given as a parable of the kingdom of heaven along with the seven in Matthew 13. It fits even BETTER than some, for we are told in Matthew 19:27 that the "early" laborers in the vineyard are the apostles! This dates the "day" in which the labourers work as the Church Age: A.D. 33--2000+?

["Like unto a man."]{.class_4258} When this occurs in the parables of the kingdom of heaven, the ["man"]{.class_4258} is nearly always God---see Matthew 22:1--2, 13, 44. Specifically, it is God the Father, which reminds the reader again that the Father was ["made flesh, and dwelt among us"]{.class_4258} (Zech. 12:10; John 1:1--15). The reader will notice that Zechariah 12:10 identifies the crucified one as "JEHOVAH GOD" of the Old Testament! (To avoid this obvious reference to the Deity of Jesus Christ, the [RSV ]{.class_4257}has changed the Hebrew "me" to "him," which of course is not even a Hebrew manuscript. It is the perversion inserted by Theodotian, who also changes the passage in Matthew 1 so as to deny the Virgin Birth. The [RSV ]{.class_4257}incorporates Theodotian's reading in Matt. 1 in its footnote.)

["That is an householder."]{.class_4258} This is defined in Hebrews 3:1--6.

["Early in the morning."]{.class_4258} There is no way to avoid the context. The ["early in the morning,"]{.class_4258} where it occurs, is the answer to Peter's question of 19:27, in relation to the [ministry of the apostles]{.class_4257}. Furthermore, the day that begins ["early in the morning"]{.class_4258} ends with twelve hours in the "evening" (see 20:7, 9). This would put the "eleventh hour workers" [at the end of the period which begins with the apostles ]{.class_4257}(see Eph. 2--3). We now have something with which to work. Here is a twelve-hour day beginning A.D. 33--70 and running approximately 2,000 years, with each hour in it containing 166.666666 years! (See the Scriptural system of sevens under Matt. 17:1.)

It is appropriate that the "day of man," under grace, should be an infinite number of six's, for man without God is not complete. Six is the number of man in Scripture (see [Bible Babel]{.class_4257}, 1960, Pensacola []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_369}Bible Press).

["Into his vineyard"]{.class_4258} takes us to Matthew 21:33 where the vineyard is Judah and Israel in the land, exactly as it is defined by the Holy Spirit in Isaiah 5:1--7. (Thank God we have a self-interpreting Bible that interprets itself without anyone's ["freedom of interpretation"]{.class_4257} or anyone's "authoritative church" to mess it up!)

["For a penny a day\...."]{.class_4258} The [RSV ]{.class_4257}translates "denarius," which is entirely unnecessary. Anyone understands the phrase "to make a buck" or "to turn a penny" or "he's after the dollar." In every case, it means money [in the plural]{.class_4257}. The labourer is not left with [one ]{.class_4257}copper penny after twelve hours labour, and to make it read ["denarius"]{.class_4257} (which no common person understands) obliterates the meaning. This is typical of the "more accurate" or "clearer" translations. Like a hog standing in a stream of clear water, they muddy it for miles downstream. (And I say this with apologies to every decent hog in this world!)

["About the third hour."]{.class_4258} In the time-table given to us by the Holy Spirit (apart from Russell, Rutherford, Besant, Cayce, Dixon, Eddy, and other prognosticators), this will bring us to the year A.D. 498--499, which, of course, ushers in Pope Gregory and the Dark Ages. On the time-table of the watches in the night (see Matt. 24--25), this would bring an end to the [first watch ]{.class_4257}at 9 P.M. called "the evening"; what follows is the [second watch]{.class_4257}---MIDNIGHT. And surely enough, from A.D. 500--1500, it is spiritual midnight and nothing else; for this is the period of Roman supremacy, Mariolotry, scholastics, Waldensian persecutions, Bible burnings, and murder in the name of "the church."

["Whatsoever is right I will give you."]{.class_4258} Thus, the conditions of hiring are given in the contract. The one who pays will ["do right"]{.class_4258} (Gen. 18:25). The amount is not given, but the workers take the job in good faith.

["Sixth and ninth hour."]{.class_4258} This brings us up to the next two divisions which coincide exactly with the four watches of the night---3, 6, 9, 12. There are four divisions of the day, each being a three-hour period, and thus, the night watches are divided into 6--9, 9--12, 12--3, 3--6, and so-named in Mark 13:35. \[Ah! the unsearchable riches of the [Authorized Version]{.class_4257}, 1611, as it brings out the true []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_370}meaning of the obscure Roman and Liberal translations!\]

The ["sixth and ninth hour"]{.class_4258} in the day watch would bring us first to the year 999.9999 (or the exact middle of the night---12 o'clock midnight in the night watch), [within one month of the year A.D. 1000. ]{.class_4257}Here, Rome is at its peak and height, in full control of Europe under the so-called "Holy" Roman Empire. (A better designation would be the "Un-Holy Roman Dictatorship.")

The ninth hour brings us to 1499.9999 or one month from the year 1500. And of course, right here, we run smack into Huss and Martin Luther, signifying the end of the midnight watch, the [cockcrowing ]{.class_4257}of Mark 13:35, and the beginning of the [morning watch ]{.class_4257}(here, in our parable, the daylight time would bring the reader to three in the afternoon, instead of three in the morning). Martin Luther, who later affectionately addressed his Holiness Pope Leo as "your Hellishness," and would write to him cordially as "most Hellish father," was born in 1485. (Leo, like John, was about forty pounds overweight and smiled at everybody to make a good impression!) The reader will not fail to note that the Holy Spirit gives only [the significant periods ]{.class_4257}of the day's work: 499.999, 999.999, and 1499.999. The last date marks the discovery of America by Christopher Columbus (1492); thus, the Bible chronology is always far superior to the invented system of Seventh-day Adventists and Jehovah's Witnesses who try to force the Scripture to meet the demands of secular history. They have it backwards---for secular history follows the dictates of Scripture.

["And about the eleventh hour."]{.class_4258} The statement is so dramatic that it is found over and over again in secular literature to indicate "the lull before the storm" or the "last stand." Where it occurs in Matthew 20, it begins the [last hour of the day's work before the coming of "the man"]{.class_4257} (see vs. 1)[ to pay off the labourers. ]{.class_4257}It would be the equivalent of 5--6 A.M. in the morning watch of Mark 13:35, but here it is the 5­--6 shift in the evening, before night descends: [thus reminding us again that the parable deals with the Church Age preceding the Tribulation! ]{.class_4257}This last hour then is 5--6 in the evening, and it brings us up into the nineteenth century with a jarring crash that should awaken every sleeping saint on the continent, for the eleventh hour (by the 166.666 timetable) began in []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_371}1837! This means that you are now in the last quarter of the last hour in a twelve-hour period, preceding the Second Advent of Christ! No wonder every one is turning back to Rome for authority and pitching out of the word of God, like gas masks were thrown off the fantails of the cruisers in 1945. At 1837, the last workers are hired. History confirms this to the hilt. In 1885 the [RV ]{.class_4257}appears, in 1901 the [ASV]{.class_4257}, in 1952 the [RSV]{.class_4257}; and as the century comes to an end, Catholics, Christians, Liberals, Protestants, infidels, Fundamentalists, neo-orthodox, Jews, and Modernists alike, all reject the 1611 translation, given before the eleventh hour began, and head back to the medieval superstitions of the Dark Age philosophy of Rome---that ancient enemy of the word of God. Time is running out, friend. You can take Russia, Rome, or Righteousness. You can choose Communism, Catholicism, or Consecration. ["Choose you this day whom ye will serve."]{.class_4258}

20:8 So when even was come, the lord of the vineyard saith unto his steward, Call the labourers, and give them their hire, beginning from the last unto the first.

9 And when they came that were hired about the eleventh hour, they received every man a penny.

10 But when the first came, they supposed that they should have received more; and they likewise received every man a penny.

11 And when they had received it, they murmured against the goodman of the house,

One cannot fail to recognize the similarity between what takes place here and what takes place in Luke chapter 15, when the older brother gets a case of the "cruds" because the younger brother has been welcomed home. The workers who began the morning shift and worked a full twelve-hour day (6--6) want to know why the ones who began at 5 P.M. get the same wage. We have here a perfect capital-labour management problem and unfortunately for John L. and Walter Reuther, the solution here in Scripture is [capitalistic]{.class_4257}. Notice that the [hirer ]{.class_4257}determines the "wage increase" [without collective bargaining]{.class_4257}. The theory is that the worker agreed for [whatever the hirer told him]{.class_4257}; therefore, he has no right to beef about it. As a []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_372}famous colored preacher said, "De moral of dis heah story is dat hit don't make no diffunce what time you went to woak; de question is---[is you on de job?'']{.class_4257}

20:12 Saying, These last have wrought but one hour, and thou hast made them equal unto us, which have borne the burden and heat of the day.

13 But he answered one of them, and said, Friend, I do thee no wrong: didst not thou agree with me for a penny?

14 Take that thine is, and go thy way: I will give unto this last, even as unto thee.

15 Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own? Is thine eye evil, because I am good?

16 So the last shall be first, and the first last: for many be called, but few chosen.

The "last workers" have worked by the time table of A.D. 1837--2004 (if our calendar is right). Verses 17 and 21 in the context indicate that the setting deals with the Second Coming of Christ and His return to Jerusalem with the Kingdom (notice especially vs. 21). Again, the amillennialist and postmillennialist cannot arrive with any light or intelligent exposition. Gazing at the Scriptures through smoked-glasses. they are like the two men who met in the dark and one said, "Is that you?'' To which the other replied, "No. Is it you?" To which the other replied, "I don't know. Let's see." So they struck a match and looked, and sure enough, [it wasn't either of them!]{.class_4257} All these two "weirdos" can see is Christ's First Coming and the "incoming" of a "spiritual kingdom." ["And if the blind lead the blind, both shall fall into the ditch."]{.class_4258}

["The burden and heat of the day"]{.class_4258} cannot be applied en toto in the Church Age, for this would make the earlier worker work the full period from A.D. 33 to A.D. 2004. This cannot be. Therefore, we must read that the early workers represent the apostles (as 19:27--30) and the last workers represent the end-time workers of the nineteenth and twentieth century. This will make excellent "sense," for it is apparent to any historian that the first and second century workers are up against lions, racks, screws, hooks, fire, sword and slavery, while the nineteenth and twentieth-century labourers (in []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_373}America at least) are only up against denominational politicians, slander, extortion, embezzlement, false doctrine, and an occasional law suit or a "hurled stone."

The "early workers," then, have a point. But the employer also has a point, which the early workers fail to see. ["Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own?"]{.class_4258} (See Acts 5:4.) Private ownership is fully stated in the New Testament [before and after the resurrection]{.class_4257}. Therefore, the Southern Baptist writer, Dr. T. B. Matson (Southwestern Seminary, 1958), is greatly and grossly in error when he writes his Communist article for the [Sunday School Adults]{.class_4257} (Third Quarter, Vol. 58, No. 3). It goes as follows, ["The owner should recognize that his property belongs, in a sense, to the entire community\...it is not his to do with as he pleases."]{.class_4257} You see, Dr. Matson and Jesus Christ would be at "odds" in a show-down over "ownership!" Dr. Matson goes on with some more Marxian speculation by contradicting our Lord with, ["It is interesting that there is little, if any, defense of the right of private property in the Bible. Such rights are assumed, rather than defended."]{.class_4257} (Lesson for Sept. 14.) We might add that it is interesting to note that Dr. Matson seems to be short on brains and eye-sight when it comes to an [AV]{.class_4257}. Without being rude or rough about it, may we state sweetly and kindly that when a man endeavors to make a liar out of the Holy Spirit (see Matt. 20:15; Deut. 19:14; Prov. 22:28, 23:10) and the Old Testament Law and the wisest man that ever lived, then it is high-time for someone to kick the seat of his britches.

We can excuse Brother Matson for his ignorance of the Tribulation passages in James 5 and Amos 8 (upon which he has commented in the Quarterly) on the grounds that he has no understanding, whatsoever, of either Testament (and this, of course, is the only charitable way to look at it). The Bible does not condone mismanagement of servants or workers, but it most certainly does speak of "MASTERS" and "OWNERS." The references are so numerous they could not be listed on one line (Eph. 6:5--9; 1 Tim. 6:1--5; 2 Cor. 12:14, etc.).

We do not need little hireling politicians at Southwestern Seminary trying to correct Jesus (Matt. 20:15), Moses (Deut. 19:14), and Solomon (Prov. 23:10) in disputes over private ownership. After []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_374}all, Dr. Matson (as thousands of block-headed book-worms) has only been hypnotized by Engels and Marx, and who were [they]{.class_4257}? Hack writers, trying to make a living.

"Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own?"

Yes, it is. But as the world becomes compact through communication and transportation, this Bible doctrine will collapse for two reasons:

1\. The necessity of "living together" will force the Christian to abandon the Bible doctrines on separation, consecration, individualism, personal accountability, and responsibility.

2\. Rome will seize the papal "bull by the horns" and command all to "unite against Communism," and this "uniting" will entail a denial of the premillennial coming of Jesus Christ, which has always been considered a heresy in the Roman Church.

Thus, "circumstances" will force America to adopt Communistic or Socialistic political programs with Roman religious concepts. Neither are Biblical nor Christian in any sense of the word. The "community" of Christians in Acts 2--4 is based on a [Bible-believing group of literalists rallying around the person of the risen Saviour]{.class_4257}! It was not a group of government sponsored apartment houses with CARE packages sent to them from "all bodies of Faith." In short, the program for the future is One World, One Church, One Faith, One Government, One Baptism, One God, One Family, One Mass, One Mess, completely and totally dedicated to the destruction of the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 Bible and the revival of pagan idolatry (see Zeph. 3:8--10; Isa. 9:11--12; Zech. 12:3; Gen. 11; Matt. 13:20--44). To sum it up: total, Bible-rejecting apostasy is "on the way," done in the name of Jesus Christ for "the glory of God." And you were warned well in advance---Matthew 24:5, 11, 24. But who can convince a self-righteous church member?

"So the last shall be first, and the first last: for many be called, but few chosen."

The teaching is clear. The ["last"]{.class_4258} are the workers from A.D. 1837--2004, and the ["first"]{.class_4258} are those from the birth of Christ to A.D. 166. Though it appears that the early workers have it "tougher," it actually balances out, for the opposition in the eleventh hour is more subtle, more disguised, more internal, and more [constant]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_375} than the pressure of the ten Roman persecutions of the first two centuries. So the "workers" are paid off with equal wages. The workers of the eleventh hour can also claim the promise of Matthew 19:29, given to the apostles (the "early" workers of the "first hour"). ["Last\...first; first\...last."]{.class_4258} This should be an encouragement to the "evening workers" of the twentieth century as they labour in a field in semi-darkness, with no food in their stomachs since 12 noon and the grape-bunches becoming dimmer by the minute in the fading light and the chill wind of October in Galilee coming on them. "Work, for the night is coming when no man can work."

20:17 And Jesus going up to Jerusalem took the twelve disciples apart in the way, and said unto them,

18 Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be betrayed unto the chief priests and unto the scribes, and they shall condemn him to death,

19 And shall deliver him to the Gentiles to mock, and to scourge, and to crucify him: and the third day he shall rise again.

As a humourous side-note, it will be observed that the expression for a mauling, or a "beating up," is found here in its original sense, ["Jesus\...took the twelve disciples apart."]{.class_4258} (Of course, anyone understands the meaning is the plain English for drawing something aside from something else. This is eventually applied to mechanical disassembly and hence to the colloquial, ["He took him apart,"]{.class_4257} meaning, "He whaled the tar out of him"; or to put it more graphically, "He turned him every way but loose" \[Southern\].)

What follows, in verses 18--19, has already been said in 16:21. This time (with more amplification), the Lord gives the twelve details of prophecy that are to take place in the future; of course, all of them come to pass literally exactly as prophesied. ["And the third day he shall rise again."]{.class_4258} (For the exact date of the crucifixion \[Wednesday at 9 A.M.\] and the resurrection \[Saturday at 6 P.M.\], see Matthew 12:40 and comments.)

20:20 Then came to him the mother of Zebedee's children with her sons, worshipping him, and desiring a certain thing of him.

21 And he said unto her, What wilt thou? She saith unto him, Grant that these my two sons may sit, the one on thy right hand, and the other on the left, in thy kingdom.

22 But Jesus answered and said, Ye know not what ye ask. Are ye able to drink of the cup that I shall drink of, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with? They say unto him, We are able.

23 And he saith unto them, Ye shall drink indeed of my cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with: but to sit on my right hand, and on my left, is not mine to give, but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared of my Father.

It is the mother of James and John who makes this ambitious request for her children. There is in it an element of egotism, ["Grant that these my two sons\...."]{.class_4258} Thus, "mother love" is a wonderful thing and at the same time a very fleshy and selfish thing. How odd it would have been to hear Mrs. Zebedee saying to Jesus, ["Grant that these HER sons\...."]{.class_4257} Mrs. Zebedee had the great and mighty privilege of being the mother of two of the greatest saints in the New Testament, but her "mother love" is far from saintly. Notice that Jesus purposely [ignores her ]{.class_4257}and presents James and John themselves with the Aptitude Test.

["Drink of the cup\...baptized with the baptism."]{.class_4258} Part of the verse is omitted in the [RSV]{.class_4257}, under the ancient and outmoded theory that it was "borrowed" from the passage in Mark 10:38. This, of course, is guesswork in the most doggerel fashion. The scholarship behind it is the browsings of C. P. B. Weisse (1858), Streeter (1924), and Wellhausen (1900). The reader can rest assured that the text of the [AV ]{.class_4257}is the correct text. ["Drink of the cup\...baptized with the baptism."]{.class_4258} The statement reinforces strongly the theme of the immediate context---verses 18--19. Our Lord is speaking of His coming death on the cross.

["The cup,"]{.class_4258} here, turns out to be ["my cup,"]{.class_4258} not the [Father's cup ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_377}(John 18:11). The personal pronouns here are very important. The FATHER'S CUP, about which Jesus prays in the Garden of Gethsemane (Matt. 26:42), is never spoken of anywhere as representing [PHYSICAL death]{.class_4257}---check the passages in Jeremiah 25:15; Isaiah 51:17; Psalm 75:8, 60:3. "Christ's CUP," spoken of here (Matt. 20:23), can be identified with physical death in verse 18. This will "square" with the other passages, as it turns out that James dies a violent death of martyrdom (Acts 12) and John tastes of the same in Revelation 1.

Both ARE baptized with His baptism in Acts 2, and this is brought home forcibly in Romans 6:4, where we are told that the baptism is associated with [Christ's DEATH]{.class_4257}. The cup, then, and the baptism stand for Christ's actual death on the cross; they speak of suffering and death, but suffering and death [ONLY]{.class_4257}. The actual spiritual transaction which takes place at Calvary, [where the wrath of God is poured out on Jesus Christ who is taking the sinner's place, is a cup of a different size and shape]{.class_4257}. (John R. Rice never did get the two cups to sit upright on the table, but they are there; see comments under Matt. 26:39.)

Notice that the baptism here is obviously NOT water baptism. Christ had been baptized in water three years before this time and so have the men to whom He is talking. Since Jesus never baptized ANYONE [in water]{.class_4257} (John 4:2), and since He baptized ONLY with the Holy Spirit (Matt. 3:11), we now reach the doctrinal truth that there is more than ["one baptism"]{.class_4258} (Eph. 4), and that [WATER BAPTISM ]{.class_4257}is not the true "one baptism." Notice that the baptism of our text (Matt. 20:22--23) is a [DEATH BAPTISM OF SUFFERING]{.class_4257}. No water is remotely connected with it in any way. As a matter of fact, [WATER is the ONE THING THAT IS WITHHELD FROM IT WHEN IT TAKES PLACE! ]{.class_4257}(See John 19:28.) Thus ends forever the Catholic hocus-pocus and the Campbellite mumbo-jumbo about water producing a new birth. The baptism of Matthew 20 is future. ["Ye shall drink\...and be baptized"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 20:22--23), and it is not [water]{.class_4257}; it is a baptism of [suffering]{.class_4257}. (For the other six baptisms, see Matt. 3:11 and notes.) The cup is "tasted" by Jesus in Hebrews 2:9, but this cup is only the taste of death! It is not the Father's cup of poured-out wrath.

["But to sit on my right hand\...."]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_378} The Lord grants them the privilege of His First Coming as a suffering servant to [die]{.class_4257}; however, He does NOT grant to them the privilege of the Second Coming to be on His right hand and on His left. The reason is obvious to a premillennialist, since he accepts the answer to the enigma given in the Book of Revelation (Rev. 11). Again, our amillennial and postmillennial brethren do not know whether they are right-side up, upside-down, backside-forward, or frontside-rearward. If James and John do not get these two places of honor---[who does]{.class_4257}? Answer: [blank]{.class_4257}, if you are a Catholic, Lutheran, Episcopalian, Presbyterian, Methodist, Campbellite, Mormon, Jehovah's Witness, Christian Scientist, Seventh-day Adventist, or Rosicrucian. Answer: MOSES AND ELIJAH, if you are a Bible believer---see Matthew 17:1--3; Revelation 11:3--10; Exodus 33:21; 1 Kings 17:1; Malachi 4:4--6; Zechariah 4:11--14. The places are already taken, and that is why James and John cannot get them. James and John have the privilege of "A" throne as one of the twelve apostles who will sit in judgment on the twelve tribes at the Advent (Matt. 19:28), but the "right hand" is reserved for Moses (the Law) and the left hand for Elijah (the prophets). ["For whom it is prepared of my Father."]{.class_4258}

20:24 And when the ten heard it, they were moved with indignation against the two brethren.

25 But Jesus called them unto him, and said, Ye know that the princes of the Gentiles exercise dominion over them, and they that are great exercise authority upon them.

26 But it shall not be so among you: but whosoever will be great among you, let him be your minister;

27 And whosoever will be chief among you, let him be your servant:

28 Even as the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.

29 And as they departed from Jericho, a great multitude followed him.

["And when the ten."]{.class_4258} Strangely enough, this peculiar division []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_379}of twelve into ten and two is the exact division of the twelve tribes of Israel in the Old Testament (1 Kings 11:31). This is the division of the twelve tribes at the time of the entering of the land (Num. 14:38) with Ephraim and Judah being faithful (Num. 13:6, 8). This age-old division accounts for Ephraim representing the ten northern tribes and Judah representing the two southern tribes; hence, we find the word "Ephraim" used for "Israel," as a whole, in the Book of Hosea (Hosea 10:1, 11, 11:1, 3, 9:1, 3, 8:3, 11), and the word "Judah" used to represent Judah [and Benjamin ]{.class_4257}(1 Kings 11:36).

Here the apostles divide themselves off into the same grouping: ten and two, and there is immediate jealously against the two on the part of the ten, exactly as we find it in Jeroboam's day (1 Kings 11--13).

["Dominion\...authority."]{.class_4258} The statement is a fine summation of the Roman system. This is the standard Gentile way of doing things, and the "great ones" of Gentile government are seen to be exactly what they are: [self-conceited, self-righteous, self-glorifying dictators]{.class_4257}. (Of course, "burdened with the great need of humanity" and having "noble aspirations to liberate down-trodden mankind" and "to provide equal opportunity," etc. Somewhere down the line someone has failed to note that all [Western DICTATORS ]{.class_4257}have Roman backgrounds and up- bringing. Kaiser Bill and Hitler were not raised [Protestant]{.class_4257}. Neither was Nero or Domitian, neither was Marx or Stalin, neither was Lenin or Trotsky, neither was Batiste or Trujillo, neither was Castro or Franco, and neither was DeGaulle, Adenaur, nor KENNEDY.) Christians have lived to see an unarmed, American citizen arrested, without a warrant, and sent to a sanitarium, [without permission, or lawful representation]{.class_4257}, under the official sanction of two Roman Catholic brothers in the Federal Government. For further information, contact General Walker. There will be more to follow. "Dominion and authority" is characteristic of Rome, not "LEADERSHIP OR EXAMPLE."

["Servant\...minister."]{.class_4258} The two words can be used almost interchangeably. The first one is the word for "deacon" (Gk. [diakanon]{.class_4257}). The second is the word for "slave" (Gk. [doulos]{.class_4257}), thereby identifying the "chief" or the "great" one as a [bond-servant, running barefoot through the dust to wait on the Master's table]{.class_4257}. Thus the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_380}Christian concept of a "great one" or a "chief" turns out to be just the opposite from the one taught by Rome.

Right here is where present-day Protestantism runs into the old "woman with the leaven" (Matt. 13:33), for when we behold the scene in the twentieth century, we find that "CAREERISM" is the goal of the present-day "calling," and the methods adopted to attain this ["successful career"]{.class_4257} are the ancient tried-and-true gangster tactics used by Rome for more than fifteen centuries---"PROTECTIVE ASSOCIATIONS," joined under threat of persecution and slander, are numerous. "GANG WARFARE" against rival groups is indulged in against those who refused to "chip-in" to missionary programs. "BLACK-MAIL" is used to threaten preachers with "no money on retirement" if they do not continue to support un-Christian institutions. "EXTORTION" is the order of the day in taking money from congregations (under the pretense of "spreading the kingdom"), and this money winds up in the hands and bank accounts of men who have dedicated their lives to destroying the authority of the Bible. Thus the ministry has become a [PROFESSION ]{.class_4257}instead of a [CALLING]{.class_4257}, and the ministers compete for [success ]{.class_4257}instead of obeying to be [faithful]{.class_4257}, and the successful are considered RIGHT and the failures are considered WRONG. Any method is resorted to, in the name of Christ, [right or wrong]{.class_4257}, in order to be "somebody" and put your name on the map. Shades of Rome!

A minister, then, is a ["servant."]{.class_4258} (See Rom. 16:1; 1 Pet. 5:1.) As an ordained elder, he is admonished to be an EXAMPLE and a ["feeder of the flock"]{.class_4257} and an ["overseer"]{.class_4258} of a flock; but [never ]{.class_4257}a "dominator," exercising ["dominion"]{.class_4258} (Latin: [Domini, Dominus---GOD!]{.class_4257}).

["To give his life as a ransom."]{.class_4258} There has been much discussion about the terminology. The word ["ransom"]{.class_4258} indicates that someone is being held captive at a price. Origen (184--254) taught that the ransom was paid to Satan in order to cancel his just claims on fallen man. Anselm, (1033--1109) in the Dark Ages, concurs with this view. ["Lutron"]{.class_4257} (Gk.) does mean ["a price paid."]{.class_4257} Furthermore, this is brought out in a dozen passages (1 Cor. 6:20, 7:23; 1 Pet. 1:18). The only place where Origen (Catholic) and Anselm (Catholic) bungle is in the recipient to whom the price is []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_381}paid. It is paid to GOD to satisfy God's Holy demands that a law be kept. It does, however, involve Satan in that Satan is God's appointed minister of wrath and vengeance. This is a hard doctrine and a deep one. Suffice it to say that:

1\. God and Satan are used interchangeably in the carrying out of a punishment or chastisement (2 Cor. 12:4--8; Job 1--2; 1 Chron. 21:1; 2 Sam. 24:1; 1 Sam. 16:14). God is the originator; Satan is the immediate author (Lam. 3:38; Job 2:3--6; Jer. 18:11).

2\. Satan is the instrument or tool used in executing the design of God that brings about "evil" (2 Thess. 2:4--10; Jer. 51:20--26).

The Ransom which Christ pays, then, is paid to GOD, and yet Satan "figures" in the picture in that [UNTIL the ransom is paid]{.class_4257}, the Old Testament saints are certainly "held captive." Study carefully, please, the following verses which are relative to the passage (Isa. 45:13, 61:1; Psa. 68:18; 2 Tim. 2:26). You will notice in the last passage that the captives are said to be [captives of the Devil.]{.class_4257}

["And as they departed from Jericho."]{.class_4258} Jesus is now going on His last earthly trip, "in the flesh," to Jerusalem. The day is Saturday or Sunday of the "Passion" week (Acts 1:3), and is undoubtedly Sunday; Saturday (till 6:00 p.m.) being the Sabbath.

20:30 And, behold, two blind men sitting by the way side, when they heard that Jesus passed by, cried out, saying, Have mercy on us, O Lord, thou son of David.

31 And the multitude rebuked them, because they should hold their peace: but they cried the more, saying, Have mercy on us, O Lord, thou son of David.

32 And Jesus stood still, and called them, and said, What will ye that I shall do unto you?

33 They say unto him, Lord, that our eyes may be opened.

34 So Jesus had compassion on them, and touched their eyes: and immediately their eyes received sight, and they followed him.

In the account in Mark, one blind man is given, and his name is Bartimaeus. There is an apparent discrepancy in the accounts, which has given Catholics and Liberals another alibi for saying, "The Bible []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_382}is confusing" or "The Bible contradicts." In Matthew they are [departing from Jericho, ]{.class_4257}and in Mark they are [coming into Jericho ]{.class_4257}(Mark 10:46). This is the kind of thing which develops such writers as those found in the [Interpreter's Bible]{.class_4257} and other slipshod publications. (It is a characteristic of lazy intellectuals that they always investigate tons of literature which [contradict ]{.class_4257}or [imitate ]{.class_4257}or [ridicule ]{.class_4257}the Bible, but never do they give any attention to the [Scriptures themselves]{.class_4257}.) For example, there are [two Jerichos ]{.class_4257}mentioned in the Bible. The first one is destroyed in Joshua chapter 6, but it is rebuilt in 1 Kings 16:34. This was proven by the excavations made at Jericho by Ernest Sellin of the German Oriental Society in 1907--1909. Another Roman "Jericho" is about one mile from the Jewish city, so Matthew is speaking of the Jewish city, and Mark is speaking of the Roman (as Luke---Luke 18:35). This scientific fact (and all of the Bible is scientific fact) stands in direct opposition to the superstitions of "Bible scholarship," which teach that a contradiction is present. There is no contradiction present here or anywhere else.

["Have mercy on us, O Lord, thou son of David."]{.class_4258} The title is Davidic and Messianic. In type, it represents the blinded Israel of the Church Age, turning to the Messiah in the Tribulation. (See Acts 13 for a perfect picture of how the blindness is obtained and the length of time that it lasts.) In application the passage gives us a wonderful picture of salvation by grace, although the doctrinal content is the signs and wonders of physical healing given to Israel through their second Moses---Jesus Christ (Deut. 18; John 1:25).

The man (or men) is [blind]{.class_4257}, and so are all Protestants, Jews, Catholics, Buddhists, Mohammedans, Confucianists, and Hindus [outside ]{.class_4257}of the regenerating grace of the New Birth (2 Cor. 4:1--4; Eph. 4:18--19). A beggar is in [rags]{.class_4257}, and ["all our righteousnesses are as filthy rags"]{.class_4258} (Isa. 64:6). ["Have mercy on us, O Lord"]{.class_4258} (Rom. 10:13). The call is sincere and pitiful and proper. They recognize that Jesus is THE LORD (1 Cor. 12:3). ["Have mercy on ME!"]{.class_4258} is the cry of Bartimaeus, one of the two men. Thus his appeal is [personal ]{.class_4257}and cannot fail to touch the heart of the Son of David.

["And the multitude rebuked them\...hold their peace."]{.class_4258} Thus has it ever been, and thus will it ever be. When a man comes []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_383}under genuine conviction and makes a genuine attempt to find Christ, he immediately runs into the opposition of "the crowd" and is taken to be a fanatic raising an unnecessary "stink." ["But they cried the more."]{.class_4258} Mark adds ["a great deal,"]{.class_4258} and thus the unsightly (and highly criticized) "emotionalism" enters the picture. One can be quite certain that some long-robed father in the procession turned to one of his proselytes and said, "That's a bunch of those holy-rollers over there!" Undoubtedly, his modernistic counterpart (the Sadducees) remarked to one of the Council members, "No education. Isn't it pitiful how ignorant these people are, praying like that? Tsk, tsk!"

["And Jesus stood still, and called them."]{.class_4258} The sentence is suggestive, ["The Master\...calleth for thee"]{.class_4258} (John 11:28; Eph 1:18, 4:1, 4). He calls the man who calls on Him (Rom. 10:13, 8:29--31). Mark adds, ["Be of good comfort, rise; he calleth thee."]{.class_4258} Then, ["And he, casting away his garment, rose, and came to Jesus"]{.class_4258} (Mark 10:50). This line is loaded with spiritual applications. The garment that is cast aside is the filthy rags of righteousness, and it is denominated ["the old man"]{.class_4258} in the Pauline epistles (see Eph. 4:22; Col. 3:9). ["And came to Jesus."]{.class_4258} This statement of four words reduces to the smallest factor the facts concerning salvation. He does NOT come to the sacraments. He does NOT come to the priest. He does NOT come to the church. He does NOT come to the teaching. He does NOT come to the principles. He does NOT come to a good life. He does NOT come to knowledge. He does NOT come to an integration of his "total personality." HE COMES TO JESUS. Have YOU ever come to Him? (John 5:40, 6:37).

In this factual account of an incident in Jewish history, the Holy Spirit has given us a perfect outline of spiritual salvation which comes in the post-resurrection ministry of the apostle Paul. Anything short of ["coming to Jesus Christ"]{.class_4257} is as unavailing as missing the entire target altogether. Since He is God's righteousness (and your righteousnesses are as filthy rags compared to His), then every delay in coming to Him just puts on one more filthy rag (if the work is good) or just strips one more off (if it is bad). Hence, theologically speaking, the sinner (any human being in any country) winds up [filthily clothed]{.class_4257} or [wretchedly naked ]{.class_4257}in the presence of a just, holy, sinless, and righteous God, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_384}[who has beforehand offered the sinner His own perfect righteousness as a free gift]{.class_4257} (Matt. 22:11, 10:1--4, 6:23)! No man who sees this great Scriptural FACT has any trouble in believing in a literal Hell. Figurative "hells" are synthetics invented for people who think they can merit heaven by their own righteousness. Deep-down they know they can't make it, but since they are too self-righteous to believe that they could deserve an unending literal hell, they "air condition" it and "redecorate it" to make it more worthy of "gracious living"!

["Lord, that our eyes may be opened."]{.class_4258} Bartimaeus says, ["Lord, that I might receive my sight"]{.class_4258} (Mark 10:51). The request is Scriptural. The condition of the blind beggar in rags (representing the self-righteous of John 1:9--10) is that he is facing the sun at twelve noon on a clear day in July but just "can't see it." This is one of the favorite expressions of self-deluded intellectuals, "I just can't see it that way." (The other being, "I'm not too bad---[I haven't KILLED anybody.]{.class_4257}")

"You say you can't see it? Of course you can't you have your back to it. Turn around." You turn and say, "I still can't see it."

"Of course you can't, stupid you have your hands over your eyes!!" You take them off and reply, "I still can't see it."

"Of course you can't, you idiot you [CLOSED YOUR EYES ]{.class_4257}when you took your hands off." You open them. You say, "I still can't see it."

"Of course you can't your're looking DOWN instead of UP." You look up. You say, ["I still can't see it!"]{.class_4257}

"Of course you can't, you sorry, good-for-nothing, sinful, stubborn, conceited, rebellious, proud, no-account nincompoop! [You deliberately turned your back to it AGAIN when you looked up!]{.class_4257}" (And after all this, men go back to their idols and their sacraments and console their wounded feelings with, "Well, if a man is [sincere, ]{.class_4257}I'm sure God will forgive him!" How quaint!)

["So Jesus had compassion on them."]{.class_4258} The statement matches John 3:16 and Ephesians 2:4. Mark adds, ["Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole,"]{.class_4258} and this is a perfect corollary to Ephesians 2:5--10. ["And immediately,"]{.class_4258} the healing, as salvation, is instantaneous. As a matter of fact, EVERY conversion in []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_385}the Bible is instantaneous. We have to leave the mountains of Bible truth and enter the subterranean vaults of "church tradition" to find the "gradual conversion," where Bible-rejecting, God-defying, self-righteous sinners kind of "grow up into Christ" by family inheritance and [evolve ]{.class_4257}into a new birth by "commitments." In Bible language, there comes a moment when you receive the Lord Jesus (John 1:12), and at that moment, you pass from death to life, from falsehood to truth, from night to day, from darkness to light, and from condemnation to sonship. If that moment has never happened in your life, [you are LOST.]{.class_4257} It is true that you are in a majority (Matt. 7:13--14), and it is also true that you are in a [sincere ]{.class_4257}majority (Prov. 14:12). Ever since Adam's fall, the ["sincere majority" has ALWAYS BEEN DEAD WRONG, without exception]{.class_4257} (Rom. 5:9--10, 14, 18--19, 3:9--23).

["And they followed him."]{.class_4258} The two men (one being Bartimaeus) do the next thing that any convert should do---they follow Him. You will notice that none of the crowd ["following Jesus"]{.class_4257} is saved by "following Jesus," all are saved by [coming to Him and believing on Him.]{.class_4257} You cannot follow someone you do not believe in. You cannot live a Christian life without Jesus Christ. You cannot get water out of a dry well, and [you cannot work out something you don't have]{.class_4257} (see Phil. 2:12--13,\
1 Pet. 2:21). Sheldon's famous pagan novel, [In His Steps,]{.class_4257} is dedicated to this doctrine of "living it," while at the same time [denying ]{.class_4257}every major doctrinal truth in the New Testament on salvation. The theory propounded is identical to that of the Methodist literature in the twentieth century, and may be briefly stated as, ["Eternal salvation lies in 'imitating Christ' and trying to act like He would act IF HE WERE HERE."]{.class_4257} You will notice that the [RSV ]{.class_4257}re-adjusts the Greek for "follow" (Gk. [mimatai]{.class_4257}) in Ephesians 5:1 to read "imitators."

This pagan theory, which makes [In His Steps]{.class_4257} a best-seller, is essentially a heathen concept similar to that of following Buddha or Confucius in their "way of life." It violates the following standard, fundamental, basic revelations of God Almighty, given by inspiration.

1\. Christ is here, and consequently the question, "What would He do IF He were here?" is indicative of monumental ignorance on the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_386}part of the questioner. The Holy Spirit is defined as the Lord (on the earth in this dispensation, 2 Cor 3:17; John 14--16), and He [indwells ]{.class_4257}the body of the believer so that Jesus Christ is actually present (1 Cor. 3:16; Col. 1:27; 2 Cor. 5:16--17). The preoccupation with Christ's earthly example as a Jewish Messiah ministering to Israel is a [past apprenticeship ]{.class_4257}for the born-again believer, in whom Christ [presently ]{.class_4257}dwells. Sheldon, therefore, is greatly in error on the first basic truth of Biblical Christianity.

2\. Trying to [act ]{.class_4257}like Christ ("Christ-like") would not mark a man as a Christian, but as a [hypocrite]{.class_4257}, for that is what the word means: "an actor!" Furthermore, the entire concept shows the unregenerate thinking behind it, for the actor would be acting like someone else, in order to [earn ]{.class_4257}that of which he himself was not deserving! Christ did not do what He did to earn or obtain eternal salvation; [He was the salvation of God Himself incarnate]{.class_4257} (1 Tim 3:16; Heb 1:1--3).

3\. [In His Steps]{.class_4257} fails to recognize that the text from which its title is derived, continues with ["who did NO sin."]{.class_4258} It also neglects to tell its reader that the same text (1 Pet. 2:21--24) is discussing the Christian [enduring suffering ]{.class_4257}as Christ did. There is certainly nothing remotely connected with the text, in the entire chapter, that would indicate that a natural-born religious man could earn eternal life by patterning his life after Jesus Christ. Thus, self-righteous people "accomodate" Scripture to their own systems to erect a tower of Babel designed to get them to heaven, [without admitting that they need a Saviour from Hell. ]{.class_4257}"Following Christ" is addressed only to a man who has believed on Him as Saviour. Thousands who "followed Him," while rejecting the Bible, follow Him today, with Psalm chapter 23; Matthew chapter 6; and 1 Corinthians chapter 13 on their lips, and nothing in their hearts but grim determination to reject Romans chapters 5--10 and every verse in the Bible "kin" to it.

Blind Bartimaeus first sees his need, then admits his condition, then calls for mercy, then accepts it by faith, then rises, and [then ]{.class_4257}goes to work. This is something entirely different from the "[In-His-Steps]{.class_4257}-crowd," who go to work while ignoring the Book of Ephesians, stepping on the Book of Romans, slandering the Book of Revelation, and changing the Book of Genesis.

The literature for the Methodist church (Togetherness and []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2245.xhtml_page_387}Concept) is filled with this peculiar brand of "Christianity." It assumes that it would be a sin to state any fixed belief [officially]{.class_4257} where it concerns a basic Bible truth, yet the same literature will run two paragraphs to define some word that the writer [invented himself]{.class_4257}! "Commitment" is a good example. "Cooperation" is another. (The apostate Protestants have at last reached the place where they dare not believe anything out-and-out, except words found [outside ]{.class_4257}the Bible!)

[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml}

# Chapter 21 {.heading_84-0}

21:1 And when they drew nigh unto Jerusalem, and were come to Bethphage, unto the mount of Olives, then sent Jesus two disciples,

2 Saying unto them, Go into the village over against you, and straightway ye shall find an ass tied, and a colt with her: loose them, and bring them unto me.

3 And if any man say ought unto you, ye shall say, The Lord hath need of them; and straightway he will send them.

4 All this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying,

5 Tell ye the daughter of Sion, Behold, thy King cometh unto thee, meek, and sitting upon an ass, and a colt the foal of an ass.

Two disciples precede the Sunday morning entry, and since this entry is a type of the Second Advent, the number "two" is important. It will be observed that---two bring up Israel from Egypt, two spy out the town of Jericho, two are faithful spies for the Promised Land, two spy out the Midianites under Gideon, two spy for David while he is running from Saul, and [two witnesses ]{.class_4257}show up in Revelation 11 to proclaim the Second Coming.

Again, we are face‑to‑face with the peculiar maze of doctrine that characterized Matthew's presentation of the King. All amillennialists and postmillennialists (like the Christ‑rejecting Jews of the Old Testament) will reason falsely on the passage. Their reasoning is as follows:

1\. Christ, here, truly enters as a King and begins the spiritual kingdom of which He is King.

2\. The Jews wrongly suppose this is a literal kingdom and therefore have Him crucified when it doesn't "jell."

3\. Christ never intended ever to set up a literal kingdom. (This is the doctrine taught, as evidenced by the fact that the Christian Reformed Bible---The Amplified Version---directly makes a liar out of Jesus Christ by omitting the word "NOW" in John 18:36. Unable to force the [AV]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml_page_389} into any setting but premillennial, the "Reformers" treat it exactly as the Catholics---they change the verses to meet their own traditional theories. After all, friend, the proof is in the pudding. Why would a man deliberately change a verse unless he was trying to prove something the verse wouldn't let him prove?)

These three amillennial and postmillennial dogmas are derived from refusal to believe the Old Testament promises to Israel are [literal.]{.class_4257}

For example, in the text before us, the Holy Spirit quotes a promise to Israel from Zechariah 9:9. When we turn to the passage we find that the prophecies on the first and second coming are jammed up together, exactly as we find them in all the prophets. No prophet sees the intervening "Church Age" between the advents, and the New Testament tells us that this is an absolute truth (1 Pet 1:10--12). Thus both comings are seen [together, ]{.class_4257}exactly as we find them in Matthew 21:1--5. [Yet this does not make both comings identical. ]{.class_4257}(Here is where the entire body of Protestant and Catholic scholars head for the snake pit, for they all insist that the two comings must be forced together to make only one coming, [to set up an invisible, spiritual kingdom. ]{.class_4257}Thus the New Testa­ment "scholar" is guilty of willful ignorance and deliberate refusal to [believe ]{.class_4257}the word of God because of the [difficulty ]{.class_4257}encountered in "rightly dividing" \[2 Tim. 2:15\] the passages. Rather than sort them out and put them in the proper place \[where the book of Revelation places them\], he will flatly deny 80 percent of the Old Testament as being literal and take the remaining 20 percent of the promises and steal them from Israel and force them on the Church. This is the standard operating procedure for all priests, bishops, and ministers in every system that refuses to accept the Book of Revelation [literally. ]{.class_4257}It is the only book in the Bible that shows the reader where to [place the verses ]{.class_4257}that appear as contradictions to the present, visible "kingdom of God" \[see detailed comments under Matt. 3:2, 4:23, and 10:7\].)

In Matthew 21:1--4 we have, for all practical purposes, the exact fulfillment of the Second Coming! This reminds us again that all of the Old Testament conditions and promises could have been fulfilled at this time [without ]{.class_4257}an intervening "Church Age," for the church is a mystery revealed to Paul (Eph. 3:1--6) and is unheard of, or []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml_page_390}unknown, in the Old Testament. No prophet makes any comment on it, and the proof lies in the fact that the ["sufferings of Christ, and the glory that should follow"]{.class_4258} (1 Pet. 1:10--11) occur together with NO intervening period of time. Up until Acts 7, all conditions are ripe and ready and in process of being fulfilled in regard to the Old Testament truth. The change and the intervening "spiritual period," before the literal promises are fulfilled to Israel, is a MYSTERY period, and it is so mysterious that it fooled two classes of religious leaders: Old Testament Jews who failed to see an intervening period---[due to sinful pride ]{.class_4257}(which prevented them from receiving a ["Saviour"]{.class_4257} before a [Messiah), ]{.class_4257}and New Testament Catholics and Protestants who fail to see the future fulfillment of the literal promise to Israel---due to sinful pride (Rom. 11:24--30).

Matthew, as usual, lists two donkeys instead of one (Matt. 21:2). (He lists two blind men for Mark's one and two maniacs of Gadara for Mark's one.) The "sending out," to bring in this donkey, is remarkable when one considers all that is involved from a Scriptural standpoint; for lo and behold, the "unridden mule" turns out to be an exact picture of an unconverted, unbroken, natural‑born sinner! (See Job 11:12.) Here, the man is born "as" ["a wild ass' colt,"]{.class_4258} which matches what is about to follow in Matthew 21, for Mark in his account has added, ["Whereon never man sat." ]{.class_4258}(The old mule skinners will tell you that no [wild ]{.class_4257}donkey has been "broken" until it has been [ridden.) ]{.class_4257}What is more remarkable about the similarity is that the donkey must be [redeemed with a lamb]{.class_4257} (Exod. 13:13). And if not, it dies the death of a hanged‑man (Deut. 21:22--23), with a broken neck!

The coincidences, in regard to this similitude, are too many to ignore. For example (as a type of the unconverted sinner), the colt is [tied ]{.class_4257}(Rom. 6:16--20), he needs to be [loosed, ]{.class_4257}they are opposed when they try to loose him (Mark 11:5; 2 Tim. 2:25--26), but the Lord [needs ]{.class_4257}him (Mark 11:3; Acts 9:15), and he is by a [door ]{.class_4257}(Mark 11:4; John 10:7). It is where [two ways meet (!) ]{.class_4257}(Deut. 30:19; Mark 11:4), he is [brought to Jesus ]{.class_4257}(Mark 11:6; John 1:42) and re‑clothed (Mark 11:7; Isa. 61:10), and the Lord is put in control of him! (Rom. 12:1--3). How remarkable is the [AV ]{.class_4257}(1611)! How far superior it is when it comes to edification, in comparison to its feeble imitators, the [RV, ASV, RSV]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml_page_391}, and the coming one‑world "holy" telephone book!

(I once received some very enlightening information on this passage from an old farmer in South Alabama. Knowing that the "wild ass" of Job 11:12 was a type of an unbelieving sinner, I asked the old man, "What do you know about wild asses?"

"Well, Brother Pete, what do you want to know?"

"Oh, just anything you know."

He sipped a little tea and began to crunch on the ice and said slowly, "Well, to begin with, a wild ass is the most stubborn creature they is in this here world."

I said, "Amen."

He said, "What did you say?"

I said, "Nothing, go ahead."

He said, "Wild asses runs around late in the woods at night, and some of theah mothers is wild."

I said, "Amen."

He said, "What did you say?"

I said, "Nothing."

He leaned back in his cowhide bottom chair and continued, "A wild ass cannot be broke in unless you done rode him. You gotta ride him to break him in."

I intervened, "Can't you [whip ]{.class_4257}him?"

"No sir, Brother Pete, he don't understand no whippin till he's broke in!"

I stared in fascination and said unconsciously, "Amen."

"What did you say, Brother Pete?"

I said, "Nothing. Go ahead."

"Well, sir," he said, "that's about it. You gotta ride them to break them in, and they ain't been broke in till they's done been [rid!"]{.class_4257}

I never have forgotten that conversation. That old man with a sixth‑grade education had shed light on the text of Matthew 21:1--7 that no Greek scholar had ever noticed. Somehow, Tischendorf, Robertson, Westcott and Hort, Streeter, Thieme, Griesbach, Tregelles, Alford, Weigle, and Trench had missed the "meat" of the text, while fumbling with the redactors.)

["And a colt the foal of an ass." ]{.class_4258}As the verse is worded (Matt. 21:5), it appears that He is sitting on TWO animals, and this gives []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml_page_392}scholars considerable theological gas on the stomach. Running quickly to the Greek (Gk. [kai]{.class_4257}), it is brought to the defense of the believer that the ["and"]{.class_4258} should read "even" (as in Hebrew poetry), and that there is no contradiction IF the [King James ]{.class_4257}is re‑translated into more "readable" English. But alas, this is the way of all flesh. (It is like Paulus \[Stalingrad\] wiring Hitler for gas supplies and an airlift to affect a breakout before the 6th Army was annihilated, and Hitler sending as much gas as the army had used [since they were encircled---]{.class_4257}i.e., practically nothing. They didn't get enough to make a breakout, and the Army was annihilated till one Division numbered 26 men. Out "in the Red ring" the German boys froze, bled, screamed, died, crawled in snow till they dropped, fought to board hospital planes till they had to throw out all the medicine and equipment to hold the load, fought to eat frozen horses, were shot for stealing bread from each other, and finally turned their pale and dirty faces to the frozen sky and perished.) Thus the Bible believer looks to his leaders for authority and power and help, and they throw some Greek at him and tell him his Bible is "out‑dated" or "inaccurate" or a "poor translation" and leave him to perish under the gunnery of Rome. If military tactics applied to church leadership, over half the "Conservative" and "Fundamental" scholars in the United States ought to be standing trial for destroying the only weapon a Christian ever had---[his plain‑English, 1611 Bible.]{.class_4257}

The figure of speech in Matthew 21:5 is so clear that there is no necessity whatsoever of running off to a Greek lexicon. A "hendiady" is a figure of speech where a "plural of class" is used to refer to an individual.

For example, "He sprang from the horses." Was he riding five of them? "From the horses" means he was on one in the group and sprang out from that group (see Acts 3:14). No one bothered to mutilate Acts 3:14, and it is perfectly understood that ["the Holy One"]{.class_4258} and the "Just One" are not [two different people. ]{.class_4257}Yet there is the ["and"]{.class_4258} (not "even"), right in the middle of them (see 1 Sam 17:40, where the ["even"]{.class_4258} is used at the discretion of the Holy Spirit when He sees fit). The colt is one animal, who is the foal of another animal, and Christ sits on the [colt.]{.class_4257}

Part of the quotation of Zechariah is omitted in Matthew 21:5. It reads, ["And having salvation."]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml_page_393} Notice how careful the Holy Spirit is to omit this at Matthew 21, as the final fulfillment of Zechariah 9:9 is found in 9:10--11 and speaks of the [literal salvation of Israel from her Gentile enemies at the Second Coming.]{.class_4257}

The passage in Zechariah is important because the [RSV ]{.class_4257}has taken time to tamper with it, and anything the [RSV ]{.class_4257}would mess up is messed up [purposely, ]{.class_4257}with an objective in mind. The [RSV ]{.class_4257}and Moffatt get rid of God's salvation by writing "victory" (Moffatt---"Here comes your king triumphant and victorious"). However, this is NOT victory, but [salvation ]{.class_4257}as you find it in the highly accurate 1611 [AV]{.class_4257}. The word is ["Yashal" ]{.class_4257}(Heb.), a Niph'al passive, and is translated "to [save ]{.class_4257}or to [deliver" ]{.class_4257}(as a verb). Thus the word is translated "victorious" only in the Hiph'il, and NEVER in Niph'al. In the Niph'al Passive, it is translated "SALVATION" or "be safe" or "be saved" twenty-one times. The odds are twenty-one to one that the [AV ]{.class_4257}is correct, as the expression "get victory" is only used once, and then in a [different ]{.class_4257}mode.

21:6 And the disciples went, and did as Jesus commanded them,

7 And brought the ass, and the colt, and put on them their clothes, and they set him thereon.

8 And a very great multitude spread their garments in the way; others cut down branches from the trees, and strowed them in the way.

9 And the multitudes that went before, and that followed, cried, saying, Hosanna to the son of David: Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord; Hosanna in the highest.

10 And when he was come into Jerusalem, all the city was moved, saying, Who is this?

11 And the multitude said, This is Jesus the prophet of Nazareth of Galilee.

["And put on them their clothes, and they set him thereon." ]{.class_4258}The first part is plural; the donkeys both have robes and blankets placed on them. The second part is singular, ["Set him thereon," ]{.class_4258}i.e., on the last animal named---the colt. Luke 19:35 brings this out clearly, proving again that the 1611 [AV]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml_page_394} is self‑interpreting. Luke 19:36 also adds that ["clothes"]{.class_4258} as well as branches were ["strawed\...in the way"]{.class_4258} (our spelling would be "strewed"). The branches laid are [palm ]{.class_4257}branches, as the city He has just left before arriving in Jerusalem is said to be "the City of Palms" (2 Chron. 28:15). There are also Olive branches, as the procession is coming East to West down into the Eastern Gate from Bethphage on the Mount of Olives---Olive and Palms (John 12:13). The Olive branches un­doubtedly symbolize Christ as the conquerer and ruler of Israel (Rom. 11:1--25), but the Palm branches, representing Jericho, the cursed city (Josh. 6--7), would represent Christ as conquerer and ruler of the Gentile World Dominion under Rome, for Rome is the [cursed city ]{.class_4257}at the Second Coming (Rev. 17--18). Thus, the "enemies under his feet" (Psa. 2) are represented by Israel and the heathen, both coming into submission; see Daniel 2, 7.

Here again, the post‑ and amillennial systems flail and thrash with the symbols till the room is cluttered with non‑descript fragments of meaningless nonsense. For both systems assume that Christ conquered both elements at [His first coming ]{.class_4257}and is now a king spreading "His kingdom" over those elements. Despite newscasts, FBI reports, telecasts, statistics, history books, and common sense, the Liberal and Catholic continue, to the bitter end, to propagate their inane drivel as "the word of God." In spite of two World Wars, a Civil War, a Hundred Years War, a Tartar invasion, the Inquisition, the Crusades, a Korean War, a Crimean War, a Boer War, a Franco‑Prussian War, and revolutions in America, France, Spain, Bolivia, and Russia, they still insist that ["the kingdom is coming."]{.class_4257} But alas, the Bible interprets for them. "A kingdom" is [coming, ]{.class_4257}but it is not Jesus Christ's; it is the United Nations under Satan incarnate (as a man), with visitors from outer space who will unite the world under Rome! (See Dan. 2; Rev. 13, 18; Gen. 6; Matt. 24--25.)

["Hosanna to the son of David\...in the name of the Lord." ]{.class_4258}The cries of the multitude are the cries of Bible-believing literalists, who recognize that Jesus is the promised Davidic, Jewish, visible, physical Messiah who is to take the literal, physical, visible Throne of David and reign over a literal, physical, visible kingdom of twelve literal Jewish tribes in the physical, literal, visible land of Palestine []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml_page_395}(Luke 1:30--33; Amos 9:15).

Thus they cry, and thus Jesus commends them for crying [correctly ]{.class_4257}(Luke 19:39--40). The Pharisees, of course, were looking for a Messiah to come who would approve of [them ]{.class_4257}and let them "run the works" when the kingdom was set up. This is the last man in the world that they want to "set up a kingdom," so they rebuke Him and His disciples. Jesus corrects them and acknowledges in so many words that the Scriptures are correct---that He is the prophesied king and that [the literal promises to Israel are exact and being literally fulfilled. ]{.class_4257}Not only the Pharisees, but the entire body of professing Christendom (with the exception of a few premillennialists) take issue with the Jesus Christ here. For from "a child" the Conservatives and Fundamentalists have used John 6:15 to prove that Christ had no intention of coming as a King the first time. The Liberal and Catholic have been taught that He came as a King, but was misunderstood because the Bible literalists did not understand the "nature" of the kingdom. (Both of these views may be placed in File 13 and forgotten.) Jesus never denies at any time that He is the promised Jewish Messiah, King of the Jews, sent to reign over the house of Jacob, in Palestine. [He never rebukes anyone who sees this truth. ]{.class_4257}The only rebuke is for those who refuse to recognize the delay and reject Him as their own [individual Saviour, ]{.class_4257}and for those who pervert the doctrine of the Kingdom to teach that God is [all through ]{.class_4257}with Israel (Rom. 11:24­--28; John 1:1--14; Matt. 21:38; Luke 19:14; Acts 15:14--18).

Where the Christ‑rejecting Jew does so (on the grounds that He did not literally fulfill the promises which are yet future), the Bible‑rejecting amillennialist (Catholic, Liberal, or Conservative) does so on the grounds that the promises were never intended to be believed [literally. ]{.class_4257}Both individuals stand and fall on the same ground---unbelief.

["Blessed is he that cometh\...."]{.class_4258} This is the "go­ ahead" for the Second Advent, for the last thing Jesus says IN the temple (that Titus is soon to destroy) is, ["Ye shall not see me henceforth, till ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord" ]{.class_4258}(Matt. 23:39). Matthew 21:9 is most certainly, then, a partial fulfillment of Zechariah 9:9. [This does not mean that it will not be fulfilled again, ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml_page_396}for the statement in Matthew 23:39 is plainly future, though it [had just taken place ]{.class_4257}in Matthew 21:9. (This is the kind of thing that makes a lazy man throw up his hands and surrender. By limiting a verse to the HISTORICAL application [only, ]{.class_4257}the average Bible teacher \[along with his student\] is caught in the hopeless mesh of conflicting passages, till he finally in desperation says, "IT'S SPIRITUAL." It's like the little boy staring for fifteen minutes at the giraffe in the zoo and finally saying to his mother, "Momma, there ain't no animal like that, is there?")

ALL SCRIPTURE HAS THREE APPLICATIONS (Eccl. 1:9, 3:15), HISTORICAL, INSPIRATIONAL, DOCTRINAL.

[Historically, ]{.class_4257}Christ is entering Jerusalem to die.

[Inspirationally, ]{.class_4257}we find a type of the salvation of the sinner, represented by the donkey.

[Doctrinally, ]{.class_4257}we are dealing with a partial fulfillment of a literal prophecy to be fulfilled [exactly ]{.class_4257}at the Second Advent. (There, the Lord got you out of it; don't get in it again until you have enough grace to believe what the Book says, as it says it, where it says it!)

["Who is this?" ]{.class_4258}The question is loaded. Who IS this? The question is more controversial than the contemporary questions on integration or birth control. Men have been arguing about it ever since He showed up, and they are still arguing about it. It is rephrased in Matthew 22:42, ["Whose son is he?"]{.class_4258}

["He is the son of Joseph the carpenter,"]{.class_4257} say[ ]{.class_4257}the unbelieving Jews and relatives of Mary (Mark 6:3), and likewise the [RV, ASV, RSV]{.class_4257}, and [New English ]{.class_4257}translations.

"He is the [son of Joseph, ]{.class_4257}Israel's son," says Phillip (John 1:45).

"He is 'A' son of God," say the [RV, ASV, RSV]{.class_4257}, and Catholic translations (Luke 2:33; Heb. 1:5).

"He is a [Great Teacher," ]{.class_4257}say Peale, Kagawa, Fosdick, Buttrick, Dahlberg, Gibran, Carlyle, Eddy, Fox, Cady, Emerson, Jones, Poteat, and other watered‑down moral­ists.

"WHO IS THIS?"

"This is Jesus the prophet of Nazareth of Galilee."

The answer the multitude gives is about 80 percent right and about 20 percent wrong. They are far more correct than the twentieth-century Bible-rejectors at Chicago, Yale, Harvard, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml_page_397}Princeton, and Colgate-Rochester, but they still fall short in their estimation of this personage. It is JESUS, but the name is a common name of the day and does not tell a full truth. (See Acts 7:45 for Joshua; Col 4:11; Acts 13:6.) JESUS CHRIST tells more truth (Phil. 2:10), but THE LORD JESUS CHRIST tells the whole truth (Phil. 2:11). Thus we see where all anti‑Christian "christians" show their hand. The Modernist prates of "the Christ" (meaning a christ‑principle, like an anointing, or a spirit), while the Liberal talks about the "teachings of Jesus" (referring to the moralistic discourses of some dead Jew), and the common man refers to "the Lord" (meaning---"the man upstairs" or some religious Santa Claus who takes care of sick folks), and part‑and‑parcel, baggage‑and‑kaboodle, [they reject the Saviour that God gave them.]{.class_4257}

This is not ["Jesus."]{.class_4258} This is the Lord Jesus Christ. Notice that this Pauline concept of Christ is taken by scholarship to be Paul's [private opinion ]{.class_4257}of "what Christ meant to him." Thus, scholarship divests the Lord Jesus of His Deity, strips Him of His authority, robs Him of His glory, and leaves Him as a martyr‑prophet, about whom several people had several different ideas. (When they face Him at the White Throne Judgment they will discover the error!)

["The prophet." ]{.class_4258}This statement is correct, in view of the fact that Christ's three‑fold office is fulfilled as Prophet first. That is, on earth, in the flesh, during his earthly ministry to Israel, He is said to be a "prophet like unto Moses" (Deut. 18:15--18). In His risen state, He is a PRIEST making intercession (Heb. 3:1; 4:15--16). At His return, He comes as a KING (Rev. 19:16; Luke 1:32--33). This Prophet‑Priest‑King is found in only [one ]{.class_4262}man in the Old Testament. Moses is a Prophet and King (Deut. 33:5), but is not allowed to offer the sacrifices of a Priest. Samuel is a Prophet and Priest, but has to choose another King (1 Sam. 11--12); however, David comes out as a holder of all [three offices. ]{.class_4262}He is Prophet (Acts 2:30), King (2 Sam. 1-- 12), and offers a sacrifice as a Priest (2 Sam. 24:25). Truly the answer to ["Who is this?"]{.class_4258} is not merely ["Jesus the prophet," ]{.class_4258}but He is truly the ["son of DAVID!\...Hosanna in the highest!"]{.class_4258}

21:12 And Jesus went into the temple of God, and cast out all them that sold and bought in the temple, and []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml_page_398}overthrew the tables of the moneychangers, and the seats of them that sold doves,

13 And said unto them, It is written, My house shall be called the house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.

14 And the blind and the lame came to him in the temple; and he healed them.

15 And when the chief priests and scribes saw the wonderful things that he did, and the children crying in the temple, and saying, Hosanna to the son of David; they were sore displeased,

16 And said unto him, Hearest thou what these say? And Jesus saith unto them, Yea; have ye never read, Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings thou hast perfected praise?

["Sold and bought in the temple." ]{.class_4258}The "buying and selling," here, is the buying and selling of [animals ]{.class_4257}for the sacrifices commanded under the Levitical law (see Lev. 1). The ["moneychangers" ]{.class_4258}indicate that the Jew in the temple is making a little "profit" on the side by transferring Gentile money into Jewish money, for many Jewish proselytes are present to make an offering also (Acts 8:27, 10:4, 22; Luke 7:5). The equivalent of this is the "cigarette exchange" for Yen in the back alleys of Tokyo, and it amounts to [thievery ]{.class_4257}in the final analysis, for Jesus calls the business ["a den of thieves." ]{.class_4258}A transfer or conversion of a Dollar into a Mark or a Ruble or a Yen or a Peso, can involve a lot of things (as any GI knows).

["My house." ]{.class_4258}Notice that at the beginning of His ministry in the first cleansing of the temple, Jesus refers to it as ["my Father's house"]{.class_4258} (John[ ]{.class_4258}2:16), yet here He calls it ["MY"]{.class_4258} house. The reference is too obvious to comment on, WE AGAIN ARE FACED WITH THE FULFILLMENT OF THE SECOND COMING PASSAGE IN MALACHI 3:1, [still awaiting future fulfillment ]{.class_4257}(see notes on 21:10--11). He is the Messiah, the King of Israel, the builder of the house (Heb. 3:3--4, 6) and owner of the true temple made without hands. It is HIS house, the same way the body of the born‑again believer is the property of Jesus Christ (1 Cor. 3:16--17; 6:19).

["The house of prayer." ]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml_page_399}Again, the quotation is from a passage that deals doctrinally with the Millennial reign, yet future (Isa. 56:7). In the passage in Isaiah 56:7, it speaks of a literal sacrifice on a literal altar (as in Ezek. 44--48), and the Sabbath is being observed (as in Ezek. 44--48; Isa. 56:4), [not Sunday. ]{.class_4257}Furthermore, the context of the quotation (Isa. 56:8) is, ["The Lord God which gathereth the outcasts of Israel." ]{.class_4258}Note how carefully God has made the condi­tions of the First Coming nearly identical with the Second, so that Catholic and Liberal Scholars will CONFOUND the two advents and confuse the issues, garble the verses, and wind up with a theology that is ANTI-CHRISTIAN. Jews in Palestine (at the First Advent); Jews in Palestine at the Second Advent. Jews return from captivity before the First Advent; Jews return before the Second Advent. Rome in power at the First Advent; Rome in power at the Second Advent. John a type of Elijah foreruns the First Coming; Elijah foreruns the Second Coming.

If there was any doubt about the location in history of the quotation of Isaiah 56:7, notice the companion passage in Malachi 1:11 ([which the Roman priest quotes to justify the repeated "sacrifice" of the mass]{.class_4257})! This demon­strates graphically how "fouled up" a postmillennialist or an amillennialist can get, and it shows conclusively that ignorance of the doctrines involved in the Second Coming can lead a man off into a waste‑howling wilderness of theology till he can even mistake a jug of liquor for the Blood Atonement! Malachi 1:11 is a direct contradiction of Hebrews 10:8--12, unless the premillennial system of placing verses in the correct dispensations is observed. Matthew 21:13; Isaiah 56:7; and Malachi 1:11 will "jive" if placed where they belong, [at the end of the Tribulation and the beginning of the Millennium.]{.class_4257} To place them into the Church Age, as "Christian" doctrine, is a glaring travesty of common sense and sound judgment.

["They were sore displeased."]{.class_4258} Running true-to-form and coming in on their habitual "beam," the priests ignore the miracles of healing in verse 14 and take up their hostile doctrinal arguments. This is their consistent pattern throughout Matthew---ignore the miracle and then question the authority of the miracle‑worker (see 16:2, 19:3, etc.).

["Hearest thou what these say?"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2352.xhtml_page_400} We have to read the Old Testament to detect the cause of this priestly griping. ["What these say"]{.class_4258} is found in verse 15 of the text, and refers to the Old Testament prophecies on a coming Messiah---note, ["Hosanna to the son of David." ]{.class_4258}The ["son of David"]{.class_4258} in the Old Testament is the Anointed One, the chosen King of Israel.

In 2 Samuel 7:12--17, the promise to David's son is an Everlasting Throne and an Everlasting Kingdom (see Psa. 89:20--37). The Jew looks for this and has a right to. The "Christian" in the twentieth century [denies this ]{.class_4257}and spiritualizes the kingdom, stealing it from Israel and appropriating it for "Holy Mother Church." Notice that this kingdom, promised to ["the son of David,"]{.class_4258} is a literal, physical, visible, Jewish, Palestinian, earthly Kingdom with a literal, physical, visible, Jewish, Palestinian throne under a covenant (study Luke 1:30--34; Acts 15:14--17; Isa. 16:5; Jer. 33:16; Psa. 102:16; Amos 9:11). Every time the Jewish listener accosts Christ with His claims as "Messiah," He goes immediately to the Davidic Psalm where David calls Him ["Lord" ]{.class_4258}(note Matt. 22:42--45; Psa. 110:1).
