To reject Paul's epistles then as merely "Pauline theology" and to return to the "teachings of Jesus" in the gospels is to commit religious and theological suicide, for without the revela­tion of the intervening period, [verses on the Second Coming have to be spiritualized to match the facts of the first, and verses on the First Coming (to Israel as a Jewish Messiah) have to be thrust on the believer in the age which ]{.class_4257}follows [the temporary rejection of Israel.]{.class_4257}

We have spoken of this method of Bible perversion many times before. It originates in the writings of Origen (184--254), Cyprian (200), Irenaeus (200), and Augustine (354--430), the North African "catholics" who devoted their lives to destroying the authority of the Scripture and exalting a man‑made political hierarchy to the place of power. Furthermore, knowledge of the correct way to interpret never has reformed these men or their followers. You will notice that in this terrific parable in Matthew 21:43--46, the postmillennial priests addressed have [full knowledge ]{.class_4257}that they themselves are "wicked men," worse than publicans or harlots (21:31), lacking authority (21:23), rebellious sons (21:30), robbers (21:38), and murderers (21:39), [and knowing this, ]{.class_4257}they still betray Jesus and murder Him!

This Bible fact points out a truth entirely overlooked in the fields of psychiatry and psychology: that KNOWLEDGE and UNDERSTANDING rarely solve a SPIRITUAL PROBLEM. The roots lie deeper. Judas (with full understanding that he is betraying the Jehovah God of Genesis 1 and Exodus 3--4) goes right ahead and sells him out. (Notice that before coming forward to kiss Christ in Gethsemane, he is first hurled backward to the ground by the force of the spoken tetragrammaton ["I am"]{.class_4258} (John 18:3, 6). Knowing that it is the eternal ["I am"]{.class_4258} that he is facing, and knowing that this ["I am"]{.class_4258} has power to knock him down with even [two spoken words, ]{.class_4257}he comes ahead anyway and collects his[ "thirty pieces of silver"]{.class_4258}!)

Returning to Matthew 21:44, you will notice that the First Advent causes a man to ["fall on the stone," ]{.class_4258}thus it is a ["stumblingstone," ]{.class_4258}over which a self‑righteous religious person trips---see the exact explanation in Romans 9:32--33 and 1 Peter 2:8. The last reference goes to the fullest in definition and says that "disobedience" causes a man to ["stumble at the word"; ]{.class_4258}thus, you are entirely justified in interpreting Matthew 21:44 in the first clause as a clause describing the consequence of rejecting the mission and purpose of Christ's First Coming---to die.

The second clause in the verse is quite another matter. ["On whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder." ]{.class_4258}The stone does not[ "fall"]{.class_4258} on anyone at the First Advent. Here Calvin and the popes completely misread and misinterpret Daniel 2:45 as referring to the First Coming, [to set up a gradually‑spreading, spiritual kingdom. ]{.class_4262}Anyone can see at a glance the stupidity of the interpreta­tion (if he spends any amount of time reading the Bible), when he observes that the smiting stone of Matthew 21:44 and Daniel 2:44--45 hits [instantly. W]{.class_4262}hen it hits,[ it does not destroy the Roman Empire ]{.class_4262}(which was in power at the time of Christ's First Coming), but it destroys a fifth power, that arises [after ]{.class_4262}the Roman Empire is gone (see Dan. 7:3--8; Rev. 13:1--2). The grinding to powder, then, is obviously a [future ]{.class_4262}reference, and by no stretch of the mangled imagination of Rome can one deduct the reason­ing that Christ "ground people to death" at His First Coming. Not even those who rejected Him were "ground to powder," nor did He fall on them. Matthew 21:44, thus, contains [both advents with a colon between them. ]{.class_4262}Notice this remarkable dual construction in the passages in Genesis 49:11, Genesis 3:15, 49:24; Matthew 21:44, second clause. ["Between thy seed and her seed"]{.class_4258}---["Binding his foal unto the vine"]{.class_4258}---["The shepherd"]{.class_4258}---["Whosoever shall fall on this stone shall be broken." "It shall bruise thy head"---"Washed\...his clothes in\...blood"---"The stone of Israel"---"On whomso­ever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder."]{.class_4258}

Further details on the coming, crushing Stone (that will smite and bruise and grind till blood comes up to horses' bridles) are found in Revelation 19; Joel 2; and Isaiah 63.

You will observe in all the passages that the First and Second Coming are spoken of as [simultaneous; ]{.class_4257}nowhere is the 2,000 year period of the "Church Age" mentioned. This serves a two‑fold purpose, as has been mentioned before.

One: it forces the Jew to accept a suffering Saviour [before ]{.class_4257}he is able to enjoy a literal, millennial reign. Two: it forces a religious leader in the Church Age to [rightly divide ]{.class_4257}passages to Jew and Christian, or else [deny the word of God. ]{.class_4257}Ninety percent,[ ]{.class_4257}of course, flatly deny the word of God and reduce all 500 passages in the Bible that deal with the Second Coming to a [spiritual, gradually developing ]{.class_4257}"kingdom of God," in which God "dumps" the Jew and exalts the obese and intemperant Italian "POPE" to the driver's seat. This Satanic perversion is accepted by all Roman Catholics, numbering 40,000,000 strong, and all regular Methodist, Presbyterian, Lutheran, and Episcopalian ministers in one form or another. (The Protestant version is that the "church" is the final authority, but this "church" is the [National Council ]{.class_4257}or [Ecumenical Council instead of the Roman College of Cardinals. "Six of one, half‑a‑dozen of another.")]{.class_4257}

[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml}

# Chapter 22 {.heading_84-0}

22:1 And Jesus answered and spake unto them again by parables, and said,

2 The kingdom of heaven is like unto a certain king, which made a marriage for his son,

3 And sent forth his servants to call them that were bidden to the wedding: and they would not come.

4 Again, he sent forth other servants, saying, Tell them which are bidden, Behold, I have prepared my dinner: my oxen and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready: come unto the marriage.

5 But they made light of it, and went their ways, one to his farm, another to his merchandise:

6 And the remnant took his servants, and entreated them spitefully, and slew them.

7 But when the king heard thereof, he was wroth: and he sent forth his armies, and destroyed those murderers, and burned up their city.

8 Then saith he to his servants, The wedding is ready, but they which were bidden were not worthy.

9 Go ye therefore into the highways, and as many as ye shall find, bid to the marriage.

10 So those servants went out into the highways, and gathered together all as many as they found, both bad and good: and the wedding was furnished with guests.

This parable, as no other parable in the Scripture, illustrates the total inadequacy of postmillennial and amillennial Catholicism to "fill the bill" when it comes to Bible interpretation. It is the tenth parable on the kingdom of Heaven, and it (along with the "wise and foolish virgins" of chapter 25) has been used by thousands of preachers over and over again, in the present "day of grace," the "Church Age." Historically, the passage will deal with the proceedings going on in the time of Christ's earthly ministry. The ["murderers"]{.class_4258} and their city which is destroyed (vs. 7) is undoubtedly a reference to the destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70, mentioned in the preceding parable of 21:41. [Inspirationally, ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_424}the passage can be applied to the gospel invitation and the various responses to it, as it is apparent that a heavenly marriage is going to take place, and this marriage involves the Lord Jesus Christ (see Psa. 45; Rev. 19:4--10).

But when one makes an attempt to apply the passage [doctrinally, ]{.class_4257}with a detailed meaning to each part (and that is the only correct way to interpret a parable according to the original author of the parable, the Lord Jesus), then all postmillennial and amillennial expositors and most premillennial expositors begin to falter and stumble.

The problem that arises from nowhere to confound the application of the verses is the closing portion of 22:11--14.

22:11 And when the king came in to see the guests, he saw there a man which had not on a wedding garment:

12 And he saith unto him, Friend, how camest thou in hither not having a wedding garment? And he was speechless.

13 Then said the king to the servants, Bind him hand and foot, and take him away, and cast him into outer darkness; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

14 For many are called, but few are chosen.

The appearance of the unsaved man at the supper (who is subsequently consigned to outer darkness) does two things to amillennial and postmillennial "scholars" that ruin them as reliable interpreters. The first thing that happens is that the Arminian position of [losing salvation is ]{.class_4257}applied to the passage; that is, the "guest" without the wedding garment is a man who had received Christ, had been caught up to the Last Judgment, and lo and behold, he has lost his clothes on the way up! (See Rev. 16:15 for confirmation of this position.)

The second thing that happens is that Landmark Baptists (many premillennial) recognize the presence of ["guests"]{.class_4258} at the wedding, exclusive of the ["bride" ]{.class_4258}(John 3:29), and they locate these ["guests"]{.class_4258} as members of the "family of God," [outside of the body of Jesus Christ in this age ]{.class_4257}(Eph. 3:15).

In conjunction with this "Baptist‑bride" position, the entire parable is spiritualized to mean "the gospel feast," as it is found in Revelation 3:20 or Luke 14:15, thus making the entire parable applicable to this age, and making the rejected guests ISRAEL, [permanently rejected, ]{.class_4257}and the accepted guests GENTILES [permanently accepted. ]{.class_4257}Both of these systems of interpretation come from the fanciful notion that a "parable teaches a general truth," and both systems are as corrupt as the Johnson-Kennedy regime.

Let us take a few moments out for a little light diversion and see how the parable will read if we actually [apply ]{.class_4257}the post‑ and amillennial systems of interpretation.

Verse 2 would be God making a wedding for Christ (spiritually a gospel celebration). Verses 3--6 would refer to unbelieving Jews who rejected Jesus Christ and, conse­quently, were destroyed in A.D. 70 (vs. 7). Verses 8--10 would refer to the calling‑out of the Gentiles ["a people for his name"]{.class_4258} (Acts 15:14), and the ["guests"]{.class_4258} would be saved people in the Church Age who believed on Christ. (Very well, all is right until verse 11!) But at verse 11, in comes a believer in Christ in the Church Age, who is [bound hand and foot and cast into outer darkness ]{.class_4257}(vs. 13). Something is badly fouled up in this standard Catholic interpretation. But it doesn't worry them and never will as long as people [look to them as the final authority in matters of interpretation. ]{.class_4257}After all, if you have 40,000,000 people sewed up financially to yield better than \$40,000,000,000 a year, what difference does it make if you stomp your dirty boots all over a passage in the Bible and come home saying, "Well, it was only given to teach a [general truth. ]{.class_4257}We must not look for a [specific ]{.class_4257}meaning."?

What is wrong? My dear friend, the thing that is wrong is that the religious leaders (to nearly the last man) have rejected the [AV ]{.class_4257}as a source of authoritative revelation, and God has consequently stifled further knowledge in regard to the Bible. The scholars (loaded with lexicons by Berry, Thayer, Robertson, Harkavy, and Gesenius) can [no longer understand a parable or explain it ]{.class_4257}(see Prov. 26:7, 9).

Now, getting down to a field‑stripping of the passage, let us dislocate every section of it, until we know [exactly ]{.class_4257}what it is talking about.

The first thing to notice (which ALL the commenta­tors have blithely overlooked) is the fact that the ["guests"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_426} at a wedding do not go to [marry ]{.class_4257}anyone. They are not even "friends of the bridegroom" (John 3:29); they are ["guests."]{.class_4258} As the "wise and foolish virgins" who go out to ["MEET THE BRIDEGROOM," ]{.class_4258}these guests do NOT go out to [marry ]{.class_4257}him. Where the body of Christ is spoken of as ["a chaste virgin"]{.class_4258} (singular) espoused to one husband (Eph. 5:25--32; 2 Cor 11:1--4), the guests are espoused to [nobody, ]{.class_4257}and they (as the ["virgins"]{.class_4258} of Matt. 25) are in the plural, not the singular. (It is a good idea to take an azimuth and get your bearings before you try to head out through a swamp at 2 o'clock in the morning.)

Whereas the "bride of Christ" (see Rev. 19:8--10, 21:9) speaks of a body of believers who are now [in Christ's body]{.class_4257} (1 Cor. 12:1--20) as Eve was in Adam's body, the GUESTS at the wedding are GUESTS and no more. They are not ["in Christ," ]{.class_4258}never have been, and never will be; therefore, all the rubbish about them being in "the family of God"[ ]{.class_4258}[in this age is ]{.class_4257}just blind shooting into the air.

Once this basic, New Testament, Scriptural, funda­mental, common sense truth has been established, the reader is in some condition to read the parable and interpret correctly. [Until this is done, ]{.class_4257}there is no hope that the parable will ever be read, taught, heard, explained, or preached profitably.

Now, beginning at the beginning---verses 1--2 are plain. Someday God is going to get His Son a bride and have a marriage ceremony for them in the nature of a "feast." The son is Jesus. The bride is the church. The feast is what it says it is (see Matt 26:29; Luke 22:30, 12:35--38). The last mentioned verse, Luke 12:35--38, will bear some close scrutiny. Note that the actual [eating ]{.class_4257}of the feast takes place [after the wedding itself, ]{.class_4257}and that the people invited to the wedding feast, or supper, are said to be waiting for someone returning [FROM a wedding. ]{.class_4257}No mention is made of anyone being raptured [up to a wedding ]{.class_4257}(1 Thess. 4; 1 Cor. 15).

Verses 3--6 are fairly intelligible. The Jews reject Christ at His First Coming and slay His servants (see Acts 12:1--6, 5:40) and imprison and "entreat them spitefully." God does destroy them in A.D. 70 and does "burn their city" (Matt. 22:7 with notes on 21:41).

Now we are in trouble.

Since the church (as the Body of Christ) is a mystery revealed from heaven to Paul the apostle (Eph. 3:1--10; and at this point Paul is not present or saved or writing anything), we are again in a place where [the two advents are spoken of as occurring simultaneously, with no intervening period between them ]{.class_4257}(see notes on 21:44).

Where the amillennial and postmillennial expositors butcher the context and mangle the verses till they have only a "general truth" left to teach, the premillennial system preserves the integrity of the passage and presents a system which will "make sense" of what follows. Note: verses 8--15 skip right over the Church Age and land the reader into the time of ["Jacob's trouble"]{.class_4258} when God is gathering out from ISRAEL a body of [guests ]{.class_4257}to be present at the marriage! He has [already called out the body of believers who make up the bride ]{.class_4257}(see Psa. 45). Notice that the guests are not called a ["virgin" ]{.class_4258}(as Song of Sol. 6:9; 2 Cor 11:1--4), but ["virgins," ]{.class_4258}as in Matthew 25 and Revelation 7---and they are numbered as 144,000 where they refer to the Jewish remnant sealed in the Tribulation. They are NOT in the Church Age. They do NOT represent Christians in "other" churches. They are NOT Russellites pretending to be "Jehovah's" Witnesses. They are NOT "Christians." They are NOT born‑again believers. They do NOT appear till after the Rapture. They are NOT "caught up" till the end of the Tribulation (Matt. 24:30--31). They are NOT in the bride and never will be, and all attempts to wrest and force the Scriptures dealing with them into the Church Age will end up on only one place---a dead‑beat at Rome, trying to heal the wounds of schismatics who have nearly bled to death fighting each other with the ["sword of the Spirit."]{.class_4258} My friend, if you will not study to "rightly divide," then you will have to "wrongly join" what God never fooled with. (In Matt. 22, by the way, He didn't invite you to "jine"; He invited you to "dine!")

Verse 8 jumps over the Church Age, which has run more that 1960 years, and goes immediately into the time when the ["guests"]{.class_4258} will be called to ["the marriage of the Lamb"]{.class_4258} (which incidentally also takes place after the "Church Age"). You will notice that the words ["church"]{.class_4258} and ["churches"]{.class_4258} disappear in the book of Revelation after 3:22 and appear nowhere in the chapters that follow 4--20, until the book is concluding in chapter 22 and the final []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_428}admonitions and exhortations have been given. Again, we see how a man's attitude toward the book of Revelation will determine the method that he uses to interpret the Gospel according to Matthew (see commen­tary on Revelation, Rev. 3:22).

The ["guests,"]{.class_4258} then, are the saved Tribulation saints of the seven‑year period yet to come, at the close of the Church Age (see Matt. 24:13--20). As this "seventieth week" of seven years is called "Daniel's Seventieth Week" (see Dan. 9), it cannot be detected without a literal belief in the book of Revelation. Revelation opens Daniel, Daniel opens Matthew, and Matthew opens Acts and Hebrews. God has it fixed so that any rejector of the Revelation of Jesus Christ (Rev 1:1) is automatically disqualified as a Bible commentator and immediately takes up his post on the outskirts of rationalism and common sense in Bible exposition. Such an appointment places Roman Catholicism in the lead as the outstanding Bible bungler and Scripture spoiler of the ages. There is not a Catholic bishop, living or [dead, ]{.class_4257}who ever had the aptitude to expound Matthew 22 or Matthew 24 or Matthew 25 correctly.

["A man which had not on a wedding garment." ]{.class_4258}The verse is still suspiciously invulnerable to exposition. Suppose the ["guests"]{.class_4258} are Tribulation saints; can a saint in the Tribulation [lose it? ]{.class_4257}(The answer of course is "yes," as there is an element of [works and faith ]{.class_4257}connected with this period---see comments on Matt. 24:13--20, 7:24, 6:14). But it does not say that the ["man"]{.class_4258} of 22:11 is a "guest." It merely says[ "a man which had not on a wedding garment." ]{.class_4258}This would tell us immediately that this is an [uninvited guest, ]{.class_4257}for those invited to an oriental wedding are all offered garments (before they come) as a "passport" through the door to the festivities. Guest or no, the man does not have a [garment, ]{.class_4257}and since the guests of the Tribulation saints are [given garments ]{.class_4257}(see Rev. 6:11, 7:9), the man has obviously not been given a garment (or lost it! see Rev. 16:15), so he is not a "saint." Nor is he a proper "guest" for the marriage of the King's Son. Notice that his destiny is that of ["the unprofitable servant"]{.class_4258} in Matthew 25:30. If he [was ]{.class_4257}a Tribulation saint, then he did NOT "endure to the end." If he was not a "guest" to start with, then who is he?

["Friend, how camest thou in hither\...?"]{.class_4258} The appellation, ["Friend,"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_429} reminds one strangely of the scene in the garden early Wednesday morning---["Friend, where­fore art thou come?" ]{.class_4258}This is addressed to JUDAS. A prophetic reference to this is found in Psalm 55:13. But how can Judas be connected with the end of the Tribulation and the "guest" of Daniel's Seventieth Week? The problem is too dense to untangle in a brief commen­tary on Matthew, and the solution to the problem is so involved in Daniel's Seventieth Week (as it is related to Satan) that a separate book would have to be written to cover the matter (see [The Mark of the Beast,]{.class_4257} 1969.) For the interested party, the following facts can be learned by comparing Scripture with Scripture.

1\. Judas was a devil, not a human (John 6:70--71).

2\. He went to ["his own place," ]{.class_4258}at death---not Hell (Acts 1).

3\. He was the king of that place (Rev. 9).

4\. His title, as such, was ["the son of perdition"]{.class_4258} (John 17:12).

5\. This title is the title of the coming Antichrist (2 Thess. 2:1--6).

6\. The coming Antichrist is called ["the beast" ]{.class_4258}(Rev. 13:18).

7\. This ["beast"]{.class_4258} is now in the Bottomless Pit (Rev. 17:5--9).

8\. He will go into perdition because he is the ["son of perdition" ]{.class_4258}(Rev. 17).

9\. He will be Satan, incarnate in the flesh (Gen. 3:15).

10\. He has eighteen types in the Old Testament: Haman, Ahab, Cain, Nimrod, et al.

11\. He will be an uninvited guest at the marriage, who will be cast out (see Esth. 4--8).

To tie these facts together would take at least another fifty pages of fine print. We may sum up the evidence by saying that the lost "guest" of Matthew 22:11--14 is either a Tribulation "invitee" who did NOT endure to the end (see comparison in 25:8, 25:30) or that it is Satan himself who will put in an appearance in Heaven [after the Rapture ]{.class_4257}and during the festivities of the marriage of the Lamb. Notice that the doom of the uninvited man without the wedding garment is: ["bound hand and foot"! ]{.class_4257}This will synchronize with Revelation 20:1--3, which occurs at the [end of ]{.class_4257}the Tribulation.

Having located the doctrinal meaning of each verse in the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_430}parable, we may turn to the devotional and practical material contained in the same passage. Notice that the gospel is a "feast" in Revelation. 3:20; Isaiah 55:1--2; and Proverbs 9:1--5. In this sense, men are invited to an eternal banquet in the skies with the fellowship of the saved of all[ ]{.class_4257}ages, the holy angels, the cherubim and seraphim, and the Trinity. Invited to a banquet in the Paradise of God, men perish in a desert of sin and damnation. Willing (as the Israelites) to die under ["the wrath of God"]{.class_4258} (John 3:36) in the familiar surroundings of a sin‑cursed world, they are unwilling to adventure into the spirit realm with the Saviour and receive the One who will make them NEW creatures (2 Cor. 5:17). (In Israel's case, they preferred death by snake bite in the wilderness to entering the Promised Land, which contained the "un­heard of" giants.)

The truth is striking as always. A man goes down the street and says, "We're having a wedding feast at the Statler (or Hilton) Hotel, and you are invited. The meal is free and will consist of baked beans, green beans, butter beans, sweet peas, English peas, black‑eyed peas, turnip greens, collards, rutabagas, 'chittlens,' candied yams, snap beans, crowder peas, field peas, steak, beef, chicken, turkey, rice, potatoes, pork, chicken pilau, chicken gumbo, chili, chicken and dumplin's, chop suey, chow mein, tamales, enchiladas, tacos, fried potatoes, boiled potatoes, spinach, egg plant, okra, iced tea, milk, buttermilk, corn on the cob, coffee, lemonade, kool‑aid, apple cider, honey, pickles, German chocolate cake, ice cream, lemon meringue pie, pecan pie, cookies, and homemade biscuits and bread! Present will be Jayne Mansfield, Hedy Lamar, Artie Shaw, Elvis Presley, Fidel Castro, Walter Reuther, Jimmie Hoffa, Richard Widmark, Burt Lancaster, Elizabeth Taylor, Perry Como, Bing Crosby, Red Skelton, Matt Dillon, Walt Disney, Roy Acuff, President Kennedy, Roy Rogers, Winston Churchill, and Zsa Zsa Gabor.[ Do you want to come?" ]{.class_4257}Well, 98 percent of Americans---Christian, Catholic, Jew, or Atheist---would break their fool necks trying to get in, especially if it were [free. ]{.class_4257}But what happens?

A gospel messenger steps up and says, "There is going to be a marriage in the skies. God has arranged a wedding for His Son. The food will be twelve manner of fruits from the tree of life, bread, honey, and broiled snapper (Luke 24:42, 14:15). Present will be Billy []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_431}Sunday, Fanny Crosby, General Booth, Moses, David, Paul, Whitefield, Torrey, Wesley, Bob Jones, Sam Jones, Joshua, Jeremiah, Frances Havergal, Livingstone, Bunyan, Fox, Isaiah, Peter, James, John, Talmage, Beecher, Huss, Wycliff, Phillip, Thomas, Matthew, Luther, and all the good and just men of every age, redeemed by the blood of the Lamb. It's absolutely FREE. It will last forever. [Who wants to come?" ]{.class_4257}And 98 percent of Americans---Protestant, Catholic, or Jew---would break their fool necks trying to get out of the sound of the voice of the messenger so they could keep on living like they wanted to live! Strange, isn't it?

In 1959, George Gallup conducted one of the most unique polls ever taken in the United States. The question was, ["Who in all history would you enjoy most having into your home for a meal?"]{.class_4257}

Jesus Christ rated eleventh on the list. Do you know the ten that "beat Him out"?

Well, No. 10 was Teddy Roosevelt. No. 9 was the old cigar‑smoking Episcopalian who wrote a history of England and then said to Billy Graham in astonishment, "Do you mean to tell me that God has an [interest ]{.class_4257}in the history of men?" (Winston Churchill) No. 8 was Mrs. Eisenhower. No. 7 was Douglas MacArthur. No. 6 was the old Pro‑Communist integrationist, Mrs. Roosevelt. No. 5 was the old cussing, cocktail‑drinking Baptist, Harry S. Truman. No. 4 was the humanitarian deist, George Washington. No. 3 was "Ike" who captured the town of Little Rock in 1958 and overthrew a population of unarmed civilians without a casualty. No. 2 was FDR, the first advocate of Socialism in the White House, and the No. 1 spot (God bless you!), so help me, was the old bartending politician from Kentucky whose letters to his generals sound like Hitler's correspondence---"Honest Abe Lincoln of Illinois!" (You see, he was not honest, [for he was from Kentucky---not Illinois.)]{.class_4257}

Thus it is seen that the contemporary American is a gullible lackey, who polishes the boots of [Time ]{.class_4257}and [Life ]{.class_4257}magazines, and is literally "carried away" with the standard Associated Press estimate of "great people." [None of the people in the list ]{.class_4257}(12, Hoover; 13, Nixon) ever professed to believe in the word of God as authoritative. None of them professed the new birth publicly. None of them ever led a soul to a saving knowledge of Jesus Christ, and none of them []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_432}ever said anything or did anything to spread the gospel. These are the "great people of the world." But of course, when a man gets to know the Lord Jesus Christ as his own living Saviour, then the heroes of this world become mighty "common place."

Verse 5 is interesting as it shows that the standard alibis for rejecting Christ are the same in A.D. 2000 as they were in A.D. 30. "Business as usual" is the ticket. One goes to the farm, and one to the shopping center. "I haven't got time right now. I've got to make a living," etc. Luke says, ["They all with one consent began to make excuse" ]{.class_4258}(Luke 14:18). Any minister who has made a militant, evangelistic effort to win men and women to Christ knows what this entails:

"There are too many hypocrites in the church."

"Well, I'm just not ready yet."

"I read the Bible, but I don't understand it."

"Well, you've got your religion and I've got mine," etc.

Men alibi ignorance, indolence, indulgence, and indifference, but it's all alibi. Like the man in the fishing boat said to his buddy on a beautiful Sunday morning, "Well, it's all right. I couldna gone to church no how. [My wife is sick today."]{.class_4257}

The man in verses 11--14 is a perfect type of a sinner clothed in the filthy rags of his self-righteousness (Isa. 64:6). He may be said to have walked into the throne­ room clothed in the earthly trappings of baptismal vows, acts of repentances, and BANG! Suddenly the glitter and gold of his moral trappings are gone! The "Robert Hall worsted," with two pairs of pants, becomes a girding of sackcloth and ashes, and there he stands like Cinderella at 12:05, in clothes fit for nothing but a camouflage net.

["Cast him into outer darkness." ]{.class_4258}Inspirationally, the command is the command of Matthew 25:41, ["Depart from me ye cursed," ]{.class_4258}and it should strike terror to the heart of the religious adherent who is counting on his "goodness" to save him. The unsaved man is counselled to put on a [free garment which God bought with His own blood ]{.class_4257}(Rom. 13:14; Acts 20:28; Isa. 61:10). The sinner is never counselled, [in this age, ]{.class_4257}to go manufacture a pair of dungarees or pedal pushers out of his church's junkyard. No one stayed at the feast without a wedding garment, [which was given by the King before the guest entered the hall ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_433}(Matt. 22:10, 12).

["For many are called, but few are chosen." ]{.class_4258}The passage is found again in 20:16 and 19:30. In 19:30 (on which there has already been comment), the wording is "first and last," but there is a definite connection. In 20:16 where He has illustrated what He said in 19:30, He adds, ["For many be called, but few chosen." ]{.class_4258}In 19:30 and 20:16, the context is the calling of the workers into the vineyard, beginning with the apostles and ending at the end of the Tribulation before the Second Coming. The context here in Matthew 22:14 is identical---verses 1--6 would begin with the time of the apostles, verses 7--8 run after their time, and verses 9--10 take you right up through the Tribulation at the time of the "Marriage of the Lamb." In Matthew 19--20, the "many and the few" would refer to many people [called to labor ]{.class_4257}in the Lord's vineyard, but few actually chosen; the reason is apparent. They cannot be chosen for reward till they have worked, and they cannot work till they choose to work themselves. The same holds true in Matthew 22:1--14. Many guests are called, but only those are chosen that have the required ["wedding garment," ]{.class_4258}and no man can get the garment unless [he chooses to take it ]{.class_4257}(see John 6:37, 44).

22:15 Then went the Pharisees, and took counsel how they might entangle him in his talk.

16 And they sent out unto him their disciples with the Herodians, saying, Master, we know that thou art true, and teachest the way of God in truth, neither carest thou for any man: for thou regardest not the person of men.

17 Tell us therefore, What thinkest thou? Is it lawful to give tribute unto Caesar, or not?

18 But Jesus perceived their wickedness, and said, Why tempt ye me, ye hypocrites?

19 Shew me the tribute money. And they brought unto him a penny.

20 And he saith unto them, Whose is this image and superscription?

21 They say unto him, Caesar's. Then saith he unto them, Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_434}Caesar's; and unto God the things that are God's.

22 When they had heard these words, they marvelled, and left him, and went their way.

The time is still Tuesday morning in Jerusalem (see Matt. 21:19--20). There is no warrant whatsoever for inserting an additional day (or [two days ]{.class_4257}as Rome does) between this time and the time of the last supper. Since the last supper is after 6 o'clock Tuesday night, then it is still the day [before ]{.class_4257}the Passover at the time these events take place (Tuesday morning and Tuesday afternoon).

["How they might entangle him in his talk." ]{.class_4258}This seems to be the sole purpose for existing, as far as the Pharisees are concerned. They lived and breathed with but one motive, goal, and desire in mind---to make a fool publicly out of Jesus Christ. There is no indication in the Gospels (up to here) that a Pharisee or a scribe or a priest or a Sadducee ever helped anyone, ever healed anyone, ever performed a miracle, or ever publicly acknowledged the Messiahship of Jesus Christ. They simply hung around in the background, like jackals in the desert waiting for a bone, and consecrated their entire energy and talents to [fighting against the word of God, ]{.class_4257}in hopes that they could install their Talmud (traditions) as the Heavenly Author­ity. Thus today, Rome dedicates her time, converts, money, energy, talents, and institutions to the overthrow of Biblical Christianity, in [hopes of replacing it with Roman Catholicism. ]{.class_4257}Ignatius Loyola and the Knights of Columbus are the past and present masters of the adept art of [talk‑entangling, ]{.class_4257}and of course, their ancestors of this sophistry were the subtle, philosophical speculations of the pagan thinkers---Aristotle, Plato, Socrates, Zeno, Epicurus, Marcus Aurelius, Machiavelli, and Aquinas.

The Pharisees, however, do NOT ["entangle"]{.class_4258} Him in His talk. They get tangled up themselves. (What follows reminds one of a man trying to change a typewriter ribbon blindfolded while trying to get his foot out of a light cord.)

["Master, we know that thou art true, and teachest the way\...."]{.class_4258} Note how similar this boot‑licking sounds to Nicodemus in John 3:1--3, ["Rabbi, we know that thou art a teacher come from God\...."]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_435} The Herodians, who are doing the questioning here (standing in for the Pharisees until vs. 34), do not realize the truth of their flattery. ["What thinkest thou?"]{.class_4258} In plainer words, "Don't give us an infallible truth under God's authority, but give us a subjective opinion that we can match wits with and debunk, so we can still retain [our own opinions!"]{.class_4257}

["Is it lawful to give tribute\...?"]{.class_4258} The question is a cylinder of TNT. (In Luke 20:20, the Holy Spirit has given you a little background of the question before it is asked. It is interesting to notice that the strategem in the question is for the purpose of delivering Jesus ["unto the power and authority of the governor" ]{.class_4258}\[which eventually is done---see Luke 23:1--2\].)

The TNT, then, is this: if Jesus says, "Yes, it is lawful to give tribute to Caesar," then He will break the commandments of God given in the Mosaic Law. In Deuteronomy 17:14--20, the Jew is [forbidden ]{.class_4257}to have any king except a Hebrew king of God's own choosing, and certainly "Caesar" is not A‑1 in this respect. (He was a Bible‑denying, pagan idolater, who burned incense to the Queen of Heaven, and taught that he himself was the vice‑regent of Heaven and the Vicar of God on this earth. This is the Caesar's "doctrine" \[Kaiser, in Germany\].) No Jew could possibly accept him as a [king ]{.class_4257}and obey the law. Contrarywise, if Jesus answers, "No, it is not lawful to give tribute to Caesar," then He is guilty of sedition and rebellion and can be arraigned as a criminal before the civil government. (This is what happens, but they have to lie about His teaching on taxation, for He not only allowed "paying tribute" but paid it [Himself---]{.class_4257}see[ ]{.class_4257}Matt. 17:25--27.)

["Is it lawful to give\...But Jesus perceived their wickedness." ]{.class_4258}It is called ["craftiness"]{.class_4258} in Luke and ["hypocrisy"]{.class_4258} in Mark. ["Why tempt ye me?" ]{.class_4258}In plainer words, Jesus sees immediately that it is a catch, or "incriminating" question, designed []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_436}to snare Him. It is certainly not asked to edify anyone, and it is not asked to get a truthful answer. It is asked to [tangle ]{.class_4257}someone in his talk. It is the old business of putting the man under the oath and asking, "Do you swear by God that you will [quit beating ]{.class_4257}your wife?" Either way, he's going to get involved. (An old colored man said to his shiftless son, "I heard you is married. Is you?" The boy replied, "Ah ain't saying ah [ain't."]{.class_4257} The old man scowled and said, "Ah ain't ask you is [you ain't; ]{.class_4257}ah asked you ain't you is?")

["Shew me the tribute money\...Whose?" ]{.class_4258}And of course, that is the question, ["Whose money is it?" ]{.class_4257}If it is God's money, it shouldn't go to Caesar, and if it is Caesar's money, tribute is all right. Here we run headlong into the classic problem of the Church‑State relationship. If a church can just seize control of the government, they can use public money [to support government projects for THEIR church. ]{.class_4257}Thus \$18,000,000 of Baptist, Methodist, and Presbyterian money (tax money) was assigned to rebuilding Catholic cathedrals in the Philippines after World War II, by an act of Congress. ["Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's\...unto God the things that are God's." ]{.class_4258}He does not answer the question! (Although the question is not answered, the answer is so strongly implied that further discussion is useless.)

The implication is that ["tribute money"]{.class_4258} is Caesar's (see Rom. 13:1--6). Caesar cannot be king unless he is [wanted. ]{.class_4257}What is to be observed on the face of it is that the Jew [would rather be under the Roman rule than under heavenly rule! ]{.class_4257}(See their own confession and declaration in Matt. 27:25; John 19:15.) Since the tribute money is Caesar's, it is LAWFUL to give it to Caesar. This does NOT break the Levitical and Deuteroriomic law, as these laws are for a nation in the will of God, under Theocratic rule (see 1 Sam. 8:7). Israel in A.D. 30 is out of the will of God, under Roman rule; by their own profession, they prefer this rule to the rule of their Creator and Redeemer. Therefore, it follows, as cars follow roads, that taxes are the standard order of procedure.

Matthew 22:21 sums up all doubtful cases (like the ones confronted by the midwives in Exodus 1--2), and it settles forever the arguments about military conscription, police forces, oaths in a court of law, and taxation. Since it does settle these problems, Rome is determined and resolved to seize control of every government on the face of the earth so its tax money may be embezzled for the "propagation of the faith." [This is church dogma defined by the Pope. ]{.class_4257}(He does use different terminology, however, preferring ["guidance" ]{.class_4257}to "control" and the word ["donation or contribution" for ]{.class_4257}embezzlement. ["They marvelled, and left him, and went their way." ]{.class_4258}The Herodians are through. 27 And last of all the woman died also.

In Lysias' [Oration against Eratosthenes,]{.class_4257} p. 125, the word is translated ["certain proofs." ]{.class_4257}Aristotle calls it ["demonstrative proofs," ]{.class_4257}the word being ["texmarion" ]{.class_4257}(not ["sameion"]{.class_4257}),[ ]{.class_4257}and it is a ["sure token."]{.class_4257} Plato translates the word ["convincing proof." Never does any Greek scholar, in the native Greek, ever translate it the way the RSV and ASV handle it. Since both the translations are Sadducee productions, neither is able to translate Greek correctly in passages that deal with the resurrection.]{.class_4257}

The Bible (in both Testaments) teaches a literal, physical, bodily resurrection of saved and lost alike (the quick and the dead), and only a conceited sophist would insinuate that there is any leeway on interpretation (see Ezek. 37; Acts 4:2, 17:31, 24:15; 1 Cor. 15; Rev 20; John 5:29).

["Master, Moses said\...."]{.class_4258} What follows is some more "third degree" under the guise of excruciating philosophical questioning. And other childish questions that anyone with common sense could solve in the time it would take to blow his nose.

The problem here is, briefly, if everyone comes up bodily in the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_440}resurrection (and they certainly will!), then what are you going to do with a woman who pops up with seven husbands? ["For they all had her" ]{.class_4258}(vs. 28). Jesus answers immediately, without the slightest hesitation, and His answer will serve as a pattern for any Bible believer to follow. ["Ye do err,"]{.class_4258} or as in Mark---["Ye therefore do GREATLY err."]{.class_4258}

Notice that the error is in not knowing things definite about [life‑after‑death. ]{.class_4257}Nowhere is the Liberal or Catholic more gravely in error than on these points, and nowhere does he damn more souls with his stupid theorizing than on these points. Unable to [believe ]{.class_4257}what God has said, they [invent ]{.class_4257}a "life-after-death-situation" and teach it as infallible truth. (Purgatory is only one example.) ["But are as the angels of God IN HEAVEN." ]{.class_4258}This statement will be the key to understanding the passage. The Sadducee cannot be answered with natural reasoning. This is good []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_442}Catholic and Rabbinic tradition---where the angels are sexless, winged beings with [hair like women and faces like women and bodies like men. ]{.class_4257}Unfortunately, it is not connected with Biblical Christianity in any weight, size, or sex. It is apparent to a poor reader that every angel in the Bible from "generation to resolution" (as the colored preacher said) is a young man---male, [not sexless. ]{.class_4257}(Study this carefully until you are absolutely certain you know what an angel is, what it looks like, what its function is, how it appears, and how it speaks---Gen. 18:1--2, 22, 33, 19:1, 5, 10, 15--16; Judg. 13:3, 6, 8--11, 13, 19; Heb. 13:1--2; Gal. 4:14; Rev. 21:17; Luke 24:4, 23.) All angels in either testament are MALE. "Ye therefore do greatly err\...not knowing the scriptures."

In one crushing blow the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 Bible puts to rout the armies of the unholy papa (who put "wings" on his angels) and the armies of the Fundamentalists (who teach angels can't marry because they are "sexless") and the armies of standard Reformed‑Orthodox theology (who teach that in the resurrection there "is no need to reproduce"). Not a single one of these pagan fantasies is anymore adequate for correct interpretation than those invented by the Sadducees themselves.

The reason they neither marry nor are "given in marriage" is because they are all [male, ]{.class_4257}not because they are ["sexless." ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_443}This puts the entire subject of the first resurrection in a different light, for it would teach that in the eternal state there are [no females! (As ]{.class_4257}a jokester put it, "That's why they call it 'heaven'!") Now, without adopting the Sadducee's position (which is an effort to compensate for unbelief) or the Pharisee's position (the Roman position, attempting to "have faith" in something that isn't so), let us examine the Bible teaching on the subject.

1\. The woman is from the man (Gen. 2).

2\. The man (Ish---Heb.) is a "He‑man"; the woman (Isha) is a "she‑man," contraction of the word: womb‑man---wombman, woman.

3\. The woman is the man's glory and his flesh (1 Cor. 11).

4\. Every saved woman has a MAN inside her (1 Tim. 2:5; Col. 1).

5\. At the resurrection, this MAN conforms the person, in whom He dwells, into His own image and bodily shape and form (Phil. 3:20--21, 1 John 3:1--3).

6\. This bodily shape and form is a 33‑year‑old MALE.

7\. It is the "angel of the Lord" (Gal. 3).

8\. It is "as the angels of God in heaven" (text).

This radical, revolutionary, Bible picture of life‑after-death cuts across the grain of Psychology, Psychiatry, Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Communism, Atheism, Evolution, Catholicism, Liberalism, and Orthodoxy. It proves again that the Bible is still the most unorthodox book in print, and by comparison, Huxley, Marx, Russell, Menninger, James Baldwin, Darwin, and William James are Mid‑Victorian prudes.

At the first resurrection the ["children of the resurrec­tion"]{.class_4258} become ["as the angels of God." ]{.class_4258}Notice that it is the angels of God ["in heaven"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 22:30). This important part of the verse is ignored in the Scofield note, at the bottom of the page, which deals with the ["sons of God"]{.class_4258} in Genesis 6. Scofield has well alibied his error with, "The uniform Hebrew and Christian interpretation has been\...." But this apology is unnecessary and untimely in the attempt to prove the ["sons of God"]{.class_4258} were Sethites. The[ "sons of God"]{.class_4258} in Genesis 6 are in [the image of Adam ]{.class_4257}(Gen. 5:1--4) if they are Sethites. They are not ["sons of God"]{.class_4258} as John 1:12, for these ["sons of God"]{.class_4258} are renewed in the image that Adam lost (Col. 3:10), []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_444}[and this Image is Christ ]{.class_4257}(2 Cor. 4:4)! They are NOT [Sethites ]{.class_4257}because Seth never had a "godly line"; all he had was a Messianic line (there is a harlot, an adulteress, and a concubine in Joseph's genealo­gy in Matt. 1). The sons of God are not human, for they reproduce [giants. ]{.class_4257}They are not Cainites, for their offspring [survive the flood ]{.class_4257}(see Gen. 6:4---["after that"]{.class_4258}) and are found in Deuteronomy 2 and 1 Samuel 17, while all the Cainites were drowned. And if that were not enough, the teaching that the ["sons of God"]{.class_4258} were Sethites entirely [spiritualizes ]{.class_4257}(for sermonic purposes) a doctrinal passage that deals with [male angels who left their first estate and brought forth perverted seed, of which Judas Iscariot is a prime representative---see ]{.class_4257}John 6:70--71. But this is "too far out." Let us return to the simple facts involved.

1\. At the resurrection, the believers become like the elect angels who are IN heaven (1 Tim. 5:21).

2\. These angels, like the Angel of the Lord, are male (Rev. 21:17).

3\. The angel of the Lord appears as a 33‑year‑old male at His resurrection, which is a type of ours (Gal. 4:14; Rom. 8:11; Phil. 3:20--21).

4\. As such He has a flesh and bone body with no blood (Luke 24:37--39; 1 Cor. 15:50).

5\. His promise is that this is the body we will receive at the "coming in the air" (1 John 3:1--3; 1 Thess. 4:11--18).

Thus, at the resurrection there are no "families." There are only millions of exact reproductions of Christ's resurrection body, and all are 33‑year‑old, sinless males, made after His image, able to eat food, become visible or invisible at will, and travel in outer space at a speed of about 150,000,000 miles per second. [(What new doctrine is this!?!)]{.class_4257}

There is a great deal more involved in these truths, but time and space forbid further exposition. (See the comments on Rev. 21:23--26 for the complete teaching of all that this involves: Commentary on Revela­tion, 1998.)

"Ye therefore do greatly err."

"Ye therefore do GREATLY err."

Nestle's Greek text (erring with the Sadducees) rejects the part of verse 30 that interprets it, preferring manuscript "B" (Vaticanus!) to the majority of manuscripts.

["But as touching the resurrection of the dead."]{.class_4258} Notice how Jesus changes the wording so that He is no longer discussing the resurrection of the ["children of the resurrection"]{.class_4258} (Luke 20:36), the ones who rise at the first resurrection from ["the children of this world"]{.class_4258} (Luke 20:34). The question the Sadducees asked shows further ignorance then, in that when they asked it, they not only presumed that the sexual distinctions were retained in heaven as the earthly relationships, but they also made a much greater assumption---they [assumed that all the dead RISE! ]{.class_4257}This is another gross error. ["Ye therefore do greatly err."]{.class_4258}

[The dead do not rise ]{.class_4257}(Mark 12:23)!

["In THE resurrection therefore, when they shall rise\...."]{.class_4258} The Sadducee (as the much cumbered Martha, Luke 10:40) had failed to sit at the feet of Jesus and learn about the two resurrections, which are separated by 1,000 years (see notes on Matt 17:9). They assumed (as do all dead orthodox and Catholic commentators) that if [there is a resurrection---they ]{.class_4257}denied this themselves but presented it in their hypothetical case---it will be one general resurrection and "last judgment" of saved and lost. They assume that all will [rise. ]{.class_4257}Notice the choice of the word carefully. ["The dead are raised"]{.class_4258}[ ]{.class_4257}(Luke 20:37), but ["rising" is ]{.class_4257}a different matter. The saints [rise ]{.class_4257}in 1 Corinthi­ans 15:49--55 and 1 Thessalonians 4:11--18, but this [rising, ]{.class_4257}which we call "the rapture," occurs 1,000 years before any [dead are raised ]{.class_4257}(Rev. 20:5).

So that there can be no doubt about this interpretation, Jesus drives home the statement that ["God is not the God of the dead, but of the living" ]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_446}(notice how He has to use this, or its equivalent, on the amillennial Martha in John 11:21--25).

["Have ye not read that which was spoken unto you by God, saying," ]{.class_4258}and then Jesus quotes Moses! (Matt. 22:31--32). This is adding frustration to aggravation and irritation, for the plotters who came to trip him began with, ["Master, Moses said\..."]{.class_4258} (Matt. 22:24). What Jesus is saying is, "You go around quoting Moses, and you haven't read enough of Moses even to know what you're talking about" (John 5:45--47).

Since God is the God of the living, He is the God of those who shall ["rise from the dead" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 12:25); He is NOT the God of those who remain dead till the thousand years are finished and are RAISED at the second resurrec­tion. This second group is called ["the dead," ]{.class_4258}and they are not only [dead physically, ]{.class_4257}but [dead spiritually ]{.class_4257}(Luke 9:60, Eph. 2:1--4). They are not ["quickened" ]{.class_4258}spiritually (Eph. 2:1--4) and, hence, are not [rising ]{.class_4257}at the resurrection of the ["quick" ]{.class_4258}(see 2 Tim. 4:1; 1 Pet. 4:5). The Sadducee, then, and the Liberal and the Catholic and the Reformed all occupy the same capsule. [They all reject, at sight, the revelation of Jesus Christ ]{.class_4257}(Rev. 1:1)[ as a reliable source of information about life‑after‑death. ]{.class_4257}They are no more Scriptural in their attitude about resurrection and eternal life after the resurrection than Karl Marx or Bob Ingersoll.

["Have ye not read?" ]{.class_4258}(Matt. 22:31). No, ["ye"]{.class_4258} haven't!

["I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? God is not the God of the dead, but of the living" ]{.class_4258}(Matt. 22:32).

Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob were, of course, the patriarchal ancestors of the Sadducees, and it might be added "the highly‑honored ancestors" (John 8:33, 39). If these highly‑honored do not ["rise from the dead," ]{.class_4258}then God has [lied in ]{.class_4257}His promises to them, for look at Genesis 13:15, 26:3, 28:13. [They did not get these promises in their earthly life ]{.class_4257}(Heb. 11:13). Here is brought home a tremendous premillennial truth. It is that there has to be a resurrection before the last judgment, [and this resurrection has to be ]{.class_4257}["from the dead"]{.class_4258}[ ]{.class_4257}or God's oath to the fathers was a false oath.

The promises to the fathers were literal, physical, earthly promises, and ["if the dead rise not" ]{.class_4258}(or to be more accurate: if the God of the living does not [raise them from the dead, ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_447}Mark 12:25, 9:10), then God misled them and lied to them. Thus the amillennial and postmillennial expositors are both revealed in their true light. They will not only [steal ]{.class_4257}the literal promises given to Israel for themselves (Acts 3:21--26), but they will duck out of calling God a liar by saying that He [didn't really mean what He said, literally, to the Jews. ]{.class_4257}Paul has something to say about this egotistical brand of expositors in Romans 11:25--27. (We are back facing the millennial issue. As a matter of fact, there is no way to escape it in dealing with correct Bible exegesis. The stand a man takes on the Second Coming of Christ \[as revealed in the Revelation of Jesus Christ, Rev. 1:1, will determine his method of handling [every verse in the gospel according to Matthew.)]{.class_4257}

["They which shall be accounted worthy to obtain that world, and the resurrection FROM THE DEAD" ]{.class_4258}(companion passage in Luke 20:35) are a separate group. They are called the ["just,"]{.class_4258} the ["quick,"]{.class_4258} the ["righteous,"]{.class_4258} and the ["children of the resurrection."]{.class_4258} They RISE 1,000 years before the White Throne Judgment of Revelation 20.

The remainder are called ["the unjust,"]{.class_4258} the ["dead,"]{.class_4258} and ["the wicked,"]{.class_4258} and they DO NOT RISE. They are RAISED to face condemnation (see Dan. 12:2; Luke 14:14; 1 Pet. 4:5; Rev. 20:5). These two groups are so clearly marked and so clearly divided that 1,000 years separates their place and time of judgment. No man who ever "studied to show himself approved unto God" ever failed to "RIGHTLY DIVIDE" at this point. But Augustine, Origen, Eusebius, Jerome, Irenaeus, Calvin, Berkhof, Hodge, Strong, and the popes fail to attain these rudiments of freshman Bible study. All teach a general resurrection and a general judgment, thus contradicting the teaching of Jesus Christ, and demonstrating conclusively that they are brethren of the Sadducees. They too "do greatly err, not knowing the Scripture!"

["And when the multitude heard this, they were astonished at his doctrine." ]{.class_4258}One can easily understand why they were. The multitude of A.D. 33, as the multitude of 1962, was a group of Bible ignoramuses, and from their blind beggars to their long‑robed priests, they were all in the dark. Like the fanatical []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_448}Catholic newspaper reporters at the ecumenical council of Pope John (1962), who knelt and slobbered and wept when the old wine‑head "blessed them," the multitude knew nothing about Bible doctrine except the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees---rabbinical tradition.

22:34 But when the Pharisees had heard that he had put the Sadducees to silence, they were gathered together.

35 Then one of them, which was a lawyer, asked him a question, tempting him, and saying,

36 Master, which is the great commandment in the law?

37 Jesus said unto him, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind.

38 This is the first and great commandment.

39 And the second is like unto it, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

40 On these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets.

Mark prepares the reader for the scene by adding that ["one of the scribes came, and having heard them reasoning together, and perceiving that he had answered them well, asked him\...."]{.class_4258} The last of the "big four" shows up. Jesus has shut the Herodians up on the Tribute question. He has shut up the Sadducees on the Resurrection question. He shut up the Pharisees (who were priests) in Matthew 21:45 on the Baptism question, and now a scribe, who is a LAWYER, sallies forth. However, the reader should notice that the lawyer has a double motive. He is not only "sicked on" to the Lord Jesus, but he has some semblance of honesty about him for we read, ["having heard\...and perceiving that he had answered them well." ]{.class_4258}This man, with an ear for legal logic, is not altogether a hypocrite. Secretly he [admires any man who can stand trial and answer the way Jesus has been doing.]{.class_4257}

But still the[ "tempting him"]{.class_4258} shows that the motive is double. He admires Jesus, but he still wants to test His mettle.

["Which is the great commandment?" ]{.class_4258}Jesus answers with the first commandment, but the answer is not from Exodus 20:1--3; []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_449}it is from Deuteronomy 6:5. Then He goes back to Leviticus 19:18 to pick up the second commandment. For no apparent reason, the Lord Jesus [wholly ignores ]{.class_4257}the Decalogue given in Exodus 20, in what we commonly know as the ["Ten Commandments."]{.class_4257} Mark gives a little light (Mark 12:29) by adding the ["Hear, O Israel"]{.class_4258} before the commandment from Deuteronomy 6:5 is quoted. The two commandments, then, encompass not only the general moral law given on the tables of stone and the tables of the heart (Rom. 2:14--15), but the specific instructions given to Israel as a nation under the priest­hood from Levi. The question is designed to call the crowd's attention to its own importance as a nation, and Jesus answers so that every man listening will be struck by the Jewish choice of the quotation, ["Hear, O Israel!"]{.class_4258} (Deut. 6:4--5).

For the second commandment, Jesus goes into the Levitical law governing conduct of Jew toward Jew. The reader will not fail to observe that whereas the Jew was told to love his ["neighbour"]{.class_4258} in the Old Testament, [he was told to exterminate the neighborhood into which he moved ]{.class_4257}(Exod. 23:26--33, 34:10--17; Num. 21:34--35). Modernists and Catholics both have trouble with this. The modernist makes "love your neighbor" a means of political coercion, to place paid litigators in areas where they are not wanted and were not led to go. (Having made the gospel of the grace of God the social gospel of [man's love for man, ]{.class_4257}he is appalled by the orders of God in 1 Samuel 15 to wipe out a populace, including the children. While he is worrying about it, the students trained in this school are wiping out cities and counties with atom bombs and M‑16's in the name of ["liberty for all" ]{.class_4257}and the ["struggle of the free world,"]{.class_4257} etc.)

The Catholic, staying with the legalistic sacra­mentalism of Old Testament ritual and never appropri­ating the FINISHED work of Christ at Calvary, is not averse from [starting wars ]{.class_4257}(by political intrigue or directly) "in the name of God," for the glory of "holy mother church." Such expeditions (like the extermination of Protestant cities in the Dark Ages) are justified on the basis of God's dealing with Israel in the Old Testament. In plainer words the Catholic is a self‑righteous Old Testament Jew who has only accepted those portions of the New Testament which can be used to add authority to his church [as a political body.]{.class_4257}

["Hang all the law and the prophets" ]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_450}shows that the entire moral teaching of Genesis-Deuteronomy can be summed up in two commandments. Paul says the same thing in Romans 13:10--13, showing that the first two commandments are eternal and binding on Christian or Hebrew alike. The ["neighbour"]{.class_4258} in the New Testament (Rom. 13:10--13) is not a racial distinction as in Leviticus 19:18. Still, caution must be exercised, or you will [find yourself submitting to a government that takes what you have and gives it to your neighbor till both of you are even. ]{.class_4257}This is where the Modernist is heading---right down the alley with Karl Marx. Going to Acts 2--3, the Liberal takes the ["equal distribution" ]{.class_4257}of property among Bible-believing Christians (giving on a [voluntary ]{.class_4257}basis) and transfers it to an authoritative hierarchy which forces [equal distribution ]{.class_4257}by practical atheists [on a compulsory basis. ]{.class_4257}This is no more "Christian" doctrine than Bingo parties or Sweepstake lotteries.

The reader must fix the truth clearly in mind. The FIRST commandment is GODWARD. The SECOND com­mandment is MANWARD. This is the eternal, fixed, and infallible order as ordained by the Trinity. As Bob Jones so aptly observed, "Every bad thing in this world is a good thing twisted." Nor can the Liberal or the Modernist be made to see or accept this rudimentary truth of a simple arithmetic: [two follows one. ]{.class_4257}One is [before ]{.class_4257}two. Unwittingly, intentionally, accidentally, or deliberately, the Modernist tunes his pitchpipe to ["man's inhumanity to man,"]{.class_4257} the "good Samaritan," and "the Ancient Mariner who loveth man and beast," etc. Then under cover of a barrage of words such as "tension," "tolerance," "integration," "cooperation," "conflict," "dynamics," "re-examination," and "the Lord's prayer," he proceeds to tear out the heart of the blood atonement, the soul of New Testament salvation, and the very Deity of God, who became flesh to suffer and die in his place (2 Pet. 2:1).

Mary Baker Eddy showed her adeptness at this business when she reversed 1 John 4:8 to read ["love is God," ]{.class_4257}instead of ["God is love." ]{.class_4258}From there on, Hollywood and [Life ]{.class_4257}and [Time ]{.class_4257}magazine (with the aid of Peale, Fosdick, Spellman, Kagawa, Weatherhead, and Allen) took up the chorus and sang it in every key and every staff on the sheet. When Jesus said, ["The second is like unto it," ]{.class_4258}He was not saying ["the second is as great as the first."]{.class_4257} He had just said []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_451}["the FIRST AND GREAT commandment" ]{.class_4258}was to love God with all your heart, mind, and soul (Mark adds ["strength"]{.class_4258}). The Communist, the Liberal, the Modernist, the Socialist, and the Humanist all ignore the simple wording and read, "The greatest thing you can do is love your fellowman and do unto him as you would have him do unto you." Unfortunately this is all you need to do to go to Hell [with him---]{.class_4257}see Romans 10:1--8; Ephesians 2:1--3; John 3:17--21. (See notes also on Matt. 7:12.) Do you understand what the trouble is? It is really very simple to analyze. If a man loves God, then [he must love Jesus Christ and love the revealed word of God in the Scripture ]{.class_4257}(Matt. 10:37; John 8:42--47, 17:17). Now we have the "Negro in the wood pile!" The Catholic and the Communist and the Liberal have only one dream---[to unite the entire world of mankind under their system and dominion.]{.class_4257} To do this you will have to [eliminate the requirement ]{.class_4257}that all "love Jesus and the word of God," for millions [do not even BELIEVE either, ]{.class_4257}let alone ["love them." So ]{.class_4257}to get out of this awkward position an expedient is resorted to: your attention is focused and riveted on the [second commandment, ]{.class_4257}and then the Tribulation passages of Matthew 25:40 and James 2:15--17 are used to convince you [that obedience to the SECOND IS obedience to the FIRST!]{.class_4257}

Thus[ "the blind lead the blind"]{.class_4258} till both fall in the ditch. Fallen man, made in Adam's depraved image, is exalted to the image of God and is then worshipped or served [instead of God---]{.class_4257}while the worshipper uses God's own word to prove that he is worshipping [correctly! ]{.class_4257}Romans 1 is a dissertation on this peculiar form of perversion, which is orthodox Liberalism.

Where Matthew closes the account of this dialogue between Christ and the scribe‑lawyer, Mark adds, ["And the scribe said unto him, Well, Master, thou hast said the truth: for there is one God; and there is none other but he: And to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, and with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love his neighbour as himself, is more than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices. And when Jesus saw that he answered discreetly, he said unto him, Thou art not far []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_452}from the kingdom of God. And no man after that durst ask him any question" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 12:32--34).

The word ["understanding" ]{.class_4258}is found in Mark 12:33 as the Bible's interpretation of the word[ "mind" ]{.class_4258}in verse 30. Jesus' reply in 12:34 shows again that the lawyer manifested a good deal more sincerity than his fellow travelers. ["Thou art not far from the kingdom of God," ]{.class_4258}points out again the MORAL quality that Israel must attain before the literal Kingdom of Heaven comes to earth in answer to the ["thy kingdom come." ]{.class_4258}(Again, the amillennial and postmillennial groups are where we usually find them---stuck in theological mud, hubcap high to a ferris wheel.)

22:41 While the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them,

42 Saying, What think ye of Christ? whose son is he? They say unto him, The son of David.

43 He saith unto them, How then doth David in spirit call him Lord, saying,

44 The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool?

45 If David then call him Lord, how is he his son?

46 And no man was able to answer him a word, neither durst any man from that day forth ask him any more questions.

["Gathered together," ]{.class_4258}goes back to verse 34, and as the text is worded, it appears that Jesus [walked over to the ]{.class_4257}group and broke into their midst with this question, after He had finished with the scribe‑lawyer.

["What think ye of Christ? whose son is he?" ]{.class_4258}The question is crucial. It is of such importance that every doctrine in both Testaments hangs on the answer. It is of such importance that religious wars, religious persecutions, minority rights, civil rights, world wars, denominational preferences, and Heaven or Hell in eternity depend on the answer. One might say the question is of such importance that really nothing on the United Nations agenda or before both Houses of Congress could ever be properly compared with it.

["Whose son is he?" ]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_453}The son of the carpenter? The son of Joseph? The son of a German soldier? "A" son of God? ["Whose son is he?" ]{.class_4258}If He is Joseph's son, then He rotted in the tomb, His flesh saw corruption, and He most certainly [did not go anywhere ]{.class_4257}after they buried Him. What would give a man the unbelievable license to think that Christ was in any way out of the ordinary if He [were Joseph's son? ]{.class_4257}Every son of Adam died and did NOT come up, and if he came up (Jonah, the Shunamites' son, the man buried in Elisha's tomb, Lazarus, et al.), he only came up[ temporarily ]{.class_4257}and death claimed him again later. If Christ were Joseph's son, [He did not come ]{.class_4257}up---and if He did, He went back down! But Romans 6:9 rebukes this foolish thinking.

Philip says, ["Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph"]{.class_4258} (John 1:45). But the fact that he cites the Law and the Prophets shows that not for a moment was he ever considering an obscure carpenter from Bethlehem. This ["Joseph"]{.class_4258} is the Joseph of Genesis 49:22--24; Zechariah 12:10.

His enemies say He is the son of Joseph (Luke 2:33). The [ASV ]{.class_4262}says He was the son of Joseph (Luke 2:33). ["What think YE of Christ? whose son is he?"]{.class_4258}

["They say unto him, the son of David" ]{.class_4258}(Matt. 22:42). So far, so good. They are correct (see Matt. 1:1). But once they have committed themselves to this confession, they lay themselves wide open for what follows---a haymaker follows.

"How then doth David in spirit call him Lord\...?"

If He is the ["son of David," ]{.class_4258}He cannot be an actual [physical son ]{.class_4257}of David, for David had no physical sons named "Jesus." He has to be an indirect descendant from the Davidic line, as Abraham's line (Matt. 1:1).[ But! ]{.class_4257}In Psalm 100, David, speaking under the inspiration of the Holy Spirit (Mark 12:36), says that his own son is ["Lord"! ]{.class_4258}(This is also what the scribes teach, for see Mark 12:35.) How can a man call his own son ["Lord"]{.class_4258}? For an Oriental, this is an impossible situation; it is so impossible that ["no man was able to answer Him a word"]{.class_4258}[ ]{.class_4257}(Matt. 22:46). Once a man admits that David's Son is David's Lord (and he must admit this or say that he refuses to believe the Old Testament---Psa. 110), then he is forced to admit further that David's Lord must be David's God [come down to earth, in the flesh, as one of David's descendants! ]{.class_4257}This [(being the truth)]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2541.xhtml_page_454} is rejected by the scribes and Pharisees, but it is too late to back out gracefully. They just stand thunderstruck and stroke their beards like they were feeling them for the first time. ["Neither durst any man from that day forth ask him any more questions" ]{.class_4258}(Matt. 22:46).

["The Lord said unto my Lord, sit thou on my right hand\...."]{.class_4258} The citation is Psalm 110, running seven verses, and each of the seven verses is a direct reference to the Second Coming of Christ. The verse is found badly mutilated in the [RSV]{.class_4257}, where the ["thou"]{.class_4258} of Deity has been preserved in the Old Testament and then the "you" of humanity inserted in the New Testament as to [force the passage ]{.class_4257}to misapply. (This is one consistent feature of the [RSV---]{.class_4257}its translators work from the predetermined position that the Bible is "full of contradictions," so they [manufacture them as they go, ]{.class_4257}to prove their point. Nice folks!)

In Psalm 110, verse 2 is the Millennial reign (notice enemies present as in Isa. 26 and Rev. 20). Verse 3 dates the ["MORNING]{.class_4258}" as the correct time of the coming (see Matt. 13:43 and notes, Matt. 17:16 and notes). Verse 4 in Psalm 110 fits Tribulation passages in Hebrews. Verse 5 speaks of the destruction of the ten kings of Revelation 17:8--13 and Revelation 19. Verse 6 speaks of the BRUISING OF THE HEAD (yet future) of Satan's seed, mentioned also in Habakkuk 3:13; Genesis 3:15; and Romans 16:20.

[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml}

# Chapter 23 {.heading_84-0}

23:1 Then spake Jesus to the multitude, and to his disciples,

2 Saying, The scribes and the Pharisees sit in Moses' seat:

3 All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do; but do not ye after their works: for they say, and do not.

4 For they bind heavy burdens and grievous to be borne, and lay them on men's shoulders; but they themselves will not move them with one of their fingers.

5 But all their works they do for to be seen of men: they make broad their phylacteries, and enlarge the borders of their garments,

6 And love the uppermost rooms at feasts, and the chief seats in the synagogues,

7 And greetings in the markets, and to be called of men, Rabbi, Rabbi.

["Sit in Moses' seat."]{.class_4258} The phrase is indicative of what has taken place since Moses no longer "sat all day to judge the people" (Exod. 18:13--14). The long-robed "fathers" (see Matt. 23:9) had usurped the legislative powers given to the prophet and deliverer of Israel and had seated themselves in the place of power, to give a variety of "ex cathedra" (out-of-the-seat!) utterances. These Talmudic commentaries and interpretations on the Scripture were superimposed (as they are today) to obscure the fulfillment of prophecies that dealt with the coming Messiah. \[A short conversation with any educated rabbi in the 1960's will bring this truth into clear view. This "ex cathedra" business of course is better known to the American and European as the "infallible decrees" and "papal bulls" of various wineheads in Rome who usurp the authority of the Holy Spirit in pretending to be the true "pillar and ground of truth." Giving credit where credit is due, the Catholic might be interested to know that there have been dozens of church fights and schisms and splits in the papacy: 241 priests in England (in 1870) []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_456}denied the council's doctrine on papal utterances; eight archbishops and thirty bishops in France said it was absolutely out of reason, and forty-five German and Austrian bishops and five archbishops said the same. Seven ITALIAN bishops turned thumbs down on it (!), as well as a cardinal from Vienna, Austria (Peterson, [Rise and Fall of the RCC,]{.class_4257} pp. 65, 33, 76, 87, and 257).\]

We should always keep clearly in mind that Rome has never had a consistent creed or doctrine and has never had a Biblical doctrine of salvation or prophecy. Constantly changing, she is never the same, and only the skeleton outline of the catechism remains the same, on paper. This, too, changes remarkably (in practice) from year to year. As Rome's main propaganda periodical, [Life ]{.class_4257}magazine, has so aptly stated, "The council has vast powers to bring about, with papal approval, sweeping revisions of both church laws and administration" (Life, Oct. 12, 1962). This is good Catholic "doctrine." That is, at any time, under any political expedient, it can always change anything it wants to change anytime it wants to change it, irregardless of what the Bible may have to say about it. ("Ex cathedra" merely means that if it is desirable to correct, revise, alter, change, or cancel the word of God, it can be done by pretending that someone is authorized by the word of God to do it! Some more nice folks.)

["All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe\...."]{.class_4258} At this point Bullinger has come forth with an exegesis which may allow some examination. He reads, ["Therefore, whatsoever they bid you observe, that you observe and do; but do not\...!"]{.class_4258} Bullinger deduces that since what follows (vss. 4--31) clearly warns them not to do what the Pharisees do, that Jesus could not be telling them in verse 3 to do them. The reading given is that He is stating a fact, that is: "You have been having the habit of doing whatever they have been telling you to observe and do, but I say unto you, quit doing it." The reading is tempting to follow, but before the student corrects the [AV]{.class_4262}, it will do well to notice that the ["observe" ]{.class_4258}and ["do"]{.class_4258} of verse 3 are both second person plural imperatives. He is saying, "Do ye" in the sense of a command, and not a fact that "you are doing." Hence, the [AV ]{.class_4262}is still correct, and we must paraphrase, "Whatever they tell you to do that Moses commanded from Moses' []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_457}seat, that do. But do not do what they themselves are doing, for they are not following Moses; they are following the Babylonian Talmud." ["They say, and do not."]{.class_4258}

["Bind heavy burdens."]{.class_4258} The reference will fit the self-righteous legalists of Matthew 11:28, and it is not hard to find the nature of the burdens, when one considers the intricate mesh of traditions and customs superimposed over the Holy Spirit by the Talmudists. ["Make broad their phylacteries."]{.class_4258} The word in the Old Testament is "Totaphoth," found in Exodus 13:16, and Deuteronomy 6:8 and 11:18. It is translated ["frontlets"]{.class_4258} or "memorial." The phylactery was a leather box, cube-shaped, closed with an attached flap, and bound to the person's wrist by a leather band. Verses of Scripture were enclosed in the boxes. The Talmud (equivalent to the Catholic "Missal") tries its best to trace the use of these head-and-arm-bands back to primitive (even Mosaic) times; unfortunately no Jew ever wore one until after the Babylonian captivity.

["And enlarge the borders of their garments."]{.class_4258} (Mark adds, ["which love to go in long clothing"]{.class_4258} \[Mark 12:38\], and Luke comes right out on the seven mountains of Rome and cries, ["desire to walk in LONG ROBES."]{.class_4258}) We must remember that both Jesus and Paul wore the common, ordinary, everyday clothing of their day and time. Neither one was worried about running around in a Mardi Gras costume to show people an "insignia" of their "consecration." Their faces were their credentials (2 Cor. 3:6, 4:2). ["The borders of their garments."]{.class_4258} The required "borders" are in Numbers 15:37--41 and Deuteronomy 22:12. After the Babylonian captivity, the borders and fringes were not only enlarged, but were traditionalized so that there were symbolic meanings given to the numbers of windings and knots. The long-robed Pharisees, like debutantes, were beginning to vie with each other in decoration. One cannot appreciate the meaning of this vanity unless one has observed the high shenanigans at the Vatican, where one can see everything and anything except anyone who looks or acts like Jesus Christ, Peter, James, John, or Paul. All of these wore the common, ordinary, every-day clothing of their day and time, and any one of them would have read a modern pope like a book at one glance. From head to toe the garments spell one thing: a proud, vain, self-sufficient, self-[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_458}inflated, ambitious charlatan. Only religious prejudice could blind the eye to miss it. Pope John XXIII was not Pope John. He was Angelo Giuseppe Rancalli---Pizza and pepperoni. The English names (as the French and Spanish) are adopted to fit the contemporary world power. If Russia, for example, wins the next war---the next pope will be Pope Ivan I, his real name being Giovanni Antonellio!

["And love the uppermost rooms\...chief seats in the synagogues\...greetings in the markets."]{.class_4258} It would not take a genius to discern this group. Yet the pity of it is there are over 2,000,000 Jews, over 40,000,000 Catholics, and over 60,000,000 Protestants in the world, who are so ignorant of the English Bible that they do not even connect the verses with anything going on in the twentieth century. Like their superstitious fathers who followed the "tradition of the elders" (1 Pet. 1:18), they limit the passage in Matthew 23 to apply only historically to the dead events of the forgotten past. This entirely overlooks the tremendous truth of the Bible---i.e. that the word of God ["liveth and abideth for ever,"]{.class_4258} and that it is ["for ever\...settled in heaven."]{.class_4258} The God who knows all from beginning to end (Acts 15:18) has pre-\
arranged a textbook to adequately meet every problem, doctrine, argument, puzzle, question, emergency, and misunderstanding in every century.

["The uppermost rooms\...and the chief seats,"]{.class_4258} of course, are to be found at the conventions, fellowships, synods, presbyteries, conferences, and councils.
