["Diotrephes, who loveth to have the preeminence,"]{.class_4258} will be found here---over-weight always, strutting around, or moving slowly with bowed head and gracious smile, humbly bestowing his blessing on all the assembled worthies. Demas is there, ["having loved this present world."]{.class_4258} He is rubbing his hands together and beaming like a Cheshire cat, as he makes arrangements to increase the denominational income (so his own salary can be raised). I believe I see Judas yonder, making sure the united-giving is all put in one unified, cooperative bag; and if I am not mistaken, I believe I saw him just then putting a few coins aside for his own pension just in case his Lord couldn't take care of him! It is always good to be in "good standing" with the Pharisees, for we are told in Matthew 26:6 []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_459}that they determined where the money went. (Don't forget that. You're liable to in these busy days!)

23:8 But be not ye called Rabbi: for one is your Master, even Christ; and all ye are brethren.

9 And call no man your father upon the earth: for one is your Father, which is in heaven.

10 Neither be ye called masters: for one is your Master, even Christ.

11 But he that is greatest among you shall be your servant.

12 And whosoever shall exalt himself shall be abased; and he that shall humble himself shall be exalted.

The passage is fantastic. It deals with religious leaders, as anyone can see by looking at the context. The ancient and lame alibi of the priest that, "You call your human father 'father,' so Christ could not have meant this literally," is absolutely out of the question. (If it were in the question, the Southerner still has a rejoinder which satisfies the question. "We don't ever call our daddy 'father,' we call him DADDY." \[This is one of several hundred words, used by Southerners, which proves that the South remained Bible "literalists" after the Civil War, while Rome took over the victorious North.\])

Getting back to the subject---the subject is religious leaders and their titles. Three titles are forbidden in the passage, so naturally these will have to be the titles that Bible-rejecting leaders will adopt. They are---Rabbi, Master, and Father. Isn't it remarkable how God foresees what man will do, warns him ahead of time, writes it where he can see it, preserves it where it is available, and then the man is powerless to keep himself from violating it once he has rejected it in his heart as the infallible, authoritative source of information? The title of our religious leaders today is the foremost and outstanding evidence against them that they can't read, nor do they believe what they can read.

Rabbi, Master, Father. The Rabbi comes as "Rab" (Bachelor of Arts), "Rabbi" (Master of Arts) and "Rabbak" (Doctor of Philosophy). The "Master," of course, is the English word "Mister." So three titles []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_460}are forbidden here (Rabbi, Master, and Father). In Psalm 111:9 and John 17:11, we find two names reserved for the Deity of God the Father, so naturally we could expect religious leaders to accommodate these names to themselves so people would honor and worship them. Summing it all up, we find in the twentieth century the amazing spectacle of "Christians" (?) getting together in huge councils and addressing their "leaders" (?) as, ["Rabbi"]{.class_4258} (Jewish: but participating in "Christian" activities), ["Master"]{.class_4258} (The lodge title: also "Grand Worshipful Master"), "Mister" (where the Christian title would be "brother"), ["father"]{.class_4258} (the Roman, long-robed priest, who binds the ["heavy burdens"]{.class_4258}), ["Holy Father"]{.class_4258} (addressed to various liquor heads in the Vatican), and ["reverend"]{.class_4258} (addressed to Protestant ministers).

All six titles are forbidden in the "Christian" Scriptures. Furthermore, all forget that in 2 Corinthians 11:12--15 they are warned that Satan has ministers, who appear as the ["ministers of righteousness."]{.class_4258}

In spite of the fair warning (given 1,900 years before the event), no one pays any attention to it. It is violated with impunity, if not plain indifference; the reaping awaits the violation, with the nation as unconcerned about it as a four-year-old boy with a table of logarithms.

In the context of the warnings on these titles is the statement, ["whosoever shall exalt himself shall be abased."]{.class_4258} Yet still the warning is unheeded and shall remain so. When one reads the passage, one is struck by the peculiar appropriateness of it to the Roman pontiff: "Know that thou art the father of princes and kings, the ruler of the world, the vicar on earth of our Saviour Jesus Christ, whose honor and glory shall endure through all eternity ?" (Or, as Luther would affectionately say, "Most Hellish Father.")

John XII (955--63) made the palace a brothel and was killed by a jealous husband in the act of adultery with the man's wife. In 1076, Pope Gregory VII excommunicated Henry IV, whereupon Henry elected a pope himself and marched on Rome. This church split terminated in a war and Gregory was exiled. Urban II took his place, outside of Rome. So with two popes (this has happened several times!), the "Vicar of Christ" sort of "got out of the will of God" or []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_461}something like that. At any rate, the "holy father" on many occasions has been an adulterer or a thief, a political shyster, a fakir, a religious quack, a spiritual charlatan, and often a murderer in broad daylight---"ex cathedra," of course!

This word "pope" is from "father," patar (Gk.), or Pater (Latin), and found in the German as vater. The word is the name of a male nurse, from "papas" (paps---Luke 23:29), and hence---papas, papa, pop, POPE. That is, the ["nursing father"]{.class_4258} (Num. 11:12---MOSES' SEAT!) is replaced by an Italian Gentile to lead an ecclesiastical hierarchy, instead of a "chosen nation."

Rabbi and Master fall under the same condemnation. The Psalmist worded it correctly when he said, ["Men of high degree are a lie."]{.class_4258} B.A. is a bunch of applesauce, M.A. is more applesauce, and a Ph.D. is "piled higher and deeper." Some men die by degrees.

23:13 But woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye shut up the kingdom of heaven against men: for ye neither go in yourselves, neither suffer ye them that are entering to go in.

14 Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye devour widows' houses, and for a pretence make long prayer: therefore ye shall receive the greater damnation.

15 Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye compass sea and land to make one proselyte, and when he is made, ye make him twofold more the child of hell than yourselves.

At this point, in His instruction to the multitude and His disciples---notice that up until this point He was talking to the Pharisees and scribes (Matt. 22:23, 34, 41, with 23:1)---our Lord suddenly leaves the launching pad with a blast of "unChristian" revilings and railings that would draw a blister on a stone wall at forty paces. The "lowly Nazarene," or "humble Galilean" (completely contrary to the ministries of the modern Liberal and Neo-orthodox ministers of this century), suddenly turns like a roaring lion on His adversaries and bares His claws (Isa. 42:13--15; Jer. 25:30).

["Woe unto you!\...Woe unto you!\...Woe unto you!\...Woe unto you!\...Woe unto you!...Woe unto you!\...Woe unto you!\...Woe unto you!"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_462} Eight times---verses 13, 14, 15, 16, 23, 25, 27, and 29.

["Ye shut up the kingdom of heaven against men\...."]{.class_4258} The "Rabbis" and "fathers" are not saved, but that is not the worst of the matter. The worst of the matter is that men look to them with respect as "soul savers" and believe what they tell them. Hence, the Pharisee and scribe are damned with a double damnation (note ["twofold"]{.class_4258} in verse 15). He is not only lost himself, but he prevents others from being saved by leading them to think that their religious duties can save them, and that by following church and rabbinical tradition they can merit righteousness with God. This is why they take souls to hell with them, for ["none of us liveth to himself."]{.class_4258} He has an eternal influence on someone (Rom. 14:7).

Historically, in the context, our Lord is talking about the present Jewish leadership, which refused to submit to the ministry of John the Baptist (see Matt. 3:2 and 21:31--32). This refusal not only blocked their passage into the Kingdom, but blocked the passage of hundreds who were watching to see what they would do; notice Matthew 27:20; John 7:48, 9:22. So today, since none of the big shots believe the [AV ]{.class_4257}is the word of God, none of the little shots do either. Since none of the big shots are premillennial, all their little shots are amillennial or postmillennial. Thus the ["pride of life"]{.class_4258} and the worldly pomp and splendor, offered Christ in Luke chapter 4, becomes the determining factor in theological beliefs. People follow their leaders, and their leaders follow Satan, "in the name of Christ" (Matt. 7:22).

["For ye devour widows' houses."]{.class_4258} These enlightening words are so enlightening that their light must be quenched as soon as possible by the "scribes," so we are not surprised to find the words (the entire verse, as a matter of fact!) omitted in the [ASV]{.class_4257} and the [RSV]{.class_4257}. Since both these Bibles are Roman Catholic texts from [Vaticanus]{.class_4257}, both adopt the Roman position towards any verse that exposes the sins of the Catholic Church, and if ever a verse riddled Catholic practice, Matthew 23:14 is the one that does it. ["For a pretence make long prayer."]{.class_4258} The statement is too clear. (As Mark Twain has aptly put it, "It's not the things in the Bible I don't understand that bother me. It's the things I do understand.") Any []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_463}priest reading Matthew 23:14 gets the interpretation just as plain and as solid as a fist in the nose. The reference is to him. If there be any argument, it may be at once answered, "Then why take the trouble to destroy the verse and remove it from the [Douay Rheims Challoner, ASV]{.class_4257}, and [RSV ]{.class_4257}versions? It doesn't offend a Bible-believing Protestant!"

"Why all the sweat, son? ['If thou doest well, shalt thou not be accepted?'"]{.class_4258} (Gen. 4:7).

Why remove the verse and then talk about "private interpretation," when any fool who reads the verse can understand it so plainly that the only way he can change it is to tear it clean out of the Bible? There is no Matthew 23:14 in an [RSV ]{.class_4257}or an [ASV]{.class_4257}.

It is in a [King James]{.class_4257}, 1611, AV.

It is also in the Old Latin versions (before [Vaticanus]{.class_4257}), the Clementine Vulgate, the Cureton Syriac (before [Vaticanus]{.class_4257}), the Freerianus (as old as [Vaticanus]{.class_4257}), and the [Textus Receptus]{.class_4257}. It is missing in the Westcott and Hort, pro-Catholic text of Nestle. (And this is the Greek text of the [RSV, ASV, NEB, Weymouth, Moffatt, Goodspeed, Fenton]{.class_4257}, Montgomery, Riverside, Mickey Mouse, and Popeye!)

Just to assert (and practice) our American liberty, let us draw judgment on the translation which Rome insists we accept; let us search for the motive that would lead a man to accept the Westcott and Hort reading of Matthew 23 (which omits vs. 14) and cause him to reject the older versions and ancient Greek witnesses.

NOTE: ["devour widows' houses\...make long prayer\...greater damnation."]{.class_4258} Can we locate this doctrine? Whoever gets the clear title deed to the woman's house gets it through praying long prayers. How can praying long prayers devour a widow's house? Now think about it. Don't just sit there like you're looking into the Grand Canyon. Think about it. Do you know anyone who makes long prayers for a widow and, in so doing, eventually succeeds in getting her property? How could a widow turn her property over to a religious leader to get him to pray? What on earth do you suppose he could be praying about that would get such a hold on a widow that she would wind up giving him her last penny? (Now think about it. Don't run off half-cocked and say, "Well, I don't []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_464}believe in private interpretations. I'll see what my priest says it says." As we have remarked before, he might be as stupid as you are, and perhaps meaner!)

Have you arrived at a solution? Review the facts. The widow is turning money over to a religious leader who wears a long robe and is called "father," and for this money he is making long prayer (which eventually winds up so long that it takes all her money to get it through), and he winds up putting her house in the treasury! What could that be? A widow. That is, her husband had died, and she needs long prayer made for something. (Oh, I wonder, I wonder what it could refer to? But, oh yes, I almost forgot; I mustn't interpret the Scriptures! Naughty, naughty! I must let my church tell me what they mean. I might have to wash my mouth out with soap!)

What does it mean? You tell me! You can read English.

In present-day application, the statement that they ["shut up the kingdom of heaven against men"]{.class_4258} can be applied spiritually (not doctrinally) to the fact that today ecumenical leaders prevent men from finding Christ by refusing to support Bible-believing works, by outlawing evangelism, by attracting attention to their own dead political groups, and by refusing to preach the gospel of the grace of God, when they have access to the TV channels and broadcast frequencies. (As Josh Billings said, "The meanest critter on the face of this earth is a bashful hypocrite." That fits the twentieth-century, wool-clothed dog perfectly.)

["Compass sea and land to make one proselyte."]{.class_4258} The tirade gets wilder by the minute and plainer by the minute. Here is a religious group that has a missionary program which encircles (compass) ["sea and land"]{.class_4258} for the purpose of a universal (ONE), proselyted church. No man who is versed in Church History (Latourette, Schaff, Newman, Eusebius, the Bede, Neander, Glover, et. al.) could fail to recognize the tone of the words. The only embarrassing thing is actually to apply them, for this will take more grace, grits, and guts than the historian can muster. To whomever the reference is (I didn't say)---the man proselyted ("a convert") is twice as damned as the man who instructed him! (Matt. 23:15). Nor does Jesus mince words. There are no pious platitudes here! ("Soften the message for ears polite, and snugly keep damnation out of sight.") ["Twofold more the child of hell than yourselves"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_465} is the choice of words. There are no[ "good words and fair speeches" ]{.class_4258}(Rom. 16:18) here, but only scorching, scathing, blistering, raging denunciation of the religious bigwigs. \[When Carl Sandburg ridiculed Billy Sunday, he built his ridicule on the point that Billy was a "ranting, raving braggart," while Christ was a meek, humble, and lowly humanitarian. I suppose if Carl Sandburg had never written that particular poem he might have gone down in history as an educated man. But no educated man would fail to read Matthew 23:13--33, and no reader would ever have the perverted concept Carl had of Jesus Christ; unless of course, he simply didn't believe what he read, and this is the case 99 percent of the time.\]

Notice that the money-grabbing "father" in the long-robe is making "long prayers" for a widow and at the same time compassing land and sea to organize one huge, universal (oops, I almost said the word!) "church." He is like a man holding a bucket of water and watching a dog burning to death and requiring a certain fee to pour quantities of the water on the dog before he is completely "purged." (A mission bum would be more decent.) Again, in verse 15 notice that the "twofold children of Hell" are interested only in their proselyte. Americans, for example, have some peculiar hallucination that peaceful coexistence with Rome is possible. But they are drugged. This is an impossible idea to Rome herself, by her own admission! One of the first movements, for example, in the ecumenical council of 1962 was to call the denominations' attention to the fact that the pope represented the authority of God for the universal church.

Rome, capitalizing on unregenerate nature, is not afraid to fight (and murder when necessary) "heretics," while American "Christians" have been brain-washed into thinking that opposing heresy is "un-Christian" (Titus 3:10). To the contrary, it is "un-Christian" not to oppose it, and if Christians will not oppose it, then Rome will assume her role of lord-high-executioner and exterminate Bible-believing people on the grounds that they are heretics. (G.O.P. = Genuflect, Or Perish.)

23:16 Woe unto you, ye blind guides, which say, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_466}Whosoever shall swear by the temple, it is nothing; but whosoever shall swear by the gold of the temple, he is a debtor!

17 Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gold, or the temple that sanctifieth the gold?

18 And, Whosoever shall swear by the altar, it is nothing; but whosoever sweareth by the gift that is upon it, he is guilty.

19 Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gift, or the altar that sanctifieth the gift?

20 Whoso therefore shall swear by the altar, sweareth by it, and by all things thereon.

21 And whoso shall swear by the temple, sweareth by it, and by him that dwelleth therein.

22 And he that shall swear by heaven, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him that sitteth thereon.

["Blind guides" ]{.class_4258}(see Matt. 15:14 and comments). ["Swear by the temple\...swear by the gold."]{.class_4258} The statement might seem a little obscure to the occidental mind, but if thought is given to it, it becomes apparent that the Lord Jesus is pointing out a common type of false reasoning found even today in religious "high places." The idea is, it is alright to say, "By the temple, brother, I swear to thee that I will do such-and-such and so-and-so, if\...etc." But that it is wrong to say, "Brother, I swear to you by the gold in the temple that I will carry out my promise to such-and-such\...."

The peculiar care taken here to protect the gold is a dead give-away to the Rabbinical traditionists of the Talmud. They are covetous (see Luke 16:14). Their Talmud is nothing but an omnibus of rules and regulations to keep them in power. In a show-down, they are not really concerned about the temple (2 Sam. 7). What they are worried about is that gold veneer Moses commanded (Exod. 37:2, 11, 22--23)! Jesus answers in American, "If God didn't bless that temple the gold is on, it wouldn't be worth a German Mark in 1919. You act like the gold was the main thing, and you act that way because you are money-crazy and stuck on a buck. You had better pay attention to the temple because My Father is getting []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_467}ready to tear it to the ground" (see Matt. 24:2). (That is a pretty free "American" interpolation, but you get the idea.)

You will notice, in the passage, that Jesus does not hesitate to use threats and use "fear" as a motive, even where it doesn't work. This, of course, is another radical departure from the flood of "positive, do-good" kind of books purchased in the religious bazaar these days. His speech is painfully plain. He is accusing the Pharisees of teaching (in practice) that "anything is alright to believe as long as we get our money." They would have praised the deceived widow of verse 14 to high-heaven, while robbing her of everything she had. They would do this also to the boy who robbed his mother and father (Matt. 15:5--6), saying, "I've dedicated this to God, so it cannot be used."

As the discourse goes on, the true religion of the long-robed fathers comes clearly into the light. Far from being the true Biblical religion of Scripture-believing Hebrews, it appears as the Rabbinical tradition of Scripture-rejecting dictators. ["Woe unto you!"]{.class_4258}

The absurdity of their doctrine in hallowing the gold, while ignoring the temple, is repeated in another illustration---they worry about the gift on the altar, but think the altar worth nothing (Matt. 23:18--19). The reason for this perversion of doctrine is exactly the same as the "temple gold" theological problem---i.e. they can't eat the altar, but they can eat the "gift" on the altar (being a lamb, cow, etc.). Their primary concern, after all, is their belly. ("Belly" is the Anglo-Saxon used in the [AV ]{.class_4257}in both places---Rom. 16:18; Phil. 3:19.) You can buy food with gold and you can eat gifts, so after all, what does the temple of God and the altar ordained by God amount to? You have to make a living, don't you? (See Commentary on Genesis, Gen. 3:4-- 8.)

The teaching is appalling, for it reveals that the greatest and foremost religious leaders of the most religious nation on the face of the earth were actually nothing better than any infidel, atheist, communist, publican, heathen, cannibal, or "drunk" on the face of the earth. Their religion, reduced to its smallest denominator and refined to its base metal, was nothing but a desire to maintain a high standard of living for themselves! Some religion! (See Matt. 23:11, 20:25--28, 16:24--25.) There isn't a drunken politician on the face of []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_468}the earth who doesn't have the same motive.

Verses 20--22 furiously wind up the first part of the discourse by concluding that the altar is not only holier than the gift on it, but that the temple is holier than the altar, and Heaven is holier than the temple, and the God that made the whole set-up (Acts 7:48--50) is holier than all. The statements imply further that a man would do better not to swear by anything and just keep his mouth shut till he knows what he is talking about (Matt. 5:33--35). God is over all, including the earth, which is His footstool. He is over the heavens, which are His throne. He is over the temple and the altar and the gift on the altar and the men offering the gift and the men eating them. Therefore the Pharisees' fine distinction about "what is RIGHT to swear by" and what is wrong is nothing but irreverent tomfoolery.

23:23 Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye pay tithe of mint and anise and cummin, and have omitted the weightier matters of the law, judgment, mercy, and faith: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.

24 Ye blind guides, which strain at a gnat, and swallow a camel.

25 Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye make clean the outside of the cup and of the platter, but within they are full of extortion and excess.

26 Thou blind Pharisee, cleanse first that which is within the cup and platter, that the outside of them may be clean also.

27 Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are like unto whited sepulchres, which indeed appear beautiful outward, but are within full of dead men's bones, and of all uncleanness.

28 Even so ye also outwardly appear righteous unto men, but within ye are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.

The tirade mounts as it raises pressure. One would think that the Lord Jesus is standing, fist-clenched, face reddened, veins distended in the forehead, and teeth flashing, as the bearded jaw works over its listeners savagely. The picture is not far-fetched. Read verses 25--33 []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_469}and let me hear you say that it is "far-fetched!" You see, the trouble is our ecclesiastical politicians have learned to "stoop to conquer," and they no longer approach the fold as wolves, but as sheep. Hence, they bring before the eyes of the world a sheepish Christ. They forget that Christ was a lion-tamer, not a butterfly collector (see Acts 4:13). The thing that marked His followers, immediately, was their boldness, not their tolerance.

The Christ of Matthew 23 and John 9 is never preached from a Liberal or Catholic pulpit, and never will be. One may say, conclusively, that there is one portion of the New Testament about which no pope, bishop, cardinal, or modernistic preacher ever talked to his church---Matthew 23:7--33. This "Christ" is not the Christ of the Sermon on the Mount. This is the Christ of Revelation 19; Daniel 2; Zechariah 14; Joel 2; Isaiah 66; Jeremiah 23; John 9; 2 Thessalonians 2; and other passages perverted or ignored by amillennial and postmillennial teachers.

["Ye pay tithe." ]{.class_4258}Surely enough, they did. The tithe, here mentioned, is the tithe of garden vegetables, commanded in Leviticus 27:30--32, which (as all Old Testament tithes) was to be brought to the storehouse at Jerusalem (Mal. 3:10; Neh. 10:38) once every three years (Deut. 14:28, 26:12). It was NOT money. It was vegetables and ANIMALS. It was NOT brought on the first day of the week. (See ["meat in mine house,"]{.class_4258} Mal. 3:10.) In the passage before us, the mint is as our mint, the anise is a dill spice, and the cummin is an herb (Isa. 28:25). The Pharisees are meticulous in tithing matters; note that. (Since the tithe is something to be eaten \[see comments on vss. 18--19\], then to be sure, it is very, very important!)

Now, it is true they should tithe. Jesus says, ["These ought ye to have done" ]{.class_4258}(referring to either the[ "tithe"]{.class_4258} or the ["weightier matters"]{.class_4258}), or else He said, ["And not to leave the other undone"]{.class_4258} (referring to either the ["tithe"]{.class_4258} or the ["weightier matters"]{.class_4258}). It would appear that the first part of the answer (23:23) would refer to the ["weightier matters"]{.class_4258} of Law, Judgment, Mercy, and Faith; and the ["not to leave the other undone"]{.class_4258} would refer to the tithing of the vegetables. This is undoubtedly correct in the light of what follows. The Pharisee is found doing something he []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_470}should do, but it is so far from being the most important thing he should do; it is compared to "straining out a gnat and swallowing a camel."

Notice that the ["weightier matters"]{.class_4258} have been entirely omitted, while the tithing has been observed scrupulously. To blazes with Law, Judgment, Mercy, Faith, man! "Bring all the tithes to the storehouse!!" (I can just hear them saying it!) Out goes Moses, out goes Justice, out goes Mercy to the poor and wounded, out goes Faith in God, Faith in the Bible, and Faith in the promises, and what is left remaining? Ah, the tithe is left! Here is something you can eat (Rom. 16:18).

"Ye blind guides, which strain at a gnat, and swallow a camel.''

The word ["at"]{.class_4258} should be "out," according to Luther Weigle. His reasoning (as the reasoning of his apostate translation---the [RSV]{.class_4257}) is that it must be "out" because you strain things "out" with a strainer; that is, you strain through a strainer to get them OUT. By this reasoning, the preposition (Gk. ["ek"]{.class_4257}) should appear in the text, or at least the Locative or Instrumental case for the noun. It does not appear. The ["gnat,"]{.class_4258} to be strained, is in the Accusative as a direct object (Gk. [konopa]{.class_4257}).

["Strain at"]{.class_4258} (as it is found in the [AV]{.class_4257}) makes just as much sense as "strain out," if one gives any consideration to the passage. But since it is the universal habit of religious leaders these days to ignore the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 readings, it would be highly improbable that any of them would find the sense in it. The Lord is here saying that, "At the appearance of a gnat, you get out the strainer to start straining, so it will not go into your soup; but when a camel shows up, you just swallow him, hide, hair, and all, and leave the sieve in the sink." ["Strain at"]{.class_4258} is the correct term. Dean Weigle (as other correctors of the [AV]{.class_4257}) is dead wrong (as usual).

"Which strain at a gnat, and swallow a camel."

The statement is highly suggestive of the moral motives of Dean Weigle and the Yale and Harvard Divinity schools. No reader of the [ASV ]{.class_4257}or [RSV ]{.class_4257}ever failed to notice how carefully they corrected nonessential words so as to be able to brag that they had produced a "better translation." Yet at the same time, they deliberately []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_471}corrected, mutilated, added, and omitted words that would change Christian doctrine to Liberal theology. They swallow the camel, worrying about the gnat. They correct ["suffer"]{.class_4258} and ["wot"]{.class_4258} and ["let"]{.class_4258} and then stamp on the Deity of Christ in Luke 2:33; 1 Timothy 3:16; and several other dozen passages. (Time forbids a list of the camels, by names, that the Liberal has swallowed in presenting his gnat-sized production, the [RSV]{.class_4257}. A few are here: Acts 13:35; 17:26; Matthew 26:29; Daniel 3:25; Numbers 21:14; Matthew 1:25; Psalms 53:1; Song of Solomon 2:1; Genesis 4:8; Acts 2:29; Hebrews 7:21; 1 Corinthians 14:1; Matthew 22:42; John 7:53--8:10; Luke 23:42; Hebrews 1:5, etc.)

It would be highly amusing to hear the Dean preach a sermon on the text, and watch him try to liken the GNAT to correct, Biblical doctrine and the CAMEL to "intolerance of your fellow man," while he himself is straining out a "spurious ending" in Mark chapter 16 to swallow the hulking camel of Bible-rejecting Communism.

"Woe unto you\...ye make clean the outside of the cup\...cleanse first that which is within the cup\...."

The bombardment continues along the same line. "You are always busy majoring in the minors and minoring in the majors." "You are forever expending your time, talent, and energy on nonessentials and forever ignoring the true need." "You are eternally scraping and scalding your religious hide with hot water and Bon Ami and a G.I. brush while inside you look like Custer's last stand." ["Thou blind Pharisee!"]{.class_4258} (vs. 26).

When one considers that Jesus here practices what He preaches, one marvels at the delay in His final arrest. Do you realize that this same type of haranguing was aimed at a host with whom He had been invited to dine? Look at the account in Luke 11:38--41. Did you ever consider the American equivalent of this scene?

The guest is invited to seat Himself at the table. The blessing is asked. The guest begins to carve His meat. The host looks up and says, "Would you care to use the restroom to wash your hands?" Then the guest replies, "Would you care to read the Bible awhile and wash your heart? Please pass the salt." (It is a miracle that they did not crucify Him three years before they did. Consider, "You forgot to wash your hands." "Well, YOU forgot to wash your HEART. Please []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_472}pass the salt.")

["Woe unto you\...ye are like unto whited sepulchres\...Even so ye also outwardly appear righteous\...full of hypocrisy and iniquity."]{.class_4258} The denouncement rises in fury and intensity, and the righteous indignation of the Son of God reaches a fever-pitch. It is so-much-so, in fact, that here the Liberal picture of the "man of Galilee" goes clean out of focus, and the Liberal is no longer able to recognize the man whose teachings he professes to follow. No Liberal follows Christ's PREACHlNG example.

They didn't use up all the white-wash in Christ's day; there are plenty of ["whited sepulchres"]{.class_4258} in the graveyard of apostate Protestantism. ["Indeed appear beautiful outward, but\...."]{.class_4258} The similitude is striking. The religious leader appears as a beautiful monument over a tomb, or an engraven door on a rock-hewn grave. It is symmetrical, graven with art of man's device, painted continually, or preserved from time and erosion by marble or granite. What is inside it? ["Dead men's bones, and of all uncleanness."]{.class_4258} The ["dead men's bones"]{.class_4258} were forbidden to be touched in the Old Testament without purification (Num. 19:16--18, see Lev. 13--15).

A moral leper, then, is the correct designation of those who occupy the ["uppermost seats"]{.class_4258} and "chief places," obeying the dictum of nature, known to every experienced sinner, "A thing is nearly always just the opposite of what it appears to be." To fully appreciate the figure, one would have to examine the decayed body of a dead man after it had been buried and pulled up about two months later. As the graves of A.D. 33, the modern grave ceremony makes it look like a nice place, if not a palace. A country boy at the graveside of a millionaire Texan looked into the grave. The recently deceased had requested that he be buried in a gold-plated Cadillac, and there he was, Cadillac and all, lowered into the grave. The country boy whistled through his teeth and said, "Man, oh man, that's livin!"

No, it isn't; it's dying.

A satin rug may cover the dirt pile, but it is still dirt---ashes to ashes, dust to dust. The casket may be so pretty that you wouldn't []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_473}mind lying down in it yourself, but it is still a coffin. (Calling a "coffin" a "casket" and a "graveyard" a "cemetery" and a "morgue" a "funeral home" doesn't change the matter any. The whitewash just covers up the real thing.) ["Even so ye outwardly appear righteous unto men, but within ye are full of hypocrisy and iniquity!"]{.class_4258}

23:29 Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye build the tombs of the prophets, and garnish the sepulchres of the righteous,

30 And say, If we had been in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.

31 Wherefore ye be witnesses unto yourselves, that ye are the children of them which killed the prophets.

32 Fill ye up then the measure of your fathers.

33 Ye serpents, ye generation of vipers, how can ye escape the damnation of hell?

This discourse reaches a crescendo, and punctuated by invectives, insults, jeers, and vilification, the Lord Jesus does everything short of damning the group on the spot. No picture of Jesus Christ like this is to be found in the writings of Poteat, Heidegger, Wieman, Cadbury, Cadoux, Schweitzer, Schleiermacher, Coffin, Rauschenbush, Ritschl, Peale, Dahlberg, and Weatherhead. This is a Christ who is totally alienated from the writings and thought of "positive thinkers," for the discourse is negative from beginning to end.

Notice the vehement, sarcastic, "Go on and do wrong then, who cares?'' in verse 32. This is nothing new in Scripture. You will find God the Father (in Amos 4:4) sarcastically commanding the religious leaders to ["Come to Bethel, and transgress!"]{.class_4258} Liberal theologians from Marcion to Brunner have overlooked the fact that the God of the universe is sarcastic, especially when He is examining their anti-scriptural "doctrines of men."

In verses 29--33 the "boom is lowered." The discourse concludes with a summary of the history of the ancestors of these ["whited sepulchres."]{.class_4258} It shows conclusively that they have a long []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_474}"apostolic line" and continue down through the centuries "in the seat" (23:1--2), while persecuting and destroying Bible-believing saints.

["Ye build the tombs of the prophets, and garnish the sepulchres of the righteous."]{.class_4258} The indictment is tremendous. These whitewashed "priests" kill men of God, bury them, build their monuments, decorate the monument, and then to add insult to murder, they "canonize" the man. History abounds with such instances.

Witness "Saint" Patrick in the forests of Ireland beating on his drum till the natives assembled from miles around. Then Patrick would mount his drum and preach hell-fire-and-damnation till the earth shook, and plead with his listeners to be "born-again" by receiving the Lord Jesus. Out of favor with the popes all of his life, the saintly Patrick believed the Old Book from cover to cover, and the worst opposition he had in his ministry came from the church that canonized him after his death.

Witness John Wesley, lauded in the twentieth century by men who have disgraced his name, defiled his ministry, blasphemed his doctrine, and stamped on his Bible. If John were to come into the "First Methodist Church" today, he would die of a heart attack before he got past the third ashtray. John preached separation, holiness, and the new birth! He never wasted five minutes on the "SOCIAL GOSPEL" which the modern Methodist preaches. The social revolution that followed Wesley and Whitefield was a by-product of old-time, Pentecostal Bible-preaching. ["Ye build the tombs of the prophets."]{.class_4258}

Witness Spurgeon, hailed by Baptists of the twentieth century as one of the greatest, if not the greatest, pastor that ever lived. However, the Baptists of his day excommunicated him in one of the worst church splits England ever saw.

Witness Sam Jones, a Methodist who shook America in his day; highly honored by the Methodist church, which today does not preach anything that even slightly resembles his messages, and which took Bob Jones' license away from him for preaching like that! ["And garnish the sepulchres of the righteous, And say, If we had been in the days of our fathers!"]{.class_4258}

But they lie. If they had been there, they would have done the same thing. They would have been partakers of the blood of the prophets, and Jesus accuses them in this fashion. ["Wherefore ye be witnesses unto yourselves, that ye are the children of them which killed the prophets!"]{.class_4258}

If Sam Jones and John Wesley were preaching today, the Methodist church would de-ordain Sam, and the Episcopal church would kick Wesley out.

If Spurgeon were preaching today, the Southern Baptist Convention would de-horn him before you could say "Lottie Moon."

If Billy Sunday were having a union campaign in Chicago, the papers would be slandering him from sun up to sun down.

If Martin Luther talked to Pope John the way he talked to Pope Leo, the cardinals would have him assassinated just like they did Abraham Lincoln, James Garfield, the archduke Ferdinand, and William McKinley. ["Ye be witnesses unto yourselves, that YE ARE THE CHILDREN of them which killed the prophets!"]{.class_4258} You worship the dead past and canonize people no longer a threat to you, but if they were alive today, their preaching would take your sorry, worthless, self-righteous hide off, hair and all, and salt it down and pack it away in the deep freeze! ["Woe unto you!"]{.class_4258}

"Fill ye up then the measure of your fathers."

Yes, go on and keep killing them just like your fathers did! Go on and do it, because you're going to anyway.

"Fill it up!"

This "filling of a cup" is found in Genesis 15:16; Romans 11:25; and 1 Thessalonians 2:16; it refers to something sinful and wrong getting higher and higher in the hour glass, till all the sand has run out. It is now FULL. Time to turn it upside down! Jesus, taunting and deriding His accusers, says, "Fill it up. When you have killed me, you'll have it full, and God will take this city and empty it!"

And this is exactly what takes place (see 23:35, 38).

["Ye serpents, ye generation of vipers, how can ye escape the damnation of hell?" ]{.class_4258}The implied answer is, "You can't." The words ["serpents"]{.class_4258} and ["vipers"]{.class_4258} are used. There can be no doubt about their application. Our terms are "snake in the grass," "dirty, low-down snake," etc. He has called them as low as a man can call []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_476}them---snakes going on their belly. Since the Bible never likens men to animals without purpose \[see Matt. 10:15--16; Acts 20:29; Isa. 40:31\], the animals here chosen are exact descriptions of their human counterparts. That is, the Pharisees and scribes are sneaky, slimy, slithering, poisonous, treacherous, lurking, terrifying, venomous, camouflaged reptiles! "Leaders of the flock!" "Vicars of Christ!"

23:34 Wherefore, behold, I send unto you prophets, and wise men, and scribes: and some of them ye shall kill and crucify; and some of them shall ye scourge in your synagogues, and persecute them from city to city:

35 That upon you may come all the righteous blood shed upon the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel unto the blood of Zacharias son of Barachias, whom ye slew between the temple and the altar.

["Behold, I send"]{.class_4258} should jolt someone in the crowd, for the quotation is from the Old Testament. The ["I,"]{.class_4258} who does the "sending," is Jehovah God, the Everlasting Father! Here, in the heat of righteous wrath, the Lord Jesus blurts out a clear statement of His Deity without any regard to the Scriptures or the crowd. He simply speaks for God the Father in the first person! ["Behold, I SEND unto you prophets!"]{.class_4258}

["And wise men, and scribes."]{.class_4258} Not all the ["scribes"]{.class_4258} are heretics, as for example Ezra, the ready scribe of the Lord (Ezra 7:6), and Baruch (Jer. 36:1--26). But the word "scribe" is derived from "script" or "Scripture" hence; the scribe is obviously a man in charge of preserving, by writing, the Holy Scriptures. To put it plainly, he is the translator in charge of the new "versions." As crooked as they were in A.D. 33, the modern scribe (Dean Weigle for example) publishes side-by-side (as though they were the same lineage) [Wycliffe, Tyndale, Geneva, AV, ASV]{.class_4257}, and [RSV]{.class_4257}. This looks like the work of a demented scribe, for the [ASV ]{.class_4257}and [RSV ]{.class_4257}are not from the text that the [AV ]{.class_4257}is from! What appears to be a "family tree" of Bibles is a group of Roman bastards thrust into the family, for the [ASV ]{.class_4257}and the [RSV ]{.class_4257}are from [Vaticanus]{.class_4257}, containing the Apocrypha and other extra-canonical books. In order to get this "bastard Bible" into the family tree, the [AV ]{.class_4257}is changed to the designation of "[KJV]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_477}" so that it will appear as [RSV, ASV ]{.class_4257}(three letters each). What the modern scribe hates about the [AV ]{.class_4257}is, the "A," for it means AUTHORIZED (from "authority"). The modern scribe's lifelong job is to rid the world of that authority and replace it with a Roman authority. And to this end work Fenton, Montgomery, Lamsa, Moffatt, Berkeley, Phillips, Weymouth, Nestle, Young, Gobey, and the rest. The ["ready scribe"]{.class_4258} of the Lord is the one who begins with a presupposition that God is right, no matter what, and accepts His authority without trying to "translate it" out of existence.

["And some of them ye shall kill and crucify."]{.class_4258} John will fit the ["kill"]{.class_4258} in the verse. Jesus will fit the ["crucify."]{.class_4258} Paul will fit the scourging in the ["synagogues."]{.class_4258} Barnabas and Paul will fill the bill for persecution from ["city to city"]{.class_4258} (see Acts). ["That upon you may come all the righteous blood shed upon the earth\...."]{.class_4258}

What follows settles once and for all any argument about the proper number of books to be found in the Old Testament canon. The blasphemous Council of Trent (1546), at this point, thumbs its sanctimonious nose in the face of God's Son and refuses to acknowledge His authority in the matter of proper canon. (I am not being "smart." The verse gives you the exact number of inspired books in the Old Testament; it is Jesus' opinion, and He purposely omits the Apocrypha and does not recognize one book of it in the Catholic Bible.)

Jesus, here, defines the proper Old Testament canon. Despite this fixing by the author Himself of the Scripture, Catholics to this day are taught to believe that a [King James Bible ]{.class_4257}is short some inspired books! Now notice how Jesus fixes the thirty-nine books of the Old Testament canon as the correct number---exactly as they are numbered in an [AV ]{.class_4257}1611.

1\. He lists the blood of the righteous slain as from "Abel to Zacharias" (Matt. 23:35).

2\. Abel is slain in Genesis (Gen. 4), but Zacharias is slain in 2 Chronicles 24:20--22.

3\. The beginning and end, then, of an Old Testament (for Jesus) was Genesis to 2 Chronicles.

4\. When we pick up the Hebrew Bible (in the Hebrew language), []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_478}what do we find? We find the books laid out in this order, with no Catholic books: Genesis, Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, Deuteronomy, Joshua, Judges, 1 Samuel, 2 Samuel, 1 Kings, 2 Kings, Isaiah, Jeremiah, Ezekiel, Hosea, Joel, Amos, Obadiah, Jonah, Micah, Nahum, Habakkuk, Zephaniah, Haggai, Zechariah, Malachi, Psalms, Proverbs, Job, Song, Ruth, Lamentations, Ecclesiastes, Esther, Daniel, Ezra, Nehemiah, 1 Chronicles, 2 Chronicles!

Notice the Holy Spirit, who gave the oracles to Israel (Rom. 3:2) by means of Jewish writers, at no time ever inspired, tolerated, or even permitted the entrance of the Catholic Apocrypha to this list.

All argument, therefore, about what should be in and what shouldn't be in is superfluous. The Lord Jesus Christ already told you what should and what shouldn't be in, and in Luke 24:44 He stated emphatically that the Jewish division of Genesis--2 Chronicles into the three orthodox groups is correct (Torah, the Law; Nabiiim, the Prophets; and Kethubhim, the Writings or Psalms). You couldn't find Judith, Tobit, and Maccabees in that with a flashlight and a pair of binoculars. The scribes did it again! They forced (against God's will) books into God's Book which are no more inspired than "Gone with the Wind" or the "Caine Mutiny." They did this, knowing that their Lord in Matthew 23:35 had already stated the bounds of the Old Testament canon.

(In the next ten years you will live to see Protestants accept these spurious forgeries as part of the "Holy Bible." They are no more holy than Holy Cow or Holy Mackerel. Jesus never bothered to quote one or even make an indirect reference to them, and when He did make a canonical statement He was careful to omit every "cotton-pickin" one of them. "Bel and the Dragon!" Bull and the Wagon.)

23:36 Verily I say unto you, All these things shall come upon this generation.

37 O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that killest the prophets, and stonest them which are sent unto thee, how often would I have gathered thy children together, even as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings, and ye would not!

38 Behold, your house is left unto you desolate.

39 For I say unto you, Ye shall not see me henceforth, till ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.

At verse 37 it seems as though He would burst into tears (and perhaps He did). ["O Jerusalem, Jerusalem!"]{.class_4258} The tirade rises to a peak and then breaks, and it seems as though the heart of the Saviour is breaking with it. He wants to save them (Isa. 49:14--17). They will not listen (John 5:40).

Hog-tied by religious traditionalism, stumbling in the fogs of ignorance and superstition, choking on the smoke of lust, and clouds from the dust of materialism, they will not come to Him. They will take the gift on the altar---it can be eaten. They will take the gold off the temple---you can buy food to eat with it. They will follow Him while He gives them bread to eat (John 6), but they will not take Him as their own. They want something to eat.

When one considers this fundamental truth of unregenerate human nature, one is compelled to go back to the original scene in Genesis 3 where the word ["eat"]{.class_4258} or ["eaten"]{.class_4258} occurs six times in six verses, preceding the man's disobedience.

It occurs fifteen times in eighteen verses to complete the telling of the "Fall." ["All the labour of a man is for his mouth,"]{.class_4258} says the wise man (Eccl. 6:7).

Meditation on this great central truth will lead a man to say what Satan said, ["Skin for skin, yea, all that a man hath will he give for his life." ]{.class_4258}Combat veterans know this; prisoners who starved in concentration camps know this. It is a fact so established that one could hardly imagine a religion that does not take cognizance of it, and yet, BIBLE CHRISTIANITY is the only religion in the world that opens its Book with a discourse on man's desire to eat and winds up with His rejection of spiritual food (John 4:32) in order to stay alive. Even in the great BREAD-OF-LIFE discourse in John 6, the fallen man (be he Scientist, Philosopher, Philanthropist, Humanist, or Atheist) still grabs at the literal bread (John 6:63) instead of the truth. If this were not bad enough, he outstrips himself in his race for satisfaction of his belly and turns the very words of the Son of God []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_480}about "eating" (John 6:53--56) into a literal act of cannibalism, and then goes one mile further.

In spite of the fact that man is told that the communion is a memorial (1 Cor. 11:26), in spite of the fact he is told that it is a spiritual body of believers being dealt with (1 Cor. 10:16--17), in spite of the fact that he is told that the flesh and blood is not literal (John 6:57), and even though he is told that no Bible-believing Christian ever needed more than one literal sacrifice (Heb. 10:8--12), man goes right on following his belly and making a literal (Mass) massacre out of the memorial of the Lord's supper.

They want something they can eat (see comments on 15:15, 16:7).

To the bitter end they will reject the living word of the living God to follow the man-made ritualism of their "sacraments." And they will treat as heresy any attempt to make them receive a Living Saviour, instead of a piece of wafer ("host"), which is called "My Lord and My God." (See notes in Missal on the "elevation of the host.")

They EAT their god.

["How often would I have gathered thy children together!"]{.class_4258} The Divine compassion breaks forth over the audience with a power and sincerity that must have touched the hardest heart. ["Even as a hen gathereth her chickens."]{.class_4258} (The passage is so anti-Calvinistic one would wonder what Pink, Shelton, Berkhof, and John Gill would have done with it. The statement given is, ["How often would I\...AND YE would not"]{.class_4258} \[see John 5:40\]. There is no ducking the implication. He would have saved them if they had been willing, and against His will, they were unwilling. Notice that He still prays for their forgiveness after this \[Luke 23:34\], and they still have a chance after the resurrection to receive Him \[see Acts 2--3, and 7\]. They would not come to Him no matter whether He would receive them or not; that is the point, and that puts Calvin on a tottering pedestal.)

["Even as a hen gathereth her chickens."]{.class_4258} The figure is as Psalm 36:7. An old Alabama farmer brought some light on the verse when he said there are four times when a mother hen calls her brood:

1\. When there is a storm coming (Matt. 24:2).

2\. When it is time for feeding (John 6).

3\. When there is danger of a hawk (Luke 17:37).

4\. When it is time to go to sleep (Acts 7:59--60).

He is willing. Are you? Or will you go along with the long-robed, belly-worshipping, gift-eating, gold-craving "father" of Matthew 23:9, 16, 18; Mark 12:38; and Romans 16:18?

"Behold, your house is left unto you desolate\...Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord."

The ["house"]{.class_4258} is the House of Israel or the House of God. In either case it is a direct reference to the Temple-worship of Israel in the Promised Land. What follows leaves no doubt about the interpretation, for in the next verse (24:1), Jesus departed from the temple. He was hailed as, ["Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord"]{.class_4258} a little earlier (Matt. 21:9). Upon entering the temple, He called it ["MY house"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 21:13). He now says, ["Behold, YOUR house is left unto you desolate,"]{.class_4258} and proceeds to walk out of the enclosure for good.

The typology is all too clear to anyone who has studied the Old Testament. The House of God (Beth-el) was only the House of God as long as God was in it (Amos 5:5; Jer. 48:13; Amos 7:10--13). When God forsook it (Jer. 7:11, 14, 22:5, 23:11), it became a smoking pile of rubble (Jer. 52).

In departing from the temple and saying, ["Behold your house is left unto you desolate,"]{.class_4258} Jesus claims the place of Deity in the Godhead and places Himself as God the Father, pronouncing judgment on a temple that He is, Himself, deserting. The word "usurp" is the word the Pharisees would use for the act. Imagine how they felt to see a man standing up and saying, "I send unto you the prophets. I am leaving, Here I go\...watch Me! Now that I'm going, you don't have any house left, because God will only bless this temple as long as I'm in it!" No wonder they crucified Him!

When Christ is not in the temple, it is a desolation. Notice that this is the condition of the unregenerate man, whose body is not the temple of the Holy Ghost (1 Cor. 3:16--17), but the temple of the unclean spirit (Eph. 2:2). In our text, the leaders have refused to accept the builder of the house (Heb. 3:3--4), exactly as an unsaved man refuses to receive his Creator, the Lord Jesus (Col. 1). Having []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2726.xhtml_page_482}refused the Builder and the Blesser of the house, they have nothing left but a stone-and-timber structure that God is no more interested in than St. Peter's or the Taj Mahal (Acts 7:48--50). ["Your house is left unto you desolate."]{.class_4258}

At a future date, the religious bigwigs will install their own choice for the ruler of the temple (Matt. 24:15; 2 Thess. 2:3--8), but this ruler is the one who comes in his own name (John 5:43). Far from being the Builder and Blesser, he is ["the abomination that maketh desolate."]{.class_4258} His city is that "mother of abominations" built on seven hills (see Rev. 17:1--5, 7--9, 18).

The Jews said, ["We have no king but Caesar!"]{.class_4258} (John 19:15); therefore, Rome will have to be the builder and blesser of the desolate "house."

["Ye shall not see me henceforth, till\...."]{.class_4258} The last word is ominous. Its teaching is clear. They will not see Him after the Resurrection. As a nation, they will never see Him again until ["the time of Jacob's trouble"]{.class_4258} (Dan. 12:1; Jer. 30:7), when they will turn to Him "in a day" (Hos. 6:1--3, Isa. 26:12--21) and be converted and healed (Rom.. 11:26--27; Heb 8:8--12) and recognize Him as the Messiah (Acts 2:36) and plead for Him to return (Psa. 79, 83, 68, 74, 44, etc.).

Deuteronomy 21 shows the elders of Israel how to put away the guilt of the crucifixion and how to manifest to the world, nationally, that they desire Him to return. When this takes place, they will see Him (as Paul did on the road to Damascus), and not until then.

From A.D. 70 to the Second Coming, "the House" is desolate. It will have a temporary occupant seven years who will be consumed at the brightness of Christ's coming (2 Thess. 1), but never again is the power and blessing of God on this house at Jerusalem until the triumphal return of the Builder and Blesser. What follows, Matthew 24, deals exclusively with this time. It is not strange, therefore, that Matthew 24 should be one of the greatest stumbling-blocks to Bible exegesis in the entire New Testament. No amillennial or postmillennial expositor could ever hope to begin to teach it correctly, for it deals with Rome's relation to Israel in the end-time. Nowhere is the church or the Christian or the plan of salvation or the new birth or the gospel of the grace of God or the rapture mentioned.

Mark 11:10 is a very interesting comment on Matthew 23:39, for here we find not only ["he that cometh,"]{.class_4258} but ["Blessed be the kingdom of our father David, that cometh\...."]{.class_4258} Thus the explanation of the passage is bracketed so that no amillennial or postmillennial expositor can make heads or tails out of it without abandoning his ridiculous theological position. The King comes with the Kingdom. The Kingdom is the Son of David's Kingdom. The King is the Son of David. The Kingdom is of THEIR father (Jewish) DAVID, and this Kingdom comes ["in the name of the Lord,"]{.class_4258} who is the Jewish ["son of David"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 1:1)! It is absolutely astounding to think that an expositor or commentator could be so blind as to go off sputtering about a spiritual kingdom that fulfills the Old Testament, in view of the plain teaching of the plain verses in the plain ENGLISH, in the plain passages. But, ["Seest thou a man wise in his own conceit? There is more hope of a fool than of him"]{.class_4258} (Prov. 26:12).

[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml}

# Chapter 24 {.heading_84-0}

24:1 And Jesus went out, and departed from the temple: and his disciples came to him for to shew him the buildings of the temple.

2 And Jesus said unto them, See ye not all these things? verily I say unto you, There shall not be left here one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

3 And as he sat upon the mount of Olives, the disciples came unto him privately, saying, Tell us, when shall these things be? and what shall be the sign of thy coming, and of the end of the world?

4 And Jesus answered and said unto them, Take heed that no man deceive you.

5 For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.

This passage opens up the fourth most difficult passage in the New Testament. (With the exceptions of Acts 2; Heb. 6; and Matt. 25, the verses in Matt. 24 are the roughest found anywhere.) Nearly fifteen cults base their heresies on the wresting of these Scriptures, and there is hardly a single denomination in the United States that does not teach in its Bible schools and seminaries that the passage applies to this present age or to the years preceding the destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70. When we arrive at Matthew 24, we have the stage set for another display of amillennial and postmillennial "scholarship," and they are never seen in worse light than here. Having denied the authority of Jesus Christ in matters of canonicity and interpretation (Matt. 13, 23:35; Luke 24:44), the entire line of scholarship from Origen to Strong goes into an Exegetical Can-Can at Matthew 24 and winds up all over the stage, slipping on the floor boards, crashing into the backdrops, and becoming tangled in the footlights. You have never seen a real asinine display of unbiblical scholarship until you have seen Origen, Streeter, Strong, Hodge, Calvin, Dabney, Gill, Matthew Henry, DeWette, Doddrige, Wetstein, Griesbach, Alford, Trench, A. T. Robertson, Machen, Warfield, Simpson, Harnack, Whitby, Dummelow, Augustine, Ellicott, Jerome, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_485}Eusebius, Kenyon, Westcott and Hort, and Berkhof tangle with Matthew 24!

["And Jesus went out."]{.class_4258} The words are to be connected with what has just taken place---["your house is left unto you desolate"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 23:38). It is left desolate because the Builder and Maker of the House is deserting it (Heb. 3:1--6). He will not return until the restoration and conversion of Israel in the future, when they shall say, ["Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 23:39, 21:9).

["And his disciples came to him."]{.class_4258} The speaker is undoubtedly Judas in the questioning that follows, and a careful reading of the passage will produce some excruciating insight into the nature and character of the type of people who are hypnotized by beautiful buildings and cannot take their eyes off the visible long enough to behold the invisible (Heb. 11:27; 2 Cor. 4:18). In Mark 13:1 it is ["Master, see what manner\...,"]{.class_4258} and the Holy Spirit calls to your attention that this question is asked by only ["one of his disciples"]{.class_4258} (Mark 13:1). There is only one who habitually calls Him ["Master."]{.class_4258} This, of course, is JUDAS (Matt. 26:22, 25; 26:49; John 13:13). Furthermore, when Jesus begins the Olivet discourse, He waits until the one who asked Him this question is GONE. Notice that in Mark 13:3 it says, ["Peter and James and John and Andrew asked him privately."]{.class_4258} In plainer words, Judas is left out of the explanations which deal with the Second Coming of Christ. Like the amillennial and postmillennial bunglers of Matthew 13, who are blinded so they cannot discern the course of the age from A.D. 70 to the Advent, so Judas represents that class of expositors who remain "in the dark" concerning the great prophetic events of the end-time. Judas is interested in the time and money gone into the building of the temple (Hosea 8:14). This is because money is involved in the work, and he (being treasurer) holds the money bag (John 12:6). We observe, right at the threshold of passages that deal with prophecy, the purposeful elimination of that type of person who is interested primarily in building programs and visible manifestations of human pomp and religious dedication. Judas never does get the answer to the question, ["Tell us, when shall these things be?"]{.class_4258} (Mark 13:4). It is given to four fishermen who did not accept the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_486}Apocrypha of the Vaticanus (LXX) as having anything to do with a "Bible."

["And Jesus said unto them."]{.class_4258} What Jesus says has already had a past fulfillment in Jeremiah 52 and Lamentations. It has a present fulfillment at the time He says it, since it involves the life of the disciples (A.D. 70) when Titus destroyed the temple, and it has a future fulfillment in Revelation 11:1--4 and 2 Thessalonians 2:3--8, where the Antichrist appears.

["There shall not be left here one stone\...."]{.class_4258} The fulfillment of this prophecy would be most unlikely, for some of them were sixty-five feet by eight feet by ten feet. Not only this, but since the temple was built by Romans, why would they destroy it? Not only this, but historically, Titus (as a man) was a very mild-mannered commander who would not order such a thing to be done. Yet it did come to pass, because the Lord Jesus said it would. The soldiers not only took the stones apart, but plowed up the ground underneath, looking for gold supposedly buried beneath the temple (see Jer. 26:18). The passage makes fine preaching as it is true not only of the literal temple, but it is true of the church (1 Cor. 3:16--17) and the universe (Heb. 1:10--12) and the human body (1 Cor. 6:13).

The standard way of handling verse 3 is to point out that three questions are asked and then to find the answer to these three questions in what follows; the first question being answered in verses 4--14, the second in verses 15--22, and the third in verses 23--25. This, however, is very improbable as we shall see upon examining the verses. The attempt is made by Fundamentalists to preserve this three-fold distinction in an effort to prove that some of the chapter must refer to something other than the past destruction of Jerusalem under Titus in A.D. 70. But this is a needless and wasteful compromise to make. If the believer will stick to the insistence that verses 14, 21, and 29--30 say what they mean and mean what they say, there is no danger at all that anyone can apply the chapter to the destruction of A.D. 70. The trouble is that the Bible believer, assailed with science on the left and beset with amillennial scholarship on the right, is afraid to insist anymore that the book says what it means and means what it says.

Applying the rule, however, the analysis would be that the first question is, ["When shall these things]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_487} (the throwing of one stone off another, vs. 2) [be?"]{.class_4258} This question would be answered in verses 4--14. Immediately, the rule breaks all to pieces, for sitting right in the middle of the "answer," like a roller skate in a doorway, is ["he that shall endure to the end, the same shall be saved."]{.class_4258} The end of what? It cannot be the end of Jerusalem, for nearly 300 brethren died before Titus ever showed up (1 Cor. 15:6--9), and the verse will not match any way with verse 14.

So without bothering to trace the other two questions, let us assume that the three questions of verse 3 are only two questions (which they are, as the question marks are found!) and that the answer to the first question is the answer to the second question. The event will take place once (locally and historically) at the destruction of Jerusalem, but the detailed doctrinal fulfillment will by no means take place until the Second Advent. Since up till here both advents appear together (see numerous notes under Matt. 1--3, 13, etc.), the two destructions appear simultaneously .

["Take heed that no man deceive you."]{.class_4258} The warning is ignored by 90 percent of all professing Christians. While quoting ["love one another,"]{.class_4258} and ["be filled with the Spirit,"]{.class_4258} and ["he that winneth souls is wise,"]{.class_4258} someone has forgotten this succinct warning: ["Take heed that no man deceive you."]{.class_4258} Since no one took heed, about 60,000,000 people who profess Christianity are taken in tow by the false prophets and "christs" of the immediate context---verses 5, 11, and 24. The first sign of the coming is deception. (See companion references in 2 Pet. 2:1--3; 1 Tim. 4:1--5; 2 Tim. 3:1--6.) ["Take heed that no man deceive you"]{.class_4258}: no priest, commentator, expositor, bishop, pope, mayor, president, deacon, elder, reader, astronaut, presbyter, teacher, philosopher, scientist, or critic---["Take heed that NO MAN deceive you. For many shall come in MY NAME"]{.class_4258} (not the name of Karl Marx or Adolf Hitler!).

Again, we see the bearing of the words on the gullible "Christian" of the twentieth century. The warning is that when the enemy shows, he will show "in the name of Christ." This is something no religious person ever could get straight, and yet right in front of their nose is the fair warning, ["Take heed that NO MAN deceive you. For many shall come in MY NAME."]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_488} They do NOT take heed, so they are deceived; they welcome the wolf who comes in Christ's name and waste their buckshot firing at Communistic quail while the wolves devour the flock (Acts 20:29).

["Saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many."]{.class_4258} Hitler, Khrushchev, and Mussolini never fooled any Christian. The only way you can fool a Christian is to profess to be one, and then act "churchy" enough so that you can pass off as one. By this standard Spellman, Oxnam, Sockman, Kennedy, and Pat Boone would have a much easier time deceiving people than Starkweather, Chessman, or Capone. ["Saying, I am Christ."]{.class_4258} The profession is not an unfair warning. They not only say, ["I am Christ,"]{.class_4258} some of them say "I AM GOD." (We have one old beer-belly over in Rome who lets people address him as God the Father \[John 17:11\], and then his followers get in a panic if people refuse to call him by such a title.) "Daddy Grace" of South Carolina used to have a peanut concession at his baptisms, where he baptized with a fire-hose and hollered, "By grace are you saved! Das me!" "Father Divine," a minstrel show version of "Father O'Toole," claimed to be a god to his followers. Unfortunately, they had to put him to bed with a shovel one day. Mary Baker Eddy did not profess to be Jesus Christ, but she claimed the title of "The Comforter" (which Mohammed also claimed!), and this is the Holy Spirit who anointed ([Christos,]{.class_4257} Gk.) the Lord Jesus Christ in Matthew 3. Bishop Johnson didn't go quite so far, but merely professed apostolic succession to Acts 2 and established a "oneness-holiness" set-up where those who sold property sent the proceeds to headquarters; i.e., HIM. ["And shall deceive MANY."]{.class_4258}

Somehow, the Christians in America have forgotten the words. The mass of unregenerate church leaders never bother even to read them. ["Many"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 7:13, 22). Out of 1,000 ministers in Cincinnati, for example, less than fifty profess to be born again, and out of those fifty, not more than twenty believe 1 John 5:13 or Romans 8:38--39 or John 10:27--29 or the book of Revelation. ["Many"]{.class_4258} (2 Tim. 4:1--8).

["Many."]{.class_4258} Not "a few," but[ "many."]{.class_4258}

The most uniform thing today among Christian people is for them to bleat innocently when some terrible heresy or infidelism is []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_489}exposed in their colleges, seminaries, churches, and translations of the Bible. The poor sheep say, "Well, brother so-and-so, do you mean to tell me that they did that deliberately? I just don't see how anyone would do a thing like that on purpose\...." And, "Well, it couldn't be that bad could it; I mean, after all, if that was true you couldn't have 'faith in your fellow man' at all!" And, "Do you mean to tell me that there are communists in my church organization and that the missionary money doesn't go where they say\...?" Yet staring the staggering sheep in the face is the text of Matthew 24:5, 11, 24. ["Many false prophets\...deceive many\...many shall follow their pernicious ways"]{.class_4258} (2 Pet. 2:1--2). Not "one or two," friend, but MANY. Don't expect, therefore, to find in any town in the U.S.A. more than one preacher out of ten preaching the gospel of 1 Corinthians 15:1--6. Enter expecting to hear the God-cursed ministry of Galatians 1:7--9 in nine out of ten pulpits and usually nineteen out of twenty. A man said, "There are so many ministers in the States and so few overseas, the proportion is all out of balance." This is not necessarily so. In a town of 5,000 people (Bay Minette, Alabama), four ministers preach the gospel, while eight do not. Of the four who preach it, only two believe the Bible system of interpreting itself through the Revelation of Jesus Christ (Rev. 1:1), and of these two, only one is not committed to sending the Christian's money to the support of Communistic institutions, Roman Catholic ideology, infidel lecturers, and political blackmail. In a town of 7,000,000 people, there is no possibility whatsoever that you could find more than thirty-five pastors who preach the truth. One Bible-believing messenger for every 200,000 people.

24:6 And ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars: see that ye be not troubled: for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.

7 For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes, in divers places.

8 All these are the beginning of sorrows.

9 Then shall they deliver you up to be afflicted, and shall kill you: and ye shall be hated of all nations for my []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_490}name's sake.

10 And then shall many be offended, and shall betray one another, and shall hate one another.

11 And many false prophets shall rise, and shall deceive many.

["Wars and rumours of wars"]{.class_4258} cannot be applied too well to the immediate time, nor to the apostles who lived before A.D. 70. There is no mention of either ["wars"]{.class_4258} or[ "rumours of wars"]{.class_4258} in the book of Acts, which runs right up to A.D. 62. It is possible that the reference is to the war of A.D. 70, but this hardly comes under the heading of wars, as the Roman power had been undisputed for 200 years and is undisputed long after A.D. 70. Thus it is seen immediately how verses 6--11 do NOT answer the first question as it applies to Titus' destruction. Furthermore, note: ["the end is not yet."]{.class_4258} The ["end,"]{.class_4258} here, is (as it is in most places) a reference to the end of the Tribulation (Matt. 24:21--29), and not the END of the Apostolic Age. ["For nation shall rise against nation."]{.class_4258} Again, it is impossible to force the passage into the A.D. 33--70 setting. Nation does not rise against nation until there are some nations to do the "rising," and this cannot be said to be really true until after the invasion of Rome by the Goths, Visigoths, Vandals, and Huns. The passage, then, undoubtedly begins with discussing the events of the END time (Dan. 7, 9, 11), and only by desperate mishandling and misapplication can the chapter be applied to A.D. 70.

Having rejected the Revelation of Jesus Christ (Rev. 1:1) as a valid revelation for understanding the word of God, the amillennialist and postmillennialist are here forced to pervert and abort the word of God. Not realizing Rome's motive for rejecting Revelation (because of the obvious revealing of her corruption in chaps. 17--18), Protestant scholars have accepted Rome's dictum that Revelation is "apocalyptic, figurative, symbolical," etc., and, thereby, have lost the key to prophecy. This key is sorely needed when one begins Matthew 24, for 90 percent of the events described in it did not happen in A.D. 70 and have to be future. But since the scholars have thrown away the key, there is nothing left to do but spiritualize and generalize the verses and condemn anyone who []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_491}professes to believe them as they stand, and say the meanwhile, "Oh, that's just your interpretation." \[All of which means that the scholar (as his Roman harlot-mother, Rev. 17:1--6) is so full of pride, he would say anything to cover up his ignorance.\]

In A.D. 70, nation did not ["rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom."]{.class_4258} Nor were there ["pestilences, and earthquakes"]{.class_4258} (24:7). Furthermore, none of the apostles here addressed were "delivered up and hated of all nations" after A.D. 70 because none of them were alive! Except John (24:9)!

Thus with a very small amount of common sense, and enough faith to equal a mustard seed, the student learns that Matthew 24 is NOT a discourse on the fall of Jerusalem in A.D. 70, described by Josephus. Furthermore, those who adopt this view reveal themselves for what they are---proud, irreverent, Bible-denying, smart-alecks who think their own interpretations are superior to the words written, where God wrote them, as He meant them to be written.

The word ["many"]{.class_4258} is found applied to six kinds of people in the remaining verses (vss. 9--12).

1\. MANY shall be offended.

2\. MANY shall betray one another.

3\. MANY shall hate one another.

4.[ "MANY false prophets shall rise."]{.class_4258}

5\. MANY shall be deceived by them.

6\. MANY shall lose their love for each other.

The only difficult part of the passage so far is verse 9. It has some application in the book of Acts (see James--Acts 12; Peter--Acts 12; Paul--Acts 28), and yet the final application cannot have reference to the audience then present, unless those apostles are resurrected at the rapture for a return ministry to Israel.

This is a wild thought (Ezek. 37), and it is so wild that we are going to drop it. But before dropping it, notice the identical type of statement in Matthew 10:23. In both statements He is telling half-truths, unless those addressed have a future ministry in Palestine. But the water is getting deeper. If L. S. Chafer begins to drown here, how shall Berkhof and Strong ever make it?

["Many false prophets\...shall deceive many."]{.class_4258} The []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_492}statement is not hard to believe if one knows his Bible. The contrast between what is going on in the Church Age already and what is found in the book is so marked that anyone can see it.

The healer cites Exodus 15:26 and Matthew 8:17 and starts the healing line, but what is the man doing taking up a collection when Matthew 10:9--10 forbids it? And why is he preaching to Gentiles, when the commission in Matthew 10:1--6 is to the Jews only? (Chapter and verse?) The man who couldn't hear now nods when he hears the tick of the watch, but the watch is placed against the bone behind the eardrum where a deaf-mute could feel the vibrations! (Chapter and verse?) The blind man can now see the hand moving between him and the floodlights in the tent, but anyone with his eyes closed can see the shadow of a hand through closed lids, if it is between him and a 500 watt bulb! You say, "You saw the goiter go down on her neck before your very eyes?" Well, did she show you the plastic flesh over the balloon before she let the air out, by way of a tube? You say, "I saw him throw away the crutches when he came through the line." Did you? Did he tell you how much he was paid to do this, so as to "help others have faith" that God could heal them? (Chapter and verse?) Harry Blackstone said, "The hand is quicker than the eye." Christ said, ["Judge not according to the APPEARANCE"]{.class_4258} (John 7:24). But who can convert a man to believing the word when he has already used it to fool himself?

24:12 And because iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold.

13 But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.

14 And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations; and then shall the end come.

15 When ye therefore shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, stand in the holy place, (whoso readeth, let him understand:)

16 Then let them which be in Judaea flee into the mountains:

17 Let him which is on the housetop not come down to []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_493}take any thing out of his house:

18 Neither let him which is in the field return back to take his clothes.

19 And woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days!

20 But pray ye that your flight be not in the winter, neither on the sabbath day:

This brief passage begins to weed out the tares of Bible commentators from the wheat. There are so many elements in it misapplied, misinterpreted, and misunderstood that it would be a hopeless task to go into a long explanation. The comments, therefore, should be limited to an exposition of what the verses say.

["But he that shall endure unto the end."]{.class_4258} This verse (dissected and lifted piecemeal from its context, like an appendectomy) has been used from the first Catholic heretic in the second century to the last Methodist in the twentieth century to prove that a born-again, blood-bought, New Testament Christian will die and go to hell if he does not hold out faithful to Christ till the end of his life. This moral absurdity could only have come from one place. It would have to come from some group who rejected the future application of Matthew 24 for a period of time called "The Great Tribulation," preceding the Second Coming. That is, it would have to come from an amillennial or postmillennial group who believed in apprehending the promises to Israel for the church. Here, unfortunately, they apprehend a CURSE instead of a promise. Notice, please:

1\. No one's life is being discussed (24:14).

2\. It is the end of a period of time, not an individual's life (24:14).

3\. There are no Christians present (24:3).
