4\. The land being discussed is Palestine (24:16).

5\. The audience addressed are Jews (24:1--3).

6\. They are then observing Old Testament Law (24:15--20).

7\. They are worshipping in a temple in Jerusalem (24:15, 2 Thess. 2).

8\. They are not spiritual Jews, and no one but a Bible pervert would distort the context to get the meaning (Rom. 2:29).

9\. The Christian already has a promise that he will endure to the end if that is what is under discussion (1 Cor. 1:7--8).

10\. The Second Coming follows the "end" in this passage, and not the DEATH of the believer (Matt. 24:14, 21, 29).

It is therefore apparent that Matthew 24:13 has nothing to do, directly or indirectly, with the salvation of anyone in the age of grace, and it was never intended to be used by anyone, under any condition, for any purpose, in that manner. Like Hebrews 3:6, 14, a period of time is being discussed, and this period of time is defined in the immediate context so that there can be no possible latitude in applying the verse.

Matthew 24:13 is one of the finest places in the Scripture to see the Catholic, Liberal, and Arminian go to pieces like an hysterical woman. The very next verse (14) defines ["the end,"]{.class_4258} and yet every Church of God, Assembly of God, Nazarene, Methodist, Church of Christ, Seventh-day Adventist, Jehovah's Witness, Mormon, Catholic, Liberal, Episcopalian, Lutheran, and Pentecostal preacher in the country absolutely refuses to read it. Reading verse 14 involves so many anti-Catholic traditions that it is just much easier to say, ["Thy will be done,"]{.class_4258} and step on the inspired writing and walk on with theological legs so awkward that they would dump you on a golf green. Verse 14 locates the ["end"]{.class_4258} as the end of that period of time, wherein the ["gospel of the kingdom"]{.class_4258} is preached. Since this gospel is not the gospel of "the grace of God" (1 Cor. 15:1--6) given to the Christian (Gal. 1:11--12) by the apostle to the Gentiles (Rom. 2:16), it most certainly would have no bearing on the life of any Christian who lived in Europe, America, Asia, Africa, Australia, Siberia, or the Indies, from Pentecost to the Rapture.

["He that shall endure unto the end," ]{.class_4258}is plainly a reference to a law-abiding Jew in Palestine immediately preceding the Advent of the Lord Jesus, and no amount of distortion could ever make it apply to anything else, unless the distorter had a motive in perverting the word of God.

["And this gospel of the kingdom."]{.class_4258} As Scofield has so ably put it, "There are four forms of the Gospel---the gospel of the kingdom (2 Sam. 7:16; Dan. 2), the gospel of the grace of God (1 Cor. 15:2--5), the everlasting gospel (Rev. 14:6), and what Paul calls "MY []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_495}gospel" (Rom. 2:16; Eph. 3:1--7)." This Scriptural division is not arrived at by any system of interpretation. It is arrived at by believing what is read, as it is found, where it is found. People who try to equate the "gospel of the kingdom" with the "kingdom of God" (Rom. 14:17) only reveal again (as they have thousands of times before) their insincerity and disrespect for the authority of the Scripture. Two things that are different are not equal.

["This gospel of the kingdom."]{.class_4258} Matthew defines it in a score of passages---Matthew 3:2, 4:17, 4:23, 8:12, 9:35, 10:7, etc. In every case, it is the literal, physical, visible, Messianic, Davidic, Palestinian kingdom promised to the Son of David, who will reign on David's throne at Jerusalem. No Jehovah's Witness is involved. No Christian is involved. No salvation is involved in this age, and no church is connected with it. These facts are evident to the most uninformed who will take time out to compare the passages.

["For a witness unto all nations."]{.class_4258} Notice that the word ["nations,"]{.class_4258} as in Matthew 28:18--19, is set over against the word[ "kingdom."]{.class_4258} The Kingdom is the Lord's and it is Jewish (Acts 3:19--26) and this message is to be preached to the Gentiles in the Tribulation as a witness. There is nothing in the passage remotely connected with missionaries spreading the gospel to all nations in this age. If a missionary in this age did obey the verse and preach that gospel, God would curse him according to Galatians 1:7--10.

Matthew 24:13--14, then, turns out to be the "parting of the ways" in Bible interpretation. Here the Catholic and Methodist grab onto the possibility of "losing it" and keep the Christian under Judaistic bondage all his life (Gal. 3--5). The Jehovah's Witness grabs it and is conceited enough to think that it is he, and he tries to ["endure"]{.class_4258} an age that hasn't even begun. The amillennialist grabs it and pretends that the Jewish gospel of the Kingdom is the plan of salvation in this age (Presbyterians and Reformed), and the missionaries grab it and break their necks trying to get to every nation so the Lord can come back! Matthew 24:13--14 is Waterloo for churches and denominations who try to eliminate the book of the Revelation as the proper key for rightly dividing the word.

Preachers, teachers, students, believers, and followers of these groups do not know what the ["end"]{.class_4258} is, what the ["kingdom"]{.class_4258} is, what the ["gospel of the kingdom"]{.class_4258} is, who the ["nations"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_496} are, or to whom the message is addressed. "Abandon all hope, ye who enter here!" (Dante).

["The abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet."]{.class_4258} Just for a respite, let us imagine that we are in the shoes of some deluded scholar like Alford, A. T. Robertson, Trench, Tregelles, Westcott and Hort, or Dummelow. How shall we properly exposit verse 15?

1\. We don't know what it is talking about.

2\. We don't know what the "end" is or what the "kingdom" is.

3\. We don't know to whom the message is addressed or when.

4\. We resent any premillennialist telling us any of the above information because "we are scholarly"!

So what shall we do with it?

We shall go to Roman Catholic tradition (found in the fourteen books rejected by the Lord Jesus), and from the book of Maccabees we shall give a private interpretation, which all Catholics hold to be infallible doctrine. The ["abomination of desolation,"]{.class_4258} by such an investigation, would be the pig offered on the altar at Jerusalem on the fifteenth of Chislev of the year 145 (Dec., 168 B.C.), by Antiochus Epiphanes. This sound Roman Catholic doctrine is found outside the Bible, as usual, in 2 Maccabees 4:11; 1 Maccabees 2:42, 7:13, and 1 Maccabees 1:54, 59. Having thus safely made a liar out of Christ by relegating the event to the past instead of the future, we may proceed and say that "Christ applies the phrase of what was to take place at the advance of the Romans against Jerusalem\...when they brought their pagan image of their gods carried on their battle standards." Thus the Holy Spirit is sufficiently quenched, and there is no fear that any of the rest of the chapter can be future (Rev. 17:1--6).

Once this course is taken, then the second coming of Christ, found in verses 29--30, has to be spiritualized to apply either to the descent of the Holy Spirit at Pentecost (!) or the the destruction of Jerusalem (!), neither of which are connected with the Second Coming of Christ. (If the illustration seems far-fetched, let the reader take note that the position above is quoted from the [International Standard Bible Encyclopedia]{.class_4257}, Vol. I, p. 17, which is considered to be []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_497}a monument of orthodoxy. In reality, it is nothing but a stuffed Roman hassock.)

["The abomination of desolation"]{.class_4258} even tripped up the great Plymouth Brethren expositor, Dr. Scofield. When he approaches Daniel 8:9, the famous premillennialist feels suddenly oppressed by Rome to fulfill his obligations as a true "catholic" and "son of the church," so Scofield writes (footnote, p. 912, [Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4257}), "The little horn here is a prophecy of Antiochus Epiphanes. He is not to be confounded with the little horn of Daniel 7, who is yet to come!" Really? Then why is it that the little horn of Daniel 8:9 goes right into the Antichrist in verse 10? Scofield has to write lamely, "This passage is confessedly the most difficult in prophecy." But the good doctor has only made it difficult by making the error on verse 9. If verse 9 is the Antichrist of Revelation 12:1--4, then verse 10 offers no difficulty whatsoever. The "difficulty" was introduced when Scofield and his board bowed to Roman tradition, as found in the Maccabees (not in the Bible) and tried to parade a little "scholarship" that was not worth the ink it took to write it down.

["The abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet"]{.class_4258} is found in Daniel 11:31 and 8:13, and neither passage has any reference to some secular "villain" of Roman-additions to the Bible. Notice that in Daniel 8:13--14, the time is given for the desolation, and it is not a time any scholar has ever found connected with Antiochus Epiphanes. Furthermore, the sanctuary was not cleansed (Dan. 8:14) when Antiochus was through. Again, orthodox scholarship beat its brains out trying to exposit a passage intelligently after insulting the Lord by appealing to Catholicism for help. Here too, the Seventh-day Adventist enters (Dan. 8:14), and taking advantage of the confusion, constructs the monstrous hypothesis that the 2,330 is a "year-day" job like Daniel 9:25 and Numbers 14:34. On this theory, we find a whole new "religion" erected with invisible judgments taking place in heaven over people here that no one knows about---French Revolutions dating riders in Revelation 6, Tartar and Mongol massacres dating vials in Revelation 16, and God knows what. (If you would like to waste several hours to become acquainted with the whole fouled-up mess, [Drama of the Ages]{.class_4257} is highly recommended, and other popular []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_498}fiction from the Review and Herald Publishing Company, Washington, D.C.)

["The abomination that maketh desolate."]{.class_4258} Daniel 11:31 clearly marks the taking away of a LITERAL sacrifice, which is being made in a LITERAL temple (Rev. 11:1--4) in the real future (2 Thess. 2:1--6). It is NOT Antiochus and it is not Titus. All passages point to a literal fulfillment in the middle of Daniel's Seventieth Week (the end-time), to take place after the rapture of the church. You will arrive at this interpretation if you read ["desolate"]{.class_4258} where it says ["desolate,"]{.class_4258} ["abomination"]{.class_4258} where it says ["abomination,"]{.class_4258} and ["temple"]{.class_4258} where it says ["temple."]{.class_4258} \[Difficult, of course, for beginners, but you'll get used to it if you don't lose your temper! \]

["The abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, stand in the holy place."]{.class_4258} The Holy Place, of course, refers to that part of the temple at Jerusalem where the shew bread, candlestick, and altar of burnt incense were kept (Heb. 9:1--5). It says plainly that there is a literal temple standing at the time these events take place. Revelation 11:1--4, in the Tribulation, confirms this, and 2 Thessalonians 2:1--6 states just as plainly that when the Man of Sin comes that he will sit down in the ["temple of God, shewing himself that he is God"]{.class_4258} (cf. Dan 11). There would not be time in several volumes to analyze all that these passages bring to mind in relation to other Scriptures, but briefly let it be noted that:

1\. The Antichrist will profess to be God in the flesh (2 Thess. 2).

2\. As such, he will install himself as the object of worship (Rev. 13).

3\. As an object of worship, he will demand sacrifice to replace the authorized sacrifices of the Jews (Dan. 8, 11; Lev. 1-7).

4\. These sacrifices will be human beings who are decapitated at the altar on the court of the temple, and the flesh and blood eaten by the black-robed priests of the "mass" (Massacre)---(John 6:40--71; Rev. 20:4; Lev. 1; Rev. 6:9--11; Isa. 6:13; Psa. 16:4, 14:4).

(We are back to the "belly-worshippers," commented on under Matt. 23:16, 18.)

Since this Man of Sin is the fifth cherub (now vacant from the third heaven, Rev. 4:7, Ezek. 1, 10, 28), and since the cherubim have []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_499}the privilege of surrounding the throne, this Cherub will resume his place in the temple as the anointed (christos!) cherub that covereth the throne (Ezek. 28:14) and will seat himself on the mercy "seat" to dictate "ex-cathedra" to the entire world!

But I hear griping in the barracks.

The troops have been reading Kenneth Wuest instead of their Bibles.

["Whoso readeth, let him understand"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 24:15)!

["Then let them which be in Judaea."]{.class_4258} Notice, how the Holy Spirit keeps driving home the fact that the setting is Jewish, not Gentile; it is under the Law, not under Grace; it is a period of time in the future, not the past; and this period is terminated by the Advent (Matt. 24:29). How silly it would be to think that Matthew 24 was A.D. 70 when you are told that the Lord Jesus returned at the end of that period of time! He could not have returned, for John writes of His coming as FUTURE, in the gospel, and John's gospel is written twenty years after the destruction of Jerusalem.

["Flee into the mountains."]{.class_4258} The details of this are found in Revelation 12:1--17; Micah 7:14--16; Jeremiah 50:19--20; Hosea 2; and many other places. It has been pointed out by advocates of the A.D. 70 theory that Cestius Gallus withdrew from Jerusalem in A.D. 70, after beginning the siege, thus giving all the Christians time to escape to the mountains. This overlooks three important facts:

1\. Christians do not observe the sabbath (Fri.--Sat.); (Matt. 24:20).

2\. Nowhere did Jesus warn Christians not to have children (24:19).

3\. Nowhere are any Christians addressed in the passage. The word "Christian" does not appear in the Bible in usage till nearly ten years after the Lord has returned to Glory (Acts 11.)

This does not mean that certain events which took place in A.D. 70 cannot foreshadow what is to take place later, for Jeremiah 52 foreshadows A.D. 70 and the future destruction under the Antichrist. But it does mean that we do not have the liberty to relegate future passages to the past simply because we are too stupid to adopt the premillennial position. Writers say that in A.D. 70 "the sun was as dim as the moon on cloudless days for a year, during the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_500}reign of Justinian, and that a new star appeared and disappeared the night Charles IX died," and so forth and so on. Our job, however, is to compare Scripture with Scripture till we know what we are talking about, instead of comparing the secular events of A.D. 70 with a fairy story in Maccabees and then trying to jam Matthew 24 into it.

["Woe unto them that are with child\...in those days!"]{.class_4258} Again the ["days"]{.class_4258} are exactly located, for we find the same expression in Luke 23:28--31. Here also, it is addressed to Jews only, and the quotation is from the Tribulation. It thus appears that the gospels are "shot through" with references which can only be interpreted by the book of Revelation. Revelation opens the Old Testament, and the Old Testament opens the New Testament. The amillennial and postmillennial bunglers of Catholicism tried to conquer the shores of prophecy without any understanding of the book of Revelation before they landed. They are like the paratroopers on D-day in Normandy, drowning in three feet of water because of excessive gear and equipment.

Only one man in the Bible was forbidden to marry or have children. It was not a priest. It was JEREMIAH (Jer. 16:2). Jeremiah was living at the time that Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon (Rev. 17!), destroyed Jerusalem. \[When this is done again, it will be by a man who is typified by Nebuchadnezzar (and seventeen other Old Testament characters), and who is connected (as Nebuchadnezzar) with ["Babylon"]{.class_4258} (see Rev. 17:1--9).\] Jeremiah, therefore, would perfectly typify the Jewish remnant in the Tribulation at the time the Antichrist takes over. This remnant is called ["the virgin of Israel,"]{.class_4258} and ["the virgin the daughter of Zion,"]{.class_4258} and is addressed by Jesus in Luke, ["Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves, and for your children"]{.class_4258} (Luke 23:28). Notice that this expression, as it occurs also in Matthew 2:17--18, is connected with Jeremiah and speaks of a nation in mourning at a time when men are calling, "Mountains, fall on us," "Rocks, cover us!" (Rev. 6:16). The ["green tree"]{.class_4258} of Luke 23:31 is Israel in her own land in peace with the Messiah present. The ["dry tree"]{.class_4258} is Israel in bondage under religious Rome, with the false Messiah in "the driver's seat" (see Ezek. 17:22--24).

Again, the Revelation of Jesus Christ (Rev. 1:1) reveals its ability []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_501}to unsnarl a passage that remains a snarl until the premillennial system unsnarls it. The natural antipathy to the book of Revelation comes, of course, from that religious system which the book attacks; that system is so clearly given in Revelation 17:2--6, 9, 18, that exposition would be embarrassing. (See [The Mark of the Beast,]{.class_4257} 1994, Bible Baptist Bookstore.)

["But pray ye that your flight be not in the winter, neither on the sabbath day."]{.class_4258} The route of this flight will be found in the Old Testament prophets, and it is clear that it is to southern Palestine (Petra), by crossing the Jordan and fleeing down the "king's highway" through Moab and Ammon. Winter time is the start of the Jordan River's flood-time and peaks with the melting snows in March and April (see Josh. 3:15). Another danger is that there will be no time to get adequate clothing for the trip (Matt. 24:18). (A woman was found buried in the ruins of Pompey in the act of snatching some jewels out of a hidden compartment in the wall of her home. There wasn't time to get them, and the precious seconds she wasted fooling with the lock and moving the picture might have been used in running pell-mell out of the town.)

["The sabbath day"]{.class_4258} offers a problem that no expositor has handled properly, if he subscribed to the old A. D. 70-Titus-chimera.

It is argued that if it was the Sabbath they would violate it by going more than a ["Sabbath's day journey,"]{.class_4258} which would get them no further than the mount of Olives (Acts 1:12). But this interpretation is strained to say the least. If the sabbath is ["made for man"]{.class_4258} (Mark 2:27) and the ["Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath"]{.class_4258} (Mark 2:28), why would it be "breaking the sabbath" to run from the Antichrist till you were out of breath?

No satisfactory explanation has ever been given, unless it is the shocking thought provoked by the CBS broadcast over station WNVY, Pensacola, Florida, August 10, 1962 (to the common knowledge) that all airlines in Israel are grounded on Saturday. Readers of Revelation 12 have not failed to notice that Israel not only "flees to the wilderness," she FLIES!!

24:21 For then shall be great tribulation, such as was not since the beginning of the world to this time, no, nor []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_502}ever shall be.

22 And except those days should be shortened, there should no flesh be saved: but for the elect's sake those days shall be shortened.

23 Then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, or there; believe it not.

24 For there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shall shew great signs and wonders; insomuch that, if it were possible, they shall deceive the very elect.

25 Behold, I have told you before.

["Then shall be great tribulation."]{.class_4258} This period of time is never to be confused (as dead-orthodoxy insists on confusing it) with the routine tribulations that all of God's people, in any age, are to expect---Acts 14:22; 2 Thess. 1:5. This period is called the Tribulation (Rev. 7:14). [(]{.class_4263}θλίψει[ ]{.class_4263}in Rev. 1:9.) It is called this to indicate that peculiar period of time in which a nation is "born in a day," exactly as a woman is in tribulation during "birth pangs" (Deut. 4:30; Isa. 54:1, 66:7--8; Micah 5:3; Jer. 30:6, 49:24, 50:43; Hosea 13:13; 1 Thess. 5:3). This tribulation, called ["the time of Jacob's trouble"]{.class_4258} (Jer. 30:7) and the ["time of trouble" ]{.class_4258}(Dan. 12:1), and ["affliction" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 13:19), will make two World Wars and a Korean War look like a Junior-Senior Prom.

["Such as was not since the beginning of the world to this time."]{.class_4258} Exactly how a man can read these lines which Jesus spoke and then deny their gravity and timeliness is a problem. As the colored man said, "If he didn't mean what he said, why didn't he say what he meant?" Is the reader so gullible as to suppose that Jesus just "thought" the destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70 was going to be the roughest thing since creation? How about the flood? No, somewhere we are going to have to quit listening to these A.D. 70 Josephusites and get back to believing the word of God.

["No, nor ever shall be."]{.class_4258} Do you get the tense? Jesus says that this tribulation will be worse than anything SINCE Genesis 1 and will be worse than anything ever to come in the future. Was the destruction of Jerusalem in 70 A.D. worse than the sacking of Rome that followed it? The destruction of Carthage under Scipio? The []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_503}destruction of Kiev under Ghengis Khan? The Crusades? Two world wars? The atom bomb?

No, the passage in Matthew 24:21 certainly has no reference whatsoever to the Titus-A.D. 70 rehearsal. (This is a ruse of conceited scholars to steal the promises from Israel.)

"There should no flesh be saved: but for the elect's sake."

In the passage, the word ["elect"]{.class_4258} is the JEW, as in verse 31. Confirmation of this lies not only in the fact that the passage is a Jew talking to Jews about a Jewish period of time when the Jews are in the Jewish homeland (and the passage occurs in Matthew's gospel, presenting the King of the Jews), but it lies also in a careful examination of Isaiah 45:14, 27:13, 65:9--12, 28:4; Zechariah 2:5--6; Daniel 7:21--22; and Isaiah 45:4. The ["his elect"]{.class_4258} of verse 31 is forced to apply to a Christian in the Church Age by over-zealous advocates of the Rapture, who fail to see the rapture of the Tribulation saints mentioned in Isaiah 26; Song of Sol. 2; Psalm 50; and numerous other places.

["Except those days should be shortened"]{.class_4258} is an expression full of mystery and dormant information.

The Tribulation is said to be an even week of years---seven years (Dan. 9:25; Gen. 29:27) in two periods of 3½ years each (see Rev. 11:2--3, 12:6, 13:5). Yet these measurements are flexible and undefined. As God worked out history to Acts 7 so that He could swing either way on man's free will (and still preserve His own sovereign plan), so here the Lord has a situation set where the ["end"]{.class_4258} can be lengthened or shortened, according to the flow of events. Notice four different measurements given for this period in Daniel and Revelation.

1\. 1260 days (Dan. 7:25; Rev. 13:5).

2\. 1290 days (Dan. 12:11).

3\. 1335 days (Dan. 12:12).

4\. 2300 days (Dan. 8:14).

Thus, a mystery is set up which no Bible expositor or commentator has ever yet worked out to satisfactorily fulfill the requirements of Scripture. There is nothing in print on it. It may remain one of those mysteries which Daniel was told would remain sealed till ["the time of the end"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_504} itself. At any rate, no commentator has worked it out. Matthew 24:48--50 describes the condition of a man in this Great Tribulation who fails to reckon on this divine "time-table."

Verses 23--24 have been commented on under Matthew 7:15, 22 and 24:5, 11. Little more needs to be said except that every day some nut shows up trying to prove that Christ came in 1880-something---"You just didn't see it," or "Here is a picture of His face in a cloud," or "Come into the room and join hands and touch the table lightly and I will summon Him up, etc." The suckers increase in the direct ratio to which men accept the [RSV ]{.class_4257}and [ASV ]{.class_4257}as the word of God. As Biblical Christianity becomes a rare species of animal that is nearly extinct, it will become easier and easier to fool the people. As someone has so aptly said, "Every day the people are coming more and more to look upon the preacher as God, not knowing that every day the preachers are looking more and more upon the churches as God, not knowing that every day the churches are looking to the National Council as God, not knowing that the Council is looking to Rome as God, not knowing that Rome is looking on the pope as God, not knowing that the pope WORSHIPS THE GOD OF THIS WORLD (2 Cor. 4:4), PRETENDING THAT HE IS JESUS CHRIST."

["Behold, I have told you before"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 24:25).

24:26 Wherefore if they shall say unto you, Behold, he is in the desert; go not forth: behold, he is in the secret chambers; believe it not.

27 For as the lightning cometh out of the east, and shineth even unto the west; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.

28 For wheresoever the carcase is, there will the eagles be gathered together.

29 Immediately after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken:

30 And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_505}and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.

31 And he shall send his angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.

["Go not forth\...believe it not."]{.class_4258} The Tribulation Jew is warned against anyone deceiving him about the Second Coming. The actual coming will be so climactic and catastrophic and "apocalyptic" (!) that the Roman-Catholic-Gentile system will be torn up like a head of cabbage going through a Saladmaster (Dan. 2). There is no gradual "spiritual coming," spoken of by the Modernist. There\
is no secret "inner relation" presented at the seance, and there is no slipping into the playroom to see the Antichrist at Rome over TV, handing out "the mark." There is, instead, a visible, powerful, heavenly coming, in wrath and vengeance, to wipe off the face of the earth the last shred of Roman power. (So every Jew understood the Old Testament, and so it shall be in spite of the Catholic and Reformed theology that attempts to exposit the New Testament without the aid of Revelation. See Joel 2; Isa. 6, 63; Hab. 3; and Zech. 14.)

As it has been remarked before, Revelation opens the Old Testament and the Old Testament opens the New. To attempt to "open" Matthew, Acts, and Hebrews without a proper understanding of the Old Testament prophets is like trying to open a can of dog food with a pocket comb. No system that steals the promises from Israel (given in the Old Testament prophets) and filches them for the "church" is a Christian system or God-given system. It is the work of a dirty, low-down thief (John 10:1--10).

["For as the lightning."]{.class_4258} The Second Coming, then, is to be visible, thunderous, appalling, terrifying, and brilliant. It is going to be the exact opposite of everything taught by the postmillennialist, who is using the New Hampshire Confession of Faith (SBC) to sneak in a kingdom of CARE packages, Red Cross funds, United Drives, Cooperative Programs, and swimming pools in every back yard.

["For as the lightning cometh out of the east."]{.class_4258} The direction is of momentous importance. While the Catholic and []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_506}Reformer waste time calling attention to the fact that Titus (here we go---A.D. 70 again!) came in from the east after advancing from the west (Rome), we shall return to the Bible content and get the meat from the verse that Pope Gregory and Calvin missed.

"Cometh out of the east, and shineth even unto the west."

This direction is the direction of seven things in the word of God.

1\. It is the direction of the restoration of Israel under Nehemiah.

2\. It is the direction of the calling out of Abraham from Mesopotamia.

3\. It is the direction of Jacob's restoration from Laban (Gen. 28--34).

4\. It is the direction of the High Priest's entrance to the Tabernacle (Exodus--Leviticus).

5\. It is the direction of Christ's triumphant entry into Jerusalem on the foal of the ass (Matt. 19--21).

6\. It is the direction the gospel was commanded to be preached (Acts 16).

7\. Being the direction of the Sun's movement, it is the direction from which the "sun of righteousness" returns (Mal. 4:1--4).

This direction is counter to the rotation of the earth!

Briefly considering the movement of the world (and ["if any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him"]{.class_4258}---1 John 2:15) which is against the ["Sun,"]{.class_4258} we notice that:

1\. Adam and Eve were driven out west to east.

2\. Cain went out in exile from west to east.

3\. Jacob backslides and runs west to east to avoid Esau.

4\. The Jews are taken into captivity west to east (Jer. 52).

5\. The gospel is forbidden to be preached west to east (Acts 16).

6\. Those tribes EAST of Jordan go into captivity first (2 Kings 18).

7\. Rome comes west to east.

Summary and conclusions of the scientific data:

All history moves east to west.

The gospel goes east to west.

Revivals move east to west.

Discoveries move east to west.

Wars progress from east to west.

"Go west, young man, go west!" (Compare with Matt. 2:1--2.)

The Second Coming of Christ, then, is NOT death, it is NOT Pentecost, it is NOT the rapture, it is NOT Titus in A.D. 70. It is a literal, visible event to transpire in power, wrath, and glory when a crucified and risen Saviour returns to a sin-cursed earth to take that which rightfully belongs to Him, as the kingly ["last Adam"]{.class_4258} (Ezek. 21:26--27; 1 Cor. 15:45; Rev. 11:15).

["For wheresoever the carcase is."]{.class_4258} The exact passage is found in Luke 17:37, and again, there is not the slightest reference to the destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70. The "carcases" are the carcases of 200,000,000 horses and riders who came from the East in a United Nations army to help Roman "Catholics" wipe out Jerusalem! (See Rev. 16:14, 14:20, 9:16, 19:15--20; and Ezek. 38, 39.) By saying Matthew 24 is fulfilled in A.D. 70, it is made to apply to past, pagan Rome, instead of future, "papal" Rome! So the pope carefully protects the bishops, archbishops, priests, and cardinals from being detected for what they are---Bible-rejecting, Bible-hating, Bible-perverting religious professionalists. If NOT, then why refuse to believe what it says, as it says it, where it says it? Why go to the trouble of adding seven books that Jesus was against? Why approve of revisions based on corrupt manuscripts? Why interpret something that already says the opposite of the interpretation given? There is no answer to these questions. Papal Rome, being more corrupt than pagan Rome, can only answer in one way: coercion, assassination, imprisonment, and lies. This is the only answer she ever gave; coercion, through Congress and the inner workings of State funds; assassination, where all else failed; imprisonment of Bible believers in Spain, Italy, and Mexico (in the twentieth century!); and lies in the revision committees, where it was necessary to eliminate the Reformation text of the Authorized Version. Rome is consistent in some things!

["Immediately after the tribulation of those days."]{.class_4258} If there is a shred of doubt left as to the time that the verses in Matthew 24 describe, this verse (vs. 29) should end it. Where is there room for the church and the Church Age if Matthew 24:1--28 applies to A.D. 70? For if it did, the Lord would have returned 1,890 years ago, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_508}and there would have been no Church Age! Where is the "conversion of the world" found about which the Postmillennialist talks so much? If the Lord returns ["Immediately after the tribulation of those days,"]{.class_4258} and ["those days"]{.class_4258} are A.D. 70, where is the "conversion of the world?"

You may as well face it---the amillennial and postmillennial systems of Bible interpretation (Roman and Liberal) are total flops. They could not be more pitiful failures if they devoted their lives to failing. In spite of fizzle after default, and washout after miscarriage, and foolsmate after fiasco, they go right on floundering and crashing all over the word of God, leaving nothing behind but torn leaves of paper, bloody footprints, muddy fingerprints, and the stink of a decayed Platonism that would cause Michael the Archangel to don a gas mask. Forced by necessity to alter and change every verse they approach (since it will not fit into their denominational systems), Origen, Augustine, Eusebius, Jerome, Aquinas, Calvin, Strong, Berkhof, and the popes drag the church fathers and their Greek lexicons with them, and walking right by the book of Revelation placed dead in their way, they go to work with the gusto of a dog digging for a bone and produce a rutted path of holes that are good for nothing but for someone to trip in. None of the events in verses 29--31 have ever taken place, and the way you know this is because what you read has not taken place. The only way you can "make it take place" is to alter what you read!

["The sun\...the moon\...the stars\...the powers\...the sign\...the tribes\...the clouds...power\...great glory\...his angels\...a trumpet, his elect."]{.class_4258} In verses 29--31, we have the completed elements of the Second Advent listed together. Phenomena in the heavens, demoniac powers thrown to the earth, a sign in heaven, the repentance of the Jews who are left, the Advent with the heavenly hosts, and the rapture of the Tribulation saints immediately preceding the Battle of Armageddon.

The companion passages are as follows (and all will bear out the literal Bible believing interpretation of the passages without resorting to Roman Catholic mythology for a past historical application):

1\. ["The sun\...the moon\...the stars"]{.class_4258} (see Rev. 6:12--13, 8:12; []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_509}Joel 2:10, 3:15; Rev. 16:8; Amos 8:9; Hab. 3:11; Isa. 13:10; Jer. 15:9; Acts 2:20). (Notice in the last reference how the rejector of the book of Revelation will have to spiritualize the reference to make it literally apply to Pentecost, even though it did NOT take place at Pentecost \[Isa. 13:10; Joel 3:15, etc.\].)

2\. ["The powers of the heavens"]{.class_4258} (Eph. 6:10--13; Isa. 24:21; Rev. 12:1--4, 7--9). Notice, that a "star" in the Bible is defined as an angel (Rev. 1:20, 9:1). Thus scientists reject Revelation for being "unscientific" while they, at the same time, give "oscars" to "stars" (!) in Hollywood.

3\. The conversion of the ["tribes"]{.class_4258} of Israel. Notice in all references that they are literal TRIBES (where it says "tribes, read "tribes." Got it?) (Rev. 1:7; Zech. 12:9--10; Amos 8:10; Jer. 6:26; Rev. 7:1--11; Psa. 105:37; Zech. 9:1; Ezek. 37--48). The Jehovah's Witness who tries to make the twelve tribes of Revelation 7 (144,000) into individual "Christian believers" is just doing what thousands have always done---cut their spiritual jugular with a blunt butcher knife. You can overdo that "spiritual Jew" business, you know (Gal. 3), and most do!

4\. ["The clouds\...with power and great glory."]{.class_4258} The references are almost too numerous to list, but since nearly all occur in the Old Testament, the Catholic and Liberal are safe from understanding them. They have approached the New Testament with the un-Christian presumption that the Old Testament has all been fulfilled in the New. This presumption comes from rejection of the book of Revelation, which inserts a key into the Prophets and shows you that 85 percent of what they said comes after the Church Age. The verses, just the same, are there, and they are just as plain as John 3:16. (Gen. 49:9--11, 49:18, 49:23--24; Num. 23:23--24, 24:9, 17, 20--24; Deut. 32--33; Judg. 5; Psa. 110, 128, 129; Jude 14; Isa. 26--27; Dan. 7:13--15; Hab. 3; Mal. 4; Rev. 14:14, Zech. 1, 14; etc.) All Jews understood these passages. All understand them today, although they are blinded as to the First Coming of the Messiah to suffer and not to reign (1 Pet. 1:8--13). The Gentiles (being wise in their own conceit and supposing that blindness has happened permanently to Israel---see the [Amplified ]{.class_4257}blasphemy in 1 Thess. 2:14--16), have cut Israel off from her promises, stolen the promises []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_510}from her, and then apprehended them to make fit clothing for the great, apostate Mother Harlot Rome in Revelation chapters 17--18. The clothes don't fit. They were shoplifted from "Steins," not "Kennedys."

["The sign of the Son of man."]{.class_4258} The ["sign"]{.class_4258} has been left to the last since it is the most difficult to locate. Amillennial and postmillennial expositors will place it first and identify it because:

1\. All heretics like to begin with an obscure passage about which there is difficulty (like Heb. 6, 10; Rev. 7; or Matt. 5--7).

2\. All heretics like to line up with Roman tradition.

3\. All heretics like to state dogmatically what an obscure thing is (like Matt. 16:18; Acts 2:38; or 1 John 3:9) so people will stand in wide-eyed amazement and say, "My, isn't he brilliant?"

To clear the ground of rubbish before we try to plant a crop of truth, let us begin by seeing what Constantine saw, and how it was incorporated into Rome as a Christian type of thing; whereas, in reality, it appears in Revelation as the Mark of the Beast (Rev. 13:15--18).

The CROSS Constantine saw was an "X" cross, not a "T" cross (Lactantius, DeMortibus Persecutorum, pp. 565--566). In the Greek this is the "CH" of "Christ" (Gk.[ Χ]{.class_4263}), "Chi." The first three letters of Christ in Greek would be actually, one and two, "Chi," and three, "Rho" (Gk. Χριστό[ς]{.class_4263}). This is the sign found outside the Catholic churches on the highways in 1970.[ Ρ]{.class_4263} with an ["Χ]{.class_4263}" below it[---☧]{.class_4263}. The G.I. term is "PX." (Post exchange). The medical term in Rx (for "prescription" in a Rexall Drug Store). This "X" was supposed to have been inscribed on Constantine's shields before battle, and with it he emerged victorious. What is not so well known is the fact that, following his phony conversion, he also conquered the apostolic church with this sign, and by A.D. 325, the church had nearly vanished in Easter bunnies, wedding rings, Christmas trees, street processions, gold-basillicas, signs of the cross, holy water, prayers to Mary, and the conversion of Bad Wednesday to "Good Friday" as the day of the crucifixion. "X" will bear some study, and there is not time for study in this commentary, but just to outline the bare truths. (See publication [The Mark of the Beast,]{.class_4257} 1994, Bible Baptist Bookstore.)

1\. The cross is an instrument of capital punishment.

2\. It signifies the death of a criminal.

3\. It represents a CURSE (Gal 3:13).

4\. In all uses, the "X" stands for an unknown, lying, superstitious personage who is yet to appear on the stage in history.

a\. "I told a lie, but I had my fingers 'crossed.'"

b\. "He gave me the 'double cross.'"

c\. "You better hadn't 'cross' me."

d\. "Cross my heart and hope to die."

e\. "If you can't sign your name, just put 'X'."

5\. Words ending in "X" have application to the Man of Sin, the Antichrist. Thus (in Roman Catholic Latin!) Max, Hex, Jinx, Hoax, Sphinx (Rev. 13:2!), Ax, Box, Sax, Tax, Flux, Lax, Flex, Pox, Pax, Vox, Paradox, Six (Rev. 13:18), Jax, X-mas, EX-cathedra, Crux, and Rex (Rev. 6:2) apply to a North African, Egyptian Hamite, who will be Syrian Jew by extraction and will take over the Roman church.

(But I sense that some of the column is lagging and the double-time was too much for untrained troops, so we shall "take ten" and march off on a black-top instead of through all this swamp.) Suffice it to say that when a Black-robed North-African Religionist "CROSSES" himself, he does what he least expects he is doing---putting himself under a curse. The "sign of the cross" is the most un-Christian thing "Christians" ever invented. Like the Jewish "Star of David" (defined in Acts 7 as a demoniac symbol), it only bears a curse (2 Cor. 5:21). How far removed is the Bible from the teachings of "mother church"! What distance between a Papal bull and a Pauline belief! What a great gulf between Christmas and Christ, and between "Easter bunnies" and Johannine theology! My, my, what a difference between "Christian education" and Biblical enlightenment! How radical and how violent is the Reformation text of the King James (1611). No wonder the Catholics and Reformers ran to the Old Testament to steal armor from Israel! They had to get prepared for the most vicious, prejudiced, intolerant, fanatical, bigoted, inhuman attack ever made on man---the attack of the Holy Bible ([Authorized Version]{.class_4257}) on the chicanery and hypocrisy of "Christian" scholarship.

["The sign of the Son of man in heaven."]{.class_4258} The statement seems to be nearly beyond exposition. It is the ["Son of man"]{.class_4258} here referred to---not ["the Son of God,"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_512} as we find it in the Pauline Epistles and in the writings of John (who had all of Paul's revelation on his table in front of him while he wrote). But ["the sign of the Son of man,"]{.class_4258} if traced back Scripturally (and this is the only proper way for a Bible believer to do it), would wind up in the following places:

1\. The planets and sun and moon are themselves for signs. (By this token, phenomena on the sun could be the sign, for the sun is said to be a type of Christ---Mal. 4:2.)

2\. The sign of the rainbow (Noah) or circumcision (Abraham) is given to bring to remembrance a covenant (Gen. 9:13, 17:13).

3\. The sign of the Sabbath, which is strictly Israelish in application, no Gentile having ever been commanded to observe it in either Testament until the Millennium (Exod. 31:13--17; Col. 2:16).

4\. The sign of the Virgin Birth (Isa. 7:14), which would be signified in the constellations by Virgo (the virgin).

5\. The sign of Christ, which was the only sign that He gave a wicked generation and is said to be ["a sign which shall be spoken against"]{.class_4258}---Matt. 12:39--40; Luke 2:30--35). This sign is the sign of Jonah the prophet, who, strangely enough, fits the requirements of a Jewish remnant which will be in the Tribulation preaching to Gentiles! But how this SIGN can be seen in heaven is a problem that has yet to be worked out. Simeon mentions ["a sword"]{.class_4258} in connection with the sign in Luke 2:34--35, and since this sword is a soul-piercing sword (Heb. 4:12), it may have reference to the word of God at Armageddon (see Rev. 19:13, 15). But the assimilation of the material has not yet been accomplished.

["The sign of the Son of man in heaven"]{.class_4258} has yet to be found. The Constantinian "sign of the cross" is about as pertinent to the subject as an article on flower arranging would be to "The Rifle Platoon in Attack." Constantine's sign is the swastika of anti-Christian demonology, not the heavenly sign of the Saviour of Israel. Constantine's was an earthly mark given to conquer enemies for the glory of Rome; this sign is a heavenly one given by Jesus Christ for the glory of God. Where Constantine used his "sign" to corrupt the church, the Lord will use this one to bring Israel to repentance and belief. \[There is a difference, my friend; there is a difference. Two []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_513}things different are not equal. Work at that dictum a few years---I believe you can get it.\]

["Shall send his angels with a great sound of a trumpet."]{.class_4258} This trumpet will be the seventh in Revelation 11:15, and since it occurs immediately after the Tribulation (in the passage in Revelation as well as here), then the ANGELS present are those coming with Christ, who have already been transformed at the Rapture preceding the Tribulation! Notice the teaching---1 Thessalonians 4:13--17 shows that Christians will be caught out before the Tribulation (1 Thess. 5). First John 3 and Philippians 3:20--21 show that they will be "like Christ" when this takes place. Christ is "the Angel of the Lord" (Gal. 4:14); therefore, the members of the body of Christ at His return are ["as the angels of God in heaven"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 22:30). Reinforcement of this teaching is found in 2 Thessalonians 1:7--10; Jude 14; Deuteronomy 33:2; and Psalms 68:17 (see comments on Matt. 22:30).

["Gather together his elect."]{.class_4258} The passage has been mistakenly applied to the church on numerous occasions and the occasions have been multiplied till non-studious preachers are now teaching that the church will go through the Tribulation because of verse 29. This teaching is typical of the trouble a man can get into when he goes thrashing through Matthew 24 without taking trouble to believe what he is reading. It is only a step to Hebrews 9:28 (which also applies to the end of the Tribulation), and the Christian in the age of grace can produce the astounding and completely absurd doctrine that there will be a partial rapture---half of Christ's body goes up, and half stays down! Since the interpretation of the text is found in Zechariah 2:5--6; Isaiah 45:4, 27:13, and 65:9--12; there is no need to botch the thing up running around in 1 Thessalonians 4 and 1 Corinthians 15. (You see the book of Revelation opens the Old Testament, and the Old Testament opens Matt. 24. Matthew 24 is not connected doctrinally with the Pauline Epistles dealing with the rapture of the church. The doctrine is Israel's.)

["From one end of heaven to the other"]{.class_4258} has led present-day expositors to speculate on the possibility of people getting to the moon and populating it before the Rapture. (The other verses used for this are Obad. 1:4 and Amos 9:2.) The interpretation seems forced in view of the fact that the ["end of heaven"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_514} can only be made here to refer to where the heaven ends, as it is seen from the earth overhead---the first heaven (see notes on Gen.1:1). Matthew 11:23 and 12:42 use the words ["earth"]{.class_4258} and ["heaven"]{.class_4258} in this same manner; the believer will not fail to note the verse preceding Matthew 24:31, which speaks of the HEAVENS (plural) in contradistinction from the heaven of 24:31. Further, the expression ["four winds"]{.class_4258} is used as it is found in Revelation 7:1, and here the context is the EARTH, not the solar system or the moon. The attempted exposition may be traced then to that peculiar jarring rattle which the mythological monster "science" seems to bring upon men whenever it pronounces a fiat. Ninety percent of the time it may be safely ignored (1 Tim. 6:20). There is no need for any Bible expositor to make any concessions to an outfit so cheap and so crumby that they would let soap sales and girdle fasteners and toothpastes quote them as "latest scientific advances prove that\...." Nuts. Science always ran 500-3,000 years behind the Bible. When did it catch up?

The summation of the teaching found in verses 29--31 is that there will be a rapture at the end of the Tribulation (Rev. 11:11--12). This is a rapture of Jewish saints (Psa. 50:2--5) occurring at an undetermined interval (see notes on Matt. 24:22) before the battle of Armageddon (Isa. 26:20--21). It is analogous with the Feast of Tabernacles in the Old Testament (Lev. 23) and completes the third and final part of the first resurrection: the other two parts being Old Testament saints at the Feast of Unleavened Bread (Deut. 16:16), and New Testament saints at Pentecost, the Feast of Weeks (Deut. 16:16). Indications are that this POST-TRIBULATION rapture of dead and living saints (typified by Moses and Elijah) will occur forty days to six months before the actual advent itself---but ["the end is not yet"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 24:6).

We now approach a parable that deals with Israel in the end time.

24:32 Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When his branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh:

33 So likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_515}know that it is near, even at the doors.

34 Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass, till all these things be fulfilled.

35 Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away.

["Now learn a parable"]{.class_4258}: that is, "get it." Don't say it is figurative or obscure or that it only "teaches a general truth." There is not one parable in the Bible given to illustrate a "general truth"; this is pure scholastic hogwash. Every parable interpreted (Jesus interprets two in Matt. 13) has an exact, specific meaning to every detail:

"He that soweth\...is the Son of man."

"The good seed are the children of the kingdom."

"The seed is the word."

"The field is the world."

["These are they by the way side,"]{.class_4258} etc. The Satanic teaching, given in all Fundamental, Liberal, and Catholic schools, that a parable is only intended to teach a general truth, is nothing more or less than the scholars inventing a system to cover up their ignorance, because they know that they cannot explain the parable! Any system other than a premillennial system will so handicap the scholar that when he gets to the kingdom parables of Matthew 13, 20, 22, and 25, he CANNOT find a meaning to every detail; therefore, he hobbles out with one leg shorter than the other (Prov. 26:7). As he goes, he kicks every Bible believer in the shins, hoping to break their legs also. ["Now learn a parable."]{.class_4258} Learn it. Don't go running around to the Greek to explain something you were taught. ["Learn a parable."]{.class_4258}

["The fig tree."]{.class_4258} No need to go to the Greek here. Since the Bible is self-interpreting, we do not have to run to anyone except the Holy Spirit (1 John 2:26--27). The fig tree is the tree of self-righteousness. It is the only tree that God ever cursed (Gen. 3; Matt. 21:19--20). It stands for religious Israel without the fruit (Jer. 24:2, 5, 8), exactly as it stood in Eden for man's attempt to cover his righteousness with dead works and no fruit (Luke 13:6--9). Where Jesus says, ["the fig tree, and all the trees"]{.class_4258} (Luke 21:29), the reference is to all nations. The parable, then, speaks of the revival of national Israel []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_516}and the revival of the spirit of nationalism as opposed to colonialism.

The first part of the parable, then, is to observe the sign of nationalism and, in particular, the beginning of Israel as one of the nations yet unable to put forth FRUIT that will please God (John 15:1--6). ["When his branch is yet tender."]{.class_4258} The reader must find some place in history where Israel begins as a nation. This cannot be Exodus 15, 20, or 40, as this is past. It cannot be Joshua 24; Judges 13; 1 Samuel 15; or 2 Samuel 1--3, for this is past. It cannot be the restoration under Ezra, Zerubbabel or Nehemiah, for this is past. It will have to be a second restoration after A.D. 70. This amazing prophecy is found in Isaiah 11:11 and Jeremiah 3:18 and 16:14. It was fulfilled in our generation, 1948, with both amillennials (Strong, etc.) and postmillennials (pope, etc.) watching, and neither believing what was taking place right in front of their spectacles. The fulfillment is rejected in the Southern Baptist "New Hampshire confession." It is rejected by the Armstrongs ("The World Tomorrow"); it is rejected by the [Amplified Version; ]{.class_4257}and it is rejected by 80 percent of professing Christianity today.[ "Let God be true but every man a liar."]{.class_4258} The branch is tender now. ["Now learn a parable of the fig tree."]{.class_4258} You see, there is no danger of saying that, because if you begin with any system that refuses to believe the Bible literally, you can't learn it even if it is fulfilled in the headlines of the morning newspaper lying on your front stoop (John 12:37).

"Trees" in the Old Testament are likened to KINGS and Kingdoms (Judg. 9:7--14; Ezek. 15, 17, 19, 31; Dan. 4). The ["FIG TREE"]{.class_4258} is Israel, and the TREES are the other nations non-designated, exactly as outside of Christ or Israel there are only "GENTILES" (heathen).

"And putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh."

The audience could not miss the illustration, for not a day earlier they had seen a fig tree with leaves, and the time that they saw it was the Jewish first month (March--April). They knew that summer was only two months away. The Song of Solomon has an interesting addition to these facts in Chapter 2 (Song of Sol. 2:10--13). Notice the appearance of the FIG TREE, and this time it is bearing fruit. \[The []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_517}Rapture is found in the passage (Song of Sol. 2:10--11).\]

"So likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things."

The ["these things"]{.class_4258} will have to refer to the events in verses 15, 20--21, 24, 27, 29, and 31. ["Know that it is near, even at the doors."]{.class_4258} ["It,"]{.class_4258} in ["that it is near,"]{.class_4258} can only refer to the Second Advent. That is, "When you see the events taking place that I have just described, you can be certain that the end of the age and my coming (24:3) are just around the corner---they are right at the doors." ["Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass."]{.class_4258}

Now we are in a tight spot again---which generation? (Me thinks I hear the scurrying of feet in all directions accompanied by the slam of Greek lexicons on the desks and the banging of noses against lampstands.) Scofield first: Greek, ["genea'']{.class_4257} means that the family of Israel will be preserved (footnote, p. 1034, [Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4257}). Unfortunately, this would be a useless statement to make as none of the audience is worried about it, none of them asked about it. and those asking represent a very small part of it and were not concerned about their RACE. If this were not bad enough, the ["this generation"]{.class_4258} never refers to Israel as a race anywhere in the Bible. (Notice in Matt. 23:36 where there is an exact cross reference; Matt. 12:45 is another one, and Acts 2:40 is another one. In none of the Bible attempts to explain itself is there ever any suggestion of the Greek spook that Scofield and his board of editors have resurrected.) Again, we must sympathize, for Dr. Scofield is hard-pressed by the Fundamental heresy that "there are no signs preceding the rapture." Therefore, he does not dare imply that the generation that sees the establishment of Israel as a nation will live to see the advent, for this would be a pre-rapture sign, since Israel has become a nation and---no rapture yet! We see how a man's theology often blocks him out of the line before he can tackle a Bible truth. Here, to run to the Greek is to cut off your nose to spite your face. ["This generation"]{.class_4258} means (hold on to your hat now!) ["this generation!"]{.class_4258} Remarkable, isn't it? What a complicated book the Bible is.

["This generation"]{.class_4258} (that sees all these things, vs. 33)[ "shall not pass, till all these things"]{.class_4258} (vss. 29--31) ["be fulfilled."]{.class_4258} The implication is thrilling. The unbelieving Jew who goes back to []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_518}Palestine and sees the establishment of Israel as a nation (1948) will be in the generation that sees the Advent. The believing Christian who lives to see it (1948) will be in the generation that is caught out before the Advent!

How long is a generation?

According to the standard set down in Genesis 15:13, a generation is an even 100 years. According to Moses in Psalm 90, it is seventy years. According to the passage in Matthew 1:17, it is about forty-two years. By this system of figuring, a man BORN in 1948 would certainly live to see the Rapture and the Advent. A man born any earlier might not, and a man born earlier than 1920 would not have too good a chance. The exact date cannot be fixed, since there is no way to ascertain the reliability of the calendar since Pope Gregory fooled with it.

At any rate, it is clear that the generation that sees the events described in verses 14--28 will undoubtedly live to see the Advent of Jesus. This generation is described as the generation that is alive and sees the budding of the fig tree, the leaf-blossoming of national Israel. This date can be fixed with certainty---A.D. 1948. The no-rapture-signs school, of course, is merely an attempt to avoid the scandal of Judge Rutherford and Russell and the Seventh-day Adventists. "Date-setting" has fallen into such disrepute that not even pre­millennialists are bold enough to profess to know the ["times and the seasons."]{.class_4258} (Notwithstanding, they can be known just the same---see 1 Thess. 5:1--7 and Luke 12:54--57.)

"Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not\...."

Professor Pinkevitch of the USSR educational system (1952) put it adroitly when he said, "The world is getting too small for that Book (the Bible); either the world will have to go or that Book will have to go." We have in Matthew 24:35 the answer to the Socialist's problem---the world will have to go. A man said to Thomas Edison: "That comet is going to hit the world, and it's going to be the end of the world." The old inventor said blandly: "That's alright, we can do without it" (Heb. 12:25--28).

["But my words shall not."]{.class_4258} This promise that the words will be preserved (since the word is ["settled in heaven"]{.class_4258}---Psa. 119:89) []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_519}poses a curious problem to the Bible-perverters who followed Westcott and Hort back to the Dark Ages. If God has promised to preserve His word (and He has in Psa. 12:7) and is all-powerful and able to preserve it, how is it that He must stoop to using men who do not believe that it is His word in order to preserve it, and how is it that the men who believe that it is His word are not able to preserve it? To put it another way, why would anybody think that some inconsistent scholar like A. T. Robertson or War­field or Westcott and Hort or Kenneth Wuest, who began with presumption that the Bible was inaccurate and spurious (and accepted the seven-extra-book "Vaticanus" as authoritative), be used of God to preserve the "best translation," while men like the King James translators (who believed they were handling the verbally inspired authority of God Himself) would be able to produce only a "poor translation"? Someone is being terribly inconsistent.

Chrysostom, Gregory, Basil, Thaumaturgus, Savonarola, Huss, Wycliffe, Luther, Wesley, Whitefield, Moody, Torrey, Sunday, Finney, Spurgeon, Goforth, and Livingstone had no trouble finding the word of God and using it with telling results. Why is it that Origen, Augustine, Jerome, Thomas Aquinas, Calvin, Berkhof, Westcott, and Hort, Lightfoot, and Cardinal Spellman cannot find it and have to rely on the investigations of Bible-rejecting linguists to get a translation? Or to cut to the bone, if God can preserve it and promised to preserve it, why would He do the outrageous thing of picking men to preserve it who didn't believe it? Answer---He wouldn't and didn't and won't.

There isn't the slightest chance whatsoever that the [RSV, RV, ASV]{.class_4257}, or Nestle's text could be anything more than a religious feather-duster to keep the benches clean. The men behind them always began with the presumption that God had given nothing except a superior intelligence to themselves to determine subjectively what the word of God was, and all assumed the men whom God used were ignorant men. You would get more truth out of an hour with Sam Jones (1880) and an [AV]{.class_4257} than five years in seminary with Weigle (1960) and an [RSV.]{.class_4257}

["But my words shall not."]{.class_4258} Somewhere you can find what Jesus said, as He said it, where He said it, the way He intended to say []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_520}it. Where? Not in the Hesychian, Egyptian, North African Vaticanus of stale Alexandrian philosophy. Never. You could find an orange grove in Finland before you could find the words of God there. You may settle it once and for all, that if God had power to preserve His word and wanted it preserved and promised to preserve it, that it is available on every dime-store counter in the world in the language spoken by most people---English, in the version that God preserved through people who believed that the Bible was His word.

["But my words shall not pass away."]{.class_4258} In spite of "researchers" who dig up Egyptian forgeries, in spite of Dead Sea scrolls designed to give the glory to scholars, in spite of the entire Catholic church devoted to the manufacture of a Devil's bible, in spite of the Greek linguist's pseudo "texts," in spite of the concentrated attacks through fifteen centuries of the most brilliant minds in existence, in spite of fifty translations on the market that are nothing but illicit counterfeits, in spite of the combined efforts of Science, Philosophy, and Psychology to rid the world of His words\...His ["words shall not pass away."]{.class_4258} You can get a copy for a dollar at the nearest dollar store; just be careful that it is not the North African plagiarism of Origen and Jerome called "The CATHOLIC BIBLE." And make sure that it is not one of the mutilated transliterations called the ["RSV]{.class_4257}" or the "[ASV]{.class_4257}," which are both gnostic depravations of the same text.

24:36 But of that day and hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels of heaven, but my Father only.

37 But as the days of Noe were, so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.

38 For as in the days that were before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noe entered into the ark,

39 And knew not until the flood came, and took them all away; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.

40 Then shall two be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.

41 Two women shall be grinding at the mill; the one shall be taken, and the other left.

["Knoweth no man, no, not the angels of heaven."]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_521} The thing that is "unknown" (according to the statement) is the ["day and hour"]{.class_4258} of the Advent. This truth cannot be brought home with too much force to the Fundamentalist, as he has been taught from his youth that the Second Coming is "IMMINENT" (not found in Scripture!) and that any attempts to set dates are Satanic and unscriptural. First Thessalonians 5:1--6 contradicts this position, however, as does also the appearance of the Laodicean church (Rev. 3:22) immediately preceding the Rapture---Revelation 4:1--2.

In the gospel of Mark, we find the additional ["neither the Son, but the Father,"]{.class_4258} thus including Christ (in His earthly ministry) as ignorant of the exact date of the Advent.

The reader will be careful to notice that the ["neither the Son, but the Father"]{.class_4258} is not found in Matthew 24:36, as it stands in the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 version of the Textus Receptus (Byzantine text of a Syrian type, preserved for soul winners in the Greek church). The Alexandrian type text (used by Nestle and Westcott and Hort) contains the reading in both Mark and Matthew.

\[This opens up a remarkable field of research in the investigation of the causes and source of corruptions found in the Vaticanus manuscript used for the [RSV ]{.class_4257}and [ASV ]{.class_4257}(Egyptian text for dead-orthodox scholars, preserved by Catholic monks). Briefly, the situation is this: "Aleph" (Sinaiticus) and "B" (Vaticanus) have added "neither the Son" in Matthew 24:36; and to this reading agree Origen (!), Irenaeus, Westcott and Hort, Nestle, Tischendorf, and Lachmann. Here we find a beautiful, cohesive, unified picture of perfect ecumenical cooperation against the [King James ]{.class_4257}reading. Notice that the men and manuscripts listed are all in agreement. They are all North African, dead-orthodox (or infidel), Bible-rejecting naturalists who accept Rome as the final authority in matters of textual criticism Rome, receiving her theology and canon from Origen in Alexandria, Egypt (A.D. 184--254).\]

Aleph (Sinaiticus), in one copy, does not have the reading found in the Catholic [ASV, RSV, RV]{.class_4257}, and [Douay Challoner]{.class_4257}. Furthermore, the majority of all Greek manuscripts do not contain the reading; furthermore, the Old Latin (before Jerome and Pope Gregory!) and the old Syriac do not have "neither the son" in Matthew 24:36. []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_522}Furthermore, the old Sahidic (second and third century, before "Vaticanus") does not have it; furthermore, Ambrosius (397) and Heironymus (420) do not recognize it as authoritative. By what authority then is it inserted into the text?

Westcott and Hort, here (as in Matt. 14:3--6), lay their cards on the table and reveal one pair with a joker, for the text of the [RV, RSV]{.class_4257}, and [ASV]{.class_4257}, while the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 translators produce a royal flush, king-high. You see, Westcott and Hort sold the committees of the [ASV, RV]{.class_4257}, and [RSV ]{.class_4257}(as well as the Greek teachers in all the fundamental schools) on the theory that the Syrian text of the Byzantine period (used in the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611) was a copy of the Vaticanus, Sinaiticus, and Cantabregensis combined. That is, they claimed that the Syrian-type text was a later text, and this was proved by the fact that it contained parts of readings from the Western-type text and parts of readings from the Alexandrian-type text. This supposed "combining" of texts is to prove that the Syrian readings of the Textus Receptus are "conflate readings," made by copying the Vaticanus (Alexandrian) and Cantabregensis ("D"). The theory is carried further that since "D" has the LONGER readings, that the short readings of VATICANUS represent a "neutral" text---the purest to be found. \[This is the theory on which the pope will force all Protestant Bible schools to accept a Roman Catholic Bible, containing the Apocrypha, for Vaticanus was written in ITALY and contains Tobit, Judith, Bel and the Dragon, etc.\]

In spite of Westcott and Hort's "high standing" among the scholars, a deathly fact remains staring the student in the face, and that is the fact that in over 7,900 verses in the New Testament, Westcott and Hort only found eight (8) cases of "conflate readings." That is, one out of about 1,000 verses.

This is the "proof" that the [ASV ]{.class_4257}(1901) is more accurate than the King James 1611.

But when Matthew 24:36 is approached, a "wild scene" takes place ("like it's way out, man, way out!"). The [ASV, RSV, RV]{.class_4257}, and Catholic Bible assume that the passage, ["neither the Son,"]{.class_4258} was removed by orthodox scribes because they resented the inference it had that Christ was not omniscient; therefore, they accept the "Vaticanus" which has the addition as the authentic reading. But []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_523}here, all logic, common sense, reason, and honesty falls apart; for if this was done, why did not the scribe remove it from Mark also (Mark 13:32)? If the Textus Receptus of the King James was derived by conflating two other type manuscripts, how is it that here both types were ignored?

You see, Westcott and Hort are up the creek without a paddle. The shearing-pin is busted on the outboard, and there are no replacements. You know, it is pitiful to see a man, who can read uncial letters on vellum parchment and conjugate the Mi-verb in Greek, stumble and break his fool neck over such a simple problem. It is like watching a man standing in wet cement, a foot deep, trying to tie his shoe laces.

1\. If Western "D" has it and Egyptian "B" has it, and the Textus Receptus is a combination (conflation) of Western and Egyptian, then the Textus Receptus has to have it.

2\. If it had it and it was taken out, why was it not taken out of Mark 13:32, where it is also found in the Western (D) and the Egyptian (B)?

3\. Is it not more reasonable (if not mandatory) to suppose that the corrupt Italian manuscripts of "D" (West-Rome) and "B" (Egyptian, but written in ITALY according to Westcott and Hort) added to the original text a favorite verse they found in Mark, hoping to emphasize the fact that Jesus was not omniscient?

4\. If this is supposed, what happens to Westcott and Hort's theory that Vaticanus is a pure text and the Syrian is a later corruption?

Matthew 24:36 reveals the Western and Egyptian manuscripts for what they are---illegitimate corruptions from forged manuscripts, written for the purpose of BROW-BEATING the soul-winning Christians of A.D. 70--400 who were using the Syrian text of the apostles (written in Asia Minor and Palestine).

You can rest assured that with all their learning and theological attainments, Westcott and Hort and their retinue (popes foremost) simply do not qualify as real Bible scholars.

["But as the days of Noe."]{.class_4258} (The spelling ["Noe"]{.class_4258} coming from the Greek, as "Noah" from the Hebrew; see Matt. 1:2--8.)

["The days of Noe"]{.class_4258} offer some difficulty. It has already been []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_524}mentioned that Matthew 24 deals primarily with the future Tribulation in a Jewish setting, and the proof of this lies also in the constant use of the expression ["Son of man"]{.class_4258} (which is not the designation for the Saviour in relation to the Christian, awaiting the Rapture). However, in this passage between the paragraphs (vss. 29, 41), there may be some reference to the Church Age. If this seems to be heresy, let it be remembered that there is knowledge of a Gentile conversion period before the Tribulation, found in Matthew 12:18--21. The Tribulation passages do not occur until verses 22--45 in the twelfth chapter of Matthew. Another thing that adds weight to the application is the fact that Enoch, a Gentile, is "caught out" before the flood (Gen. 5), and since he is the only man in the Bible who never has died or never will die (see notes on Matt. 17:3--4, 11), he cannot represent a Tribulation saint, either Jew or Gentile---he will have to represent the Body of Christ caught out (John 11:26; 1 Cor. 15:50--53).

That isn't all---Noah preserves animals through the flood, and animals are stated to be "Gentiles" (see Acts 10:12, 15, 16, 19; Matt. 15:26). The passage, then, in Matthew 24:37 may have some reference to the end of the Church Age. It is certain that the ["one shall be taken, and the other left"]{.class_4258} in Luke 17:34--37 cannot be the Church Age, for the ["days of Lot" ]{.class_4258}are said to be ["in the day when the Son of man is REVEALED"]{.class_4258} (Luke 17:30). The gruesome banquet of carrion in Luke 17:37 further identifies the place and time of the passage as the END of the tribulation. But the ["days of Noe"]{.class_4258} are said merely to be ["the DAYS of the Son of man"]{.class_4258} (Luke 17:26); there can be an overlapping into the Church Age, and there probably is. (We do not want to make the mistake of O'Hair, Stam, and Bullinger in supposing because the mystery had not been revealed till Eph. 3, that it was not there. Eve is a type of the Church in Gen. 2--3, before Paul's mother knew she was going to have a baby!)

The passage, then, in Matthew 24:37--41 can have some application to the Rapture, by virtue of the other things involved in the rest of the Bible. It is certain, however, that no one at the time of this discourse could have had the remotest idea about the application (John 16:10--12). If the FLOOD is a type of the Tribulation, then the ["days of Noe"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_525} are found in Genesis 4--6, and this would account for the fanatical obsession of neo-orthodoxy to do away with the literal belief of this portion of the Bible. If we are on the eve of the Rapture, we should be paying heed to Genesis 4--6. If there is application, then the ones ["TAKEN"]{.class_4258} are those saved, while those ["LEFT"]{.class_4258} are left to judgment. In the days of Lot, the reverse is true; ["THOSE TAKEN"]{.class_4258} are killed, those ["LEFT"]{.class_4258} are as Lot. Thus the Lord gives a picture of both ends of the Tribulation: Noah at the end of the Church Age; Lot at the end of Daniel's Seventieth week.

24:42 Watch therefore: for ye know not what hour your Lord doth come.

43 But know this, that if the goodman of the house had known in what watch the thief would come, he would have watched, and would not have suffered his house to be broken up.

44 Therefore be ye also ready: for in such an hour as ye think not the Son of man cometh.

The exhortation again is for the HOUR (vss. 36, 42, 44) and the DAY (vs. 50). Mark cuts it further to WATCHES (Mark 13:34--35, 13:32). The context is still Jewish at the end of the Tribulation, and this is confirmed by Luke's statement in Luke 12:36, where the Lord is said to be returning from a wedding (see Matt. 25:11). Since the marriage of the Lamb (2 Cor. 11:1--4; Psa. 45; Song of Sol. 6; Rev. 19:6--10) takes place after the Rapture and before the Advent, then it is certain that the expression ["return from the wedding"]{.class_4258} (Luke 12:36) is a reference to the servant waiting for His Lord to return at the ADVENT, not the Rapture. (Notice how Fundamentalists get this balled up in Matt. 25 and make the ["virgins"]{.class_4258} Christians waiting for the Rapture to get married; whereas, none are Christians, none are going to get married, none are the bride, none marry the bridegroom, and none get the OIL "free," by grace!)

In the passage before us (Matt. 24:42--44), the Son of man is likened to a thief (hence, we are not surprised to find Him crucified between two thieves!), and His coming is a night-time coming to a house where the goodman of the house is sleeping instead of watching (1 Thess. 5:1--5). The applications therefore are numerous. []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_526}Historically, the advent could have occurred before Acts 7, and therefore, the day and the hour are unknown to Christ until AFTER His ascension (Acts 1). Spiritually speaking, the Lord can come anytime; therefore, we should watch and not get sleepy (1 Thess. 5:1--10). Doctrinally, we find four watches in a Jewish night, representing 500 years apiece in the Church Age; Christ's departure being as the sun going down (John 11:9--10) and His return as the sun coming up (Mal. 4:2). The first watch ends at 9 P.M. with Pope Gregory the Great; the second watch ends at midnight with the Crusades; the third ends at 1500 with Martin Luther (1520); the Christian is now in the fourth watch (knowing the times and seasons perfectly) and is awaiting the RISE of ["the DAY star"]{.class_4258} (2 Pet. 1:19). He knows in what watch his Lord is coming, although he does not know the ["day"]{.class_4258} or the ["hour"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 14:25). This, of course, all lies hidden until the writing of the Pauline epistles and could have remained dormant and unfulfilled if the Jews had received Stephen's message and brought on the deliverance of Israel from Rome by a bloody and vengeful advent from heaven. (But the water is getting deep. I heard a premillennialist, back yonder, going down for the third time.)

24:45 Who then is a faithful and wise servant, whom his lord hath made ruler over his household, to give them meat in due season?

46 Blessed is that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.

47 Verily I say unto you, That he shall make him ruler over all his goods.

48 But and if that evil servant shall say in his heart, My lord delayeth his coming;

49 And shall begin to smite his fellow­servants, and to eat and drink with the drunken;

50 The lord of that servant shall come in a day when he looketh not for him, and in an hour that he is not aware of,

51 And shall cut him asunder, and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

The man discussed is a SERVANT, not a "friend" (John 15:15). He is waiting for the Lord to return at the ADVENT, not the Rapture. He has a possibility of losing salvation (!), and he loses it. (Again, I hear the Presbyterians and Baptists running to their notebooks to explain how he just professed "to have it," but it is useless. Somewhere down the line, you will have to believe what it says and trust God to iron out the wrinkles.)

\[One of the by-products of rejecting the [King James Bible]{.class_4257} as the word of God was the end of revelation to the Bible-believing segment of America. Whereas God continued to reveal new and wonderful truths through His word up to 1904, at this point there began a slackening off. With the exception of an occasional Scofield on the "Body Mystery" or a Stam on the book of Acts, revelation ceased to progress. The Baptists stayed where they were stuck and likewise the Methodists, Presbyterians, Lutherans, and Episcopalians (the Catholics, of course, were stuck like ply-bond before 1546). The years 1904 to 1960 witnessed the Bible-believing leaders running to the corrupt Westcott and Hort Vatican text for vertical studies in the New Testament. Consequently, all they did was find PROOF (in "the Greek") for the doctrines they already believed; none discovered anything new, and the proof of this lies in the flamboyant and exaggerated attempts that the modern teacher makes in an effort to produce a "nugget." All he produces is a piece of burnt charcoal. Typical is Kenneth Wuest's vain and fruitless effort to prove "from the Greek" that the "falling away" of 2 Thessalonians 2 is not a "falling away," but a catching up! Running the mi-verb back to another root, the poor man overlooks the shocking fact that the Holy Spirit would not have even used a verb about which there was any doubt in discussing the rapture: the verbs in 1 Corinthians 15; 1 Thessalonians 4; Song of Solomon 2; Psalm 50; Isaiah 26; Revelation 4, 11, which deal with the subject, have nothing to do with "falling away" or anything like it.\]
