To return to the passage in Matthew 24, we see that (and twice again in Matt. 25) an ordinary reader could tell immediately that a man who had salvation lost it! The Holiness people have no trouble with the text anymore than they do with Hebrews 6 and 10. They simply believe what is written. The Baptist goes crazy running to the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_528}Greek (for example, Dr. DeHaan's exposition of Heb. 10, making it "the judgment seat of Christ") to prove that "you can't lose it," steps all over the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 and winds up by saying: "this passage is a little obscure; a better reading would be, a more accurate translation is, etc." Thus the Conservative, by going to the "original Greek" (there is no such thing), slams the door of revelation in his face; in his desperate attempt to reinforce his denominational traditions, he backs off a cliff, where God can no longer show him anything. Where the Holiness preacher makes the mistake of not seeing where the passage goes and thereby teaches a Christian in this age can lose it, the Calvinist does just as badly (or worse) and perverts the passage to make it fit this dispensation, and it does not fit.

Facing Matthew 24:45--51, the Lord is ["his lord"]{.class_4258} and ["My Lord,"]{.class_4258} and the man is in the household. The Lord is ["the lord of THAT servant,"]{.class_4258} and the servant does go to hell---at the Second Advent, not the Rapture. (Notice the same excuses in Matt. 25:20--30.) The man loses it. Here we come upon one of the great dispensational truths of prophecy. Stam, O'Hair, and Bullinger could not find it, for they believed that the church was the subject of revelation, and Israel the subject of prophecy; therefore, they did not waste too much time with Matthew 24--25; Mark 13; Luke 17 and 21. Scofield, DeHaan, Ironside, and Theodore Epp could not find it, for all were raised on Premillennial doctrine which teaches that the passages in Matthew 24 and 25 refer to "PROFESSORS," not "POSSESSORS." The Catholic and Liberal could not possibly find it, for they have always taught salvation by works and damnation by lack of works, and they would agree with the Church of God and Assembly of God and the Pentecostals on the interpretation: the man lost it.

Since the student is commanded to search the Scriptures and to study, it might be well to simply abandon all pre-conceived teachings on the subject and begin with the premise that if a man (ANY MAN) has to change the verses to prove his point, he obviously is either ignorant or a deliberate liar. This line of reasoning is safe. It presumes there is an absolute, perfect standard, infallibly correct, without error. It is based on the POSITIVE supposition that is the thesis of this commentary: i.e., that until it []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_529}has been proved conclusively to be in error, the King James 1611 is always dead-right, and the scholar (be he liberal, Catholic, Neo-orthodox, Evangelical, Conservative, Reformed, or Fundamental) is always 100 percent dead- wrong.

This is a fair and decent bias to assume, considering the fact that no one has ever assumed it before, and considering the fact that all commentators assume that they know more than God, if God did really have a hand in the [AV]{.class_4257}. Since all commentators write from the bias and prejudice that they are well able to correct the God-honored text, we write from the biased and Christian prejudice that the [King James ]{.class_4257}1611 Bible is well able to correct any of them (Heb. 4:12--13).

Now, examine the Bible facts that stand without mutilation, distortion, or "Greekifying."

1\. No man from Adam to Christ is "BORN AGAIN."

2\. The NEW BIRTH begins with the regenerating work of the Holy Spirit (John 3:3--6; Titus 3:5; Rom. 8:14--17).

3\. John the Baptist ends a line ["born of women"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 11:11; Luke 16:16).

4\. The New Birth brings in a dispensation in which the believer is made an organic part of Jesus Christ (1 Cor. 12; Gal. 3; Eph. 5).

5\. As being ["IN CHRIST"]{.class_4258} (2 Cor. 5:17; Col. 2:10; Rom. 7:1--4), the believer is part of a BODY that is eternally secure (John 14:16--17; 1 Cor. 1:8--9).

(This truth the Calvinist has; the Arminians miss it running around in Matthew, Acts, and Hebrews.)

6\. There is no indication, whatsoever, that under the Law (Exod. 20 to Acts 2) any believer but David had this security, nor is there any indication that any believer will have it one second after the Body of Christ is caught up and out off this planet.

7\. To the contrary, every verse which deals with the Jewish dispensation to follow the Church Age indicates that faith and works justify, and that WORKS are not only a part of the fruit of salvation, but part of the operation that justifies the man. (Read carefully and prayerfully the following references, foregoing any attempt to change them into what you were taught they meant: Rev. 12:17, 14:12; James 1:1, 2:24, 5:19--20; Matt. 25:1--9, 25:26--30; Heb. 3:6, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_530}14, 6:4--6, 10:26--31.)

8\. Notice that in the last reference (Heb. 10:25--31), there is not one reference to a born-again believer! The name of the book is "HEBREWS," and right in front of your face (in vs. 30) is the quotation from Deuteronomy 32:35--36, with the ["his people"]{.class_4258} defined as it is defined anywhere in the Bible---Israel, not the Body of Christ. A man says, "but the spiritual Israel, etc." Yes, but there is a time when decent people should shut their mouths and learn, instead of just blowing hot air all over the place to cloud the mirror of God's word.

9\. There isn't a verse in the Bible that teaches that a man "CAN LOSE IT" that does not fit perfectly into Daniel's Seventieth Week, without ANYONE distorting ANYTHING in the verse. To resort to the Westcott and Hort Greek to destroy the [AV ]{.class_4257}is only proof of two things: one, you are so lazy and ignorant you will not study to learn the truth; or two, you are so stuck on yourself that you would change the Bible to prove your own point. (The pope will fit either slot; how about YOU?)

A man, who believes the Bible and believes that a God who is powerful enough to inspire it can preserve it, can state dogmatically and with absolute assurance that the SERVANT of Matthew 24:46--51 had it and "lost it"! He did NOT ["endure to the end,"]{.class_4258} as instructed---see Matthew 24:13---in the context. His oil "ran out." Not being IN Christ (and being unable to get IN Christ, because the Body of Christ has left at the Rapture), the believer of the Tribulation (see Rev. 6--7) is saved by faith and works, and his works must hold up till the END of the Tribulation even unto death. This period, then, is not TODAY, ["the day of salvation,"]{.class_4258} it is not "the day of grace," and it is not "the dispensation of the gospel of the grace of God." It is an Old Testament, legal, Jewish dispensation in Daniel's Seventieth Week where the archangel Michael stands up for Israel (Dan. 12). More than fifty verses in the New Testament refer to this "losing of salvation" by a man who does not "hold out faithful."

Where the Holiness groups failed to study to "rightly divide the word," the Baptists and Presbyterians did worse: they changed the word to force it to comply with their own limited knowledge. Either way, you turn off the light that God has given and go right on in the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw2889.xhtml_page_531}same old rut, which winds up between seven mountains in Italy.

If we have been too harsh on "the brethren" in calling these things to their attention, may it be mentioned that the MOTIVE for both perversions is a very fine thing. The Holiness preacher wants to do justice to the word as written and believe that God has said something that is final, that is a good motive. The Calvinist wants to enforce the doctrine of the eternal security of the believer so believers can enjoy the liberty and grace they have in the gospel. Both motives are excellent. Both overlook a very, very important truth. This truth may be stated as follows: the entire Bible is not written to be taught as Church-doctrine to New Testament believers between Pentecost and the Rapture; whereas, the entire Scriptures are for comfort and learning and "examples" (Rom. 15:4; 1 Cor. 10:8-- 14) to the Christian.

It would be the wildest conceit to think that God devoted 773,692 words to a small group of people between A.D. 70 and A.D. 2000. Three quarters of the Bible is written before Jesus appears in the flesh. (This illustrates well the folly of the supercilious "Knights of Columbus," who advertise the Bible as the "CHURCH'S BOOK" and a "catholic book." There were no Catholics and no Church of Christ present when three-fourths of the Bible was written!)

Thus, with well meaning, best wishes, sincere resolutions, and kindest regards, the Calvinist and the Arminian (as their Catholic ancestors) charge into Matthew 24--25; Hebrews 6, 10; and 2 Peter 2 butchering up the verses till the Bible looks like Hiroshima on the 7th of August, 1945. Unable to grasp the premillennial system of unraveling the verses (or being unwilling to accept what appears when the unraveling begins), the entire body of Christ has now resorted to the "Greek" to prove what already has been shown. In so doing, they have deliberately refused to accept any further light. They are afraid that further light will upset some of their pet doctrines, so they join their Catholic forefathers and sit down on their blessed assurance and wait for 666 to show up!

The "servant" in Matthew 24:51 "HAD IT" in the Tribulation and missed the calculation on the end of the seven years (see notes on Matt. 24:6, 22) and "LOST IT" and went to hell. That isn't Baptist doctrine, but it's Bible doctrine.

[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3182.xhtml}

# Chapter 25 {.heading_84-0}

25:1 Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins, which took their lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegroom.

2 And five of them were wise, and five were foolish.

3 They that were foolish took their lamps, and took no oil with them:

4 But the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.

5 While the bridegroom tarried, they all slumbered and slept.

6 And at midnight there was a cry made, Behold, the bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him.

The passage introduces the eleventh parable on the Kingdom of Heaven; the others being 22:1, 20:1, and 18:23. (The first seven are all found in one chapter---Chapter 13.) Since this eleventh parable is part of the Olivet discourse of Chapters 24--25, it is not surprising to find 95 percent of all interpreters going berserk on the passage and tearing off through it like a year-old pup through a covey of quail.

The first verse offers a marvelous chance for the Westcott and Hort corrupters to insert a reading which would have prevented a wrong interpretation of the passage (at least by premillennialists). "D" and "Theta" (representing Caesarian and Western witnesses), along with the complete Latin tradition and most Syrian translations, insert "to meet the bridegroom and the bride!" (This is confirmed, however, in the [AV, ]{.class_4257}without the help of the Greek, in the English of Luke 12:36. Thus, the Lord has performed a remarkable double-check throughout history to prevent anyone from misinterpreting anything; where men have referred to corrupt manuscript evidence to back it up, and where a corrupt witness occasionally has a correct or helpful reading, the translators of the [AV ]{.class_4257}have, unwittingly, inserted the reading in English in another place!)

The passage, as it stands, has long been used by Arminians to prove you "can lose it," and long used by Calvinists to prove either that five virgins were "professors" or else that since it doesn't make []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3182.xhtml_page_533}sense, a parable only "teaches a general truth" and cannot be applied specifically. But both of these decrepit and senile interpretations belong to the same category---the sick bay. If you cannot take what it says, as it says it, where it says it, you obviously have an ulterior motive in changing it. Just for refreshment, let us presume that the [AV]{.class_4257} is correct as it stands and meets the requirements of all 31,000 verses connected with it without any contradictions or inconsistencies.

To understand all the elements involved, let us include the entire passage before attempting to comment.

25:7 Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lamps.

8 And the foolish said unto the wise, Give us of your oil; for our lamps are gone out.

9 But the wise answered, saying, Not so; lest there be not enough for us and you: but go ye rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves.

10 And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came; and they that were ready went in with him to the marriage: and the door was shut.

11 Afterward came also the other virgins, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us.

12 But he answered and said, Verily I say unto you, I know you not.

Now, beginning at the beginning, and carefully and reverently sorting the facts, assessing them in the belief that the Bible is far superior to any commentary written on it, let us notice:

1\. Those involved are virgins (plural), not "a virgin."

2\. They go to marry no one; they go to MEET Someone.

3\. They go to meet Someone who is already married (Luke 12:36).

4\. All had oil to start with, and some lost it (vss. 4, 7--8).

5\. If oil is a type of the HOLY SPIRIT, works are involved in obtaining a fresh supply of the Holy Spirit (vss. 8--9).

6\. In 2 Kings 4:7 (where a transaction similiar to this one takes []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3182.xhtml_page_534}place), the entire setting is JEWISH.

7\. There is no "twinkling of an eye" or "in a moment" or anything else involved in the passage.

8\. It is the "Son of man" coming as a married bridegroom (vs. 13), NOT "the Son of God" coming for His bride! Things different are not equal.

If the standard evangelistic (Calvinistic) interpretation is taken, then five possessed while five professed. But this application is ridiculous if it be taught as DOCTRINE. (If preached inspirationally, for the salvation of sinners, it would make some sense as God could honor the general truth that "not all who profess are ready." But this is a piece-meal way to handle the divine and holy word of the Living God.)

For example, if the thing is true DOCTRINALLY, then how can those who profess ["buy"]{.class_4258} the Holy Spirit (Acts 8:20)? If those who possessed are part of the "bride," why is it that they do not go out till after the marriage and then only go to meet the Bridegroom? Someone is badly in error. To goof off completely, the entire body of Reformed Scholarship links arms and states (in tones of sanctimonious authority): "A parable is only intended to teach a general truth. You cannot teach doctrine from a parable." You can't? Who can't? YOU? Why can't you? If the Lord can, or the Holy Spirit can, then why do you say "you" as though it included everyone? (We had a saying back in the Infantry \[1940--1945\] for this type of thing, but I will not repeat it.)

In the standard Holiness (Arminian) message, the thing comes out with a Spirit-filled Christian losing the Holy Spirit and running out of the Holy Spirit, because: one, there was not enough to go around (!)---verse 9, and two, because you had to WORK to get it (vs. 9). Thus the Holiness comes out with a split rapture, based on Hebrews 9:28, with half of the body of Christ going up, and half amputated and staying.

\[Something again is not only "rotten in Denmark," but "putrid in Alabama, Georgia, and Mississippi."\]

Since the Holy Spirit's method of teaching is Scripture with Scripture, studying right divisions and believing what is written, where it is found (1 Cor. 2:11--13; 2 Tim. 2:15; Isa. 28:9--13, 29:11--[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3182.xhtml_page_535}14), let us lay out the Scriptural definitions of the words found in the Scripture, using the Scripture's interpretation of itself.

1\. ["Virgins"]{.class_4258} (plural) are found in the Tribulation, not the Church Age (Rev. 14:1--6).

2\. "The VIRGIN bride" of Christ (2 Cor. 11:1--3) is never called ["virgins"]{.class_4258} anywhere, and is always denominated one chaste virgin, in distinction from ["virgins"]{.class_4258} (Song of Sol. 6:8--9, Psa. 45:13--14).

3\. The ["virgins"]{.class_4258} of the Tribulation follow the bride (Psa. 45:13--14), and they follow here at the end of the Tribulation (Matt. 24:29--31, Psa. 50:3--5).

4\. There IS an element of WORKS connected with their salvation. (See comments on passages in Matt. 24:13, 15--20; Rev. 14:12.)

5\. Some of the ["virgins"]{.class_4258} can lose it and DO (see comments under Matt. 24:47--51). Consequently, Hebrews 9:28 is fair warning that there will be a split-rapture at the end of the Tribulation.

6\. Since Old Testament times return (law and all!) when the Body of Christ leaves (see comments under Matt. 24:45--51), there is an element of WORKS connected with salvation (Rom. 10:5), and David fully realized that these works were connected with losing the Holy Spirit! (Psa. 51:11).

Thus, the parable is an accurate, scientific, exact, detailed, doctrinal description of a future time and place in which faith and works justify (James 1:1 and 2:24). Every detail of it has a meaning, and it means what it says. It does not mean what any seminary or university has TAUGHT that it meant for 1920 years. The only group who could find the meaning would be premillennial Calvinists (a shocking minority!). Before they would teach ANYTHING that seemed like a compromise with the "gospel of the grace of God," they would destroy the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 Bible, and so have they done.

Rather than give an inch and admit that someone else had perhaps found a truth (undiscovered by them), the eternal-security believers have relegated the entire New Testament to Pauline church doctrine for the Gentile believer. In so doing, they have pulled the blinds shut on the sunlight of future revelation and are left to hash and rehash the work of Pember, Larkin, Scofield, Gaebelein, and Pettingill---all "precious shining lights in their day"; but it is no longer "their day." It is later than you think.

25:13 Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day nor the hour wherein the Son of man cometh.

The passage then, historically, applies to Israel in the first part of Acts, contingent upon their receiving the Kingdom message, which they did not receive. Having refused it, the passage becomes correct doctrine for an age in which they WILL receive it---the Tribulation.

Inspirationally, the passage teaches, "some have it---some don't." Illuminating passages on the parable, which set forth types of the doctrine, are found in 2 Samuel 15:16 where David leaves concubines, Esther 2:1--3 where virgins are sought for a King, and Job 3:6 which shows that the Tribulation (Job has forty-two chapters for each month, seven days for each year, Uz for the place of persecution, Satan for the author of persecution, TURNED CAPTIVITY for the results) is not to be numbered in the one-day thousand-year theory that has been laid out in Matthew 17:1; Luke 9:28, 13:6. The midnight of Matthew 25:6 (as Job 3:6) is the midnight of a period of time not included in "nighttime" of this dispensation, already discussed under Matthew 13:43.

25:14 For the kingdom of heaven is as a man travelling into a far country, who called his own servants, and delivered unto them his goods.

15 And unto one he gave five talents, to another two, and to another one; to every man according to his several ability; and straightway took his journey.

16 Then he that had received the five talents went and traded with the same, and made them other five talents.

17 And likewise he that had received two, he also gained other two.

18 But he that had received one went and digged in the earth, and hid his lord's money.

19 After a long time the lord of those servants cometh, and reckoneth with them.

20 And so he that had received five talents came and brought other five talents, saying, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents: behold, I have gained beside them five talents more.

21 His lord said unto him, Well done, thou good and faithful servant: thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.

22 He also that had received two talents came and said, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me two talents: behold, I have gained two other talents beside them.

23 His lord said unto him, Well done, good and faithful servant; thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.

24 Then he which had received the one talent came and said, Lord I knew thee that thou art an hard man, reaping where thou hast not sown, and gathering where thou hast now strawed:

25 And I was afraid, and went and hid thy talent in the earth: lo, there thou hast that is thine.

26 His lord answered and said unto him, Thou wicked and slothful servant, thou knewest that I reap where I sowed not, and gather where I have not strawed:

27 Thou oughtest therefore to have put my money to the exchangers, and then at my coming I should have received mine own with usury.

28 Take therefore the talent from him, and give it unto him which hath ten talents.

29 For unto every one that hath shall be given, and he shall have abundance: but from him that hath not shall be taken away even that which he hath.

30 And cast ye the unprofitable servant into outer darkness: there shall be weaping and gnashing of teeth.

Again, the expositor is faced with identically the same situation found in the first thirteen verses of the chapter. Since the entire chapter deals with the Advent of the King (vs. 31), and not the rapture of the church, it is not surprising to find the main theme of works and "earned" salvation throughout (vss. 34--40). This makes the chapter a "dilly" for the modernist. Puffed up by their inflated []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3182.xhtml_page_538}sense of "goodness," Weigle, Buttrick, Peale, Kagawa, the Masonic Lodge, Weatherhead, Ferre, and the "liberals" (Socialists!!) pounce on the passages in Matthew 25 like a kitten on a ball of string (and get just as strung-up and balled-up!).

Their Ebionite method is as old as Celsus and Porphry. It is adopted by all liberals: i.e., you reject God's salvation by grace through faith in John 3, 5, 9, 10, 20; Romans; and Galatians, on the grounds that these writings are "Johannine" and "Pauline" theology which picture the mistaken concept that some men had of Jesus after He died. The return to the historical "Jesus" is then resorted to, in an effort to find a higher authority than John and Paul. For this purpose, the Jewish, pre-crucifixion, Old Testament teachings of Jesus to Israel are chosen. The element of "works" is first and foremost in them. Since no modernist thinks that he is rotten enough to burn in hell (if he lives a good life), the "teachings of the Master" have a tremendous appeal to him. Thus, he damns his soul under the pretense of being a true follower of Christ; that is, he claims that his religion comes from the "teachings of Jesus" and not from some mistaken theological concept Paul had concerning Jesus.

Matthew 25, as Matthew 24 and the Sermon on the Mount, are the God-given passages for the primary purpose of damning a Liberal or a Modernist. They do an excellent job, since all Liberals and Modernists are humanitarian Pharisees who deify human nature (using Scripture of course!!), and who love to think they are good enough to work their way to heaven. But the Bible is a TWO-EDGED SWORD. One of its purposes is to damn religious smart-alecks (2 Thess. 2:1--12; 2 Cor 2:15--16). Paul wrote THOSE passages, but what "Ebionite" ever listened to Paul when he could quote in rebuttal "the teachings of the Lowly Galilean!"?

Matthew 25:14--30 is plainly the case of a Tribulation worker who does not ["endure unto the end"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 24:13, 25:30)

While the Liberal damns himself and his congregation trying to fit the passage into the Church Age for salvation, the Fundamentalist fouls up the word of God by claiming the SERVANT (vs. 24) was "never really saved to start with." Both positions (as well as the Arminian position of Christians "losing it") show the ignorance and irreverence of their devotees.

Paul is right. Jesus is right. John is right. The Bible NEVER contradicts. It is never as difficult as the Catholic would pretend, and it is never as complex as the Greek scholar would have you to believe (so you can run to them for interpretation!).

John writes his gospel and three epistles with the entire New Testament before him, and thus, John writes with a universality not found in Matthew, Mark, and Luke (who all finish their gospels before the Pauline prison epistles are written). John's gospel appears to be unique, and it is always held in low esteem by the self-righteous, Christ-rejecting, science-worshipping, Catholic-boot-lick- ing, sacramental churchman who is working his way to Hell, while professing to be a "follower of Christ."

Where John, Jesus, or Paul seem to contradict, the reader (who is saved and consistent) must look for the proper divisions in applying truth, which will enable him to place the verses so that they do not contradict. To place the lost servant of Matthew 25 into the Church Age is to force the passages in John and Paul to contradict.

To do this is to do no better than the Bible-rejecting hypocrite who translated Psalm 110 as "SIT THOU" (professing that "THOU" referred to Deity), and then re-translated the same quotation as "YOU SIT" in Matthew 22:44 (professing that the "YOU" stood for a human being, not Deity). This despicable and un-Christian thing was done in the translating of the [RSV]{.class_4257}, for the purpose of divesting the Lord Jesus Christ of His Deity, against its own construction, to contradict itself so its self-righteous critics can prove that it does contradict.

25:31 When the Son of man shall come in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then shall he sit upon the throne of his glory:

32 And before him shall be gathered all nations: and he shall separate them one from another, as a shepherd divideth his sheep from the goats:

33 And he shall set the sheep on his right hand, but the goats on the left.

34 Then shall the King say unto them on his right hand, Come, ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3182.xhtml_page_540}prepared for you from the foundation of the world:

35 For I was an hungred, and ye gave me meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave me drink: I was a stranger, and ye took me in:

36 Naked, and ye clothed me: I was sick, and ye visited me: I was in prison, and ye came unto me.

37 Then shall the righteous answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, and fed thee? or thirsty, and gave thee drink?

38 When saw we thee a stranger, and took thee in? or naked, and clothed thee?

39 Or when saw we thee sick, or in prison, and came unto thee?

40 And the King shall answer and say unto them, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these my brethren, ye have done it unto me.

41 Then shall he say also unto them on the left hand, Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels:

42 For I was an hungred, and ye gave me no meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave me no drink:

43 I was a stranger, and ye took me not in: naked, and ye clothed me not: sick, and in prison, and ye visited me not.

44 Then shall they also answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, or athirst, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and did not minister unto thee?

45 Then shall he answer them, saying, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye did it not to one of the least of these, ye did it not to me.

46 And these shall go away into everlasting punishment: but the righteous into life eternal.

Again, we come to a passage which affords the Modernist and Liberal a real opportunity to demonstrate their ignorance and disrespect for the word of God. (A man's respect for it can easily be determined by the length to which he will go to change a verse or deny the literalness of a verse,in an effort to make you think that his []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3182.xhtml_page_541}opinion of the verse is correct doctrine. The Catholic Church, of course, has majored in this type of exegesis for 1,500 years and is a master at it.) \[See publication, [Rome---The Great Private Interpreter,]{.class_4257} 1969.\]

For the sake of copiousness and impartiality, let us begin by giving the standard Roman Catholic teaching on the passage, which has been subscribed to by infidels and liberals for over 400 years.

1\. The King is Christ at the Last Judgment.

2\. The Sheep are saved people who got saved by good works.

3\. The Goats are lost people who go to Hell because of bad works. (Here the Liberal departs and defines "hell" as "spiritual ignorance" or "estrangement from truth," etc., while the Catholic stays literal.)

4\. The "BRETHREN" are any persons, anywhere, at anytime, in any age, to whom the good works are ministered.

5\. The issue determined by the good works boils down to this: How have you treated your fellowman? Salvation, consecration, separation, justification, holiness, and spirituality are thus reduced to the basest level possible. They are reduced to the Aristotelian and Platonic guesswork of heathen Greek philosophy. The First Commandment is ignored completely, and it is taken for granted, without any regard for the New Testament whatsoever, that the Second Commandment is the greatest possible goal in life (see notes on 19:16--19).

Before giving the Bible interpretation of its own statements, it would be proper (and unavoidable) to list the twenty things wrong with the above private interpretation taught by the Catholic Philosophy Club.

1\. The scene is not the Last Judgment; therefore, the entire foundation of the interpretation is off-base. At the Last Judgment, there are BOOKS present, and here there are no books. At the Last Judgment, Heaven and Earth have passed away, and here the Lord is on a throne on earth. At the Last Judgment, the unsaved DEAD are judged, while here no one is raised, and nations are being judged. At the Last Judgment, the criterion is a name being in the book of life; here, the criterion is "works toward someone." At the Last Judgment, no one "inherits a kingdom," but here, they do (cf. every verse, please, in Matt 25:31--46 with Rev 20:11--15) Notice that []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3182.xhtml_page_542}where the Roman church has deliberately perverted the statements of fact to obtain a private interpretation, the Bible has taken liberty to state a series of facts which deliberately nullify the Roman interpretation.

2\. The sheep are not saved people from this age, for their judgment is the Judgment Seat of Christ (1 Cor. 3:6--14), not the Last Judgment.

3\. The sheep are not saved people from this age as they are not "going to be saved by works"; they are already saved by grace! (Eph. 2:8--10; John 3:36, 5:24).

4\. The sheep are not saved people from this age because saved people from this age are not only "sheep," but they are called Christ's "BRETHREN" in the sense that they are His BROTHERS and SISTERS spiritually (Heb. 2:11; 1 Pet. 3:8; Rom. 8:6--18; John 1:10--13; 1 Pet. 2:1--4; Col. 1:2). (If it be pointed out that the "BRETHREN" then are "CHRISTIANS," the entire Catholic interpretation goes to pieces, for the Catholic and Liberal both assume that ["the least of these my brethren"]{.class_4258} refers to any being representing humanity.)

5\. The sheep are not saved people from this age, for saved people in this age became sinless angels at the first resurrection (Matt. 22:30) and are found with Christ at this judgment (Matt. 25:31--32). They are not being judged by Him (see Dan. 7:8--11).

6\. The sheep are not saved people from this age because these are called ["the righteous"]{.class_4258} in the story (vs. 37), and nowhere is a Christian ever said to be "a righteous one" or "the righteous." The Christian's righteousness in this age is OF JESUS CHRIST (Rom. 10:1--4), and it is imputed righteousness, purchased by a Blood Atonement (1 Cor. 1:30; Phil. 3:9; 2 Cor. 5:17)!

(The Catholic teaching, therefore, is an Ebionite teaching, which rejects the Pauline Gospel of the Grace of God, and it is little less than a ranting blasphemy at the word of God.)

7\. The lost people in the story did nothing wrong; their sins in the passage are ALL listed as "sins of OMISSION" (see vss. 42--45).

8\. None of the lost people go to Limbo or Purgatory; they all go to ["everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels"]{.class_4258} (vss. 41, 46).

9\. None of the lost people in the passage are at the last judgment for none are dead, none are resurrected, and all appear before []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3182.xhtml_page_543}["the throne of his glory"]{.class_4258}---NOT the ["great white throne"]{.class_4258} of Revelation 20.

10\. None of the lost in the passage are condemned to hell for the sin in this age that condemns a man to hell---note this in John 16:7--9.

11\. None of the lost in the passage are aware of the fact that they are lost, none mention rejecting Christ, none mention rejecting the gospel, and no gospel is mentioned, nor any church, in regard to their damnation---note Matthew 25:44, ["when saw we thee?"]{.class_4258}. The entire passage is treated as a discourse on human treatment of humans, without any reference to Biblical salvation whatsoever.

12\. The lost of the passage are condemned for not ministering to Christ's "brethren," but if Christ's "brethren" are the SHEEP in this age, when were unsaved goats ever commanded to minister to Christ's sheep?

13\. The lost of the passage are not condemned until the Second Coming of Christ, yet every man from the resurrection until this present writing has already been condemned if he has not received the Lord Jesus (note John 3:16, 20, 36)! \[Here comes old "Johannine theology" again to mess up the humanitarians! John is working on the first commandment, and an Ebionite (Catholic or Liberal) just can't stand that.\]

14\. The ["brethren" ]{.class_4258}in the passage cannot be saved, for the SHEEP are saved, and therefore, they are identical, if they both represent saved in this age. As it has been said before, this would mean that Christians are saved for seeing other Christians in jail (!)---(vs. 36), and the lost are condemned for not doing prison work!! You see how ridiculous the private interpretations of Romanism get when actually analyzed.

15\. The ["brethren"]{.class_4258} cannot represent "ANYONE," for Christ likens the BRETHREN to Himself. He has made it abundantly clear that He not only is not "in" everyone, He does not even approve or tolerate "everyone." (See John 8:42--47, 17:9, 14, 16:1--3; Matt. 10:25--27, 23:24--33; John 3:36.)

16\. The ["brethren"]{.class_4258} in the passage (if taken to be the BRETHREN, where it occurs in the same gospel---and this is []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3182.xhtml_page_544}interpreting by Scriptural comparison instead of Catholic superstition) are flesh-and-blood Jews! Read the following carefully: Matthew 28:10, 23:8, 20:24, 12:46--48. In every instance, it is believing Jews. This will also tie with the passage in Hebrews 2:12--17, which it must be remembered is not written PRIMARILY to Gentile Christians at Rome, but to JEWISH BELIEVERS---Hebrews.

17\. If the ["brethren"]{.class_4258} be taken in this sense, the passage will make sense, and it will make nonsense until this is done.

18\. Notice that if the ["brethren"]{.class_4258} are believing Jews at the time of the end (Matt. 24--25), preceding the advent of the Jewish King (Matt. 25:30--31), then the SHEEP are from the nations who treat the Jew hospitably and lovingly at this time, and the GOATS would represent the standard Roman Catholic treatment of the Jew (Loyola, Aquinas, Hitler, Stalin, et. al.). No wonder Rome insists that her private interpretations are divine and that no Protestant can "rightly divide the word of truth!" (2 Tim. 2:15). Rome has so very, very much that needs to be covered up!

19\. Once this position is taken, watch the puzzle straighten out. ["The throne of his glory"]{.class_4258} becomes what it always was---the throne of David on earth (Matt. 19:28; Jer. 14:21; Psa. 102:16; Luke 1:30--33). It is never the "white throne" of the last judgment.

20\. The time and place becomes the end of the Tribulation, exactly as it is found in every place in Matthew 24--25. The place becomes the place that the Bible says it "becomes" (Joel 3:9--15), and the identical order of the passage in Joel will be found in Revelation 14:15, 19. The saved sheep and the lost goats are extracted individually from among those nations assembled, being NOTHING before the extraction (Joel 3:2, 12, 14). They are judged for their treatment of those people who are never numbered among the nations (see Num. 23:9)! The everlasting punishment IS everlasting fire (Matt. 25:41, 46), and the life eternal (vs. 46) includes a millennial inheritance (vs. 34; Col. 3:24; Eph. 5:5; Gal. 5:21; 1 Cor. 6:9), which is not heaven.

Thus, the Scriptures define their own terminology, give their own interpretation without reference to Greek or Hebrew, and yield the absolute, correct, authoritative, infallible, exact, and proper interpretation. At no time are they dependent upon Roman []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3182.xhtml_page_545}tradition, Roman interpretation, Roman churchianity, or Roman leadership. To sum it up, it may be said that (in view of the concrete and incontestable evidence) the Liberal and Catholic "interpretation" of Matthew 25 is a senseless debauchery and a shameful disgrace to the name of Jesus Christ. It is not worthy of the slightest consideration nor respect, and the believer may reject it at once, without any hesitation whatsoever, as being unfitting for a respectable person to teach. Consideration has been given to it here, solely for the purpose of revealing its infantile absurdity.

["Come, ye blessed\...Depart from me, ye cursed."]{.class_4258} Practically speaking, the two statements sum up the doctrinal controversy over intermediate states. You are either blessed (Rom. 4:1--10) or you are cursed (Gal. 3:10--13), and unfortunately your church and your sacraments are not involved either way. The blessed are given eternal life which is called ["everlasting"]{.class_4258} (John 3:16; Luke 18:30; John 3:36, 6:47, 12:50; 1 Tim 1:16; Acts 13:46; John 3:15, 4:36, 17:3; Acts 13:48; 1 John 5:11, 13, 20; Jude 21), and the cursed are given everlasting fire which is called ["everlasting punishment"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 25:46; Dan. 12:2; Jude 6--7---["eternal fire"]{.class_4258}\[!\], Mark 3:29).

Practically speaking, the passage defines eternal life as never ending fellowship with the King in His favor and light. (Scofield has an excellent note on the subject in his reference Bible---page 1353, f.n. 4.) Eternal death is the other side of the coin, and it is always pictured as conscious suffering, punishment, and agony. Nowhere has any Greek scholar ever been able to produce any evidence from the "Greek" that hell has been "air-conditioned" or "re-decorated" since the advent of Higher Criticism and the "Age of Enlightenment." When you hear a preacher busy refinishing hell, you can be certain that it is because he is about to move in and wants the most comfortable quarters possible! (As an old hill-billy preacher said one time: "You wouldn't want folks talkin' bout YOUR home thet way, woodja?")

Where the Catholic invents a Babylonish "purgatory" (Judas Maccabeus 12:43, from Jewish tradition---see Edersheim, Vol. 2, p. 759), the Jehovah's "Witness" flees backwards into the Old Testament to take shelter in primitive revelation (Solomon and []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3182.xhtml_page_546}David), so he can reject the New Testament teaching. (The New World Translation, of course, is not based on ANY Greek manuscript authority; it is a translation of Vaticanus, re-translated into English. This was done to get rid of the embarrassing references that equate Jesus as Jehovah \[John 9, 12, 15; Rev. 19:10--16, etc.\].)

With the completion of the study of Matthew 24--25, the student will have passed over one of the roughest portions of the word of God. The word is called not only ["bread,"]{.class_4258} but milk, apples, honey, and meat. And if ever the believer sat down to a meal of raw sirloin steak, Matthew 24--25 is the place. With the exception of Acts 2 and Hebrews 6, 10, this is the outstanding pitfall of Bible expositors and commentators. None who adopt a postmillennial or amillennial view can even hope to approach the passages, and few premillennialists can face them honestly once having approached them. Only when they are read and believed and faced squarely will they yield their rich and tremendous truths in regard to Daniel's Seventieth Week, and only when these truths are discovered will the Body of Christ ever be able to combat the insidious operations of the old harlot on the seven mountains, who avails herself of these passages (and dozens more) to prove that a believer can lose salvation.

Genesis 1--Exodus 20 is grace operating through conscience.

Exodus 20--Matthew 26 is faith and works operating through the law.

Matthew 26--Acts 8 is an intermediate period of transition.

Acts 8--Hebrews is grace operating through faith in the gospel.

Hebrews--Revelation 20 is faith and works operating through the gospel and the law.

Revelation 20--22 is works only.

This is the only placement of divisions which will enable the believer to preserve the New Testament intact, without violating its plain writing or going to Westcott and Hort to destroy it.

If you don't think so, violate it, and you'll find out.

[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml}

# Chapter 26 {.heading_84-0}

26:1 And it came to pass, when Jesus had finished all these sayings, he said unto his disciples,

2 Ye know that after two days is the feast of the passover, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified.

3 Then assembled together the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders of the people, unto the palace of the high priest, who was called Caiaphas,

4 And consulted that they might take Jesus by subtilty, and kill him.

5 But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there be an uproar among the people.

The passage takes place on Tuesday evening before 6 o'clock (see comments under Matt. 12:39--40). Since the passover was killed on Wednesday (not Friday, as found in A. T. Robertson, Trench, Alford, Westcott and Hort, Cardinal Spellman, Castro, and Pope John) and eaten on Thursday (Num. 28:16--17), it is still ["two days" ]{.class_4258}before ["the feast of the passover." ]{.class_4258}Mark 14:1 confirms this, as the passover was eaten [after ]{.class_4257}6 p.m. Thursday. The disciples go out to choose the room for the "supper" on Tuesday, before 6 p.m. Again, we find Rome greatly in error and insulting to the word of God.

It must be remembered that the first split in the Catholic church (and there have been dozens of them!) came over the fact that the Greek church (which used the Syrian Text of the Apostles, written in Asia Minor---the text of the King James and Luther's German Bible) [objected to the date set by the Catholics on Easter. ]{.class_4257}This controversy also came up again in the Church of England at the time of St. Patrick, Cuthbert, and Columba. The Catholic's "GOOD FRIDAY" is an arbitrary, non‑consequential designation that has no relation at all to the death of Christ or the resurrection (see Matt. 12:40). It was set to impress the body of professing Christendom with the pope's authority and ability to overrule the God‑breathed words of Scripture.

In the passage found here (Matt. 26:5), the event takes place Tuesday and the [meal ]{.class_4257}of 26:6 is earlier in the week, before the [supper ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_548}Tuesday night (see 26:17), where the day of Wednesday begins at 6 p.m., Tuesday night.

The conniving of the Pharisees, here, is the obvious outcome of what they began to consider in John 11:47--50 a week before the actual killing, and a "KILLING" is what it is (Acts 2:23, 3:14--15, 4:26--27). Judas knows of it, for in Mark 14:10 and Matthew 26:14, he knows right where to go when his feelings get hurt over the money‑bag business. The chief instigator (as in the assassination of Abraham Lincoln) is [a priest. ]{.class_4257}The reader will not fail to notice the numerous occurrences of the word in these last two or three chapters of Matthew (26:3, 14, 47, 51, 57, 59, 62--63, 65, 27:1, 3, 6, 12, 20, 62, 28:11). The priest, then, plays the major role in the betrayal, arrest, and murder (Acts 7:52). His motive of course is "for the religious good" of his proselyte, but that does not change his guilt. It only proves that he placed more value on his own righteousness than the righteousness of God (Rom. 10:1--4; Luke 18:10--15).

26:6 Now when Jesus was in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,

7 There came unto him a woman having an alabaster box of very precious ointment, and poured it on his head, as he sat at meat.

8 But when his disciples saw it, they had indignation, saying, To what purpose is this waste?

9 For this ointment might have been sold for much, and given to the poor.

10 When Jesus understood it, he said unto them, Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath wrought a good work upon me.

11 For ye have the poor always with you; but me ye have not always.

12 For in that she hath poured this ointment on my body, she did it for my burial.

13 Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, there shall also this, that this woman hath done, be told for a memorial of her.

The passage here, as in Mark 14:3--9, is undoubtedly parenthetical, for in John 12:1--8 it is said to occur [before ]{.class_4257}the triumphal entry (see John 12:1, 12). In both Mark and Matthew, the wording is: ["and being in Bethany" ]{.class_4258}or ["Now when Jesus was in Bethany." ]{.class_4258}The actual night of the Lord's supper is on Tuesday night, and [He was not in Bethany. ]{.class_4257}The passage is obviously parenthetical, and goes back and recalls an incident that took place during the week.

["Simon the leper" ]{.class_4258}is unidentified, but it would not be stretching things a bit to suppose that this is the LEPER who was healed in Luke 17:12--15, and who returned to give thanks. Although Martha, Mary, and Lazarus are present, the meal is evidently not in [their ]{.class_4257}house (John 12:3).

To accumulate all the evidence and facts regarding the incident, the gospels of Mark and John must be taken into consideration along with Matthew.

["A woman having an alabaster box of very precious ointment."]{.class_4258} In John 12:3, it is also called ointment, but it is named as ["a pound of ointment of spikenard, very costly." ]{.class_4258}Where Matthew and Mark say: ["poured it on his head" ]{.class_4258}and ["she brake the box, and poured it on his head" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 14:3), John records, ["Mary\...anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair: and the house was filled with the odour of the ointment" ]{.class_4258}(John 12:3). The incident is almost identical to the one found in Luke 7:39--50. However, here the man addressed as ["SIMON"]{.class_4258} is a Pharisee, not a leper, nor does he think as a leper; he thinks as a Pharisee (Luke 7:39). The passage in Luke 7 occurs at least two years before the crucifixion, but it shows that the anointing of the head and feet of a guest was not an uncommon thing. (There is a possibility that the event in John 12 could be an earlier one than the one in Matthew 26 and Mark 14, but the chances are slim, considering the same words are spoken in both cases, and in both cases, Judas is involved in the same way.)

Mary enters with a pound of spikenard in an alabaster box. (Pure nard---Nardus Spicatus, which is the plant Nardostachys Jatamansi---a pointed, yellow‑brown plant looking like the tail of an ermine. It grows in the Himalayas.) She breaks the box, pouring the liquid []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_550}perfume on the head and feet of Jesus as He SAT at meat. The odor fills the dining room, and Judas speaks up (as in Matt. 24:1, Mark 13:1). Only the disciples are mentioned in the plural, but in John the exact disciple is demarked (John 12:4).

["Why was this waste of the ointment made?" "To what purpose is this waste? For this ointment might have been sold for much" ]{.class_4258}(300 pence) (more than 300 pence), ["and given to the poor" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 14:5; Matt 26:8--9; John 12:5). It will be noticed that the complainer is Judas, but his griping is infectious, and we read, ["And THEY murmered against her" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 14:5). The trouble-maker is spotted. It is Judas, the one who "followed in the steps of the lowly Galilean," and who lived by the Sermon on the Mount (Matt. 7:21--23). The word ["waste"]{.class_4258} in Matthew 26:8 is the same word for Son of PERDITION in John 17:12 and ["DESTRUCTION"]{.class_4258} in Revelation 9:11 (Apollyon). As usual, the one guilty of the crime is doing the accusing; thus, one is reminded that the greatest accuser of the crime of private interpretation is Rome---proof enough that she herself is the King of Private Interpreters! (See [Rome---The Great Private Interpreter,]{.class_4257} 1969.)

The "burden" for the poor folks is that famous United Fund, Community Drive, Care‑package type of thing so prominent in the twentieth century. The hypocrisy behind it is easily discerned by the desire of the bag‑hold­ers to have [all the money put in one jackpot ]{.class_4257}(compare SBC "cooperative program"). This lets "the cat out of the bag" so to speak, and a spotted cat it is---its name is [embezzlement. ]{.class_4257}If this is not its name, then why object to each individual [designating his money? ]{.class_4257}The answer is apparent to any man who is acquainted with the primitive facts of human nature; the bagholders are going to use the money for their own purposes and for the support of things they know the givers [would not give to if they were told about it. ]{.class_4257}This is the standard operating procedure in Christianity today. The "Love Offering" for the evangelist goes to pay the gas bill for the church. The "Missionary Offering" goes to buy a coke machine for the state secretary in the home‑office "Mission building," and the "donations for Christian literature" wind up paying for a five year subscription to Socialistic propaganda. It is all easy to do in religious circles, much easier than "out in the world" where someone less []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_551}gullible and unsuspecting is liable to haul you to court.

Judas is not worried about the poor. He is worried about any money going anywhere except into the "United Fund," which he held and controlled! Thus in the day of judgment, some poor unsuspecting soul is going to find out that 90 percent of what he gave the John Birch Society and the Peace Corps wound up in salaries for Catholic Bishops. Judas' piety is only "bag deep." He is worried about the Second Commandment, [not the First One. ]{.class_4257}The woman before him is demonstrating her love on Jesus Christ as God's Son, in obedience to the [First Commandment. To ]{.class_4257}do this, she is violating a law of the Second in the eyes of the audience. (See remarks on Matt. 25:35--44.)

["Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath wrought a good work upon me." "Let her alone: against the day of my burying hath she kept this" ]{.class_4258}(John 12:7; Mark 14:6). ["She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 14:8).

Several practical lessons stand out in this passage.

1\. An act of love toward Christ is greater than the "visiting, feeding, and clothing" of our fellowman (see Matt. 25:32--45).

2\. Jesus always accepts worship as proper always and never refuses it. He tolerates and approves of the belief that He Himself IS GOD (John 20:28; Matt. 14:33).

3\. If you have done what you [can, you ]{.class_4257}have done all you can do. If you have NOT done what "you could," then you have no room to gripe or criticize others.

4\. Proper worship of Christ, by a believer, will COST something. [Something has to be broken ]{.class_4257}before real love can be poured out (Acts 9:16).

5\. [The death of Christ is ]{.class_4257}the most important thing, not His birth; He is born to die. The woman does well in anointing Him ahead of time, for those who came to do it later (Matt. 28:1‑4; John 20:1--3; Mark 16:1--4) [came too late!!]{.class_4257}

6\. Real devotion to Christ cannot be hid, and it will fill all the house if it is truly there (Prov 27:16, 1 Cor 8:3).

7\. Real devotion will immediately attract the criticism of those moralists and religious formalists who are trying to work their way []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_552}to heaven by works of righteousness (John 16:1--4; 1 Thess. 2:13--16).

Notice in the passage that Jesus places the POOR second to Himself (Matt. 26:11). This is rough on Karl Marx, who always did consider himself to be smarter than Jesus Christ when it came to economic and class struc­tures.

"[Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world."]{.class_4258}

The statement (Matt. 26:13) is a Sphinx. The "this gospel" is left undefined, although the context (as in 1 Cor. 15:1--6) is Christ's death. But if this be the case, then there is wholesale violation of the commandment for rarely, IF EVER, is the incident mentioned in preaching the gospel of 1 Corinthians 15:1--6, and Paul never mentions it anywhere.

If the gospel is that of the Kingdom (Acts 2--3; Mark 16:16), it still is not mentioned, not even at Jerusalem, let alone "the whole world." It is said to be told [for a memorial of this "Mary,"]{.class_4257} but this Mary is NOT the mother of Jesus, and no preacher to my knowledge has ever mentioned the incident when preaching the gospel. The passage awaits some research. The Greek will certainly yield nothing, and neither will critical attempts to lift the verse out of the Bible as unauthentic help any. There are no "short cuts" to profound truth. This is something every Greek linguist should learn. Lexicons, dictionaries, and grammars do not yield up "gold nuggets"; they only amplify [vertically ]{.class_4257}what anyone could have found out horizontally. The explanation to Mark 14:9 lies somewhere in the remaining 31,172 verses of the English.

26:14 Then one of the twelve, called Judas Iscariot, went unto the chief priests,

15 And said unto them, What will ye give me, and I will deliver him unto you? And they covenanted with him for thirty pieces of silver.

16 And from that time he sought opportunity to betray him.

Mark 14:10--11---["And Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went unto the chief priests, to betray him unto them. And []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_553}when they heard it, they were glad, and promised to give him money. And he sought how he might conveniently betray him."]{.class_4258}

The spotlight turns on Judas at this point, and from here to his ignominious end in Matthew 27:3--5, the lid is lifted off the mysterious and dark interior of this devil who walked as a man (John 6:70--71).

["One of the twelve" ]{.class_4258}is the peculiar wording that is found in connection with Judas on several occasions (note John 6:70--71; Luke 22:3, 47; Mark 14:10, 20; Matt 26:14, 47). Oddly enough, this would call one's attention to the number 13. There are 13 letters in "Judas Iscariot." If the student cares to take out the time and trouble to search the Scripture and obey the divine injunction of Revelation 13:18 to ["count the number," ]{.class_4258}he will find that the 13's in an [AV ]{.class_4257}show a design and organization far beyond the scope of unintentional translation. Eighty-five percent of all 13's in an [AV ]{.class_4257}refer to Sin, Satan, or Destruction. Ten percent are good. Five percent are neutral.

["Judas Iscariot." ]{.class_4258}The word, as it stands, is Judas from "Judah," "Is" from Heb. ["ish,"]{.class_4257} and "cariot" from Heb. ["keriot."]{.class_4257} "Judas, a man from Kerioth." Kerioth is [east ]{.class_4257}of Jordan in the region called "Syria." It is closely connected in its population with the [half‑breed Moabites of Hamitic extraction. ]{.class_4257}(See Jer. 48:1, 9, 11, 24, 35, 41; Gen. 19:32--38, 26, 13:12; 10:6, 19.) Judas, then, is a [half‑breed, Syrian Jew who is indirectly kin to Nimrod ]{.class_4257}(Gen. 10:6, 8, 15, 19). Further, he is said to be ["A DEVIL"]{.class_4258} (John 6:70--71), not a human being. Although he claims a human father,[ "Simon"]{.class_4258} (John 13:26), the claim is as disputed as the patrimony of those beings called "giants" in Genesis 6 and Daniel 2:43.

As a devil, Judas had power to work miracles (Rev. 16:13--14), and although he was baptized and partook of the Eucharist (John 13:2), [he was lost and he goes to the bottomless pit ]{.class_4257}(Acts 1:25; Rev. 9:1--11). (But again, I hear a loud coughing and clearing of throats in the vestibule, and I realize that "the brethren" are catching cold from over‑exposure to the word. We will have to wrap them up again in their denominational training robes.)

["What will ye give me?" ]{.class_4258}The modern expression is apparent---"What'll ya give me," or "What'll ya gimme?" Since []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_554}["the love of money is the root of all evil" ]{.class_4258}(and not "a root of all kinds" as the [ASV ]{.class_4257}and [RSV ]{.class_4257}wrongly translate), it is fitting that the greatest crime in history should be carried out for monetary gain. Judas' motive is money. The Pharisees' motive is ENVY (Matt. 27:18). Pilate's is the "fear of man," and the soldiers' is a brutal "letting off" of steam. Of the group, the Pharisees have more to account for than anyone (Matt. 23:14), for their reasons are personal hatred based on a desire to justify themselves instead of the word of God. This motive is not [necessary. ]{.class_4257}It is not [objective. ]{.class_4257}It accomplishes no moral or social good, and it causes others to stumble (Matt. 23:13). Judas can be excused on the grounds of his very nature---a devil. Pilate can beg out on being human; that is, weak. The soldiers can beg out on ignorance (John 19:24), but the long‑robed "father" (Matt. 23:9) has no cloak with which to cover himself (John 15:21--25). He is guilty, caught red‑handed, in the act, manifesting his true nature---a ritualistic, self‑righteous, sacramental, hypocritical, religious MURDERER, who only rejoiced to see God's Son killed (Mark 14:11---["they were glad"]{.class_4258}). In a word ---CAIN---the "salvation‑by‑works" religionist who covered his self‑righteousness with a pile of figs, turnip greens, artichokes, bananas, potatoes, cabbages, avocadoes, and grapes, pretending that this was a real sacrifice of [blood ]{.class_4257}(Rom. 10:1--4). In Cain's "mass," he had faith to believe the junk really was [blood. ]{.class_4257}Unfortunately, the Lord is not to be fooled that easy. (See [The Bible Believer's Commen­tary on Genesis,]{.class_4257} Gen. 4:3--6.)

["Thirty pieces of silver."]{.class_4258} Judas unwittingly fulfills the passage in Zechariah, without being aware of the fact that the reference is to him (Zech. 11:13). From this we learn a tremendous truth. It is the truth that a man can fulfill prophecy without knowing it and be used as an instrument in his own damnation, unaware of the fact that [it has already been told beforehand and written down. ]{.class_4257}Cases in point are Balaam, who went ahead and finessed his reward AFTER knowing what would happen if he did (Num. 22:12, 31, 35, 24:1--3, 31:8); Pharaoh, who went ahead knowing that God was able to kill him and would (Exod. 5:2--3, 8:8, 19, 9:6, 26--27, 10:17); Caiaphas, who prophesied the atonement while planning murder (John 11:49--52); and the Pharisees (Matt. 21:43--46), who knew what they would do, to whom they would do it, and what God []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_555}[would do to them for doing it]{.class_4257}---and[ ]{.class_4257}still went ahead. It is apparent that if a man goes by the light he has, he can be aware of the forthcoming destruction before it takes place, but if he does not, then he is powerless and helpless even to find the warning that refers to him (2 Thess. 2:2--12). ["Thirty pieces of silver" ]{.class_4258}is the price of a slave, male or female, in Exodus 21:32, and the estimation of a female for a "vow" (Lev. 27:4).

In the passage in Zechariah (Zech. 11:11--14), the devout student will not fail to notice the reference to the Antichrist in the context (vss. 15--17), and the fact that he is an ["idol shepherd" ]{.class_4258}(see Rev. 13:13--16). (Dummelow, with the [RSV]{.class_4257}, has forced the Bible to contradict itself \[as we remarked under Matt. 24:14--30\] by writing "treasury" for ["potter"]{.class_4258} in Zechariah 11:13. The money was [not cast into the treasury ]{.class_4257}\[Matt. 27:6\], nor did it ever get there, nor was it intended to get there. The [RSV ]{.class_4257}reading, as Dummelow, is a travesty of common sense and reveals another prejudice against the word of God. Moffatt has no ["POTTER"]{.class_4258} in either clause \[Zech. 11:13\], yet [it is in the Hebrew. ]{.class_4257}Again, the Textus Receptus of the King James proves far superior to the mutilated "Vaticanus" from which these Old Testament passages are taken.)

["And he sought how he might conveniently betray him" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 14:11).

This word "convenient" needs examination as it is the professional by‑word of modern advertising---"for your convenience," "conveniently located," "how convenient," etc. The word is kin to "expediency," "suitable," "oppor­tune," "advantageous," and, philosophically speaking, "pragmatic." Although it occasionally is used in a good sense (1Cor. 16:12), the Bible uses it three times with telling effect: Herod (Mark 6:21), Felix (Acts 24:25), and Judas (Mark 14:11). In all three cases, it marks a man who is looking for convenience only and never for truth. This is the modern use of the word.

Convenience means: "there is no need to go out of your way and do things the hard way when it is just so much easier to do it like everybody else does it." This philosophy is the road to World-Wide-United Ecumeni­cism, which will enable God to burn the assembled []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_556}nations at one time, instead of separately (Zeph. 3:8; Matt. 13:30). (Here, again, is a perfect example of knowing the truth ahead of time, and being powerless to avoid it because it is rejected. Light rejected becomes lightning. The United Nations can no more avoid total divine destruction than Judas could avoid a hang‑man's noose. Both put prestige and MONEY ahead of the word of God. Neither can stop the word from coming through right on the nose, without losing a jockey.)

26:17 Now the first day of the feast of unleavened bread the disciples came to Jesus, saying unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare for thee to eat the passover?

18 And he said, Go into the city to such a man, and say unto him, The Master saith, My time is at hand; I will keep the passover at thy house with my disciples.

19 And the disciples did as Jesus had appointed them; and they made ready the passover.

20 Now when the even was come, he sat down with the twelve.

["Now the first day of the feast\...when the even was come." ]{.class_4258}The chronology dates from Tuesday afternoon until after the beginning of Wednesday (which to a Jew would begin at 6 p.m. Tuesday night). Luke writes: ["Then came the day of unleavened bread, when the passover must be killed" ]{.class_4258}(see Luke 22:7). Mark confirms with: ["And the first day of unleavened bread, when they killed the passover\...." ]{.class_4258}There is no trouble at all getting the days straight if a reader goes by the Bible information and does not follow the wild‑goose chase of Catholic commentators who try to conjure up a "Good Friday."

The disciples make ready the passover, and ["he sat down." ]{.class_4258}The word ["SAT"]{.class_4258} is defined in the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 in Amos 6:4 as "reclining on a couch," so there is not only no need to run to a Greek lexicon, but there is no need to brag about what a great artist Da Vinci was. Da Vinci couldn't read a Bible in eighth-grade Italian. He painted all the disciples sitting bolt upright, like Italian politicians. (His painting is just about as authentic as the Pope's Bible and the priests' "continued sacrifice.")

26:21 And as they did eat, he said, Verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.

22 And they were exceeding sorrowful, and began every one of them to say unto him, Lord, is it I?

23 And he answered and said, He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish, the same shall betray me.

24 The Son of man goeth as it is written of him: but woe unto that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! it had been good for that man if he had not been born.

25 Then Judas, which betrayed him, answered and said, Master, is it I? He said unto him, Thou hast said.

The passage is remarkable in its doctrinal and practical riches. ["As they did eat"]{.class_4258} (vs. 21), ["And as they were eating"]{.class_4258} (vs. 26) settle it once and for all about whether or not Judas partook of the "sacrament" (or "Eucharist")---[he did. ]{.class_4257}He did most certainly, for he does not get up and leave the table until the eating is over (John 13:2,30), and the communion and "eucharist" are given [while they are eating ]{.class_4257}(Matt. 26:26). All attempts to make Judas an outsider and an "unfit communicant for the Holy wafer," etc., is just twiddle‑twaddle. The Greek scholar, A. T. Robertson (an amillennialist who worshipped the Vatican text of Westcott and Hort), goes to some length in his "harmony" to prove that Judas did NOT take the communion. The length gone to is wasted walking. Judas took it. (If you can "prove from the Greek" that he did not, then that settles it for certain---HE TOOK IT!)

The next thing that is remarkable is that all the disciples question their own fidelity to the Saviour except one---John. John does not ask, ["Lord, is it I?" ]{.class_4258}Absolutely confident of his love for his Lord, John quietly and humbly asks, ["Lord, who is it?" ]{.class_4258}(John 13:25)! His love is so great that even if he suspected HIMSELF, he would have been willing to be exposed publicly before the group. This is the disciple who outruns Peter to the tomb, and who goes [all the way ]{.class_4257}while Peter "follows afar off" (see John 18:15--17).

The next thing that is remarkable is the form of address which Judas uses in the passage. While all say, ["Lord, is it I?"]{.class_4258},[ ]{.class_4258}Judas asks, ["MASTER, is it I?" ]{.class_4258}Judas always uses ["MASTER"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 26:49; []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_558}Mark 13:1). He seems loathe to recognize Jesus as the Lord (1 Cor. 12:3). Judas is thus characterized as a man who "followed in His steps" and loved to call Him "the Master." \[This has an old familiar sound to it, like a folksong we have heard somewhere before. Episcopalians and Methodists, especially, should recognize the tune.\]

"He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish, the same\...."

To understand the actual proceedings, one must picture the table in the form of a horseshoe, with the Lord at the bend in the center. The supper is over (John 13:2); they have had the "Eucharist." Jesus has washed their feet (John 13:3--20). (He has warned of the betrayal, but [before ]{.class_4257}the actual dipping of the sop and the departure of Judas, you will find the "eucharist" given \[Luke 22:19--22\].) Notice that Luke 22:21--23 gives the information found in Matthew 26:23--24 [after ]{.class_4257}the communion, whereas Matthew gives it [before. ]{.class_4257}Mark gives it before also---Mark 14:19--21. However, what must be observed is that Judas' hand is [still on the table when the eucharist is over ]{.class_4257}(Luke 22:21). The dipping of the sop takes place at this point, proving that Judas sat through and partook of the Paschal supper.

Stepping ahead to the actual dipping of the sop, we find the details in John 13. John is sitting on Christ's [right hand, ]{.class_4257}and Simon Peter is sitting right next to John. Judas is sitting on Christ's [left hand. ]{.class_4257}Peter asks John for the information (13:24), and John asks the Lord (13:25). The Lord tells John (13:26), but in a low [voice, ]{.class_4257}where not even Peter---one seat down---can catch it (13:28). So when Judas departs, the presumption is that he is going to buy something for the feast that would be eaten the next (Thursday) night. (The supper was a preparation---see Lev. 23:5 and Luke 22:7.)

["Master, is it I? Thou hast said." ]{.class_4258}As we would say, "You said it." "You told the truth."

26:26 And as they were eating, Jesus took bread, and blessed it, and brake it, and gave it to the disciples, and said, Take, eat; this is my body.

27 And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and gave it to []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_559}them, saying, Drink ye all of it;

28 For this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many for the remission of sins.

29 But I say unto you, I will not drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father's kingdom.

30 And when they had sung an hymn, they went out into the mount of Olives.

This is the salient passage for the papist to reprove the Protestant for not believing the Bible literally. So much blood has been shed about the subject---literal blood---that we shall not take up too much time going back over the ground. It will suffice to say that any [ritual ]{.class_4257}sanctioned by religious leaders who profess to make it "HOLY" (and can back it up by awesome pictorial, impressive, dramatic gestures and formulas), if continued through a long enough period of time, can convince just about anyone. Goebbels was a master at this type of thing, and the Associated Press was not far behind him in getting the Kennedy regime installed and glamorizing Michael King Jr.

1\. If the passage is literal, [the CUP]{.class_4257} is His blood, not the fruit of the vine [in it!]{.class_4257}

Now stop and think about that. No Catholic ever did.

While the priest professes to be a [literalist, ]{.class_4257}he is not at all. The statement is: ["He took the CUP...saying, Drink ye all of IT\...." ]{.class_4258}One can see immediately that to take this literally would be to violate common sense. You do not drink [a cup ]{.class_4257}any more than a [kettle boils. ]{.class_4257}If you are going to be literal, be literal, but don't profess to be to get an excuse to drink fermented liquor and then [back out ]{.class_4257}when faced with your own prooftext.

2\. If the passage is literal, Christ's body is [divided into twelve individual bodies, ]{.class_4257}for each disciple gets a piece, and the statement is, ["THIS is my body." ]{.class_4258}To go literal here is to apply for a Section 8, [for His literal body is holding the bread! ]{.class_4257}Thus we find the rock‑bottom foundation of the Catholic mass is built on a LIE (and an inconsistent one at that). The Catholic has done what the disciples did in Matthew 16:7, 12. He has believed it [literally ]{.class_4257}where it is [not ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_560}literal. Notice the same error in John 6:26, 55--63.

Thus a rule is established: A [passage is always literal unless it is absolutely impossible to take it in the literal sense. ]{.class_4257}The Catholic will take John 6:53--56, which is not literal blood, literally to justify drinking liquor, and then reverse the field and [pretend to turn it into blood so ]{.class_4257}that he can profess to take the passage literally! But after all, some of us have a little sense left and we know that you just can't fire 30 caliber shells all day out of a 12‑gauge, double‑barrel shot‑gun. If it is literal, it is, and if it ain't, [it ain't! ]{.class_4257}If it is blood, then it is blood, not liquor, and if it is liquor, it is not blood.

"But" says the priest, "the eye of the faithful can apprehend that\...." Yes, and I knew a man who thought he was driving to New York, and he had faith to believe he was. When everyone else told him he wasn't going that way, he stuck to his "faith" and was "faithful" to the end of the road. He wound up in Baltimore; he was headed the wrong way (Prov. 14:12).

(A man poisoned a cup of liquor which a priest used at "mass," and when he got all through with the mass he was a mess. You see, it [killed him, ]{.class_4257}even though he did have faith to believe it was Christ's blood and not poisoned liquor. You say, "He didn't have enough faith" \[Mark 16:18\]. Yes, but that is what a powerless, backslidden "healer" said to somebody in the line whom he could not heal! Nice way to get out of a mess, let alone "a mass"!)

That isn't all. The text says, ["Drink YE all of it."]{.class_4258} If it was fermented liquor (which it was not), he still didn't drink any, nor is there any passage that says He ever touched it. The Catholic fable on this is so persistent that Protestants today are giving in and becoming pizza‑eating winoes with the alibi that "Jesus turned the water into wine." He did? [Did He drink it ]{.class_4257}(Prov. 31:4)?

That isn't all. They got the bread AND THE CUP. Why has the priest for 1,500 years saved the cup for HIMSELF? Pretty sorry priest, wouldn't you say? Some­one said, "Well, the bread represents His body and that includes His blood, so\...." Really? [Then why is it that His literal flesh and blood are sitting right there, and neither are in the cup or the loaf? ]{.class_4257}And why is it that "His body" is defined after the resurrection as a spiritual body of believers (1 Cor. 10:16--17), and NOT a corpse? And why is it that to repeat the sacrifice is to []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_561}blaspheme the blood that was shed once (Heb. 10:11--14, 29)? (No wonder the African manuscript "Vaticanus" \[[RV, RSV, ASV]{.class_4257}\] stops before Hebrews 10! My, my, we can't have the word of God [ridiculing ]{.class_4257}priests who offer sacrifices \[Heb. 10:11\]. Why that would be "downing somebody's religion!!")

26:31 Then saith Jesus unto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad.

32 But after I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.

33 Peter answered and said unto him, Though all men shall be offended because of thee, yet will I never be offended.

34 Jesus said unto him, Verily I say unto thee, That this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.

35 Peter said unto him, Though I should die with thee, yet will I not deny thee. Likewise also said all the disciples.

["For it is written\...." ]{.class_4258}Here Jesus warns the disciples with a quotation from Zechariah 13:7. The warning falls on deaf ears (exactly as the warning of Zechariah 11:13 had just done in the case of Judas). Again, we are struck with the astonishing fact that the Scriptures always come to pass regardless of anyone's attitude toward them; their unbelief in them and their knowledge of them does not alter their power or invincible fulfillment. Balaam is warned, ignores the warning, and it comes through on him; Pharaoh does likewise; Judas does likewise. (Rome will do likewise, with full knowledge of the fate of the city on seven mountains, already written 2,000 years ahead of time in Rev. 17--18.) Here, Peter and all the disciples, in their zeal to prove their love, step on the Scriptures and say in effect: "Regardless of what Zechariah 13:7 says, the passage is figurative, and [I will not be included ]{.class_4257}in those numbered in the text." But they were! Notice, ["Likewise also said ALL the disciples"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3314.xhtml_page_569};[ ]{.class_4258}Peter is not the only culprit, although his braggadocio is often preached about in sermons on "over‑confidence." They are all over‑confident. Of the twelve who say, ["Likewise,"]{.class_4258} John turns out to be the only one who does not turn coward and head for the bushes when the cards are stacked and the dice are gaffed.

["But after I am risen again\...."]{.class_4258} This promise to go to Galilee is fulfilled (John 21:1--8; Mark 16:7). It is touching to notice that it is mentioned again to Peter ONLY in Mark 16:7, as a reminder of the context in which it was first spoken---["before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me\..."]{.class_4258} (see Matt. 26:32, 34). The Lord is greatly concerned about Peter after His resurrection. ["Though all men\...yet will I never." ]{.class_4258}The statement must be read in view of the fact that Jesus has just quoted the infallible word of the living God, which never fails to accurately foretell every detail of the future.

Peter's denial, therefore, at the time of falling is actually an anti‑climax. The climax is here in the text (Matt. 26:31, 33), where Jesus has just finished quoting the Bible, which said: ["All ye shall be offended\...for it is written\...."]{.class_4258}

The quotation escapes the thought and attention of the disciples, as Bible quotations often do. Here, as elsewhere, the final and supreme authority is rejected on the grounds of well‑meaning or good‑intentions. The results of the rejection are just as disastrous as if they had handled the verse like Celsus, Porphyry, Origen, Jerome, or Calvin. (Calvin denied the inspiration of Acts 7:15--16; Matthew 27:9; and Zechariah 11:12--13.) We thus learn that the rejection of the Bible, for whatever motive is present, is sinful and tragic.

The [RSV ]{.class_4257}(always ready to confound the word) translates: "You will all fall away because of me." This curious reading blames apostasy on the Lord Jesus, for that is what the word apostasy is---a falling away from a thing. (Greek: [Apo]{.class_4257}---"from," "away from," [istimi]{.class_4257}, "a standing position.") The word is not "falling away," however; it is rather "SCANDALIZE" ([skandalizo]{.class_4257}), and the offense is the offense of being caught with someone of ill‑repute in an embarrassing position (see Mark 14:51--52). The [AV ]{.class_4257}1611, as usual, preserves the accurate reading of the original, while the [RSV ]{.class_4257}preserves, as customary, the traditions of intellectual sacred cows.

Jesus knows that Simon Peter will go back to his fishing after the denial (John 21:1--5), so He purposely thrusts at the old commercial fisherman by saying, ["I will go BEFORE YOU into Galilee." ]{.class_4258}He knows where Peter is going!

26:36 Then cometh Jesus with them unto a place called Gethsemane, and saith unto the disciples, Sit ye here, while I go and pray yonder.
