Cornelius is a proselyte to it (Acts 10), a Eunuch from Africa has read the Old Testament (Acts 8), there are Greeks at the feast desiring to see Jesus (John 12:20), and the mythological fable (taught in Yale and Harvard and Chicago []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_605}University), to the effect that the ancient people "didn't have a chance" and that, therefore, the Bible was only "one religious Book among thou­sands," is a FIB.

Plato often used to remark that all Socrates knew, he had received from the Jews. Anyone who has read ECCLESIASTES is well aware of this, as the entire field of philosophy is covered by Solomon 1,000 years before Christ shows up. Capitalism, Socialism, Communism, Naturalism, Realism, Idealism, Fascism, Subjectivism, Existentialism, Rationalism, Empiricism, Scepticism, Deism, Theism, Pessimism, Stoicism, Epicurianism, Agnosticism, and Utilitarianism are dead subjects of the forgotten past by 800 B.C. Socrates is about 400 years too late to give any additional revelation. You can judge all philosophers from that point as being just that much later in getting on the gravy train.

[Jesus Christ unites enemies ]{.class_4257}in a common bond of hatred against HIMSELF. This puts the Ecumenical movement on an entirely different set of wheels---[its tires are tubeless and treadless.]{.class_4257}

Somewhere, someone has overlooked the fact that not only can born‑again, Bible‑believing Christians be united by their common love for Jesus, but sacramental, ritualistic, Bible‑rejecting [Romans ]{.class_4257}can be united by their common [hatred ]{.class_4257}for Jesus. The catch lies in the fact that those uniting [against Jesus]{.class_4257} will profess to believe in Him, nor can they be detected for the hypocrites they are until one sits down with them and an open Bible and finds out what they actually believe in. This is why the Catholic is given a DIFFERENT Bible, is warned against discussing the Bible with anyone, is told to send "the discusser" to his priest, is told to stop Protestants from witnessing by saying: "that's just your [interpretation," ]{.class_4257}and is forbidden to join a Bible‑believing, Bible‑preaching church. Unity among Romans is a mutual and common hatred for the authority of the word of God---[professing of course to believe it. ]{.class_4257}(The proof is in the pudding, old buddy; if you did love It, you would enjoy talking about It, studying It, and hearing It preached \[John 8:47\]. Amen and Amen.)

We now have arrived at Matthew 27:15 in the commentary, the companion passages being Mark 15:6--15; Luke 23:13--25; and John 18. Pilate, having received Christ back from Herod, says to the people: []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_606}["But ye have a custom, that I should release unto you one at the passover: will ye therefore that I release unto you the King of the Jews? Then cried they all again, saying, Not this man, but Barabbas. Now Barabbas was a robber"]{.class_4258} (John 18:39--40).

Barabbas (Bar‑son, Abba‑the father: Son of the Father!) is a "pistol ball" (as they say in Carolina). He is not only a ["robber," ]{.class_4258}but he is a seditionist and a murderer (Luke 23:19). Pilate's third attempt to wash his hands of the mess fails. He has tried [carelessness ]{.class_4257}and [cowardice; ]{.class_4257}now he tries [cleverness. ]{.class_4257}He put two and two together and figured that surely the people would let an innocent prophet go before they would release a thief and a murderer! But Pilate's two‑and‑two (as Peale's and Weath­erhead's) turns out to be five.

["Said unto them, Ye have brought this man unto me, as one that perverteth the people: and, behold, I, having examined him before you, have found no fault in this man touching those things whereof ye accuse him: No, nor yet Herod: for I sent you to him; and, lo, nothing worthy of death is done unto him. I will therefore chastise him, and release him" ]{.class_4258}(Luke 23:14--16).

27:19 When he was set down on the judgment seat, his wife sent unto him, saying, Have thou nothing to do with that just man: for I have suffered many things this day in a dream because of him.

20 But the chief priests and elders persuaded the multitude that they should ask Barabbas, and destroy Jesus.

21 The governor answered and said unto them, Whether of the twain will ye that I release unto you? They said, Barabbas.

22 Pilate saith unto them, What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ? They all say unto him, Let him be crucified.

23 And the governor said, Why, what evil hath he done? But they cried out the more, saying, Let him be crucified.

Pilate is in a pickle. He has gotten himself into the worst mess a []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_607}man ever got into or ever can get into. He had Jesus on his hands and couldn't get rid of Him. He had it all figured. If he just had Jesus whipped, then when he brought Him up and placed Him beside Barabbas, the people would surely choose Jesus, [not Barabbas. ]{.class_4257}Pilate was going to use his head. Cleverness would produce a [compromise, ]{.class_4257}and instead of having Jesus crucified, he would just have Him [whipped.]{.class_4257} "I mean, after all, all the people wanted was the sight of a little blood." They were stirred up like spectators at a prize‑fight looking for the shine on the left glove, or like motorists passing a wreck and looking for red spots on the highway.

"I mean, after all, they were [good people, ]{.class_4257}and did not 'the kingdom of God' dwell somewhere in them?"

"I mean, really, isn't there a [little good in the worst ]{.class_4257}of us, and somewhere is there not a [divine spark ]{.class_4257}residing within that can be fanned to divinity?"

"Look at it positively. Think beautiful thoughts. Life can be beautiful. Judge not!"

Pilate didn't judge.

Like a faithful liberal, he just compromised and slipped through the tunnel, greased in political butter and religious vaseline, [and damned his soul doing it ]{.class_4257}(James 4:4).

["But the chief priests moved the people!" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 15:11).

AH, the priests!! As in 1962 (Dec.), when Jesuits destroyed churches and homes of the Assembly of God people in Brazil, as Jesuits in Portugal, Spain, Uruguay, and other Catholic countries demanded the revocation of Chessman's sentence from the Papist Pat Brown, so the priests here put on the pressure!

In [Christian Heritage]{.class_4257}, March, 1960, Kennedy was quoted as saying, "I don't take orders from Catholic organizations"; yet when Cardinal Dougherty of Philadelphia ordered Kennedy not to attend the dedication of the Memorial Chapel honoring the chaplains of the "Dorchester" (Dec. 21, 1959---[U.S. News and World Report]{.class_4257}), the lion-hearted P.T. Captain became the chicken-hearted Catholic and backed out---after [accepting the invitation!]{.class_4257}

["But the chief priests\...persuaded the multitude" ]{.class_4258}(Matt. 27:20).

All of Pilate's "cleverness" came to no avail.

The crowd is roaring: ["Crucify him, crucify him!" ]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_608}Pilate goes out to them and says: ["Behold, I bring him forth to you, that ye may know that I find no fault in him!" ]{.class_4258}(There it goes again! Three times Pilate prostitutes his sacred trust of office of governor.) What would have happened, do you suppose, if he had suddenly disrobed and placed his own toga over the bleeding back of Jesus, and transplanted his laurel wreath to the brow of God's Son, and then stepped forward and yelled at the top of his voice: ["Take me and crucify me; I'm a guilty sinner, but this is my Saviour, my God, and my King---take me; let me receive the due reward of my deeds, for this Man hath done nothing amiss!"]{.class_4257}

The thought is paralyzing. If Pilate could have done this---if he just could have risen to the occasion and seized the opportunity by the throat---his name would have gone down in history as the greatest man who ever lived outside of Jesus Himself. There would have been more books written about Pilate than Abraham Lincoln or Napoleon. But he couldn't make it. Pilate had everything but backbone, and if he ever needed that, [he needed it then.]{.class_4257}

["Then came Jesus forth, wearing the crown of thorns, and the purple robe" ]{.class_4258}(John 19:5).

Out He steps on the porch, above the heads of the people.

To fully understand the effect this appearance would have on the throng and on the governor himself, one must read the passages in the gospels that precede this appearance.

27:26 Then released he Barabbas unto them: and when he had scourged Jesus, he delivered him to be crucified.

27 Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the common hall, and gathered unto him the whole band of soldiers.

28 And they stripped him, and put on him a scarlet robe.

29 And when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon his head, and a reed in his right hand: and they bowed the knee before him, and mocked him, saying, Hail, King of the Jews!

30 And they spit upon him, and took the reed, and []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_609}smote him on the head.

["And the soldiers led him away into the hall, called Praetorium; and they call together the whole band. And they clothed him with purple, and platted a crown of thorns, and put it about his head, And began to salute him, Hail, King of the Jews! And they smote him on the head with a reed, and did spit upon him, and bowing their knees worshipped him" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 15:16--19).

["Then Pilate therefore took Jesus, and scourged him. And the soldiers platted a crown of thorns, and put it on his head, and they put on him a purple robe"]{.class_4258} (John 19:1--2).

Christ is sent back to Pilate from Herod with the gorgeous [purple robe]{.class_4257} on; this is stripped from Him in the hall of the Roman guardsmen, and a [scarlet robe ]{.class_4257}takes its place during the mockery. The praetorian guardsmen, of course, were the mercenary elite soldiers of Caesar; their modern equivalent is the Pope's SWISS (to imitate the Reformation!) guard or Hitler's "SS troops."

The crown of thorns is a ["stephanos" ]{.class_4257}(Greek), which is a [victor's crown, ]{.class_4257}not a [King's crown ]{.class_4257}which is a ["diadem" ]{.class_4257}(Greek). Thus, the soldiers unwittingly (and with malicious intentions) crown this King with the proper crown---for never forget for a minute that what is taking place here is a battle‑royal between the two crown princes of this world (see comments under Matt. 26:49--58). The brutality of the Roman soldiers is the common work of the ["spirit that NOW worketh in the children of disobedience" ]{.class_4258}(Eph. 2:1--3), and it is ["the PRINCE of the power of the air"]{.class_4258} (Eph. 2:1--3, 6:10--13) that is operating here in the Praetorium Hall. He is closing for hand‑to‑hand combat with God's Christ (Isa. 50:5--9) to get Him to quit. He does not quit.

Pilate is upstairs arguing with the crowd: ["What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ?" ]{.class_4258}The question is pathetic and ridiculous, yet it contains the greatest practical truths imaginable. In the setting, the question is absurd. What business does a Roman governor, in an army of occupation (who has pronounced a Man innocent twice already), have in asking a mob [what he is to do? ]{.class_4257}My God, is the man a governor or a janitor?!

"What shall I do?" This is the despairing cry of a lost child, not the firm arm of the law enforcing justice. "Do anything you want to Pilate; you're the governor!"

Pilate is a political Lucas instead of a Truscott. He is an Eisenhower instead of a Montgomery. He is a Clark instead of a Patton. He is Petain instead of a DeGaulle. Right or wrong, he hasn't got what it takes.

"What shall I do?"

Go play golf.

"What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ?"

The question, on the other hand, is remarkable in its construction, for it sums up the dilemma faced by every man who ever breathed. It is the sum total and epitome of every problem which ever faced a man. In one pathetic question, Pilate sums up the New Testament and the decision that every man will have to face. Cleverness, cowardice, compromise, and carelessness have failed to relieve him of the responsibility. Sophistry, sacraments, alibis, Scriptural excuses, philosophical conundrums, religious traditions, abstract conjectures, theoretical possibilities, situation ethics, religious tolerance, humanitarian penances, and "good intentions" all fail to solve the problem. The problem is: ["What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ?"]{.class_4258}

The problem is NOT what will "THEY" do, but what shall I DO?

The problem is not "What shall I do with the sacraments and the church?" but ["What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ?"]{.class_4258}

You must do something with Him. You can curse Him like an atheist, spit on Him like a Jew, eat Him like a Roman, ignore Him like a church member, or take Him as your own (John 1:10--13). ["What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ?"]{.class_4258}

Pilate had already had one thing done with Him; he has had Him whipped. ["What shall I do then with Jesus?"]{.class_4258}

What did He do for YOU? What SHOULD you do with Him? What will He do with you someday?! Think about that!

"What evil hath he done? But they cried out the more, saying, Let him be crucified!"

"Jesus is standing in Pilate's hall, friendless, forsaken, betrayed by all---hearken! What meaneth the sudden call? []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_611}[What will you do with Jesus? ]{.class_4257}Will you evade Him [as Pilate tried, ]{.class_4257}or will you take Him what ere betide? Vainly you struggle from Him to hide! [What will you do with Jesus? ]{.class_4257}What will YOU do with Jesus? Neutral you cannot be. Some day your heart will be asking: ['What will He do with me?'"]{.class_4257}

Whissslap! goes the whip! Fpffuttt!, the spit showers; Whap! go the fists! They are already doing [something ]{.class_4257}with Jesus, ["which is called Christ."]{.class_4258}

A crown of thorns is platted. (You can find the size and shape of this crown growing along the banks of the Suwanee River near Branford, Florida. It has spikes over an inch long.) The crown cannot be jammed down on His head without tearing the hand of the "jammer" to pieces, so the crown is [placed ]{.class_4257}on His head; then a man with a bamboo reed [beats it down ]{.class_4257}over the head till the thorns are stuck in the forehead and the top part of the ears; the blood welters out, down the face, and through the black hair. [The New Testament is well on the way.]{.class_4257}

The "king" now has a robe and a crown. He needs a scepter. A reed is stuck in His hand (since this is the flimsiest scepter possible), for Satan, working the hearts and hands of the mob, knows that when Jesus finally does take the throne (Luke 1:30--33), it will be to rule ["with a rod of iron" ]{.class_4258}(Rev. 12:5).

Now the King is coronated. The soldiers salute Him as a Superior and give the "Heil" of the soldier (Hail) and kneel before Him in mock subservience and submission: ["Hail, King of the Jews!!"]{.class_4258}

The scarlet robe is taken off; the King is stripped to the waist. He is then tied, hands over His head, to a large upright timber. The mercenaries of the garrison take out the long leather thongs, knotted with pieces of bone and flint, and the scourging begins---["forty stripes save one"]{.class_4258} is the standard recipe (2 Cor. 11:24; Gal. 6:17). The lash hisses through the air, and there appears a long pink stripe on the bare back; in seconds it has reddened and turned blue. The lashes slice the air and cut the back, the arms, the legs, the shoulders, the wrists, the neck, the buttocks, the side. The thongs curl around the body and hit the face, the breast, and the stomach. After thirty lashes the kidneys are nearly laid bare. The raw purple []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_612}welts, break and blood streams out, and the leather hits the raw inner flesh; the Lamb writhes and twists under the torture but opens not His mouth (Isa. 53:5, 7). He has challenged the King of ["the children of pride"]{.class_4258} to engage Him in a battle to the death, and He is conquering (Col. 2:15; Rom. 8:35--37).

["With his stripes we are healed," ]{.class_4258}the prophet writes and Peter assents (1 Pet. 2:24). ["Thou shalt not steal"---]{.class_4258}wwwhisslap! ["Thou shalt not kill"---]{.class_4258}wwwshissslap! ["Thou shalt not commit adultery"]{.class_4258}---wwwhisslap! ["All unrighteousness is sin"---]{.class_4258}wwwhisslap! ["The thought of foolishness is sin" ]{.class_4258}---wwhiisslap! ["Sin is the transgression of the law"---]{.class_4258}whisslap! ["Thou shalt not covet"---]{.class_4258} whisslap! ["Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world!!"]{.class_4258}

Never mind the Harvard five‑foot shelf! Never mind the precepts of Socrates, Buddha, and Lao Tze! ["Behold the Lamb of God!"]{.class_4258}

["Then came Jesus forth, wearing the crown of thorns, and the purple robe!" ]{.class_4258}(John 19:5).

The robe has been replaced, and now at the height of the drama, Pilate stands on the balcony where he has been arguing and trying to appease the crowd. There is a movement behind him, and a quarternion of soldiers step aside and let their charge stumble forward into the sunlight. There He is. Pilate is so impressed; he fails to speak. The crowd is so impressed that for a moment they almost forget the instructions of the chief priests. There stands the Prophet of Nazareth, head bloody and bowed. Blood running down the face, down the back, down the arms, over the shoulders, between the legs, through the toes, onto the pavement. The beard has been plucked until the face is peculiarly grotesque and unbalanced. Spit and tears are drying on the face, and the ghastly crown still brings forth fresh blood over the drying scabs, where He has been struck with fists. There stands the Son of God in all His agony---a man of sorrows and acquainted with grief. There is no hysterical weeping; there are no screams of anguish; there are no pleas for mercy. Jesus stands beside the Roman governor, thoughtful, silent, and locked in battle with His invisible adversary who has brought Him to this hour.

"Behold the man!"

Pilate is deeply impressed. Like the Roman soldiers who failed to []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_613}arrest Christ (John 7:46), Pilate is convinced that it is no ordinary Man standing beside him.

Breathless, the old Roman rascal waits for the results of his clever artifice. [Surely now they will let Jesus go and kill Barabbas! ]{.class_4257}He has it figured! Like a million men after him in every century, Pilate thought he was smart enough to GET AROUND the decision of receiving the Lord Jesus. (The greatest religious systems man ever erected, when analyzed, amount to nothing more than [complex attempts ]{.class_4257}to avoid John 1:10--13. I knew of a man who devoted his life to Archaeology so he could prove the Bible was a hoax, and thereby rid himself of the necessity of obeying John 1:10--13.) Pilate has it figured. The blood will placate the people, and they will spare Jesus. [But they don't.]{.class_4257}

["When the chief priests]{.class_4258} \[ah!\] [therefore and officers saw him, they cried out, saying, Crucify him, crucify him!"]{.class_4258} (John 19:6). ["And they were instant with loud voices, requiring that he might be crucified. And the voices of them and of the chief priests ]{.class_4258}\[ah!\][ prevailed" ]{.class_4258}(Luke 23:23).

Pilate in a fit of anger yells: ["Take YE him, and crucify him: for I find no fault in him!" ]{.class_4258}This is his last ditch stand to get out of it. He tempts them to take the responsibility for themselves and thereby to rebel against the government; but even this clever trick does not work, for they throw it back in his face with: ["We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God" ]{.class_4258}(see John 19:7). That ties the rag on the bush. Pilate is already keyed up to be a candidate for sleeping tablets. With the eclipse of his last hope of getting rid of the "king," another ISSUE suddenly presents itself---a religious issue! ["He made himself the Son of God."]{.class_4258}

To add vinegar to persimmons, Pilate's wife shows up at this inopportune moment (John 19:8) and says: "Honey\...?" (Well, it wasn't that, but you follow it.) ["Have thou nothing to do with that just man: for I have suffered many things this day in a dream because of him" ]{.class_4258}(see Matt. 27:19). Put yourself in Pilate's shoes and see what kind of a position it is. Isn't that what a modernist teaches you to do? Well, try it! Step out of your shoes a minute and get into Pilate's robe and go back into A.D. 33 into the judgment hall. []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_614}Just as you are about to sit down, your wife comes in to retell a dream she had that morning!

Now there you are. You are a big shot. You are a Chief Justice. You have been called upon to make a decision. You have thoroughly examined the Culprit, and called in another judge for consultation; you have punished the Offender MORE than He deserved; you have stated publicly THRICE that the man was innocent and have attempted to throw the case out of court. At this juncture you hear:

1\. The man may be [supernatural.]{.class_4257}

2\. Your own wife has dreams about this, [and believes it! ]{.class_4257}While Jesus is suffering under the lash, Mrs. P. Pilate is tossing on her couch and trying to sleep, late as usual, and she has been having nightmares all morning; it is now nearly 8 o'clock.

Pilate's condition, by now, can be described in colloquial Americana as "all shook up" or about "out of his skull." Being religious enough to know that the Jews were a peculiar people, and having read enough of the Old Testament to know how to officially cleanse his hands after an unclean act (Matt. 27:24; Deut. 21:6--7), old Pilate is just spiritual enough to [begin to doubt himself. ]{.class_4257}Could this Man whom he is trying [be God's Son? ]{.class_4257}The thought upsets Pilate so badly, he goes to the medicine cabinet.

["]{.class_4258}\[He\][\...went again into the judgment hall, and saith unto Jesus, Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no answer" ]{.class_4258}(see John 19:9). The question betrays Pilate's chain of thought. ["WHENCE art thou?"]{.class_4258} He already knew Jesus was from Galilee (Luke 23:6--7). The question, then, has nothing to do with Christ's EARTHLY domicile. It is a [space question! ]{.class_4257}["WHENCE art thou?" ]{.class_4258}["Where ]{.class_4257}are you from?" (Acts 14:11).

No answer. There is no need to answer. The man facing Jesus has already sinned against the light he has; he has already refused to ACT on what he knew, and so---[light rejected becomes lightning ]{.class_4257}(2 Thess. 2:2--12).

Pilate is trembling. His nerves are on edge; he is a perfect audience for a "positive thinking session" with Dr. Peale. Somehow, life is no longer "beautiful." His wife is behind him twisting a handkerchief up (and he'll have to live with her a few years!). The crowd outside is going to have the Roman army down on him if he []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_615}cannot disperse them soon. He has been dragged out of bed before 6 o'clock, and he is irritable. If that were not enough, the Man with whom he is called upon to communicate is not dressed decently, and His appearance would make a Marine Corpsman sick at his stomach. Jesus is standing opposite him silent. There is the blood, there is the crown, there are the whip marks, there is the ghastly mutilated face, and there is a spirit there that is still so unbroken that it refuses to answer a civil question! Pilate is infuriated. ["Speakest thou not unto me?" ]{.class_4258}The inner thought is, "I'll teach you some respect if I have to kill you! By God, don't you realize to whom you are talking---I'm [Pontius Pilate!" ]{.class_4257}["Knowest thou not that I have power to crucify thee, and have power to release thee?" ]{.class_4258}(John 19:10).

The appalling figure before him answers: ["Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath the greater sin" ]{.class_4258}(see John 19:11). This is the last word that Pontius Pilate ever hears from God's Son. It is a three letter word---SIN (John 3:16--20). It is that word that all philosophers scrupulously AVOID in academic discussions, and it is that word that is TABOO in the modern pulpit except as it is used to point out how ridiculous ["absolute truth" ]{.class_4257}is, or how biased Bible believers are about the subject. Fortunately, the Bible has defined the term in the simplest wording possible---["All unrighteousness is sin" ]{.class_4258}(1 John 5:17). But such definitions are "highly objectionable" to the self‑righteous mind of the Bible‑rejecting "Christian."

Jesus has here pointed out a fact which every ruler [instinctively ]{.class_4257}knows---at least if he is honest with himself. Pilate (as Nero, Alexander, Ghengis Khan, Charlemagne, Tamerlane, Hannibal, Castro, Bloody Mary, Jack Kennedy, Batiste, Count Tully, Loyola, Pope John and Francisco Franco) knew that without the operation of [some higher power, ]{.class_4257}that he himself could never have attained to his present position of authority (Rom. 13:1--5). Nearly all authorities recognize this fact, although they forget that this power is first delegated to SATAN, as the ["god of this world" ]{.class_4258}(2 Cor. 4:4). Jesus, though bound, whipped, weak from loss of blood, half‑naked, beaten, bruised, reviled, and captive---still cuts his adversary to the heart. Notice what follows: ["And from thenceforth Pilate sought to release him!"]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_616} (John 19:12).

Jesus had made every allowance for Pilate's circum­stances and had accused the Jews themselves of the greater ["SIN,"]{.class_4258} but that three‑letter word stuck fast in Pilate's craw; he couldn't extract it. Sin, sin, sin, sin, sin, sin. That is the word that is never given SERIOUS consideration in [History of Religions]{.class_4257} or [Religious Psychology]{.class_4257}. It says too much. The word says: ["You're wrong and you've done wrong, and no matter whether you think you have or not, or convince yourself that you have or not, you still have ]{.class_4257}(Lev. 5:17--19)." This is the "guilt complex" psychiatrists are paid \$35.00 an hour to remove in private bull sessions. (A lady came to one of these nut‑doctors with a fried egg over her head and two strips of bacon over her ears and said: "I want to talk to you about my BROTHER!")

Pilate goes out of the hall again (he is running in and out like a waitress serving a table) and is just about to tell the crowd to break it up and go on home, when the Jews cry out: ["If thou let this man go, thou art not Caesar's friend: whosoever maketh himself a king speaketh against Caesar" ]{.class_4258}(John 19:12). "Look out for your JOB, Pontius!"

The Jew here has used Roman law with which to bait the Roman. The statement is true, and Pilate himself has just heard Jesus admit that He is a King of some sorts (John 18:37). Now he is between a rock and a hard place. The crowd must be dispersed, his wife must be calmed, the Pharisees must be appeased, he himself must get some rest, he must not murder a Man he knows is innocent, and he does not dare offend Caesar at Rome! Pilate is in a predicament. Here is the Bible picture of man caught trying to serve two masters, torn between two loyalties, and trying to satisfy both. How well this is brought out in Acts 4:26 and 3:13! Pilate was ["gathered together" ]{.class_4258}against Christ and ["determined to let him go!" ]{.class_4258}This is the Bible version of "man against himself," the split personality. Pilate eventually finds out what every man finds out who tries to duck personal accountability to Jesus Christ; he learns that you cannot please men and God at the same time (Gal. 1:10; Acts 4:19). He tries to hold the world in one hand and God in the other, but they just won't "fellowship." (See 1 John 2:14--17.) He has only slid deeper []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_617}into the quicksand, and now he is faced with the possibility of losing his job, his means of making a living, and maybe even being exiled (which he was!) if he gets out of political favor with Caesar, the "Holy Father" of Rome. (Caesar, at this time, is the half‑mad and diseased Tiberias.) Pilate winds up letting a thief and a murderer go, siding with a nearly insane, pagan despot, and [killing ]{.class_4257}the Prince of Life. Plastic, pliable, vacillating Pilate! He had everything but backbone, and lack of this vital quality finished him.

["When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he brought Jesus forth, and sat down in the judgment seat in a place that is called the Pavement, but in the Hebrew, Gabbatha\...and he saith unto the Jews, Behold your King! But they cried out, Away with him, away with him, crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Shall I crucify your King? THE CHIEF PRIESTS ]{.class_4258}\[ah!\][ answered, WE have no king but Caesar!" ]{.class_4258}(John 19:13--15).

The die is cast, the lead has been poured, the mold is hard, and the image isfixed. Pilate can choose [Jesus ]{.class_4257}or [Rome. ]{.class_4257}The Jews have already chosen. They officially choose ROME as the nation to take [their ]{.class_4257}place as the "chosen people," and upon their choice, their history is determined (as the history of Europe) for the next 1,900 years. From A.D. 33 to the Second Coming, the old Roman Harlot of Revelation 17:1--6 [will reign over ]{.class_4257}["the kings of the earth"]{.class_4258}[ ]{.class_4257}(Rev. 17:18). The "eternal city" on the seven mountains (Rev. 17:9) is destined at this point by virtue of [religious election ]{.class_4257}to guide the world to its final catastrophic end under the leadership of Satan. Germany, France, England, U.S.A., Russia, China, and Spain may come and go; but Rome goes on to the end (Rev. 17:17--18). Nothing stops her short of the advent of the One she crucified! He comes in vengeance and wrath when He comes, and pays back fourfold a recompense to her bosom (Rev. 18:6).

The judgment seat here is outside in the court of the castle of Anthony; it is Pilate's last stand. One might say it is the end of a tragic and pitiful character who had to murder to keep the friendship of this world. He knew he was already beaten in John 19:12, and in the secret recesses of his heart, he knew that if it came to a showdown, [he would do anything to maintain his position and income ]{.class_4262}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_618}(1 Sam. 15:30). To taunt his tormentors, he says the last thing sarcastically: ["Shall I crucify YOUR King?" ]{.class_4258}But the priests (!) have the answer ready: ["We have no king but Caesar."]{.class_4258}

In one stroke, they deny their national, God‑given birthright as a nation of priests (Deut. 17:14). They sell out to Rome to keep [their own positions and income ]{.class_4262}(John 11:48--52). ["The love of money is the root of all evil" ]{.class_4258}(1 Tim. 6:10). As Rome controls the money and positions, the entire world will sell out and accept HER as the final authority (Rev. 17:18). The die is cast; the mold is hard, the lead is now being poured! ["What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ?"]{.class_4258}

27:24 When Pilate saw that he could prevail nothing, but that rather a tumult was made, he took water, and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, I am innocent of the blood of this just person: see ye to it.

25 Then answered all the people, and said, His blood be on us, and on our children.

The passage contains so much in its few words that infinity could hardly contain the truths that it unlocks. Occurring at the climax of the betrayal and condemnation of the Son of God, the verse capstones every verse in the Bible for an explanation of the subsequent course of history. This is the climax, for after here we only read:

["He ]{.class_4258}\[Pilate\][ delivered Jesus to their will" ]{.class_4258}(Luke 23:25), ["Then delivered he him therefore unto them to be crucified. And they took Jesus, and led him away"]{.class_4258} (John 19:16). (The passages in Matt. 27:26--30 have already been dealt with.)

["That rather a tumult was made." ]{.class_4258}Pilate compro­mised to "avoid trouble." Like Neville Chamberlain, Roosevelt, Lyndon Johnson, and Nixon, he "went along" for the sake of "maintaining peace in the family" (see Luke 12:51). Pilate does the CONVENIENT thing, as Judas and Felix; he takes the short‑cut over righteousness to maintain PEACE, not knowing that real PEACE is always spoken of (in the Bible) as [dependent on righteousness ]{.class_4257}(James 3:17; Zech. 8:16; Heb. 7:2). History is a 4,000‑year demonstration of the fact []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_619}that men prefer peace to righteousness and that they never attain peace because they never attain righteousness (Matt. 6:33). (Rejection of the word of God is the source and root of the failure, and no group of leaders in this century will admit "the word" [that they want to accept is ]{.class_4257}the unholy and ungodly and uninspired Vaticanus, containing seven romantic novels \[for people who believe in universal peace instead of righteousness\]. \[Rev. 13:2--8.\])

["He took water, and washed his hands." ]{.class_4258}Obeying the Old Testament Jewish ordinance of "washings" (Heb. 6:2) for "purification of sins" (Acts 22:16), Pilate does what any Campbellite or Catholic would have done under the circumstances. To wash away his sins, [he uses water instead of blood. ]{.class_4257}Where Pilate is excusable (since the crucifixion has not yet taken place), the Campbellite and Catholic are both inexcusable, as they use the Jewish passages from Acts 2:38 and 22:16 to deceive their congregations. [The blood has been shed ]{.class_4257}(Heb. 10:1--14).

"What can wash away my sins? Nothing but the\..."---the what?

Certainly not "holy water" and the "holy sacra­ments" and the "Holy see" and the "holy saw" and the "holy socks!" The blood is given to PURGE, and it [has purged, so ]{.class_4257}that neither fire nor water (Num. 31:23) are used any longer in purification (Heb. 1:3; 9:14). Pilate obeyed the custom that a Jewish proselyte would obey---Deuteronomy 21:7; and in so doing, unwittingly places himself into the one chapter in the Bible that tells Israel how to get the blood off their hands! Read the chapter; it is remarkable.

1\. The subject is [innocent blood ]{.class_4257}(Deut. 21:8).

2\. The context is a [hanging on a tree ]{.class_4257}(vs. 22).

3\. He is to be taken down, as Christ (vs. 23).

4\. The valley is the valley of Gehenna (vs. 4).

5\. The city "nearest" is Jerusalem (vss. 2--3).

6\. The restitution has to be made by the LEADERS (vss. 2, 6).

7\. The accusations in the chapter are made against Christ in the same form (vs. 20).

8\. The blood is not forgiven Israel [officially ]{.class_4257}until the ceremony of Deuteronomy 21:2--9 is performed; when it is, ["THE blood shall be forgiven them!" ]{.class_4258}[(Notice the article---]{.class_4257}["the"]{.class_4258}[---as in Matthew 27:4.)]{.class_4257}

["Innocent of the blood of this just person." ]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_620}Notice the fourth and final statement of the judge as to the culprit's innocency. [Pilate says what his wife said in Matthew 27:19!]{.class_4257}

["Then answered all the people." ]{.class_4258}This is the fatal hour. What passes off as a mob response to an irrelevant issue becomes the ordained [retribution ]{.class_4257}for a nation through 1,900 years of history. From the first Jew nailed to a tree outside Jerusalem in A.D. 70 to the last of 6,000,000 cremated and liquidated under Hitler, [the answer of all the people ]{.class_4257}is the determining factor.

["Then answered all the people." ]{.class_4258}They are ALL in on it as predicted (Exod. 12:6). ["Then answered all the people\...His blood be on us, and on our children." ]{.class_4258}The choice is self-determined. When the priests start belly­aching in Acts 5:28 about the apostles' teaching, they don't have a leg to stand on. How can they accuse the apostles of bringing ["this man's blood upon us," ]{.class_4258}when they have already shouted the thing out and encouraged the whole crowd to do the same? ["His blood be on us, and on our children!!!"]{.class_4258}

If Christ were an ordinary religious martyr, the history of the Jews from this point forward would have been differently written. The surest proof in the world that the Man's blood being required was God's man, with God's BLOOD (see comments under Matt. 27:4), is the subsequent history of Israel. ["His blood be on us, and on our children."]{.class_4258}

If the blood is not out of the ordinary, there is nothing to sweat about. [But if it is God's blood, ]{.class_4257}as reported in the text of Acts 20:28, then a fearful history must ensue. No one who knows Jewish history could sanely doubt the tragic implication of this cry: ["His blood be on us, and on our children." ]{.class_4258}If you did not believe in the Deity of Christ by any other token than history from [A.D. 70 to 1945, ]{.class_4257}you would have ample, irrefutable evidence.

[Ah, the bush that is burned without being consumed ]{.class_4257}(see Mal. 3:6).

On the cross, our Lord says: ["Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do," ]{.class_4258}and so the Father grants the rulers of Israel another opportunity to receive the King and the Kingdom (Acts 1--7). But this time [they know what they are doing ]{.class_4257}(Acts []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_621}7:30--51), and the long history of reaping the decision begins.

In 135, Hadrian had 580,000 Jews killed. In 1020, Canute banished all Jews from England. In 1006, a holy war was started in Europe to kill all Jews not submitting to Roman Catholic baptism (called "Christian" in the school books). In 1272, Edward I confiscated all Jewish property and exiled 16,500 from England. ["His blood be on us, and on our children!"]{.class_4258}

They were banished from Prague and Constantinople in 1560--1600. Dominicans ran them out of Spain in the Inquisition (1492). In Alsace, Rhineland, Franconia, Bavaria, and Austria, in 1684, they were killed. In 1919 when England reneged on the Balfour Declaration (siding with the Pope's Arabian Ally, the Grand Mufti), they were left without a country, packed, starving, and dying on ships that no port would receive but Singapore. In Ukrainia, in 1942--43, 5,700,000 were butchered or disappeared. Dr. Bela Fabian, President of the Hungarian Independent Democratic Party, said 5,000,000 were gassed and cremated at the murder factory in Auschwitz (Silesia). This was done by dropping Cyclon B (crystallized prussic acid) into chambers where naked people were jammed in so tight they couldn't fall down. (Ilse Koch, wife of the Commander of Buchenwald, had prisoners with "interesting" tatoo marks killed so their skins could be used for lampshades.) Rudolph Hess said you could tell when the people in the chambers were dead because they stopped [screaming.]{.class_4257}

"His blood be on us, and on our children!"

How say ye, then, that it is ordinary Blood? It can't be.

Nowhere did God (or history, if you are a stupid evolutionist) ever exact such a toll on [one race. ]{.class_4257}The Jew in history is the final unanswerable proof that the Bible account of the Virgin Birth, Atoning Death, and Bodily Resurrection is absolutely correct and is as established a scientific fact as the laws of expansion and contraction of gases.

The scene now changes from conversations between Pilate, Jesus, and the chief priests to the torturous and weary walk through Jerusalem, out of the city gate and up to Calvary, ["the place of a skull." ]{.class_4258}Before commenting on the verses, a few critical notes may be made of the texts involved in the trial.

The [RSV ]{.class_4262}has omitted the words ["just person" ]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_622}from Matthew 27:24. This blunder in translation comes from putting Vaticanus ahead of the majority of early and late manuscripts. Manuscripts A, C, E, F, G, and Aleph (Sinaiticus) agree with the Textus Receptus reading of the King James. Only "D" (Italian in the west) and Theta (ninth century) agree with the [RSV ]{.class_4262}reading which denies Christ the title of ["just person."]{.class_4258}

The [RSV ]{.class_4257}omits the statement on the "cock-crowing" in Mark 14:68. This perversion is the left over garbage from an ancient superstitution that cocks crow only [once ]{.class_4257}before six o'clock, and that therefore, the account in Mark is in error. However, the [RSV ]{.class_4257}admits in verse 72 of the same passage that the cock was to crow [twice. ]{.class_4257}Cocks, as a matter of fact, DO crow long before sun‑up (once around 3 a.m. and again around 6 a.m.), but this is a common habit of nature hidden to the [RSV ]{.class_4257}translators. The omission of the passage, again, is due to the weight of Vaticanus with the translators.

The word "WOOD" is inserted for ["TREE"]{.class_4258} in the passage in Luke 23:31, so the connection with Israel in the Old Testament is lost. The [RSV ]{.class_4257}translators are all post- and amillennial, so they would be especially anxious here to side‑track the reading. [The Greek says ]{.class_4257}["tree"]{.class_4258}[ in all manuscripts.]{.class_4257}

The [RSV, ]{.class_4257}with its sister‑translation the [Amplified Version]{.class_4257}, is careful to omit the word ["NOW"]{.class_4258} in John 18:36. (The word ["nun" ]{.class_4257}\[Gr.\] is found in over 600 manuscripts, thus proving that the [RSV ]{.class_4257}and the [Amplified ]{.class_4257}both are deliberate, intentional attempts to deceive the reader into believing something that is not so. They both belong in file 13.)

27:31 And after that they had mocked him, they took the robe off from him, and put his own raiment on him, and led him away to crucify him.

32 And as they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name: him they compelled to bear his cross.

33 And when they were come unto a place called Golgotha, that is to say, a place of a skull,

34 They gave him vinegar to drink mingled with gall: and when he had tasted thereof, he would not drink.

35 And they crucified him, and parted his garments, casting lots: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, They parted my garments among them, and upon my vesture did they cast lots.

36 And sitting down they watched him there;

["They took the robe off from him." ]{.class_4258}When Jesus goes to the cross, then, [He goes robeless, ]{.class_4257}and the garments for which the soldiers shoot dice are not "His Robe, His Robe," etc. They are called ["raiment" ]{.class_4258}and ["vesture," ]{.class_4258}but never [robes. ]{.class_4257}Lloyd Douglas' "The Robe" (as the song and the movie) is produced in the best possible Roman Catholic tradition that assigns holiness to "things." Of the two robes worn, [purple and scarlet ]{.class_4257}(Matt. 27:28; John 19:1--4), neither are Christ's and neither are holy. Since they are the official colors of Roman Catholic potentates, we are not surprised to find them on the old Mother Harlot (Rev. 17:1--4): ["purple and scarlet." ]{.class_4258}(Read it; don't believe me--- I might be a Protestant!!)

["And led him away to crucify him." ]{.class_4258}Although Christ begins the journey by carrying His cross (John 19:17), it is not long before a man is apprehended to carry it for Him: ["Simon a Cyrenian," "the father of Alexander and Rufus"]{.class_4258} (Mark 15:21). The journey is a fifteen‑minute climb (or was in 1889), and the destination is undoubtedly GORDON'S Calvary, north of Jerusalem, ["without the gate"]{.class_4258} (Heb. 13:12). The Catholic Calvary, inside the city, is typical of the perverted, obstinate, and private interpretations of Rome to write her own account contrary to Scriptures. One of the requirements of the gospel in this age (1 Cor. 15:1--6) is that the account be ["according to the scriptures," ]{.class_4258}and there is nothing Scriptural at all about a brown‑haired Christ getting crucified on Good Friday inside the city walls of Jerusalem. This is an Irish Christ eating fish and staying "in the camp" to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season; it has nothing to do with the Bible or the Crucifixion or "Christians" or the New Testament church.

Typical of the obscene and indecorous myths of Rome is the one that is supposed to have taken place in the context of Luke 23:26--33. On the Via Dolorosa, Christ is supposed to have fallen, and an imprint of His bloody face was supposed to have been caught in a [handkerchief, ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_624}placed there by a supposed saint whose name was supposed to have been "Veronica." This (with the supposed "authentic" description of Christ by the Roman Lentulus Justinian) accounts for the thousands of Errol Flynn like pictures of Christ, adorned with red­brown hair of a European Irishman. But there are too many "supposes," and in view of the fact that the description is contrary to Scripture (Song of Sol. 5:10--13), it may be discounted at once as merely "more of the same." Rome never tires of religious clap‑trap and nonsense.

["Simon\...a man of Cyrene" ]{.class_4258}would imply that the man was colored, although this is not stated and cannot be proved. Cyrene is the modern "Benghazi," or near to it, where Rommel fled, retreating from Alamein to Tunisia. It is on the North African coast.

Before they reach the place of the skull, Luke 23:27--31 records: ["And there followed him a great company of people, and of women, which also bewailed and lamented him. But Jesus turning unto them said, Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves, and for your children. For, behold, the days are coming, in the which they shall say, Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bare, and the paps which never gave suck. Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us. For if they do these things in a green tree, what shall be done in the dry?"]{.class_4258}

Jesus is one of the few criminals whose death is not cheered; instead, there is weeping and lamentation accom­panying His final march down Death Row. The women especially are affected (Luke 23:49, 24:10; Mark 15:40--41). This is not hard to understand when one remembers that the difference between women being beasts of burden and being civilized individuals is in the difference between [oriental ]{.class_4257}(or African) [religion ]{.class_4257}and [Bible Christianity. ]{.class_4257}Since the English‑speaking nations have always used the [King James Bible]{.class_4257} (1611), their women have privileges and opportunities no other women have. In Africa and Asia, the woman is a beast of burden; and in Spain, Italy, Mexico, Columbia, and Venezuela, she is little more than a breeder and cook. As a famous evangelist once said: "The worst moral monstrosity in this world is a woman who doesn't love []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_625}Jesus Christ."

["Daughters of Jerusalem." ]{.class_4258}The expression is always used doctrinally in referring to the believing remnant of Israel, whether in the Old Testament or the coming tribulation (Lam. 2:13; Micah 4:8; Zeph. 3:14; Song of Sol. 2:7, 3:5, 5:8). In the context of Luke 23, the reference is to the immediate destruction of Jerusalem, but doctrinally, it can refer to nothing except the Second Advent. Notice:

1\. Verse 29 is parallel to Matthew 24:19.

2\. Verse 28 is the children who inherit the blood guilt of Matthew 27:25.

3\. The reference to the mountains and hills (vs. 30) is found in Revelation 6:16--17 and Jeremiah 17:2--3.

4\. The "green tree and dry" (vs. 31) are found in Ezekiel 17:23 and Hosea 9:10. The ["green"]{.class_4258} is Israel of the first advent and the[ "dry"]{.class_4258} is Israel at the second.

This is another thing that the dead‑orthodox scholar can never get straight. He has, by inheritance, obtained a sorry system of interpretation which states that a verse can have only [one meaning and one application. ]{.class_4257}This is a disastrous teaching in dealing with a book that is the Living word of a God who knows [past, present, and future ]{.class_4257}in one look. Luke 23:28--31 could refer to the destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70, but the passage is plainly a PROPHECY, and where the companion verses occur (that are corollaries to this prophecy), all point to the Second Advent. This is what lead Calvin, Berkhof, and Hodge to assume that the Second Coming was the destruction of Jerusalem and that, therefore, the book of Revelation [had already been fulfilled.]{.class_4257}

["They were come unto a place called Golgotha." ]{.class_4258}Luke adds that ["there were also two other, malefactors, led with him to be put to death"]{.class_4258} (Luke 23:32). Golgotha is ["Calvary"]{.class_4258} (["the place of a skull"]{.class_4258}). The [RV ]{.class_4257}prints only "skull" at Luke 23:33. Correct translation would be a ["bare skull," ]{.class_4257}and this of course is the Latin for [a bare skull---"Calvaria]{.class_4257}." Since the Chaldean ["Golgotha"]{.class_4258} is this kind of a skull, the [AV ]{.class_4257}of Luke 23:33 may be retained as accurate---Calvary. The place is called "the skull" either because condemned criminals were beheaded there (Je­rome's teaching, 346--420), or []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_626}because the hill had a skull‑like shape or appearance to it (the modern view), or because of the tradition coming from Origen (184--254) [that Adam was buried there.]{.class_4257}

["They gave him vinegar to drink mingled with gall." ]{.class_4258}Mark says: ["They gave him to drink wine mingled with myrrh: but he received it not" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 15:23). This is the "drink of the condemned," recommended by the seer in Proverbs 31:5--7 (a text very highly prized by beer‑heads and winoes!). Jesus does NOT drink it since ["wine\...is not for kings, O Lemuel" ]{.class_4258}(Prov. 31:4). As a condemned criminal, Jesus has a right to drink it, but even in this, the hour of His death, He is careful [not to drink wine, ]{.class_4257}lest some European booze‑hound would ever use it as Scripture proof that liquor should be used in the Lord's Supper. (In spite of the extra precaution, even at the hour of execution, the European went right ahead anyway and said He drank wine, [when He did not.)]{.class_4257}

Psalm 69:21 describes the event 1,000 years before it takes place, and the reader will not fail to notice the reference to Mary's other children, by Joseph, in the context---verses 8--9 (see John 2:17).

The standard form of crucifixion was [nailing ]{.class_4257}(Col. 2:14), and there is little, if any, authority for the theory that the two thieves were [tied ]{.class_4257}instead of nailed. There is considerable argument about what kind of a cross it was, whether a "T" or an "X" or a "+," but at any rate, it spells and signifies one of the most terrible deaths devised by man, who devotes his centuries to finding ways to destroy himself.

Pilate writes a title which is put on the cross before it is lifted, and it is written in three languages---Hebrew (Old Testament), Greek (New Testament), and then Pilate's own language---"Latin" (standing for the corrupt additions to the Bible). In the three languages chosen is found the key that unlocks the Babel of tongues; for Latin opens Spanish and French, Greek opens Russian and Slavic and German and then English, while the Hebrew opens Arabic and Hindu and, hence, Chinese and Japanese.

Hebrew, Greek, Latin.

There we find the three main groups of mankind combining in one grand ecumenical movement to do away with the Word of God. []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_627}The religious world is represented by the Hebrew, the intellectual world is represented by the Greek (1 Cor. 1:22--25), and the political world by the Latin. The trinity of leaders is remarkable. [The Hebrew ---]{.class_4257}there is the Pharisee. [The Greek---]{.class_4257}there is the Sadducee. [The Latin---]{.class_4257}there is the Herodian. [The Hebrew---]{.class_4257}there is the Jew. [The Greek]{.class_4257} is a liberal, and [the Latin ]{.class_4257}is a Catholic. Hence: [Protestantism ]{.class_4257}(apostate liberal); Catholicism (apostate Roman); Judaism (apostate Hebrew): the grand ecumenical alliance (Luke 23:12).

["Write not, The King of the Jews; but that he said, I am King of the Jews" ]{.class_4258}(John 19:21). In plainer words, "Don't present the thing as a fact; present it as His [own personal opinion."]{.class_4257} But Pilate has been through too much. His patience is "wore out." He has had enough Jews in one day to poison a parish, and he just mutters nastily: ["What I have written I have written." ]{.class_4258}\["The moving hand having writ, moves on, nor all thy piety, nor all thy wit\..." (Rev. 20:12--14).\]

The victim is stretched on the wood. His back is already fiery red and swollen from dozens of lacerations. The head thrown back on the cross-piece drives a few thorns in further. Having lost pints of blood, the pale and wracked body is held down, while soldiers kneel on the arms and legs and drive the spikes in. The cross is then raised by three men. When the base of the upright is slanted into a hole (dug for that purpose), it is suddenly hoisted up and falls with a sickening thud into the hole, which is about three feet deep. The body tears on its metal hangings, and as all three crosses go up amidst the cursing of the other two criminals and the jeers and cries of the mob, Jesus is heard to say: ["Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do."]{.class_4258} There is no explanation for the prayer at this time unless there was a possibility that at this particular moment, the Almighty had taken about all the "long suffering" He could take (Gen. 6:13) and was poised on the rim of eternity ready to wipe out humanity with a stroke, if Jesus so desired. (See Num. 14:1--2 for example.)

["And sitting down they watched him there." ]{.class_4258}What they see is a half naked (if not entirely naked) Jew dying. A paper flutters over His head saying: "THIS IS JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS."[ "THIS IS ]{.class_4258}(Matthew, Luke) [JESUS ]{.class_4258}(Matthew, John) [OF NAZARETH]{.class_4258} (John) [THE KING OF THE JEWS ]{.class_4258}(all)." His body is []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_628}turning pale; the eyelids are heavy. With the loss of blood, there is intense dehydration. The blood is drying on His face, chest, and legs. Every muscle in the body is cramped and strained. The bones are pulled out of joint from the continued weight of the body forward. The tears and the spittle are co‑mingled on the countenance. ["Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world!"]{.class_4258}

Bullinger has proposed that there are FIVE crosses present, not three. The proposition is not as wild as it sounds. He first of all points out that [two malefactors ]{.class_4257}and [two thieves ]{.class_4257}are present. Although a thief is a malefactor, a malefactor is not necessarily a thief. Bullinger says that the criminals mentioned in Matthew and Mark are not brought to Golgotha until AFTER the garments are divided (Mark 15:27; Matt. 27:38). Further, in John 19:32--33, the soldiers go by two men [before ]{.class_4257}they get to Jesus, so there must be five, for Jesus is said to be crucified "in the midst" (see Luke 23:33; Mark 15:27; John 19:18)! If He is between them, then there is a malefactor and a thief on His right and a malefactor and a thief on His left. That isn't all---it is abundantly clear in the Bible that "5" is the number of DEATH [(not grace!): ]{.class_4257}Genesis 5:5; Acts 5:5; Romans 5; three men killed below the fifth rib, Adam opened here for Eve, David's five stones, Benjamin's five-fold provision for his mother's death, [and five open wounds in Christ ]{.class_4257}(one in each hand, one in each foot, and one in His side). If that were not enough, there are five pieces of clothing divided at His death (see John 19:23--24). One is made especially so that there CANNOT be six pieces.

However, since the synoptic accounts are not always chronological (as we have seen from comparing the passages on the trial of Christ), and as different words are [occasionally ]{.class_4257}used to designate the same person (Pilate, Pontius Pilate, the Governor, etc.), it will be safe to hold to three crosses.

["Then the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments, and made four parts, to every soldier a part; and also his coat: now the coat was without seam, woven from the top throughout. They said therefore among themselves, Let us not rend it, but cast lots for it, whose it shall be: that the scripture might be fulfilled, which saith, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_629}They parted my raiment among them, and for my vesture they did cast lots. These things therefore the soldiers did" ]{.class_4258}(John 19:23--24).

["Then said Jesus, Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do. And they parted his raiment, and cast lots. And the people stood beholding. And the rulers also with them derided him, saying, He saved others; let him save himself, if he be Christ, the chosen of God" ]{.class_4258}(see Luke 23:34--35).

What appears as a brutal and cruel trick is actually SOP in Army routine. If you killed every G.I. in America who had taken a luger or Samurai sword or flag or pair of binoculars off a wounded or dead prisoner, you would cut down the manpower of the United States infantry considerably---at least the combat factor. Soldiers are soldiers. Someone is going to get the clothes, unless they are thrown away with the corpse. Who gets them? If the soldiers have to do the dirty work (and they do), why should they not get the clothes? And if there are not enough to go around, why not shoot dice to see who gets what? It is like the German infantryman said in World War 1: ["Today we pass through the scenes of our youth like travellers. We are burnt up by hard facts; like tradesmen we understand distinctions, and like butchers, necessities. We are no longer untroubled---we are indifferent."]{.class_4257}

The Lord only knows how much unscriptural expositing there has been done on the passages dealing with the crucifixion, for when the scene is approached, the [doctrinal ]{.class_4257}implications are so involved that men forget the actual scene. The Pharisees are not venomous beasts out of the primitive past; [they are gathering in the councils in the twentieth century! ]{.class_4257}These soldiers rolling bones are not inhu­mane Roman monsters; [they are infantrymen in the Ardennes in 1945. ]{.class_4257}The three men hanging are not pictures in a storybook. Two of them are criminals caught by the FBI in 1950, and only one is the sinless Son of God who is dying a death unlike any death that ever took place.

When one considers it, the Lord Jesus Himself is the only One "out of the contemporary scene" in the picture. He would have been out of place in any century since He did not have that sinful human []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_630}nature that His "brethren" had. The only figure in the scene that is [unreal ]{.class_4257}(in the true sense) is the Lord Jesus Himself; all else remains the same. The scene can be transferred with no trouble at all into the twentieth century where the evolutionists flatter themselves with the stupid conceit that ["if we had been in the days of our fathers, WE WOULD NOT HAVE\...." ]{.class_4258}(You would too. You probably already have and just aren't honest enough to admit it!)

Everywhere in the text, the Holy Spirit is careful to avoid the mistake that Lloyd Douglas and his Roman following made---"THE ROBE." [It is never called "a robe." ]{.class_4257}The "robes" are the strict property of the Romans who mistried, whipped, reviled, and murdered Him. [Robes are Roman, ]{.class_4257}not Christian. What the soldiers deal with is called: vesture, garment, raiment, clothes, coat---but [never ]{.class_4257}"robe." Folks say, "Well, that's just the way the [AV ]{.class_4257}happened to put it!" Yes, that's right. Isn't it marvelous how CHRISTIAN and how UN‑ROMAN the [AV ]{.class_4257}is?

The coat, as the coat of the High Priest (and as the tabernacle construction), is a single piece, woven so that it resembled a poncho. Only one man could use it, as there were no seams by which to divide it. So they shoot dice, without being conscious of the fact that this vesture which clothes Christ is a type of that vesture which clothes God the Father---the universe. This further makes the TABERNACLE a type of the universe (Heb. 9:8--9, 23--25). (But we are too far in orbit already; I sense the orthodox "brethren" about to object and say that it is a "type of salvation", so let us return to the text.)

27:37 And set up over his head his accusation written, THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS.

38 Then were there two thieves crucified with him, one on the right hand, and another on the left.

39 And they that passed by reviled him, wagging their heads,

40 And saying, Thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest it in three days, save thyself. If thou be the Son of God, come down from the cross.

41 Likewise also the chief priests mocking him, with the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_631}scribes and elders, said,

42 He saved others; himself he cannot save. If he be the King of Israel, let him now come down from the cross, and we will believe him.

43 He trusted in God; let him deliver him now, if he will have him: for he said, I am the Son of God.

44 The thieves also, which were crucified with him, cast the same in his teeth.

["Wagging their heads" ]{.class_4258}is a vivid description. It is nodding the head from side‑to‑side and up‑and‑down like a dog's tail, as if to say, "Oh yeah, yeah, sure, sure I know!" It is one of the most sarcastic gestures used with speech in the Near East and among the Latins.

["Save thyself!" ]{.class_4258}The sarcasm is blasphemous, yet unintentional. The meaning is simply, "If you're so good at getting people out of trouble, let's see you get yourself out." Job says this in Job 12:4, and you will find it again in the proverb: ["Physician, heal thyself" ]{.class_4258}(Luke 4:23). As it comes out, however, it has doctrinal import (in view of subsequent revelation) as: "If [You're the Saviour of man, practice what You preach! Save Yourself so we can believe it!" ]{.class_4257}The irony of it is that if He HAD come down and saved Himself, [you couldn't have been saved! ]{.class_4257}(Rom 5:9).

At this point, we come to the heart of the meaning of the CROSS, spoken of so much in Galatians and Romans. The cross is so misunderstood by most people that many of them wear a copy of it around their neck, thinking this means something. Others sing: "On a hill far away stood an old rugged cross," with a kind of far‑away dreamy look in their eye and a hollowness in their voices. Yet for a true understanding of the Cross, one must not merely see Jesus dying, but understand what He is going through emotional­ly and mentally while the mob jeers.

Sam Jones says, "A cross is where your will runs this way (---) and God's will runs that way [(l]{.class_4263}). Do you see where they cross? Well, that's a cross!" There is an element of truth in this saying, but it does not touch the subject deeply enough. E. L. Maxwell has an excellent devotional book called [Born Crucified, ]{.class_4257}which discusses the cross as []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_632}it is related to the world, the flesh, the devil, the Law, and the Christian. However, rarely in it does he dissect to the marrow of the matter. The word "cross" is usually pictured as synonymous with [death. ]{.class_4257}But [death ]{.class_4257}can be comfortable, and if not comfortable, it can be [sudden, ]{.class_4257}and if not [sudden, ]{.class_4257}it at least has an [end. ]{.class_4257}The Cross involves more than a shameful, lingering, agonizing death, although it does involve all these things (Heb. 13:12, 14; Gal. 2:20).

The heart of the cross is that the One on it [had the power to get off it, ]{.class_4257}and in the face of temptation to do so (and mockery if He didn't), [He stayed on it.]{.class_4257}

Now think about that for a few minutes.

Come to think of it, meditate on it a few [days, ]{.class_4257}when you consider your own position and the fact that the Lord [has not ]{.class_4257}answered your prayer! Could He have answered? You do believe that "with God all things are possible," don't you? Of course. [Then why has He not answered? ]{.class_4257}Consider Christ (Heb. 12:3). No sin in the way, no faults or errors blocking prayer---He is in the will of God, wholly surrendered. He does not [deserve ]{.class_4257}what He is suffering; He can get relief from it in a minute. The crowd below does not believe this. They [would believe ]{.class_4257}(Matt. 27:42) if He can get the prayer through. He can prove His point [and glorify God ]{.class_4257}by showing them His power. Without this proof, they will think they are right and will go to hell believing they are right! What should He do? [He stays on the cross.]{.class_4257}

Have YOU ever carried a "CROSS"?

Don't answer glibly with references from the New Testament about the [doctrinal application ]{.class_4257}of the BIBLE to the CROSS. Answer from the heart: "Do you know what it is like to be near deliverance and to have it within your grasp and be unable to attain it; yet having faith to believe it is there, and then have the hooting mob deny your Saviour and your faith because [neither could deliver you?"]{.class_4257}

Ah, there is the diamond in the mine!

["If he be the King of Israel, let him now come down from the cross, and we will believe him." ]{.class_4258}The taunt is Satanically inspired by that one who offered the King the CROWN in Matthew 4 and was insulted and told to get out (Matt. 4:10). Since the main subject of the Bible is a Kingdom, we find the whole issue "coming to a head" on "the SKULL." That is where the crown is []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_633}placed. That is where Jesus is crucified. That is where Christ will land when He returns (Gen. 3:15). Yes, the "head" figures. Here, the head of a new race (born of God; Rom. 8:1--20) faces the head of the serpent and fights out the last few minutes of the final round. He came for a CROSS. He will come for a [crown ]{.class_4257}(Luke 1:30--33). Satan hurls his last bolt against the helpless and bloody Son of Man, who has come to take His kingdom from him (Matt. 12:29). Satan knows who this is---this is the KING. Only one thief on the hill recognizes the truth. While the religious leaders are being used of Satan to try to get Jesus to call for help, one of these thieves renders true homage to the One who will be[ "King of kings, and Lord of lords" ]{.class_4258}(Rev. 19).

["And one of the malefactors which were hanged railed on him, saying, If thou be Christ, save thyself and us. But the other answering rebuked him, saying, Dost not thou fear God, seeing thou art in the same condemnation? And we indeed justly; for we receive the due reward of our deeds: but this man hath done nothing amiss. And he said unto Jesus, Lord, remember me when thou comest into thy kingdom. And Jesus said unto him, Verily I say unto thee, To day shalt thou be with me in paradise" ]{.class_4258}(Luke 23:39--43).

As if to take sides with Hell, the [ASV, RSV]{.class_4257}, and [RV ]{.class_4257}all attack this precious portion of Scripture with all the ammunition they can load and carry. It would seem that they are determined, if possible, to steal salvation even from a dying thief! ("Honor among thieves!") One is hanging, and the others are plundering his "last will and testament" by changing the words that he speaks. No­where in the Bible can the hatred of liberal and Catholic scholarship for the doctrine of GRACE be found any clearer than in this passage. Catholic and liberal unite in a spirit of perfect ecumenicity to [steal ]{.class_4257}God's salvation from the dying man. Keep reading.

["If thou be Christ." ]{.class_4258}Notice the ["IF,"]{.class_4258} as though it were lifted bodily out of the similar passage in Luke 4:3. Satan speaks [three times ]{.class_4257}in the Scripture---once to Eve, once of Job, and once to Jesus. To Eve, he questions: ["Yea, hath God said?" ]{.class_4258}(Gen. 3:1). To God (concerning Job), he says: ["Doth Job fear God for nought?" ]{.class_4258}To Jesus, he says: ["If thou be the Son of God\...." ]{.class_4258}Notice the favorite device of ["questioning the premise," ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_634}the approach of all modern education and science to matters of final authority. There is a QUESTION about every speech. The dying thief voices his father's manner (John 8:44) when he spits out: ["If thou be Christ!" ]{.class_4258}In the light of this evidence, what a travesty of rationalism it is to say that ["God is everybody's Father." ]{.class_4257}Such a thing is absolutely impossible (see Eph. 2:1--4). God never sired anyone who yelled at Christ: ["If thou be the Son of God\...save thyself,"]{.class_4258} or ["If thou be Christ, save thyself and us!" ]{.class_4258}The impenitent thief says this in cadence and chorus with the leading religious leaders of the council (Luke 23:35).

In this dark and lonely hour, when Judas has sold Him out, Simon Peter has cursed Him, the soldiers have stripped and whipped Him, God is about to turn His face from Him, and Thomas is doubting Him (along with the Pharisees and Sadducees); the truth of God suddenly pierces the heart of one of the condemned.

Somewhere in the yelling, jeering, howling madness of the moment, a man thinks clearly. Something fails to make sense to this man. It is a miracle that he is able to think at all under the conditions of the hour. Nevertheless, it strikes this man as odd that everyone is picking on Jesus! That is the simple and inordinate truth that gets through to him. It is like a flashlight, piercing the darkness of a swamp on a moonless midnight. [Why is everyone picking on Jesus? ]{.class_4257}It doesn't make sense. 27:45 Now from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the land unto the ninth hour.

46 And about the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani? that is to say, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?

47 Some of them that stood there, when they heard that, said, This man calleth for Elias.

48 And straightway one of them ran, and took a sponge, and filled it with vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink.

49 The rest said, Let be, let us see whether Elias will come to save him.

["Sixth\...unto the ninth hour" ]{.class_4258}figures from noon until three o'clock in the afternoon. Since it turns dark here in the early afternoon, Southerners have always referred to this part of the day as EVENING. When a Yankee says "evening," he means [after 6 ]{.class_4257}p.m. The calling of 12 to 3 "evening" is obtained from a literal belief in Exodus 12:6 as applied to the crucifixion. The apparent discrepancy found in John 19:14 is due to the fact that the Romans, as all Gentiles, would [begin a day at midnight. ]{.class_4257}To a Roman (as an American), 12:05 a.m. signifies five minutes into the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_646}[beginning of a new day. ]{.class_4257}Thus, in the army and navy, 2400 signifies [midnight ]{.class_4257}and the END of the old day. The sixth hour for a Roman would be 6 a.m. in the morning, which is exactly what it is in John 19:14. This however cannot be the Jewish sixth hour, for Mark 15:25 says it was the [third hour ]{.class_4257}that they crucified Him. Since the Jew would count from 6 a.m. as the beginning (see parable of Matt. 20:1--6), the third hour would be [nine ]{.class_4257}in the morning. Thus, Jesus' final appearance before Pilate is around 6--6:30. His final mocking and disrobing is around 7 a.m. (Matt. 27:31). His gauntlet to Calvary goes from about 7:30 until 8:00, and the cross goes into its socket near 9:00 a.m. He has been hanging three hours before the sun is darkened. The conversation with the thief took place during this time.

["Darkness over all the land." ]{.class_4258}Mark concurs exactly (Mark 15:33); Luke concurs (Luke 23:44) and adds: ["the sun was darkened" ]{.class_4258}(Luke 23:45). Moffatt identifies the sound of the overhead shells at this point and joyfully contributes his portion to the barrage, as a confederate of the same army. ["Owing to an eclipse of the sun." ]{.class_4257}("Owing[ ]{.class_4257}to Moffatt's stupidity," would be a more "accurate rendering.")

The word ["darkened" ]{.class_4258}(used by Matthew, Mark, and Luke) is translated "dark or darkness" in every passage, and there are more than forty of them---all stem from skotia, skotos, or skoteinus. Before this darkness takes place, our Lord commits His mother to John's keeping (John 19:25--27). Again (as in John 2:4), He is very careful NOT to acknowledge her as His "MOTHER." He never refers to her as "mother" anywhere, and deliberately calls her "woman" so no one will think that He might have said: "Mother."

"Woman, behold thy son!"

(It seems as though Jesus, at this time, was peculiarly aware of what would follow in the centuries ahead, and He words every last saying with doctrinal significance. For those who would honor Mary for nineteen centuries and pray to her, Jesus says: ["Woman." ]{.class_4258}No one in the book of Acts honors her or prays to her, [ever. ]{.class_4257}Mariolatry is a POST­BIBLICAL teaching.)

The darkness settles. At about three o'clock in the afternoon, it looks like the sun has already set. No more is heard from the thieves. They have been hanging six hours. The muscles are swollen and cramped. The blood vessels and arteries are constricted. With poor circulation, infection will set in over the rusty nail holes; tetanus and fever will lay hold of the victims. The tongues are becoming thick and dry, and the infernal weight of the body forward, so a man cannot straighten up and rest for a moment, is beginning to tell.

Somewhere in the black haze, a voice is heard muttering curses (a painful death doesn't always convert!). Many a soldier died under shell‑fire with a bleeding stump or blown‑out stomach or a blown‑away cheek, and [never did]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_649} obey John 1:10--13. (Once, when I preached in the Federal penitentiary at Atlanta, the warden drew me aside and showed a picture of the three crosses, painted in a small chapel across from the main assembly room. The picture was unique. The ray of sunlight, that normally lights the dying Saviour's face, was instead slanted to illuminate the face of the [impenitent thief. ]{.class_4257}Christ's back was toward the observer. The spotlight and focus of the picture was on the face of the Christ‑rejecting thief \[who, like the translators after him, majored in [stealing]{.class_4257}\]; I have never seen a more horrible look of beastial defiance than that which was before me. Not in the cartoons of [MAD]{.class_4257} magazine or in the trick photography of Jekyll and Hyde or Frankinstein would you ever behold a face like that. I found out the artist's secret in the next moment, for the warden said to me: "That was painted by a lifer, here. When he finished it and I asked him what the big idea was, he replied: 'You [see that face there? ]{.class_4257}\[indicating the impenitent thief\]\...[well, that's just how I feel about it]{.class_4257} [too!'"]{.class_4257} He had painted HIMSELF into the picture!)

In the darkness, on the other side of the cross, is a weary voice whispering hoarsely over and over again: "God be merciful to me a sinner---God be merciful to me a sinner---God be merciful to me a sinner---God\...."

At the foot of the cross the soldiers sit. They are getting a little bored by now. I hear one of them say: "I wish the ‑‑‑‑‑ would hurry up and die; I gotta deal back at the garrison that can't wait all day\...."

The Pharisees and scribes stand silent now, smirking in grim satisfaction, and perhaps one of them says to his fellow councilman: "Look at that! See the sky!? I told you He was a devil!"

"Truth, truth, Dr. Sneezingstein; that will be a good example for all these Bible‑quoting separatists!"

Suddenly, a cry cuts the air like a knife: "Eli!! Eli!!!" It is not the converted thief. It is not the impenitent one. It is the One in the MIDDLE (Mark 15:28), ["numbered with the transgressors."]{.class_4258}

["Eli, Eli!!!" ]{.class_4258}Not a soul in the crowd could have guessed why this panic‑stricken cry sounded forth. [The invisible war had reached the high water mark, and not a soul could see it take place.]{.class_4257}

["Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani!!" ]{.class_4258}The Hebrew given is absolute []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_650}proof that Matthew wrote in Greek and inserted other tongues where necessary. The "Q" document theory of Lamsa, Bullinger, Streeter, Weiss, and Westcott and Hort is a fraud. ["Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani? that is to say, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?"]{.class_4258}

Lamsa tries to drive home his Catholic legend by translating the verse, "My God, my God, for this was I kept," but somehow the translation has a peculiarly SOCRATIC sound to it that reminds us more of a discussion by Stoics on the Stoa than the murder of the Son of God. Nestle and Westcott and Hort get into a very embarrassing position at this point. It is apparent that Lamsa's Aramaic reading has its origination in Vaticanus and Sinaiticus. Both read ["Eloi, Eloi"]{.class_4257} (Greek) which is a transliteration of Aramaic.

Now the noose closes. Matthew is writing in Greek, and Matthew uses a transliteration of the HEBREW (["Eli"]{.class_4257}) for the passage and then interprets it in Greek, where the King James translates it into English. Since Westcott and Hort, Nestle, Streeter, and the pope [all ]{.class_4257}see the necessity for [inventing ]{.class_4257}a gospel in Aramaic from which Matthew is supposed to copy (see remarks under Luke 23:43), [all want to change the Hebrew "Eli" to the Aramaic "Eloi!" ]{.class_4257}But they lose courage in the face of evidence! Here sits Sinaiticus and Vaticanus, written in Italy, with the Aramaic Eloi in them, to suggest the theory that there is an Aramaic gospel---so it can be produced with "KIPHA" in it (Matt 16:18), proving that Peter is the "ROCK."

"Eloi" is found in Mark 15:34, [but not in Matthew, ]{.class_4257}and since it is Matthew 16:18 of which the pope needs a new translation to prove his hoax, the Aramaic translitera­tion of "Eloi" is transferred from Mark to Matthew in the Vaticanus and Sinaiticus!
