But Matthew is the gospel of the Jews, written to present Jesus as King of the Jews! Matthew, therefore, writes "My God, my God" in Hebrew, [not Aramaic. ]{.class_4257}Thus, the internal evidence of the unity of the Scriptures [defeats ]{.class_4257}the critical theories aimed at perverting them. Mark gives the Aramaic, Matthew gives the Hebrew, and the pope would have it in reverse.

["My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?" ]{.class_4258}Twelve hours earlier He knew this was coming. He had prayed to avoid it if possible; it had been unavoidable. The cry is [not ]{.class_4257}the cry of a martyr. []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_651}Martyrs died in bold assurance and confident faith of God's presence and blessing (1 John 5:13).

The cry is not "My father, my father, why hast thou\...," but ["My God, my God."]{.class_4258}

At this point we enter into the mystery of the atonement, undiscernible to the eye of physical science and Bible‑rejecting church members. In this short period of time, Jesus bears the infinite wrath to be handed out to people who trust their sincerity, religion, church, sacraments, and beliefs instead of HIM (see John 1:10--13). The divine nature, the Son of God, is NOT forsaken by the Father in this hour (John 11:41--42), nor is there division in the Trinity. [Eternal death ]{.class_4262}reveals itself at this point in the human consciousness of the [Man Christ Jesus ]{.class_4262}(1 Tim. 2:5), and for a dreadful moment the human consciousness of the mediator misses that comfort it has had until now with the Divine Nature of the Son of God. For a moment it becomes FULLY AWARE of the Divine Wrath poured out on sin [(the cup!!), ]{.class_4262}and in that hour when He became SIN for us (2 Cor. 5:21; Gal. 3:13), He calls out with the theme song of the lost in Hell: ["My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?"]{.class_4258}

"Friend of the friendless, betrayed and denied. Help of the weak, in Gethsemane cried. Light of the world, in gross darkness He died---

Jesus: Wonderful Lord!!"

The elements are all there. It is a [lamb slain and roasted ]{.class_4257}(Exod. 12:1--12). It is roasted without water (Exod. 12:7--8). It is [burned ]{.class_4257}(Exod. 12:9). It is a Lamb [as a substitute ]{.class_4257}(Exod. 13:13). It is a Lamb [suffering for someone else ]{.class_4257}(Isa. 53:6, 12; John 1:29; Heb. 9:28; 1 Pet. 2:24). It is a [propitiation ]{.class_4257}(payment to satisfy), it is an [expiation ]{.class_4257}(to clean from sin), it is [redemptive ]{.class_4257}(to buy back something lost), and it is [effective ]{.class_4257}in that those who trust in it receive immediate, absolute assurance of eternal life, as a present and permanent possession.

All the crowd sees is an overcast sky, a hazy atmosphere, the outline of three dying criminals, and the uncomfortable chill of early spring. That is all any infidel ever saw in the death of Christ; if he saw any more, he only saw a Man dying for "what He believed." All the Catholic saw was [a continuation of the Lord's supper ]{.class_4257}(to be continued on the "day of the sun‑god," at 11 o'clock!!).

["This man calleth for Elias!" ]{.class_4258}The ["some of them" ]{.class_4258}(who say []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_652}this) are a little more anxious than most of the onlookers, for if the student will study the passages, he will find the connection between the sun "being darkened" and Elijah coming as a forerunner to the Second Advent (Amos 8:9; Isa. 5:30, 13:10; Amos 5:18, 20; Mal. 4; Zeph. 1:15). Someone has begun thinking that perhaps He is the Messiah, and that Elijah is going to come back down, [as he went up ]{.class_4257}(2 Kings 2), and rescue and anoint the "King of Israel."

Jesus immediately drops His head and mutters: ["I thirst." ]{.class_4258}This is what the rich man in Hell said (Luke 16). It follows ["why hast thou forsaken me?" ]{.class_4258}The similarities are all too apparent; [there is a literal hell somewhere. ]{.class_4257}When He says, "I[ thirst,"]{.class_4258} those waiting for Elijah decide to "give Him a break," but it is not much of a break, really. Jesus only asks for a drink two times in His life (Rev. 14:7): once He is accommodated by a religious argument (John 4:1--20), and this last time He is answered with a sponge full of vinegar. This time He takes it, [since it is not wine ]{.class_4257}(see Mark 15:23). (Bullinger gives three drinks: Mark 15:23, refused without tasting; Matthew 27:34, tasted and refused; and John 19:29 received. Either way, it is obvious that He did not drink fermented wine, either at the last supper or during the crucifixion.) Our Lord in deep concern for HIS Church is careful to abrogate anyone's teaching on either wine‑drinking or "reverencing" Mary. His last few words and movements are deliberate and pointed.

Standard theology teaches that the last words on the cross are ["Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit"]{.class_4258}; this will not hold up at all under examination.

27:50 Jesus, when he had cried again with a loud voice, yielded up the ghost.

Mark says identically the same thing in Mark 15:37, but Luke quotes what it is that is "cried with a loud voice." It is: ["Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit: and having said thus, he gave up the ghost. ]{.class_4258}"

But John [adds ]{.class_4257}an additional statement, that is not "cried with a loud voice." Notice: ["When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, It is finished: and he bowed his head, and gave up the ghost." ]{.class_4258}It is apparent that the humble bowing of the head and the quiet saying, ["It is finished" ]{.class_4258}is an [anti‑climax ]{.class_4257}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_653}to ["Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit." ]{.class_4258}The natural reasoning of the commentator is that the [commending ]{.class_4257}must be the last thing, as the commentators take for granted that this is done [immediately upon request, ]{.class_4257}and therefore, He has time to say nothing else. However, this betrays ignorance of the English Text, for you are plainly told that Stephen cries: ["Lord Jesus, receive my spirit!" ]{.class_4258}And then after this, he kneels down and says: ["Lord, lay not this sin to their charge." ]{.class_4258}THEN he dies (see Acts 7:59--60).

The standard teaching, then, is in error. The ["loud voice"]{.class_4258} precedes the ["It is finished." ]{.class_4258}When Jesus says: ["It is finished," ]{.class_4258}brother, ["IT IS FINISHED." ]{.class_4258}It is NOT finished until He says: ["It is finished," ]{.class_4258}and once He says that, there [              ]{.class_4258}is no ["Father, into thy hands\..." ]{.class_4258}or anything else: ["It is finished." ]{.class_4258}Salvation is finished; His work is finished (Luke 2:49). The Old Testament sacrifices are finished; the Atonement is finished; and woe be to the man that steps on this final cry and tries to "repeat" or "continue" anything by which to get saved! (Although not comprehended doctrinally by Peter in Acts 3:19--20, it is not long before Peter gets it straight, and once he gets it straight, he talks like a Christian---not a "catholic" \[Acts 15:11\].)

27:51 And, behold, the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom; and the earth did quake, and the rocks rent;

52 And the graves were opened; and many bodies of the saints which slept arose,

53 And came out of the graves after his resurrection, and went into the holy city, and appeared unto many.

This passage is admittedly the most difficult in the New Testament. There is no literature available on it anywhere in the world that would adequately explain the verses so that they would not contradict other verses in the Bible. When a passage like this is confronted, the amillennial Catholic (and Reformer) and the postmillennial infidel (and Liberal), have two reactions to it.

The Catholic and Reformer give their standard church []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_654}interpretations that confine the authority and power to their own church organizations, even though they conflict with other passages. Sit down with a domini, priest, or Presbyterian minister and ask him about the passage, and you will discover an amazing thing; he has not even considered the passage to be of enough consequence to investigate it! While yammering to high heaven about the "teachings of the master" and the "authoritative interpreting church," neither Catholic nor Reformer is able even to give or list the problems concerned with the interpretation of the passage! Roman Interpretation is so "PRIVATE" that it cannot even intelligently define its own terms in the light of the Scriptures. The infidel and liberal (companion travelers) get rid of the work involved by merely assigning the passage to pre‑scientific ghost stories. "Someone just thought they saw the bodies, because they had a guilt complex about the execution of the innocent prophet," etc.

The passage is an enigma. Its true interpretation will be found, as all true interpretations are found, within the covers of the plain, English 1611 [AV]{.class_4257} by comparing Scripture with Scripture, but so far, history has not produced a man who could "level" with the verses as they appear.

["The veil of the temple." ]{.class_4258}This much is very clear: the veil was a type of the Christ's flesh, and since this flesh was ["God\...manifest in the flesh,"]{.class_4258} it is a type of "entrance to the heart of God." Further, if God is where the Bible SAYS He is, then the torn veil would indicate clearly that there should be a "rent" in the north end of the universe, somewhere above Alpha Draconis---a "door," into [which ]{.class_4257}a [man can enter!]{.class_4257}

Hebrews 6:19 and 9:23--25 shed great light on the truth of the ["veil."]{.class_4258} Hebrews 10:20 defines the ["veil"]{.class_4258} as a type of ["his flesh,"]{.class_4258} so there can be no doubt about the interpretation. The veil is rent, the door is open, the way is clear, and we have access to the ["throne of grace" ]{.class_4258}to find mercy and to obtain ["grace to help in time of need." ]{.class_4258}The rending of the veil symbolized an entirely new order of things, and although this new order is not thoroughly realized by Peter until Acts 10 (and by Paul until Acts 13, and by James until Acts 15), [it begins right here. ]{.class_4257}Rrrrippp!! goes the veil; no more priesthood of Levites, no more tithes to the storehouse, no more []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_655}lambs burnt on the altar, no more incense burned in the temple, no more holy oil and burning lamps, no more long‑robed "fathers," no more "holy of holies," and [no more temple ]{.class_4257}(Acts 7:47--50; Mal. 3:10--12; 2 Cor. 3; 2 Tim. 1:9--10; Gal. 2--3; Heb. 10:8--12)! [The New Testament is in effect with Matthew 27:51 ]{.class_4257}(Heb 9:16--17).

Woe be to the Judaizers and Papists who have damned the souls of 15,000,000,000 people in the Church Age, trying to drag them back outside the veil, and make them approach the "most High" through doorkeep­ers, attendants, car‑hops, bell boys, chauffeurs, waiting maids, liaison officers, and other "in betweens." The veil is rent. It is torn ["from the top to the bottom," ]{.class_4258}not from "bottom to top." In plainer words, God Himself does the tearing. Woe to any pernicious, supercilious, religious upstart who goes prancing around in a Halloween costume telling you that the true, infallible church that "Christ founded" is a church that has a priesthood of bachelors, set up to stand between you and the throne (Matt. 23).

["The earth did quake, and the rocks rent." ]{.class_4258}Out on the south rim of Jerusalem, swinging over the abyss on his self‑appointed gibbet, the body of Judas shakes, trembles, and then drops rope, limb, tree, and all into the valley of Gehenna. The rocks and dirt come tumbling down the hill after this assembly and partially cover the body of an "apostle," now split from chest to groin by the impact on the boulders, the stomach and intestines spilling out over the torn robes (Acts 1:18--19).

["And the graves were opened\...went into the holy city, and appeared unto many." ]{.class_4258}This is the wrench in the machinery. There are three or four great Scriptural truths, still kept under lock and key by the Holy Spirit, which would be opened if these verses could be properly expounded. Among those who have really tried to DIG and get down to work on the passage, the following explanations have been produced:

1\. The bodies came up and lived a while and then died again (as Lazarus).

2\. The bodies came up and then after a few hours were caught up to heaven (like Moses, who was taken up [after ]{.class_4257}being buried).

3\. The bodies were not literal but only spiritual, and went on up with the Lord Jesus at His ascension (Eph. 4:8--11).

4\. The bodies were literal, but returned to the grave to come up later with Jesus at the resurrection.

5\. The bodies did not come up UNTIL the resurrection of Christ and then went up to heaven with Him.

All of the suggested solutions are attendant with difficulties. In the first case, what is the point of a dozen repetitions of Lazarus, and why did not ALL of the Old Testament saints come up, and why did they just go into Jerusalem? (Some of them---Adam, Noah, Shem, and others, died more than 200 miles from Jerusalem.)

In the second case, why were only a FEW bodies caught up? If ALL were not caught up, why not, and when will they go up?

In the third case, the bodies have to be literal, for it is said that they SLEPT, and nowhere in the Bible are "spirits" or "souls" ever spoken of as "SLEEPING." [The body is what "sleeps" ]{.class_4257}(Acts 7:59--60, 1 Thess. 5:9--10). ("Soul sleep" is a mythological fragment borrowed from Judge Russell's interpretations.)

In the fourth case, the interpretation is nonsensical, for we are told that the bodies did not come out until ["after his resurrection" ]{.class_4258}(Matt. 27:53). Why would they die again between Matthew 28 and Acts 1? There would be no purpose in them appearing in the first place.

In the fifth case, we have the most logical explanation, but it leaves two problems still starkly standing: why did not ALL the bodies come up, and since all did not, [then when do the rest of them come up?]{.class_4257}

Reviewing what the Holy Spirit has revealed about the first resurrection (see notes on Matt. 17:1--6, 12:39--40, 3:16), several things are clear even if all the details of Matthew 27:51--53 are not clear. One: the first resurrection comes in three installments---Old Testament saints (Matt. 27:53), typified by the feast of the first‑fruits (1 Cor. 15:21--23, Deut. 16:16); New Testament saints (1 Thess. 4; 1 Cor. 15:50--55), typified by the Feast of Weeks (Pentecost---Deut. 16:16; 1 Cor. 15:21--23); and Tribulation saints (Matt. 24; Rev. 11), typified by the Feast of Tabernacles (Deut. 16:16; 1 Cor. 15:21--23).

Now, applying the text of Matthew 27, the only possible place that it can fit is the "feast of the first‑fruits" (1 Cor. 15:23), yet not ALL the Old Testament saints come up---only []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_657}["MANY BODIES" ]{.class_4258}(Matt. 27:52--53). This would be the equivalent of saying that the "first fruits" represent only the first part of a crop to be harvested LATER (and so we find it in Lev. 23:10--11). This obvious truth takes the passage a step further, for if Matthew 27:52--53 is only the first of a harvest to occur later, then the remainder of the [bodies of the Old Testament saints]{.class_4257} will have to go up in the Rapture or else at the END of the Tribulation.

Eliminating alternate choices as rapidly as possible, it is certainly [improbable ]{.class_4257}that Old Testament saints will go up in the Rapture of the Body of Christ (for reasons noted before distinguishing the difference between the Body and the Kingdom, Israel and the Church, the Christian and the Jew).

This would make Old Testament saints come up at the post‑tribulation Rapture and Resurrection of Isaiah 26:18--21; Ezekiel 37:1--14; Matthew 24:29--31; and Psalms 50:1--6. If they come up at the Rapture of the church, then a very peculiar situation will arise, for they will be resurrected as Lazarus, to go into the Tribulation on earth in Palestine. No [Old Testament saint was "born again," ]{.class_4257}and none were given glorified bodies, and none are said to be "like Christ" in the resurrection. Contrary to the promises of the Christian (Phil. 3:20--21; 1 John 3:1--3), the Old Testament saints retain their male and female distinctions and their separate identity in the resurrection.

The remaining Old Testament saints, therefore (that did NOT come up at Christ's resurrection), have three future opportunities: one, at the Rapture of the church; two, at the post‑tribulation Rapture; three, at the last judgment (White Throne Judgment---Rev. 20). Choice two is the most likely, but it cannot be proved from the Scripture. If it is chosen, another more knotty problem arises than this one. It is, "when do the saved from Adam to Moses come up?" Moses to Christ can be the reference to saints who went ["into the holy city," ]{.class_4258}as Jerusalem is Mosaic and connected with the occupation of Palestine, by Israel, under the Mosaic law; but [where is Adam and when does he come up? No ]{.class_4257}one knows but the Scripture, and no one has been in the word enough to come up with the answer.

["Many bodies of the saints" ]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_658}indicates that there are more to come up. WHEN is the question. Of the many that came up, there is no way to tell whether they represent the dispensations between Abraham and Moses, or between Moses and Calvary, or BOTH.

27:54 Now when the centurion, and they that were with him, watching Jesus, saw the earthquake, and those things that were done, they feared greatly, saying, Truly this was the Son of God.

Synonymous accounts in Mark and Luke say: ["Truly this man was the Son of God" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 15:39), and ["Certainly this was a righteous man" ]{.class_4258}(Luke 23:47).

The conversion of the centurion affords the [RSV ]{.class_4257}another opportunity to reveal its hatred for the Lord Jesus Christ (as any conversion---the dying thief, for example, is a target for the [RSV]{.class_4257}'s true intent). Where the centurion has said: ["Truly this was the Son of God," ]{.class_4258}the [RSV ]{.class_4257}rushes in and strips the dying Saviour of His Deity, translating "Truly [this was a son of God," ]{.class_4257}thus making Him as Schweitzer, Kagawa, Peale, Jones, Buttrick, Oxnam, Cronin, Weatherhead, Poteat, Dahlberg, and other third‑rate humanitarian‑socialists.

Oddly enough (or not so oddly if you are acquainted with the sloppyness of the [RSV]{.class_4257}), the translators of the "reversed vision" have translated ["THE Son of God" ]{.class_4258}right in the same chapter at verse 43! Thus, the [RSV ]{.class_4257}admits the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 is right but refuses to grant the correct translation where the conversion of a sinner is concerned! Again, the slip is showing, and it is red from hem to halter. Marx and Lenin couldn't have sewed a better one if they had sat up all night studying Home Economics.

["THE Son of God" ]{.class_4258}is the correct translation, exactly as you find it in the King James 1611 [AV]{.class_4257}. The same Greek construction is found in verses 40 and 43, although Nestle's has relegated the passage in verse 43 to the footnote (in spite of the fact that it is found in the manuscripts that Nestle and Westcott and Hort accept as the "final authority!"). The reasons why anyone can be quite sure it is THE son and not "A" son is because "a son of God" is not a Scriptural expression; it is nowhere taught in either Testament, for Jew, []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_659}Gentile, or church. "A" son of God is a modernistic interpolation.

["Sons of God" ]{.class_4258}are found (Job 1--2, 38). ["The Son of God" ]{.class_4258}and ["The Son of Man" ]{.class_4258}are found (Matthew, Mark, Luke, John), but "A" son of God is a mythological fabrication of degenerate Greek philosophy that is not even considered important enough by the Holy Spirit to discuss in the Bible. There is no such thing in Scripture as "A" Son of God. Christians are "sons of God" (John 1:12--13). The only time "A" son occurs is in Hebrews 5:8 where the immediate context says "MY SON" (vs. 5), and the "a" Son, here, is comparing the [human relationship ]{.class_4257}to the divine relationship in the matter of obedience---[not of birth. ]{.class_4257}The reading in the [RSV]{.class_4257}, therefore, is totally corrupt, and it is a dishonest attempt to steal more than a seamless robe by a crap‑shooting game; it puts the Roman soldiers in better society than the [RSV ]{.class_4257}translators.

27:55 And many women were there beholding afar off, which followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering unto him:

56 Among which was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of Zebedee's children.

57 When the even was come, there came a rich man of Arimathaea, named Joseph, who also himself was Jesus' disciple:

58 He went to Pilate, and begged the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded the body to be delivered.

59 And when Joseph had taken the body, he wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,

60 And laid it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn out in the rock: and he rolled a great stone to the door of the sepulchre, and departed.

61 And there was Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting over against the sepulchre.

The mention of the ["many women"]{.class_4258} attending the final hours of His earthly life bears testimony to the effect that the Lord Jesus Christ had on this part of humanity. Women, in the Orient, are little more than beasts of burden, and to this day, many of them are contracted for marriages without any say‑so about the prospective []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_660}part­ner. Women in Africa, India, China, Iran, and Arabia are a "sight different" than women in England, Germany, and the United States. The women of Christ's day knew what He professed to be and knew the effect that He would have on MEN, if men truly followed Him. They recognized in Him not only a Saviour from sin, but a Saviour from the curse placed on nature (as for example, the woman who was healed of the bloody flux) and manifesting itself in their own bodies (Gen. 3:15--18).

These women are weeping on the Via Dolorosa (Luke 23:27) and are said to compose a ["great company"]{.class_4258}; they are further said to have ["followed him, and ministered unto him,"]{.class_4258} in Mark 15:41. No woman ever hated or opposed Him in the entire forty-two months in which He heralded the kingdom; they knew He was their Deliverer, and as a famous old-time preacher once remarked: "The greatest moral monstrosity this world will ever see is a WOMAN who does not love Jesus Christ." How could a woman, who by nature loves flattery, attention, consideration, bravery, chivalry, gentleness, and leadership, [not trust Him as her Saviour?]{.class_4257}

By the time we arrive at verse 56, Mary becomes "Mary, Mary, quite contrary," for lo and behold, FOUR Marys show up at the cross.

Mary, [Magdalene.]{.class_4257}

Mary, [the mother of James and Joses.]{.class_4257}

Mary, [the mother of James and John.]{.class_4257}

Mary, [the mother of Jesus.]{.class_4257}

Note that Mary, Jesus' mother, is there (John 19), Mary Zebedee is there (Matt. 27:56), Mary Magdalene is there (Mark 16:9), and Mary Cleophas is there (John 19:25).

The fact that there is a Mary (the mother of James and Joses) who is obviously not the virgin‑mother, Mary, has led the pope and his Gestapo to teach that this Mary is the Mary of Mark 6:3, where Jesus' "brother and sisters" are mentioned. The theory is enticing since the first two names of this Mary's children are ["James and Joses"]{.class_4258}[ ]{.class_4257}(Mark 6:3). However, as it has been pointed out before (see comments under Matt. 1:25), these brother and sisters are brothers and sisters---not "cousins." Where the Holy Spirit wishes to use the word "cousin," He writes "cousin," not "brother" or "sister" (see []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_661}Luke 1:36, 58).

James, Joses (Joseph), Mary (Miriam), Judas (Judah), and Simon are as common to first‑century Jewry as Bill, Joe, and George are to twentieth-century Americanism. There are FOUR Marys at the Cross.

["Joseph of Arimathaea" ]{.class_4258}now appears out of obscurity to claim the corpse. Joseph is evidently one of the "secret disciples" of John 12:42, and in the argument of John 7:47--53, Joseph is undoubtedly a fifth‑amendment Christian taking everything in. In the final outcome, Joseph comes out, and he comes out boldly---["he\...had not consented to the counsel and deed of them" ]{.class_4258}(Luke 23:51), and ["went in boldly unto Pilate, and craved the body of Jesus" ]{.class_4258}(Mark 15:43).

The wording is remarkable if any attention is paid to it. The man goes boldly in to the judge who had the criminal executed (and a judge who officially had power to kill any Jew) and then "CRAVES" the corpse. The word [crave, ]{.class_4257}from "craving," means to implore, beg, implead, or exhaust every means possible to get the desired object. "CRAVEN" implies that the person being dis­cussed has reached the point where one thing has possessed him to the place where he would do [anything ]{.class_4257}to get it or to avoid it. Joseph, in the last hour, comes out as boldly as John the Beloved. He shows his colors on a flagpole 100 feet high and dares the Roman government to haul them down! Blessed example!

"Then Pilate commanded the body to be delivered\.... And there was Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting over against the sepulchre."

Pilate marvels that Jesus is already dead (Mark 15:44) and does not release the corpse to Joseph until he is absolutely sure that Jesus IS dead. (Here, the Holy Spirit has carefully guarded the truth so that the "swoon‑theory" of the resurrection cannot be a cataleptic‑state. Pilate gets his information on the deceased from a [professional soldier, who has just bled the last drop of blood from the dead man. ]{.class_4257}Jesus is dead---not "unconscious" (Rev. 1:18).

Mark and Luke supplement details of the burial, and we learn that the tomb was a new one, in a garden, wherein never a man had been laid, and that the body was wrapped in swaddling clothes \[fine []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_662}linen\] (Mark 15:45--47; Luke 23:52--54). John adds that spices were included in the grave wrappings, ["as the manner of the Jews is to bury"]{.class_4258} (see John 19:40).

Three Marys maintain a vigil around the sepulchre: one is obviously Mary Magdalene; the other is "Mary, the mother of Joses," which would be Mary Cleophas.

The STONE, rolled across the entrance, is rolled by two men---Nicodemus and Joseph. At the last minute, up shows the ["ruler of the Jews," ]{.class_4258}who ["came to Jesus by night" ]{.class_4258}(John 3), and brings ["myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pound weight," ]{.class_4258}to help bury the body (John 19:39). He and Joseph do the wrapping between 5 p.m. and 6 p.m. Wednesday night (not Friday!), and by Wednesday night (the beginning of Thursday for a Jew), the body is in the tomb, the spirit is with the Father, and the soul is ["three days and three nights in the heart of the earth!" ]{.class_4258}(See notes on Matt 12:39--40, and do not fail to note how ridiculous and un‑Christian the Catholic teaching on the subject is.)

27:62 Now the next day, that followed the day of the preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees came together unto Pilate,

63 Saying, Sir, we remember that that deceiver said, while he was yet alive, After three days I will rise again.

64 Command therefore that the sepulchre be made sure until the third day, lest his disciples come by night, and steal him away, and say unto the people, He is risen from the dead: so the last error shall be worse than the first.

65 Pilate said unto them, Ye have a watch: go your way, make it as sure as ye can.

66 So they went, and made the sepulchre sure, sealing the stone, and setting a watch.

These events all take place on the evening of Wednesday night. ["The next day, that followed the day of the preparation," ]{.class_4258}of course, would be [Thursday, ]{.class_4262}which to a Jew would begin Wednesday night at 6 p.m. The thing that threw all the commentators (Protestant and Catholic alike) was the insertion of ["the day before the sabbath" ]{.class_4258}(in Mark 15:42). Catholics assume that this Sabbath is []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_663}the regular Friday‑Saturday Sabbath of a Jew, which occurs weekly on the seventh day. However, this is greatly in error for two reasons: one, the Sabbath here is not a [regular Sabbath, ]{.class_4262}but a ["high day"]{.class_4258} (John 19:31), and two, the Greek (God bless the little "original Greek scholars!") says "SABBATHS" in Matthew 28:1, implying that more than ONE of them took place during the week! Thus, we learn that devotees of "the original" [cannot even read what they profess, and will blindly follow tradition, even where it crosses the Greek, so great is their hatred for the Authorized Version, which teaches Jesus died on Wednes­day---not Friday!]{.class_4262}

["The chief priests." ]{.class_4258}Notice again, the ring leaders are never Communists, bootleggers, extortioners, kidnappers, or atheists; they are the long‑robed [chief priests ]{.class_4262}(see notes under Matt. 10:17, 23:1--9).

["We remember that that deceiver said\...." ]{.class_4258}Again, we observe the rankest hypocrisy manifested by the council leaders. Not twenty-four hours before this they were asking Jesus questions, as though they knew NOTHING about "what He said." (See John 18:19--22.) Notice that throughout, what they do not want people to know is that there can be a resurrection FROM the dead, before the last final judgment (see Matt. 26:61; Acts 4:2, 33).

The whole issue then is the [amillennial and post­millennial issue. ]{.class_4262}The arguments over the virgin birth, the infallibility of Scripture, the Deity of Christ, and the authority of the church are all contingent on the position taken toward [the future resurrection and judgment. ]{.class_4262}This is so clear from surveying the book of Acts, that to state it would hardly seem to be necessary, and yet, the issue has been so carefully camouflaged that to this day there are people who think that the attitude toward the second coming of Christ is a nonessential, "controversial" subject.

What these Pharisees hate is the teaching of a resurrection ["from the dead"]{.class_4258}; they are not so deluded and superstitious as the Greek scholars, who got upset about a [resurrection "of" the dead ]{.class_4262}(Acts 17:32). Scholars and scientists, as a class of people, are so ignorant of prophecy and so jackassical in their attitude toward revelation, that it would be considered a tremendous step of faith for them even to believe that God would resurrect them [at any time ]{.class_4262}and []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw3631.xhtml_page_664}hold them accountable for their conceited and asinine attitude toward the word of God. The priests, however, are a step ahead---they know there is a future reckoning and judgment, but they want this judgment on their terms, which is "sacrifice without mercy" (Matt. 9:13). In a capsule, they want a judgment whereby righteousness is determined by individual fitness and capability to live up to the legal, moral, man‑wrought righteousness of the Old Testament Law. The best way to promote this kind of theology is to teach one general resurrection and one general last judgment, [where all are judged according to works, ]{.class_4262}on a man‑made, self‑righteous basis. (The present-day liberal only inserts "LOVE" for the ceremonies of the Old Testament, but still inwardly clings to the belief that his own love toward the "down‑trodden classes" and "oppressed humanity" will merit him heaven by virtue of [his own moral quality.)]{.class_4262}

Premillennialism entirely upsets this legalistic system. It teaches TWO resurrections (see notes on Matt. 17:23) and states plainly that those who are resurrected [from the dead ]{.class_4262}(Matt. 22:31--32) are resurrected on the basis of the [imputed righteousness of Christ's resurrection, which also was ]{.class_4262}["from the dead" ]{.class_4258}(see 1 Cor. 15:1--50).

["The chief priests,"]{.class_4258} then, are set and devoted to seeing to it that such a doctrine CANNOT BE TAUGHT. Like the modern Catholic "priest" or Methodist or Episcopalian minister, there is one thing they will not tolerate being broadcast or printed---the [resurrection of those saved by grace, before those are resurrected who are counting on their own religion to save them!]{.class_4262}

This cannot be tolerated, for Christ's personal resur­rection proves that He alone was uniquely [righteous; ]{.class_4262}therefore, if any of His "believers" arise before the "last judgment," it is plainly proved that they have a righteousness not to be found in church membership, sacraments, prayers, alms, fastings, and "golden rules." ["The chief priests and Pharisees"]{.class_4258} go immediately to Pilate to make absolutely sure that this premillennial doctrine is not going to be taught, or at least will not find an audience if it is taught.

Pilate's answer is amusing. It is so ironical that it might be considered as a pathological response.

"Make it as sure as ye can."

When the statement is read carefully, it is a gem. Pilate cannot possibly mean: "I'm in this thing with you boys, and I don't want false doctrine taught anymore than you do, so let's really seal that thing up!" No, he is not saying this. Any study of the conversations that took place between Pilate and this ecumenical council in John 18--19 would clearly reveal that Pilate is NOT sympathetic; he is sarcastic. The implication is "go and do what you can, little boys, but personally, I don't think you are going to make it---I think He's going to come out!" (This may sound far‑fetched, but read the words again, and remember what Pilate said in John 19:22, with the words of his wife \[Matt. 27:19\] still ringing in his ears.) Pilate is not at all sure that ANY watch will keep the body in the tomb. He, like Herod (Luke 23:8; Mark 6:14), was getting so shaken with Jewish theology, Old Testament prophecies, religious controversy, Scripture‑quoting Rabbis, earthquakes, sun-darkenings, and converted centurions, that he was beginning to doubt his own infidelity:

"Make it as sure as ye can."

The words are spoken wearily, indifferently, and skeptically; he doesn't think they can do it.

["Sealing the stone, and setting a watch." ]{.class_4258}The scene is reminiscent of the one in Daniel 6:17--18, where Daniel, as a type of Christ, foreshadowed the events of the gospel. To properly appreciate what follows in Chapter 28, the student must observe the facts: the tomb is closed, sealed, and guarded. It is closed by the [owner, ]{.class_4262}it is sealed by the [government, ]{.class_4262}and it is guarded by the most ferocious and experienced military group the world had produced in 1,000 years---the Roman Legionnaires.

If Christ arises and makes an exodus from this kind of a place, then a genuine miracle has happened; for throughout, the Holy Spirit has pointed out the [historical facts ]{.class_4262}which must be faced by the infidel and skeptic, if he is to deny the literal resurrection. If anyone steals this corpse, he is going to have to have more cunning and power and stealth than Houdini and John Dillinger combined.

[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml}

# Chapter 28 {.heading_84-0}

28:1 In the end of the sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week, came Mary Magdalene and the other Mary to see the sepulchre.

2 And, behold, there was a great earthquake: for the angel of the Lord descended from heaven, and came and rolled back the stone from the door, and sat upon it.

3 His countenance was like lightning, and his raiment white as snow:

4 And for fear of him the keepers did shake, and became as dead men.

One of the proud and popular Liberals of the twentieth century, heralded by the ecumenical council as a real "Christian leader" (one who believes church unity is more important than fidelity to the word of God), has written: "anyone who thinks that the accounts of the resurrection are intelligible has obviously never read the four accounts\...they are full of discrepancies and contradictions, which make them impossible to reconcile."

For the benefit of this unenlightened heathen, the Bibliotheca Sacra \[Feb., 1845 (!) p. 187 sq.\] has a complete account of the resurrection appearances, in order, with the contradictions examined. Halley, on p. 328 of his [An Examination of the Alleged Discrepancies of the Bible]{.class_4257} (1958), has a similar account. Exactly why the writers of the [Interpreter's Bible]{.class_4257} could not catch up with literature written in 1845 is rather mystifying. "Up to date" implies that the National and Ecumenical councils should at least be "read up" until 1900!

1\. Two women come to the tomb; Mary Magdalene and Mary Cleophas (Mark 16:1). Others, unnamed, come with them (Luke 24:1); JOANNA is one that is named in Luke 24:10.

2\. Mary Magdalene goes to tell the disciples, while the other women stay at the open tomb (Luke 23:55--24:9; John 20:1--2). Mary Cleophas and Joanna (Matt. 28:5---["women,"]{.class_4258} not "woman") enter the tomb and receive the message from the Angel of the Lord. The two women depart immediately to go tell those outside (with the []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_667}spices) what they have seen.

3\. Meanwhile, Peter and John, warned by Mary Magdalene, arrive, look in, and go away, Peter entering and observing the clothing. (Since they do NOT see any angels, they suppose the two women to be lying when they get the report \[Luke 24:11, 23\].) Peter goes into the tomb to check.

4\. As the apostles and the women leave the tomb, unable to embalm and anoint a body that is not there, Mary returns and stays weeping, and at this point the Lord appears to her---the first post-resurrection appearance (John 20:11--17).

5\. Mary Cleophas and Joanna return, after this, with the women they have contacted, and this time they see TWO angels (Luke 24:4; Mark 16:5). Going the second time, they meet Jesus, immediately after He has appeared to Mary Magdalene (Matt. 28:1--10).

The four gospel accounts are supplementary, not spurious; they are complementary, not contradictory; and they are amplifications and not discrepancies. The liberal and infidel attitude, expressed by the National Council of Churches (in its leaders), is the attitude of a two-year-old Nigerian, venting his convictions about the finer points of qualitative analysis---it is absolutely worthless.

28:5 And the angel answered and said unto the women, Fear not ye: for I know that ye seek Jesus, which was crucified.

6 He is not here: for he is risen, as he said. Come, see the place where the Lord lay.

7 And go quickly, and tell his disciples that he is risen from the dead; and, behold, he goeth before you into Galilee; there shall ye see him: lo, I have told you.

8 And they departed quickly from the sepulchre with fear and great joy; and did run to bring his disciples word.

9 And as they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, All hail. And they came and held him by the feet, and worshipped him.

Before commenting on the verses in Matthew's text, it would be a good idea to gather together all the intricate and marvelous details that accompanied this resurrection ["from the dead."]{.class_4258} This is []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_668}especially necessary when one considers the Resurrection as it is celebrated on Easter throughout the world, and as it is pictured and preached from the Liberal pulpits---which still observe it, although they do not believe in it.

The resurrection, for the liberal or infidel, is the continuation of the spiritual ideal and moral teachings or principles which Jesus taught before He died. This "resurrection" is often nearly "transubstantiated" from the council pulpits by trying to make Christ's life so "real" (His earthly ministry to Israel!) that you can "feel His presence," etc. This method is so universally effective that hardly anyone, anywhere, does not believe that Christ "rose from the dead," yet when pressed for the literal resurrection of a sinless body that contained "God's Blood" (see notes on Matt. 27:4), we suddenly discover that 85 percent of the "Christians" have believed in a resurrection not found anywhere in the Bible. (This is why Paul is careful to insert the expression, ["according to the scriptures,"]{.class_4258} TWICE in his presentation of the gospel that is to be preached in this age---see 1 Cor. 15:1--6.)

The "resurrection" for the Associated Press and the National Council is actually the "ever living," "always present," eternal "verities" of the human life and human teachings of an exemplary human who could "show the way to God." This "resurrection" is the Greek-agnostic resurrection (or Oriental transmigration of a spiritual nothing into a philosophical less-than-nothing). The resurrection ["from the dead,"]{.class_4258} in the passage before us (Matt. 28:7), is an entirely different thing. Its rejectors, sterile socialists, cannot let it go, and they cannot handle it. They are like a blind man using his fingers to make a honey-and-jam sandwich.

Notice, first of all, the ["infallible proofs"]{.class_4258} (Acts 1:2--3) of a literal, bodily resurrection of the same man who was crucified and buried:

1\. The body was missing.

2\. Those most anxious to recover the body never recovered it (Matt. 28:15).

3\. The body was seen by more than 500 witnesses (1 Cor. 15:5--8).

4\. If it was stolen, whoever stole it had the power to hypnotize []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_669}Roman soldiers long enough to steal it (Matt. 28:4).

5\. If it was stolen, the stupid thief wasted fifteen precious minutes unwinding the body and folding up the grave clothes in a neat little pile (John 20:5, 7).

6\. If it was stolen, the Roman government and the Jewish Sanhedrin could not find it in forty years, even though the integrity of both was at stake!

7\. If it arose only "SPIRITUALLY," it could still eat and drink (Luke 24:42).

8\. It had "flesh and bones" (Luke 24:39) and testified of its own resurrection for forty days after it arose.

To knock all these historical FACTS in the head, the new translations have all loaded, aimed, and fired with .00 buckshot at Acts 1:3. Since the most corrupt "translation" on the market, outside of the [RSV ]{.class_4257}(1952), is the [ASV ]{.class_4257}(1901), we would not be surprised to find the word ["infallible"]{.class_4258} knocked clean out of the text. Again, the [ASV ]{.class_4257}translators reveal what slovenly "scholars" they are, for the word here is ["texmarion,'']{.class_4257} not ["doxima.'']{.class_4257} The "texmarion" is a "sure proof " \[Lysias "Oration against Eratosthenes" \[p. 125\], translates "certain proofs." Aristotle calls it "demonstrative proof" \[whereas ["sameion"]{.class_4257} leaves room for doubt\]. Plato calls the word "convincing proof.") All of which shows that the Greek scholars will even cross and contradict their own scholarship and their own sources of translation, if these sources ever agree with the orthodox character of the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611, Textus Receptus, Byzantine text. The translation ["infallible proofs"]{.class_4258} is, without doubt, correct. The [AV ]{.class_4257}here, as in a thousand other places, proves to be a far more scholarly translation than the perverted [ASV, RV,]{.class_4257} or [RSV]{.class_4257}.

Tremendous truths can be gleaned from Jesus Christ's resurrection, which will be concerned with the future state, interplanetary travel, the true nature of Heaven, the coming Kingdom of God, and the resurrection body of the believer. All these great and precious truths are designedly kept from the "wise and prudent" and revealed to "babes" only (Luke 10:21), as the Lord seems to take special satisfaction in making asses out of scientists and scholars (1 Cor. 3:18--20).

1\. The Christian's future resurrection will be like this one (1 Cor. []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_670}15:20--50; Rom. 8:11, 6:5; Phil. 3:20--21).

2\. The resurrection body therefore is a 33½-year-old, male body (see notes on Matt. 22:30; Phil. 3:20--21; 1 John 3:1--3).

3\. It is a flesh-and-bone body, but not a flesh-and-blood body (1 Cor. 15:50; Luke 24:39).

4\. This superhuman, glorified body can travel at the speed of light. (Mary cannot touch Him for He has not yet "ascended," yet in less than two hours He has ascended and returned and is TOUCHED! (see John 20:17; Matt. 28:9). The word is "I ascend" (present tense), not "I will ascend" as it takes place later in Acts 1.

5\. It is a body that materializes or dematerializes at will, by an instant re-arrangement of atomic structure (John 20:19; Luke 24:31).

In short, the physical resurrection of the Saviour guarantees five things which will be hated, fought, denied, slandered, or stamped-out by religious leaders in the twentieth century.

1\. There is a resurrection before the last judgment.

2\. It is a resurrection of born-again Bible believers who have accepted the gospel of 1 Corinthians 15:1--5.

3\. It is proof that no religious person is saved if he is trusting his own righteousness, motives, sincerity, good works, sacraments, religion, church, or intentions to save him.

4\. It is a guarantee that men will have to arise and face God bodily for their deeds in the body and give an account to a Supreme Being who has no more respect for "religion" and "belief in one god," than He does for a drunken atheist.

5\. It is a guarantee that by simple, child-like belief and trust in the works and death of God's Son, the humblest, most ignorant human being can have access to Him and everything that Bible-rejecting science has been working to "bring in" for over 4,000 years!

(No wonder Pope John had Bible-believing tract distributors arrested at the ecumenical council in 1962 for distributing gospels of John!)

"He is not here: for he is risen."

The words ring through the darkness of religion and sacramentalism as a siren splits the midnight.

["He is not here: for he is risen."]{.class_4258}[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_671} He is NOT "stolen"; He is risen.

Go to the tombs of the world's great religious leaders, and you will find that they all have one thing more in common than "belief in a supreme being," "faith in their fellow man," or "spiritual insight into the inner man"; yes, they have one great communal ground far more "vulgate" than these---they are all dead and maggot-eaten!!

Ashes to ashes, dust to dust!

Name them: Buddha, dead. Lao-tze, stone cold dead in the market. Mohammed, deader than a mackerel. Confucius, kicked the bucket. Zoroaster, colder than a dead Eskimo. Pope Pius XII, so corrupt now you wouldn't want to look at him. And if all the dust that was left of them were blown into your face on a windy day, you wouldn't bother to wipe your eyes.

An ecumenicity that neglects to tell its constituents that salvation is not religion, that the Saviour is not a teacher, and that the new birth is not a transcendental experience is nothing but a religious cooperative for slave-labourers.

"He is not here: for he is risen."

Go to the tomb outside Jerusalem, and you will find an empty tomb. The body is gone. It was never recovered. You never found it, and the blind and stupid writers, historians, poets, and anthropologists of this world never found it, and you never will until you're dead and gone. Then you will discover, too late, that the "great example" and "great teacher" and "master" was your Creator. ["He is not here: for he is risen."]{.class_4258}

["Why seek ye the living among the dead?"]{.class_4258} (Luke 24:5) is the question that the angels ask the women on their second return to the tomb. The question is appropriate for a twentieth-century scientist or scholar. "Why seek YE the living among the dead?" Why look for LIFE on a dead planet like the moon, or why look for the origin of life in dead, decayed matter, or why look for LIFE in the sinful pleasures of a world gone mad, dead in trespasses and sin? (see Eph. 2:1--4). ["Why seek ye the living among the dead?"]{.class_4258} (John 10:10).

"And they came and held him by the feet, and worshipped him."

Since Jesus refused to let Mary touch these same feet not two hours earlier, we are left with the conclusion that He ascended and descended in that time. This would involve a brief interplanetary flight, beyond Alpha Draconis, at a speed of several million light years per second. (No great matter for one who could create the same universe \[Col. 1:15--18\], but an extremely difficult task for some dirt-generated earthling!)

["And worshipped him."]{.class_4258} As usual, the Lord accepts the worship without any qualms whatsoever. He always accepted worship as the natural and right thing, and in this respect, He was a Bible-believing Fundamentalist in His attitude toward Himself. He believed that He was "God in the flesh" and acted accordingly. If He were not, then of course, He was a hoax. Dr. Charles F. Potter ([The Lost Years of Jesus Revealed]{.class_4257}) believes that Jesus rearranged, restated, and transmitted through His own original personality and experience the "idea of God" until those who listened to Him, even afar off, caught enough of His radiant religion to pay Him the highest tribute their "limited vocabulary" knew. Potter surmises that they deified Him, in spite of His protest, "Why call ye me good? there is none good but one, that is, God."

This is a classic example of higher education blissfully wedded to phenomenal ignorance. In the first place, RELIGION, in Scripture, saved NO ONE. In the second place, Jesus always accepted worship and never refused it. In the third place, the quotation that Potter has cited is as much as saying: "If I am not God, I am no good"---which, of course, Potter cannot interpret as he thinks that he himself (Potter) is pretty good! When anyone deified the Lord Jesus (John 20; Matt. 14; Luke 24), He always accepted it as proper. The rich young ruler, in the story Potter quoted, is not deifying Jesus; he is "apple-polishing'' Him!

The purpose of the trip into the third Heaven (Heb. 8:1--6, 7:14, 9:7--14, 23--25) is typified by the high priest in the Old Testament and is connected with the presentation of the blood of the sacrifice before the mercy seat. However, this type cannot be pressed, as Christ's blood has been shed entirely before John 20, and any presence would only be official proof of finished work, not an actual presenting of "blood."

28:10 Then said Jesus unto them, Be not afraid: go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.

11 Now when they were going, behold, some of the watch came into the city, and shewed unto the chief priests all the things that were done.

12 And when they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave large money unto the soldiers,

13 Saying, Say ye, His disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept.

14 And if this come to the governor's ears, we will persuade him, and secure you.

15 So they took the money, and did as they were taught: and this saying is commonly reported among the Jews until this day.

["Go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee."]{.class_4258} Other important appearances follow those in Jerusalem by the tomb, and these are recorded in detail in Mark, Luke, and John: briefly, first to Peter (Luke 24:34; 1 Cor. 15:5) later in the morning, then to the two on the road to Emmaus (Luke 24:13--31), then to those in the upper room, minus Thomas (Luke 24:36--43; John 20:19--24), and then eight days later to the eleven with Thomas present (John 20:24--29). Then the appearances are to 500 brethren at various times and with the incident taking place at Galilee in John 21.

One of the most touching things about the appearances is that when the women entered the tomb, the angel quoted Jesus as saying, ["Go\...tell his disciples and Peter\..."]{.class_4258} (Mark 16:7). The Lord is fully aware of Peter's state of mind, from the time of his denial to the moment that he rushes into the tomb in hopes of finding his Lord. James is also granted a special resurrection-appearance (1 Cor. 15:7), as he seems to be the only "brother of the Lord" (Gal. 1:19) who believed on Him; the rest were infidels (John 7:3--5).

["They gave large money unto the soldiers."]{.class_4258} Before concluding his gospel, Matthew gives a remarkable flash-back into the activities of the watch that was set to "make it as sure as you []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_674}can." The account is especially interesting in view of the fact that for 2,000 years infidels have agreed to the bribe. The whole scene is one of brilliant and blistering hypocrisy. The soldiers are to say the body was stolen, yet if it was stolen while they were sleeping, they would have received the death penalty. Not only this, but if they were asleep, they would not know who stole it; yet they were told to say: "The disciples stole it!"

The chief priests, in order to save their amillennial and postmillennial theology (one "general resurrection" with salvation by works), bribe not only the soldiers, but the GOVERNOR (Matt. 28:14)! To top it off with sour buttermilk, one "accredited doctor" (always good for a chuckle!) says: "Professor Kennard's\...considered opinion after careful and exhaustive research and study is\...we do not have to think the empty tomb was an inference from the visions." So he concludes his long study with the statement: "Thus, when Jews spread abroad the story that the disciples of Jesus had stolen the body, they spoke the truth!"

Two thousand years of history prove that neither Prof. Kennard nor his devotees have the slightest idea in the world what on earth they are talking about. The folded napkin, the grave clothes, the senseless soldiers, the missing corpse, the bribery, and the post-resurrection appearances (witnesses more reliable than Kennard or his friends) are proof positive that liars in the first and twentieth century think and act the same way.

28:16 Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, into a mountain where Jesus had appointed them.

17 And when they saw him, they worshipped him: but some doubted.

18 And Jesus came and spake unto them, saying, All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth.

The ["but some doubted,"]{.class_4258} right in the middle of the passage, has a back reference to the events of John 20:21--28, which took place before the appearance in Galilee of John 21. John describes the appearance in Galilee in detail in the passage; several things must be noticed.

1\. Jesus walks through a shut door in John 20:19.

2\. "Doubting Thomas," as the modern Liberal, does not believe in anything except the demonstrable proofs of physical science.

3\. The demonstrable proofs of physical science prove, conclusively, that Christ arose, LITERALLY, from the dead (John 20:27).

4\. This physical, literal, visible, scientific, infallible proof is proof that Jesus WAS ["God\...in the flesh"]{.class_4258} (1 Tim. 3:16), and any opinion to the contrary is obviously the opinion of a self-conceited, self-righteous, deluded sinner (John 20:30).

5\. Jesus accepts worship as "Lord and God" and never even tells Thomas to get up; He just lets him lay at His feet (John 20:28). Compare this with "pope" Peter's treatment of those who prostrate themselves (Acts 10:25--26) or an angel's treatment of the same class (Rev. 22:8--9). If a pope accepts the kneeling, humble, bowing adoration of anyone, then he obviously is a charlatan and a rascal. If Jesus was not God in the flesh, then He, too, is in the same category. (There it is; chomp on it!)

6\. Since Peter denied three times, he has to confess three times (John 21:15--17).

7\. Jesus calls Peter by his old name "shifting-sand" (Simon) all three times, thus reminding him how true it is that the "spirit is willing, but the FLESH is weak" (John 21:15--17).

8\. The appearance in John 21:14 is the third---the other two being in Chapter 20, verses 19, 26.

9\. Note references to the rapture of the church in John 21:20--23, since John is a type of the Body of Christ (cf. Rev 4:1--2; John 13:23; Gen. 2:21; Eph. 5:25, 29--31).

The final passage in John 21 is the prooftext used by the Roman Church for adding books that are non-canonical and uninspired. The standard method, when reduced to its practicality, amounts to using the Bible to prove that it is Scriptural to add traditions, books, and practices that contradict the Bible as it is written. Hence, the Catholic, to this day, uses the Bible to enforce his own teachings which are contrary to the Bible. One example will suffice: Peter is a married, Jewish fisherman, who baptized Gentile adults after they were born again by believing a message of free grace; as such, Peter never lets anyone bow to him, and on occasion, Peter himself []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_676}teaches false doctrine and cusses (see Acts 10:24, 26, 42-44, 48, 15:11, 8:14--15; Gal. 2:11--12; Matt. 26:74).

The Catholic's answer to this crushing evidence is: "Oh, that's just YOUR interpretation." But his lame and imbecilic alibi does not accomplish anything at all, for none of the passages were interpreted---they were listed. The most damaging evidence to the Catholic additions is not what Protestants teach the Bible says, but what the Scriptures themselves actually say. There is no "interpretation" to the fact that Peter was a Jewish fisherman who was married and taught false doctrine and cursed and swore; the facts recorded attest that he was and did! Any judge, in any law court, in any country, would judge the facts as so, if he believed what he read was the fact.

Briefly, then, the Catholic system is the only system in the world that insists on appealing to the word of God for the authority to blaspheme the word of God. In other words, it is the only religious system in the world that is chronically pathological and psychotic in its attitude toward truth (John 17:17).

["All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth."]{.class_4258} Here is what the Roman is after---"ALL POWER!" Notice the "heaven and earth," which would mean that anyone capable of usurping the place of the Lord Jesus could claim spiritual power (Heaven) and temporal power (Earth). This is what the Roman bishops did in the sixth century; they appealed to Scripture to set up a church-system that was non-Scriptural. The Roman Catholic publication, [LIFE ]{.class_4257}magazine, has well put it by saying: "Roman Catholic policy\...sees the church as an authoritarian body, which rigidly, infallibly rules its members---though the Dutch, German, and French Liberals\...they want a return to the early Christian idea of the church as humble servant and insist that the council must define and face the new spiritual and moral issues of today." The statement immediately "lifts the veil off the Vatican," for the Early Church and "Christian idea" are denominated "the tradition of Paul!" And the holy Scriptures state, dogmatically, that the church was a mystery revealed to Paul, and Paul only, and that by direct revelation---not by tradition (Gal. 1--2; Eph. 2--3).

["All power given unto me in heaven and in earth"]{.class_4258} is what the pope claims for himself and his Roman denomination! This is []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_677}stolen by claiming "delegated authority," and this delegated authority can be proved in a moment to be UNCHRISTIAN by the glaring and continual fact that the Roman denomination majors in ANTI-SCRIPTURAL, ANTI-BIBLICAL tradition which opposes the plain, clear, simple statements of doctrine, found in the Bible. The Catholic pleads "complexity," "obscurity," "difficulty," and "lack of learning" for his ignorance of the word, when the trouble is actually the trouble with any Bible ignoramus---SIN (John 3:18--20). His need for an "authoritative church" to "interpret the Scriptures" for him is little more than the need of a condemned man, needing a crooked lawyer to get him through a loophole in the law. Whenever he is faced with the truth of the law, he turns it over to his lawyer to twist so he will not be held accountable.[ "Full well ye reject the commandments of God, that you may keep your own tradition"]{.class_4258} (Mark 7:6--13).

["All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth"]{.class_4258} is absolute, unlimited power. On earth, this power is delegated. The first delegation, as usual, is to the one Jesus addressed in Simon Peter when He first mentioned the church (Matt. 16:16--18); the name of this one is the original, first and foremost, ecclesiastical authority of all time: SATAN (see Matt. 16:23). In their haste to get temporal power, the Roman cardinals and bishops have forgotten that Romans 13:1--4 includes the devil himself, first and foremost (see Matt. 4:9). The fifth and sixth century Roman bishops made the disastrous mistake of thinking that since Augustine was right in his millennial teaching, that the "kingdom had come," and therefore, it was all right to use the Bible to claim temporal power and authority and rulership over the earth system itself, controlled by Satan (1 John 2:15, 5:19). What God intended to be a group of "faithful" Christians (Matt. 13:31) turned into a buzzard's roost for "the faithful" (Matt. 13:32); meaning those enslaved, religious perverts subject to a Satanic dictatorial usurper, who was never FAITHFUL or Scriptural in the true sense a day in his life.

Millennialism is the issue, always has been, and always will be until the "King" returns. Wherever and whenever the amillennial or postmillennial systems are adopted, there follows militant attempts in the realm of politics to gain control over the unregenerate world-[]{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_678}system (2 Tim. 4:10). Catholics and Reformers alike fall for the bait. They use Scripture for the purpose of obtaining these ends. (See publication: [The Kingdom of God Versus The Kingdom of Heaven,]{.class_4257} 1969.)

["All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth"]{.class_4258} means what it says and says what it means. To properly evaluate the statement, Scripture with Scripture is needed on what constitutes "power," the types of power available, and the kinds delegated to the various components of the universe. The Roman "Papa" never takes any of this into consideration. Every statement printed by the Knights of Columbus on "Power" assumes from the start that the Roman Catholic "mother" church is the supreme guide, director, ruler, nourisher, controller, and dictator of every human being on the face of the earth, and every government on the face of the earth. If this is stretching the point, then one should read the publications and stop criticizing statements that he is too ignorant to appreciate. See Knights of Columbus publications: [The Bible is NOT our Sole Guide, The Way to Everlasting Life\...the Catholic Church]{.class_4257} (???), [The Infallible Church, What do you mean Only one True Church, A Short Story of the Popes.]{.class_4257} \[See [Rome---The Great Private Interpreter,]{.class_4257} 1969.\] The publications reveal clearly that Catholics claim for themselves, and their church, the exact power of Matthew 28:18-- ["All power."]{.class_4258} No Communist ever claimed divine sanction for his devilment; he merely appealed to Marx, Trotsky, and Lenin.

"All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth."

1\. All power is from God (Rom. 13:1--6).

2\. This power is given to His Son (John 5:20, 22--23, 26).

3\. The Son delegates this power throughout the universe. The first to receive POWER is Satan (Heb. 2:14; Luke 4:6).

4\. The next to receive POWER are principalities and powers under Satan (Eph. 6:12; Rev. 13:17). (By over-looking these first two truths, the modernist assumes that Satan is "God the Father" \[2 Cor. 4:4\]. The Catholic assumes that POWER is always a GOOD thing, if it is religious or "churchy" \[2 Cor. 11:12--14\]. The Buddhist assumes that any contact with the "presence" or the "power," is transcendental in the GOOD sense and, therefore, highly desirable. All reject the Bible revelation on the matter.)

5\. The next to receive power are human rulers and leaders (Rom. 13:1--6). Their power is physical and political power to control economics, laws, law enforcement, and affairs of State.

6\. The next power is SPIRITUAL and is given to born-again believers who have been regenerated by the Holy Spirit (not water!!---Eph. 1:19; John 1:12, 2 Cor. 6:7). (The Catholic insertion of the word "baptism" in John 3:5, where only the word ["water"]{.class_4258} appears is typical of the private interpretation of a church no more infallible in interpretative matters than Bridey Murphy's Scotch Terrier.)

7\. This spiritual power is given first to Simon Peter, before Pentecost, in a prophetic form, to give him authority to open two doors---the kingdom of Heaven to the Jew (Acts 2) and the Gentile (Acts 10); (see notes on Matt. 16:19). The "remitting and retaining" is given to more than fifteen disciples (see notes under Matt. 16:19; 18:18). Disciplinary power within the local church is given to all Christians (2 Cor. 5--6) with the ruling body being married bishops (1 Tim. 3), teaching deacons (1 Tim. 3), labouring elders (1 Pet. 5; 1 Tim. 5), and praying, Bible-believing Christians---not tradition-following "catholics!"

Now, these Bible facts, as listed above, are incontestable to anyone who believes what the Bible says, the way it says it. Until now, the Catholic response through the years has been to alter the meaning of each passage presented to oppose its own teaching; after 1,500 years of private interpretation running CONTRARY to Scripture, Rome has at last produced the hybrid monstrosity of Matthew 13:32, to which the ecumenical birds are now "flocking." Birds of a feather, flock together.

In the early years of the church, where no real Scriptural problems presented themselves (except definitions of the person and nature of the Lord Jesus Christ or the Trinity), the "catholic" could pass undetected, but once Constantine played "puss-in-the-corner" with the Roman bishop, a whole series of verses had to be produced to prove that the Roman bishop was the authoritative ruler of the Mediterranean churches. This issue summoned forth every verse in the Bible that dealt with the Church---what constitutes it, who has authority in it, how much authority it has, etc. As verse after verse was produced to prove Paul's revelation of the Body was []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_680}the true church, it had to be opposed with the Scripture that proved the Jewish church of Simon Peter, with water baptism as an essential (Acts 2:38), was the true body. Back and forth through the Bible ran the bishops from Dan to Beersheba, in an effort to maintain political and economic control of the body of Bible believers. Sooner or later, they came face to face with 1 Timothy 3, 5; 1 Peter 5; Acts 15; 1 Corinthians 5--6, and other passages. Once confronted with these, the Roman pontiff slipped quietly back to Matthew 16:16--18 and 28:18 to derive authority for changing all the other verses; this was done by privately interpreting the verses to mean what Rome wanted them to mean, instead of what they said. Verses privately interpreted by Rome now number into the thousand, and with the "new versions" changing several hundred more verses, the twentieth century is witnessing the spectacle of a church with no authority, using a Bible that is not the Holy Bible, to claim all authority over "Bible believers!" It is a wild situation (Rev. 17--18).

["All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth."]{.class_4258} It means what it says, the way it says it. To find where this power was delegated and to whom it was given, the Scriptures must be searched, comparing Scripture with Scripture. Rome is totally unqualified to do this job, for she has obviously, already, flatly rejected a hundred plain, clear, definite, simple passages dealing with her own religious set-up. Whatever church Rome is, she certainly is not connected with the New Testament revelation given to Paul (Eph. 1--4). Paul's "church" maintains doctrines that do NOT contradict other passages in the Bible itself. Paul's church calls no religious leader "father"; it baptizes no babies; its leaders are all married; it never quotes the Apocrypha; it never claims temporal power in this age; it awaits a "catching out," instead of a "bringing in"; it ignores Peter's pre-Acts 15 teachings; it has no nuns, no monks, no monasteries, no buildings, no property, no stocks, bonds, armies, navies, police forces, no councils, no vestments, no "sacraments," no rosaries, bells, candles, holy water, beads, images, prayers to saints, purgatory, indulgences, or popes. In short, the New Testament church is as far removed from Rome's ecclesiastical dictatorship as house-to-house personal work is removed from a Mardi Gras. Anyone who does not know this is either under the age []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_681}of accountability or unregenerate or a rank, hypocritical rascal. Or to turn a phrase, "if any man say these churches are the same, let him be anathema" (cursed!---Prov. 26:2).

28:19 Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:

20 Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world. Amen.

The "Great Commission" is found here, and it is taken for granted by 95 percent of Christendom (the body of people who profess to believe that something like the Bible should be half-heartedly followed in a vague way) that this commission is the present commission to the church, in this age.

Bullinger, O'Hair, and Stam have pointed out some difficulties with the "commission," however, which should not be overlooked.

1\. It is not the same Commission as Mark 16:15--18, contrary to the teaching of the non-instrumental Campbellites. (Mark 16 is in Jerusalem before the appearance in Galilee and is followed in vs. 19 by the ascension itself, upon the return from Galilee. Matthew 28 is in Galilee on a mountain, and the Lord does not ascend upon giving the Commission. )

2\. The Commission includes ["Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you,"]{.class_4258} which would mean footwashing, healing the sick, cleansing lepers, raising the dead, not taking up an offering, going to Israel only, and keeping the Sabbath, plus pork-abstaining, no haircuts, no shaves, and the Sermon on the Mount (see Lev. 11; Matt. 5--7; John 13; and Matt. 10).

3\. No one can find a clear statement of Trinitarian baptism anywhere in the book of Acts, and no one can find the eleven "going into all the world"; they stay at Jerusalem (Acts 8:1--3, 14, 25).

4\. Peter, James, and John wind up preaching to "Jews only," not the nations (Gal. 2; Matt. 28:19--20).

5\. ["The end"]{.class_4258} (Matt. 28:20) is nearly always found referring, not to the end of the Church Age, but to the end of the Tribulation---see []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_682}Matthew 24:13; Hebrews 3:6, 14, etc.

With this many difficulties involved in the text, it is only natural that Rome and the Reformers would grab it like a drowning man in order to keep asserting the political authority of their ecclesiastical organizations. Note: the passage is handmade for ["teaching,"]{.class_4258} instead of PREACHING; ["baptizing,"]{.class_4258} instead of winning to Jesus Christ; and proselyting instead of converting (Matt. 23:15). Trust Rome to base all essential doctrines on controversial passages and then refuse to explain the passages in the light of the clear passages found elsewhere! (This is typical of all heretical teaching, and it is the consistent practice of Bible-quoting hypocrites throughout the centuries---they begin with an obscure passage to prove their own opinion and then refuse to face the clear passages that would change the interpretation of the obscure passage they picked.)

The word ["world"]{.class_4258} is the word for an "age" or a period of time, which is apparent to anyone without the Greek, for the twentieth-century presses use the word continually to mean this. The [AV]{.class_4257}, therefore, is the correct translation, according to popular usage, and the useless reference to ["aion"]{.class_4257} and ["kosmos"]{.class_4257} is just another attempt to divest the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 of its authority.

Positively, and speaking inspirationally, there are several great spiritual truths found in the passage.

1\. The Trinity has one name---the Lord. The three persons, Father, Son, and Spirit, all claim this one name in Philippians 2:11; 2 Corinthians 3:17; and Jude 5. Therefore, baptism ["in the name of the Lord"]{.class_4258} (Acts 10:48) is not the baptism of Acts 2:38, given to Israel.

2\. The ["therefore"]{.class_4258} in Matthew 28:19 is tied with the ["All power"]{.class_4258} in the preceding verse, showing that the commission is for spiritual power to do a spiritual job. This uninterprets the Catholic understanding of the pope's power, for there is no reference in the context to physical power "on earth," but only power for teaching and baptizing Gentiles, in accordance with what Christ taught. Christ did not teach temporal power, and stated so clearly in front of a Roman "pope" (John 18:36).

3\. Verse 20 teaches that we are to obey Him (not Rome) and follow His commandments (not Rome's), and He (NOT Rome) is with []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_683}us to the end of the age. Since Rome crucified Him, then that is the last place on earth any of us with any "Christianity" would desire to be.

Doctrinally speaking, the commission has a direct application to Jewish evangelism of the nature of Jonah's and Paul's, which will take place in the Tribulation, at the end of the age. (Compare Matt. 10; John 1; Acts 9; and Rev. 7, 11.) The commission spoken is correct for this age and would remain unaltered until Acts 7. (Matthew, Acts, and Hebrews! That's where they bust their necks!) After Acts 7, the commission is altered by a gospel of salvation by grace, through faith in a blood atonement (Acts 8, the eunuch), for the Gentiles (Acts 10), and is explained as a free gospel of grace---plus NOTHING---in order to be justified (Acts 11, 15). It winds up as a gospel which Paul calls ["my gospel"]{.class_4258} (Rom 2:16; 1 Cor. 15:1--6), and it includes also the mystery of the revelation of the Body of Christ---the True Church.

But all of this is purposely obscured from the Catholic Bishops and Priests and the amillennial followers of Calvin and Luther. Nor is the senseless alibi that, "Oh, you think you're right and everyone else is wrong" of any avail, for the clear passages are so clear that it is apparent that anyone disagreeing with them has a blind prejudice. The obscure passages (as in Matthew, Acts, and Hebrew) are so constructed that any interpretation of them will be wrong that begins with the assumption that Christ is not coming back to set up an earthly, literal, visible, Messianic, Jewish, Davidic Kingdom.

Thus God makes foolish the wisdom of this world. He purposely puts the key to Bible interpretation within the covers of the Bible itself; and if that were not lethal enough, He puts the first key in the covers of the last book, which is immediately taken to be "symbolic," "figurative," and "highly apocalyptic," etc. Scholarship, Religion, Science, and Rome contrive (cooperatively) to throw out the key; once thrown out, they are left to their own private interpretations which contradict the passages they are trying to interpret.

Having arrived at the end of Matthew's gospel, we are struck again with the importance of properly placing the Jew in God's plan for the Gentile nations. Matthew is a Jew, who writes a Jewish Gospel to Jews. Twenty-six chapters of his gospel are devoted to God's Old []{#I9B3O0KMSoa6V5WRoptyGw4089.xhtml_page_684}Testament dealing with Israel, with special reference to the application of the New Covenant at the end time of God's restoration of Israel. Not one verse out of four in the Gospel has any doctrinal bearing on the Church or the Christian, and there are no references to Rome except in the bad sense. Prooftexts, wrested and extracted from Matthew, are responsible for thousands of unscriptural ideas circulated in the twentieth century by the Catholic, Reformer, and Liberal; among these, none are so damaging, un-Christian, socialistic, and pagan as those extracted from "the sermon on the mount." Matthew's Gospel, in the text of the [King James]{.class_4257} [AV]{.class_4257} 1611, proves to be the text authorized by the Lord for distribution among English-speaking people (and English is now the universal language), and time after time the Received Text of the Syrian-type (Byzantine) has been proved superior to the corrupt and mutilated Greek texts of the [ASV, RSV]{.class_4257}, and [RV ]{.class_4257}(Egyptian, Hesychian-type texts). The [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 is undoubtedly the correct text, and the Bible believer may rest securely on it with the firm and resolute conviction that any and all deviations from it are by religious leaders who resent what it says, either because they are unable to understand it or because what it says is an insult to their theology.

Matthew's gospel was written by Matthew, under the inspiration of the Holy Spirit. It contains no contradictions or errors, as preserved in the [AV ]{.class_4257}1611 text, and its difficulties lie in the fact that it (as Acts and Hebrews) is a transitional book, with progressive revelation taking place in it, as God's dealings slack-off with Israel and begin with the Gentiles.

In short, it, as the other sixty-five books, is the infallible, final, and absolute authority in all matters of faith and practice on which it touches. It is always to be considered of superior authority to the creeds, teachings, sayings, fiats, dogmas, councils, bulls, and declarations of any church, anywhere, at any time, where said teachings cross, contradict, distort, nullify, or revise any of its own inspired words. Amen and Amen.
